reviews.review_text: This course sucks but I have no complaints really about Dr. Li, it's been decent with him and he clearly works very hard to make students understand. He is accessible and sincere and covers material at a reasonable pace, and is happy to answer questions. Course is insanely work-heavy (take fewer credits) but all versions of it are.
man his midterm is soooooooooooo hard
he's a good and very friendly guy, a little bit of stuttering but its fine.

I am in CMPT201 and while this is a freaking tiring hard course, it's clearly intended to be that way and not a reflection of Prof Li. He really cares about explaining in lecture, and does a lot of demos and reviews of quizzes/assignments. Overall a really positive experience; this course is a beast though. But so far Dr Li has made it better.

AVOID THIS PROF AT ALL COSTS. DO NOT TAKE. Wait for Dr Brian or Steve to teach this class. This lord Stutters, unable to hear clearly, does not know what he is talking about. Be prepared to self study. Midterm was fair but final exam had a curveball with some random out of topic question requiring CMPT210 knowledge. Horrid prof.

Lecture attendance doesn't matter—fewer than 15 of 100+ students show up. The lectures just read the slides. Exams don't test understanding or coding, only memorization. Assignments take 10–20 hours and are worth 70%, but if you fail the exam average (15% each), they won't help. One bad exam can ruin your grade—drop early if caught off guard.
This prof will help you and guide you through your obstacles. However those obstacles aren't particularly difficult.

LOVE HIM! Really nice, really helpful and always there to help you out. Always available through emails and office hours. Would answer hundreds of questions after class, cuz there would literally be a line of students rushing up to him and he was always so nice about it.

It's a really helpful course of basic HTMLCSS knowledge and skill. And Stephen is doing really well as a new teacher here. All HTMLCSS video casts are uploaded on Youtube which is extremely helpful. Python is a little bit tough, but he will give you many resources to guide you learn yourselves. Nice professor, excellent course, highly recommanded

He is a new teacher and doing extremely well in teaching. He genuinely cares about his students and wants them to do well. If you've got any questions about the course content, he will very willing to help. Sent him multiple emails asking for help, he replied to every single one of them in a lot of detail.

excellent prof, he's wants all his students to succeed. finishes lecture 10 mins early but uses those 10 mins as time to help students stuck on assignmentslabs. he's very clear on what is expected on the midtermfinals as long as you attend lecture and do well on the assignments.. this class will be a GPA booster ;)

He is helpful and nice to student. He will extend assignments and have passion towards teaching. Best professor ever in SFU, if you pay attention in lecture and can easily get minimum easy B or above. Highly recommended!!!!!
Nice guy! and easy take!
Is clearly a tech guy who never learned to teach. Poorly planned out course where he read off of slides and made sloppy mistakes
easy course....! and fair marking
really easy going prof. Cheat sheets are allowed for midterms and final. he sometimes finishes lecture early but would stay for students to ask questions. very awesome class with him.

super nice prof super easy midterm! get cheat sheets for both midterm and final exam. Usually finishes lectures early but he would stay for students to ask questions. Very awesome class, very awesome prof.

Geoff is a really good person, but a horrible lecturer. He's very slow, doesn't explain things well in a lecture setting (he's much better one-on-one) and because of these reasons a lot of students aren't taught the important content that show up on the exams. If you're taking 121, take it with another prof if at all possible.

Took this course in the summer and it was a disaster. His lectures are extremely boring and slow. The exams are extremely hard with mt 59%avg no scaling and final ~55% avg before scaling. Does not know how the final will scale by this point. Final+mt is worth 60%. No practice for the final. Prairie learn questions were not similar to the final.
Outstanding professor, explains things extremely well!

Excellent Professor, taught the content well and took time to make sure to answer questions after some hard concepts. went to his office hours and he was patient and answered my questions in a nice manner. Although there were a few assignments that was tough, the rest and the exams were alright as long as you were listening and doing the practice.

This guy is the least responsive instructor I've ever seen. He gives too long C++ assignments and does not care about his students. The assignments are almost the same as CS 225 of Illinois University. He is also quite rude when you go to his office hours. Avoid taking his course at all cost. There are many other good instructors in UBC.
Geoffery explains complicated problems really well, I really enjoyed his lectures.

Professor Tien was an absolute delight. He taught my APSC160 class at UBC and he always made sure to go through the clicker questions afterwards (solving them) so we understood the content. The first half of the course (which he taught) was enjoyable and made sense.
Interesting and fun labs. Lectures give sufficient information. Homework is completely unexplained by the lecture notes. Midterms and final can be very hard, sometimes unreasonably.

Geoffrey was an absolute legend. The man gives the most in depth, comprehensive replies to every question whether it is a tough question or a simple question. He does an amazing job explaining every concept and his Clicker questions are very thought provoking.

Geoffrey Tien could stand to be more engaging with his classes. His lectures were incredibly boring, I found myself falling asleep many times. The course is a hard one so not falling behind is important but the style of teaching made it much harder to endure.

He is the best cs prof at UBC I had ever met. His lecture ppt and notes are clear and pretty helpful. He makes a very hard course easier. I definitely recommend you to take his classes. Really nice guy.
His lectures are really good and he is very organized. He's also decently funny and very friendly. There really aren't any cons to him. two thumbs up
Geoff is amazing. His lecture slides are perfect and super helping. He makes every concept very clear and straightforward. He is by far the best instructor in Comp Sci department.

Honestly, you probably won't get a better professor than Geoff for 259. He is VERY organized and is also a nice guy in terms of marking/scaling/accessibility. Pretty funny too! The midterms can be a bit vague/difficult, but the material itself is not that hard.

Geoff is an amazing instructor. Although his midterms and exams can be slightly vague and a bit hard at times, he puts his very best in explaining concepts in lectures (do go for his lectures). He is pretty generous with his office hours (do go for his hours, it's pretty empty) and is very patient in making sure you understand thoroughly.

Good professor. Got through the material, sometimes got lost in his own examples and lecture slides. For 221, lots of online material and lots of other resources to learn the material, so lectures weren't ultra important. He was very helpful in office hours, and a pretty nice guy. NOTE: Cinda is better though.

One of the better profs I've had in my time at UBC. Lectures are very clear, very engaging with humor that's actually funny and not a desperate call for engagement. Prepares you really well for the exams.

Superb instructor! Very clear lectures with great examples and explanations. He definitely knows his stuff and can answer any questions that come up. CPSC221 as a course is fairly heavy in material by nature so I don't blame Geoff for the difficulty. Lectures are often very funny as well!

Geoff was really great! I personally thought he was one of the best lecturers I've had at UBC. He's very clear and concise with concepts and what you need to know for the course, and he patiently answers any questions that arise in class. In office hours, he'll be very determined to help you solve any problems you have!

Geoff's no doubt a great prof, and though some of my peers think he's a little monotoned, I find his lectures to be very clear and I love how he explains the difficult concepts to you as if you were an 8 year old. Super easy to follow, super easy to understand. He's accessible during his office hours, answers questions very patiently.

His lectures are very clear and concise, and he is always willing to answer questions. So the lectures become quite easy to follow. He is nice and helpful in his office hours(4 hrs per week). Take 221 with Geoff!!!!
One of the best computer science profs ever. I wish he would go over more binary search tree's though.
STAND NAME BAD LECTURER STAND MASTERGEOFF
I love him.
All around solid professor. Does a great job in lecture of explaining concepts thoroughly. Grading criteria is very detailed and fair. Would take his course again.

Grading for assignments and exams were fair - final exam was difficult and midterm was easy. He is late when releasing grades for assignments and exams. Somewhat disorganized, as there were times during lecture when he wasn't certain of something he was driving to prove. He is helpful if you seek his help outside of class and he's really funny.

Not very good with time management during lectures. Spends a lot of time with simple topics then blasts through the hard topics with no introduction. Spends most of his time going through proofs instead of actual examples , so if you want to do well on the exam, the textbook exercises are way more helpful.

Not prepared for lectures and spends too much time going through irrelevant proofs that aren't helpful for assignments/exams. During one lecture, he stopped during a proof because he did not remember how to work it out. The assignments/exams were fairly easy, but lecturing skills were poor.

The exams and assignment questions were fair, but he is in a bad lecturer. He's disorganized, as he gets lost in his own proofs and doesn't understand his slides. Each set of slides seem to have at least one mistake. He does not release grades until far after the exams/assignments are complete. But, he is accessible outside of class for extra help.

The assignments were fair, I guess, but the exams are ridiculous difficult. I'm not sure what I wrote on that final or how I ended up passing. I've never walked away not even understanding the exam questions.

The only thing I can say is that if you're having trouble, go to his office hours. He can be a little disorganized during lecture but I guarantee you'll be able to clear things up if you talk to him in private. Yes, his exams are hard but they get scaled.

I believe that he can be a good professor, but I think he wasn't able to manage time properly given that it was a 6 week summer course. Because of this, much of the material was rushed and I don't think he was able to gauge the level of the class come examination time and ended up giving us an insanely difficult final.

Even when dealing with family problems during the semester, Geoffrey still finds a way to cater to students before exams and assignment due dates with extra office hours. He's the type of professor that is more than willing to explain things again during office hours if you're stuck on an assignment or don't understand a concept. Huge respect!

Geoff is a good professor. Had him as a sessional lecturer at UBC for our intro algorithms course. The midterm was easy but the final was hilariously difficult - I'm not sure if half the class passed before scaling. Just know how to apply the material - you can expect many coding questions on his exams.

Assignments and exams are tough but he is there for you .If in doubt, ask him questions via E-mail, attend office hours. Stays well over his office hours if students need help. He is the kind of professor we need in our department.

Geoff delivers good lectures and is helpful in his office hours. His assignments are above average difficulty (compared to John Edgar's which are viewable online). His exams are quite difficult and he gives you no way to prepare for them. Overall, he teaches well with kind of hard assignments, but asks unreasonably difficult questions on the exam.
Assignments are ok but final is hard. Don't expect that he will be grading on a curve
Good teaching skill but the final is hard for this semester. One of my friend said it was easy last year.
Geoff is a very clear lecturer. He also gives good help in his office hours. He is a difficult professor though (tough tests and assignments). He'll teach you well, but he'll expect a lot out of you.

Geoff is a very good professor. He's clear in lecture and in his expectations for what you should know. He's pretty quick to help you via E-mail too. His exams and assignments are neither super difficult not easy, they were pretty reasonable in my opinion.

The course itself is pretty hard. His lecture was not the best, but it was acceptable, given the materials were extremely hard. Couple professors from YouTube were more clear. 1/3 of people still failed the midterm after he changed the total mark from 35 to 32. Surprisingly almost no one failed the final, guess he either bumped up everyone grades.

He is a really nice person. USE HIE OFFICE HOUR, it will help you a lot. Do the assignment ASAP and ask any question you don't know. midterm and final are ok. Do all the lecture note's questions, that is the key to pass this class.
Helpful when asked. Assignment criteria is clear. Little feedback for marks. Rarely open to understanding an answer from another point of view. Lectures are often dull.

Very amicable and helpful in one-on-one interactions, especially during office hours. However, lectures can get somewhat monotonous, which might just be a reflection of the course material. Make sure to clarify questions during exams because some of them can be worded ambiguously.

He has been really helpful during office hour and explained materials well. Also the marking criteria are well justified. The only thing that might catch you off guard is the amount of effort each assignment requires. Try to do them ASAP and ask for help if you are stuck somewhere. Midterm and final are alright with a few tricky questions.

GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS! Probably one of the nicest and passionate guys I've met so far in the field (I saw him stay for hours beyond his scheduled hours if students needed the help). My only issue with him is the range in difficulty of the assignments. They are either very easy or painfully hard. Start the assignments ASAP so you can ask for help.

Pay close attention in lecture and you'll save yourself time later on the assignments and on the exams. Do the problems he provides in class. Geoffrey is very helpful during office hours and after lecture. While the grading scheme is not easy, he is very fair and will go over your assignments with you to show what you need to work on.

She was kind, but the course wasn't that great. However, her exams were mostly based on the assignments and practice questions she provided. It was difficult in the sense that 80% of the students failed, and the highest grade was a B.
Although her exams are easier, her practices are not enough for a beginner. Take CMPT130 with Yonas if possible as he provides lots of practices that help you pass his exams.

While I was taking this course with this instructor, it was clear to me that if I'm not writing the code in her way, that means I'm totally wrong. Also, her grading is so bad; one time I made two mistakes, and I received less than half the mark. I do not recommend taking this course with this instructor.
good job!!
dont take
sure
Sometimes She doesn't explain things well. If you're taking class with her, you better study the ppt and watch some coding on youtube. \
So strict

She doesn't explain the theory behind each code she just goes through roughly, but I am a fxxking rookie!! and this makes my learning very difficult. DO NOT TAKE HER!!! It is a waste of money, sleep, brain and time. Finally, if you ask her a question, it is better a small issue. otherwise she will mock you with her eyes.
Do not asking question if you don't want to be in her black list.
Never take this teacher Worst
never take this prof
i will not recommend taking this class with her.

Started this course with no background in programming. I just listened to every word she said, went to every lecture, did every homework and even found some programming projects on my own. Please, read the chapters before coming to class and ask questions. Thank you Jiang Ye!
I made a few fake reviews back in august so that i could take 135 with yonas
Follow her advices and you will learn a lot. They did a great job for first year computer science courses.
I contacted her about reviewing my final exam and grades, and she changed my grade up from c- to c+ after finding her own mistake. I think she give grade fairly.
Will give you grade u deserve

She is nice prof. She helps people out and is funny. She has really inspired me to learn more about coding and I will not have got this grade without her. Professor Jiang, thank you, for the help, for the challenge, for the inspiration. Take her and you will not regret!

She is very strict with coding style format and all, she is good if you are really good at coding, she don't explain she just revise and this makes it difficult for new coders I would HIGHLY NOT RECOMMEND her it's just waste of money and time.If you do not do well or fail in first quiz, you wouldn't pass any other quiz. Please don't take her.

I wouldn't recommend her, she is very strict regarding coding style, and her teaching skills are low, according to her 80% of the class fails which you will very well understand after taking her class. She deducts marks for small mistakes, even though your code is running and the logic is correct.

Very awful communication. Her homework and exams are not difficult, but there are a lot of strange requirements (though it may seem useless) like coding style and format. Failure to comply with these requirements will result a low grade of your work.
Jiang's much better in those in-person classes, don't choose Yonas who has much much more homework than jiang trust me don't even think about yonas

She is unnecessarily strict. Even if you do the whole coding and make a simple mistake, she will grade you 1 or 2 out of 25 or 30 marks. I do not suggest anyone new to computer science take her course. If you already know how to code, you could take her course. She does not inspire the students to work harder.

If you never knew anything, ANYTHING about c++, I suggest you not to take this yet. Her teaching isn't good enough! Bad explanation and too strict on codings. I can barely catch up to her lecture so I used Youtube to get good grades instead.

Once you don't do well in the first two quizzes you can't pass any other. would believe you cheated in the rest and upgrade world you get full marks in. tough grader cant explain her work and gives what she cant solve. don't take if you have never coded before. I have a good coding background and still got a d. Got an A in with the next instructor.
Probably the worst professor i have studied under. No sense of communication. Toughest examinations.

Ive coded since High school and I even made an app for my friends. This class is just a basic class but prof make it hard. She didnt explain well plus when you ask question she is angry about that. Friendly advice: Dont take her class tough grader plus cannot explain well.
exams are much tougher than work done in class and there is so much chance you'll repeat the course

Very tough grader. If the code working but the output wrong, she will mark you nearly zero. If the code output right and the logic didn't satisfy her. She will half your grade. This is my second time taking it and I don't even know If I can make it through this I feel hopeless because of the way she grading. I tried so hard. She doesn't effort.

do not doubt yourself, because you are right !!!! she really does not know how to teach, i study by myself for whole semester. and hard to communicate!!!! run away as fast as you can! unless you want to waste your money!!!
RUN AWAY AS SOON AS POSSIBLE !!!! Do not choose her class, so terrible.

Bad in English, Hard to communicate. I have started writing program since secondary, she makes every hard, and she is so stubborn that you must follow her rules. She doesn't even let us use float data type.
Wost prof I ever had. Avoid her at ALL costs.

I have been coding since before high school so this course should have been a cakewalk, but she made it difficult. For example, according to her 2.4 is not a float data type, it is only double. Her coding knowledge is very limited and she will not accept anything other than what she knows. NOT AT ALL RECOMMENDED!

She is the weirdest kind of human on this planet. she is a racist and has a language problem as being from China. you could not really understand her. there is a lot of communication gap. please don't waste your semester and money with her.
She is really caring to her students .

Honestly shes alright, if you put in a bit of work you'll get a good mark. She has a little difficulty explaining concepts clearly and she doesn't like to repeat herself especially if she explained it in detail already but gives A LOT of feedback on your work. Does teach you good coding style - useful in future

Recommend to have some basic knowledge of C++, it's really easy in Week 1&2, but it'll be different after that. She got a heavy accent, need some time to get used to it. Her voice may be aggressive sometimes and may misunderstand your question. But she would still try to explain it, so I still encourage u to ask. And don't copy the codes.

First of all, I know that most students find it hard to understand what she's saying. I agree with that. That is why she gives us the textbook to read. The way she teach is not enough for me to understand. However, the way she grade and show me where I got my question wrong is a big thumbs up.

Worst instructor I've had so far. I think she's good at C++ herself but has a hard time explaining things clearly to the class. Get ready to do a lot of reading by yourself cuz her lectures won't help. Attendance is important and she expects you to code like a pro even at a beginner programming course like CMPT130. Avoid taking her class if you can

The worst prof ever. Doesn't teach anything properly. I got 70% in the midterm and still failed the final and the course. Because she doesn't teach and if you ask her questions she shouts at you rather than explaining.

Worst Teacher Ever !!!!! I swear if reading out from the notes and not explaining anything at all was the only qualification required to become a teacher , i could teach the whole class better than her. She should Retire early. If your dad has a lot of money then go for Jiang Ye , else Mr . Yonas is the best CMPT130 teacher to go for.
Just don't, trust me. I believe she does not know what she is teaching...
Good professor.
good teacher
good, youll get a decent grade if you did all exercises.
if you work hard, you will get a great mark

She might know how to code but has no idea how to explain it to the students whatsoever. Her explanations don't make sense. She lacks what it takes to explain a concept to someone. And you're fricked if you have doubts because she doesn't take them.
WORST TEACHER EVER
Helpful
She is so inspirational and always help you to get your goal in life as well as very helpful inside and outside the class.
She is the best prof. that I ever took in my life. I really want to take more classes with her.

I can say that i have never given a prof a bad score. I am in SFU and we are going through OOP again. The only thing i will say to you is that she is an incompetent teacher and person. Do not take her class if possible, if you do, do not confuse yourself with her classes just read the book and online sources so that you can learn.

If you can take other prof's cmpt classes, please do not think about her. Her exercise is very little and useless for the tests and exams. And I think she do not know when should teach what, she just give everything without ordered. if you are taking the course for first time, you will not understand what she is talking about.
She is very knowledgeable but she doesn't have a grading criteria and sucks at teaching. If you can avoid, don't take her class.
Tough exams :(
jut don't take her course, it will make you having headache

She's a great teacher. I appreciate her teaching on indenting and coding habits. They help you produce better quality codes. And guys, you need to read the book more and do more practice if you hope to get good in coding. No one can hold your hands and guide you step by step in university. You'll realize it the more programming courses you take.
she always said" you should read!!!" but never tell you what the problem of your code, and also yeil that" your codes are very wrong."
She is knowledgeable but she yells at students when ask questions. Not patient at all. Expects you to get everything in the first time and does not answer any questions. Im afraid of her.

Now One thing you need to know is that Jiang Ye is not going to baby you to pass her course. You are in University not High School. You need to listen and PAY ATTENTION. ALWAYS Ask questions. She only gets frustrated, when she has to re-explain something to those PARTICUlAR students who werent listening or paying attention. She is a great Prof
Worst prof i have had yet . I dont know how she has become a teacher

decent teacher. she is helpful. But this is a difficult course. Don't skip class or it will be extremely hard to catch up because she didn't provide much info on her lecture notes. you gotta read not only one but other books about computer design and that help you understand her lecture. if you can't, drop it or cmpt250 will let you down
Tbh I think she's ok. Don't really understand why all the hate. If you do well on her occasional tests, chances are you'll do well on the final.

She seems alright, a little tough on the grading but squeezes out the bad students. Give really good feedback. Study well and you'll do great. Uses sarcasm sometimes lmao. Funny if your first language is english or if you're really good in english. Has a lil broken english but was more then understandable for me. :) Overall would take herclassagain

Course material isn't that hard and she's really helpful and explains well enough. It's just that her midterm and quizzes take a long time to do and you're very short on time. And she's pretty harsh on the marking too. I managed to get an A+ though.
I don't know why I just got so low grade, before final I have still 78%, and the 2nd assignment is not clear in the description..
Avoid her she likes making the course more difficult then it actually is. Avoid her at no cost!!!
very bad. she doesn't know how to teach. I feel sorry for you if you have to take her class. she makes everything complicated than it should be.
Awful prof!! Haven't seen a prof so bad as her.Sadistic Woman, loves to scream on students.

She really cares and is very passionate about the course topics. Some people are mad about the final curve but that is just because everyone scored very high on assignments and there needed to be some grade distribution. Not everyone can get an A

I took 419, 310 and 376w with Angelica and she was great for all 3. This class in particular was the most interesting and we were given a lot of space for creativity with the group project. She is passionate about teaching and that translates really well to her lectures. Definitely recommend taking any course she is teaching.
Pretty good Prof, Cares and is thoughtful

Absolutely absurd grading criteria. Like 89+ will be an A-. Bruh. Prof herself is super overrated imo. She wasn't even able to answer challenging questions during the class. The material provided was easy and useless considering this is a 400 lvl course. Overall not too bad but she is just mediocre. Other 419 profs like Nick Vincent is far better.

CMPT376W is not a fun course. We barely learned anything related to writing, and the majority of the focus is on ethics. As a result, the course becomes "learn how to think in a correct way that won't get you canceled". In addition to this, she doesn't really have enough office hours. Which means reaching her is difficult.

The professor often struggled to explain the slides. Homework grading was severely delayed, with two weeks of no activity. The final exam had numerous errors requiring multiple corrections. Given the high ratings, I was very disappointed. Take the other professor because you can get a better grade, you have to do self-learning anyway.

The final exam was terrible. Although calculators were allowed, the exam had obscure questions, mistakes, and unclear wording that baffled even the TAs. It felt more like a test of our patience and endurance than of our understanding of the material.

Active answering on Piazza, lectures comprehensive. Exams more convoluted/confusing than necessary, other UD CS & math exams more straightfoward. Midterm: No calculator but had a long calculation w/ decimals. Final exam: Calculator allowed but asked obscure question, mistakes in questions, unclear wording that even TA was confused with when asked.

Angelica's class was decent and I'll cut her some slack since she tried to redo the course with new material. The course contents were pretty much identical to UCB. Overall the lectures were good and exams were fair apart from some final questions. Enjoyed my time in the course.

Garnered a lot of sympathy with performative caring (talks about reading RMP + Discord), but did little to actually help students. Idc that she uses someone else's slides. I do care that she didn't bother learning them first. Her reputation is propped up by her demeanour rather than teaching ability. It's clear she put in minimal effort.

Too much individual assignment weight (only 3 assignments, not enough topic covered) + TAs make them, so can be too hard, also require knowledge outside prerequisites. Lectures had mistakes sometimes, but overall organized selection helpful to introduce AI. Exams feel like trivia, need to memorize names and read textbook (some info not on slides!)
One of those petty instructors who refuses to post notes online because otherwise no one would come to their pointless lectures. This course was a waste of time and she only served to make it worse.

If you just want easier UD credits for grad, this is the course to take. It takes some work to get good grades yes, but you can literally coast (spending 3 hrs max / wk) to class avg. She is nice, but don't expect to get much out of her lectures. All borrowed material including proj (few). Wkly exercise = free marks. Again, EZ UD credits.
Angelica is awesome, you can tell she genuinely wants us to learn about the material
She tells you not to use ChatGPT for your assignments while she copied all her slides form Berkeley without referencing them. What does that tell you?

Dr. Lim presents the course material straightforwardly, and I can tell a lot of thought was put into developing the content. It only includes the relevant parts in a traditionally VERY content-heavy, outdated and boring course. Assignments/tests are graded on understanding- not memorization. Make sure you go over your math fundamentals. Fun class!

Dr. Lim is an excellent professor and I recommend 310 with her. The pre reqs aren't up to date as of now so be sure to have a little calculus and linear algebra but she has helped the class get up to date. Challenging topics so be ready to learn outside of class and use the resources she makes available. Dr. Lim cares about her students success

I'm just entirely amazed at the lazy negative comments for Prof Lim… it makes no sense… you're in CS… at SFU, people. Do you think it's easy? Ignore the negative course feedback if you're actually here to learn. For serious students who are actual adults: Prof Lim is extremely bright, she's very accessible if you actually ask her questions!

I came here to comment that our midterm for this course was the 29th so take the bad reviews leading up with a grain of salt, great prof imo and makes tough content manageable. We got to do weekly activities in place of an extra lecture and they help me a lot.

Love this class & prof. Content challenging but manageable. Follow lecture examples (versus just re-doing assignments/activities) for exams, my learning. Dr. Lim has entertaining, engaging lectures and integrates topics within the broader picture (industry, current research). Huge course, good to be familiar with topics.10/10 recommend.

The recent comments about Dr. Lim's instruction in CMPT 310 have been rather harsh. Dr. Lim has great expertise & is doing her best in taking a traditionally difficult/confusing course, and delivering it to us with modernity, clarity, and depth. She's clear, approachable, provides relevant resources. If you're taking 310, she is a solid choice.

The professor is kind and genuinely passionate about the subject, but she struggles to teach effectively. In one class, students had to explain content from her own slides to her. The slides themselves are taken from Berkeley/Stanford, and she often comes unprepared for lectures.

Seems like a nice person, but her 310 class is definitely not good. She can't explain the slides well (as they are copied from Stanford/Berkely), and attending lecture becomes essentially useless. 100% of my learning in this course has been from Youtube.

I was excited to take 310 with Angelica after seeing all her great reviews. She copies all her course materials from Stanford and Berkeley and does not know what she's talking about. She does not prepare for lectures and all her materials (including the ridiculous assignments) are made by the TAs. She only gets by with her "nice" personality.

Professor Lim is one of my favourite professors I've encountered at SFU. Assignments were a lot of work but taught you a lot. Exams were difficult but fair. Final project was great. She really cares about her students and is very approachable outside of class if you need any help. You can tell she's passionate about her work. 10/10 highly recommend
Angelica is a solid pick for 376. Fair exams and good delivery. No complaints here.
She's one of the best instructors in the computer science department. I felt lectures were much more tolerated with her. Will definitely take every course possible with her

Hearing Angelica lecture is such a pleasure, she clearly has so much passion for her subject and for getting people interested in programming! I would take any class she took, she really prioritizes explaining complex topics in relatable ways we can understand. Come to class because her code-along activities help you really learn and REMEMBER it!

If you have a chance to take a class with Dr. Lim, TAKE IT! CMPT120 is pretty easy in the first place, but that's only because Angelica makes learning so easy and enjoyable. She explains concepts clearly and codes examples in class, and is very approachable for office hours. Assignments and exams were very fair. Overall amazing prof.
Best professor hands down. I would take classes in the future that she teaches just to have her again.

Solid professor. The only thing I would wish for would be video recordings of the lectures as certain topics could use some clarification. 80% of your grade is split between the midterm, final exam and a coding project, so make sure to take those seriously.

Great teacher, lots of Python in class coding so do your reading before lectures. Test heavy, as 60% of your grade came from them but their difficulty is fair. Make sure you start weekly assignments and the final projects EARLY. I would've gotten an A in the course, but I bombed the final project because I procrastinated hard (14/60 lmao).

Dr.Lim is one of the best profs! She is so caring about her students and genuinely wants the best for you to succeed. All her lectures were well explained and she was always open to questions. Go to office hours, even if you have no questions. The course itself is not hard for beginners and the midterms and final were fair. Would recommend 100%

Honestly wonder if a better prof than Dr Lim exists at sfu. So caring and sweet, you'll like the class even if you're not a CS major. Explanations are done well enough that you don't need to read the online textbook but if you choose to, it's in full understandable detail and it's quick to read.

Great prof! She made programming enjoyable and l looked forward to attending each lecture. Her slides and audio recordings are always posted on Canvas, so anyone who misses a class can catch up. Assignments are also super fun!

Angelica Lim is an amazing professor, she is very clear and thorough with her explanations. The issue is the final exams and midterms for this course, which Angelica doesn't create. They're very far off from the class material and mock midterms given before each examination. Have a background in coding for this course, it is not beginner friendly.
Great and energetic prof. Very organized and lectures were easy to follow.

She is a very good professor and made programming very fun. Her lecture materials were very relevant to the weekly coding exercises but she wasn't very helpful when you ask her about content on the midterm since she doesn't make them. CMPT120 is very beginner-friendly but having any prior programming experience in the past will be helpful.

The most interesting elective I've taken but also the hardest (I had no coding experience). An intro class but I found it very hard to grasp the concepts. Assignments were helpful for learning but they also took longer than expected (5-10hrs). Angelica was very helpful and nice but lectures can be confusing at times. Tests were extremely difficult.

Very good prof! Teaches concepts very clearly, tries to make it easy for beginners, and is overall a very caring person. Make sure to read the assigned readings before class, as that helps you understand the lecture's topics better.

Very nice/understanding professor who's always available for help outside of class however her lectures were very different from assignments and midterms/finals. Also, she gave mock exams to prepare us but those did not reflect the difficulty of the actual exams whatsoever. Office hours with the TAs also sucked and were waste of time.

Never taken programming before but made it super manageable for me, used great metaphors, had a great time in all lectures. Would definitely take both class and prof again. Did a great job at accommodating students and answering programming/general questions. Super sweet.

She's really nice and makes an effort to teach at our level. One thing to be wary of are the tests as they don't 100% reflect what she teaches or assignments in terms of how hard they are. Otherwise, pretty solid. Oh and read the readings for sure.

Angelica Lim is an excellent prof no doubt! The class was really fun in terms of assignments but the examinations were extremely difficult. They provided mock exams yet the real exams didn't even reflect 1% of it. I wish they had provided better exams that actually reflected the level of difficulty we were used to and what they prepped us for.

honestly she's a rly nice prof. the class starts off relatively easy (at least for me) but rly gets challenging after the first midterm so make sure u start grinding lol. the TAs for my section were kinda unhelpful tbh but the prof has pretty good office hours and answered all my questions
Really great professor, everything is organized so well especially the readings. Excellent lectures and easy grading for assignments. Tests are pretty hard though. 100% would recommend.

Angelica is a great prof, 120 is a hard class but she made it a lot more easier to understand. Midterm was harder than I thought and nearly nothing similar to the mock she gave to us. Also some of the weekly assignments will took a long time to finish.
really good prof, wants to help her students. her weekly assignments are a bit tough, but not too bad. exams were pretty hard tho

Angelica really pulled through with office hours after each lecture to try and answer everyones questions. She tries to make it as easy as possible to get you to follow along in class, lectures are fun(for what compsci classes can be lol) and her tests are reflective of her assignments. Take her class - best prof in the program.

By far best prof and maybe teacher I've ever had. Super understanding, caring and knowledgeable. Some course work is challenging for new programmers like myself however very doable. As it was online, the course was done through canvas and it was the most organized and nice formatting I have ever seen. Also free textbook recourses so that's a plus.

A caring prof. Materials are conveyed well. Weekly homework 100% sets you up for mid term. I recommend watching the 4 hours Python video on YouTube, which covers over half of the entire CMPT120 course. She is how all profs should be like.

Was hesitant to take this class as I had no coding experience but Angelica is the ideal prof for an intro to coding. Lectures are done at a very manageable pace and you never feel overwhelmed afterwards. Keeping up with weekly practice is absolutely necessary to do well. If you can can do the assignments in a reasonable time, you will get an A
She was my best prof so far I loved the class with her. She also explained really well so if you listen well you don't have to read too much. Take a class with her without any second mind.
She's good at explaining concepts, especially for beginner coders.
No complaints here. She was nice and seemed to use feedback to adjust the course based on how the class doing. Overall, would take a class with her again.

Professor Lim is hands-down one of the best teachers I have had so far in my life. She is caring, adds humor when teaching, goes over examples well, and even shows real-life applications for what we can do with computer science. If you are looking to learn more about computer science or want to get involved with it, look no further than this prof.

A very sweet professor who cares about her students. I had 0 programming background before taking this course and now I know all of the basic fundamentals of coding. Easy class if you pay attention and attend lectures.

Inspirational. Dr. Lim really teaches you in way that is approachable and gives confidence in CS. I didn't have much experience with computers before her class but I learned so much, and enjoyed it so much that I have switched to Data Science major. Grading and work were very fair. If you have the ability to take her class do not hesitate.

Angelica is the best professor I've ever had at SFU. She cared about the course so much and was just an absolute pleasure to be taught by her. The course material is incredibly accessible and engaging. I will drop everything to take another class with Angelica. Do yourself a favour and take any class with Angelica.

Angelica is a sweet prof. You can tell that she is really passionate about computing sciences. I had no prior programming experience, and I definitely found the material challenging at times, but Angelica taught at a fair pace. I recommend reviewing the exam banks as many of the same questions appeared on her exams.

Funny, caring, and kind. A good prof and a great person. She makes classes fun and engaging. You actually feel like you learn alongside her, as she is very humble. Exams can vary in difficulty, but are manageable.

Angelica was the best professor I had in my 3 years of Uni. I only took CMPT because I was interested in the topic, with no previous programming knowledge. Her lectures are clear, her tests tricky but fair and she is very approachable outside of lectures. Highly recommend
She is the nicest prof I 've met. Even if you don't understand any concept, at any point in the semester, she will take her time and go out of her way to explain it to you. Very very sweet. 10/10

The professor is very nice and informative, even though i have no background of coding and never went to the lab to ask for help. I could still learnt alot from Dr Lim, just by attend the lecture(it is extremely helpful). The exams are not too hard, but some of them maybe tricky. However, if you pay enough effort, it will be a gpa booster.

Dr. Angelica is nice and very sweet, though her exams are quite difficult, specifically her coding questions. Although there are only 2 questions, I've heavily been relying on my cheat sheet to SOMEHOW get a code that at least consists of some of the python elements for both midterms. TERRIFIED FOR FINAL BC NO CHEAT SHEET.
Dr. Lim is truly a sweetheart and that is prevalent in her lectures. You can see that she does anything and everything in her power for her students to understand the lecture material.
Her class are very fair and very doable to get a good grade. She is a sweet lady, but don't let it fool you because her quiz and exam are not easy. Make sure you study all of her notes very well.
Super nice and a lovely prof. Her lectures are a bit stale, but she teaches with enthusiasm. Would not mind if she taught all my courses.

She seems nice, lectures are well done. However I can't say much because I really haven't learned a thing from this course and I think that is more because of the curriculum. She organized it and executed it well for what the course is. Has potential to be a good teacher for sure. Pre-study java and android a bit before taking this ;) it helps.

One of the best professors I have ever taken for a course at SFU. She is very passionate about what she teaches and is also a very kind and inspirational person. If you have a chance to take one of her courses, do it.

She's a decent 1st time prof with good explanations. She posts everything and records lectures so that's no need to attend but you should. Homework was free marks but her exams had hard MCQ and she didn't allow a cheatsheet in the final so memorize your syntax! My other complaint was she only had 1 office hour per week but she helped after class.

She encourages everyone to bring their laptops to code along with her during lectures, so you actually leave each lecture having learned something. She made this course really enjoyable, especially for those of us who had no prior computer science experience
Angelica is super sweet and patient. I love her lectures and I did learn something in her class!
Angelica is a really nice prof. You can tell that she is very passionate about computing science. She even gave us cookies on the first day. All of us.

He is very kind, caring, and approachable. He grades generously and records classes, which I found useful. Books aren't required, but suggested. The assignments have the right amount of difficulty and are largely relatable to the lecture materials. Tip: Although exams are open-book, watch out for some tricky questions that test your understanding.

Miao is a great prof. Don't listen to these other negative comments. His assignments were extremely helpful and the exams were more than fair. They were open book and he marked very leniently. Very nice prof, I recommend.

Exams are difficult and assignments have a time-consuming, frustrating setup. Lectures are uninteresting. This course is better suited for self-taught students who can learn independently. The main upside is the lenient grading rubric and generous scaling at the end. Also, the prof is kind of cute! :P
Awful all the way around

Assignments are doable, but is an awful lecturer. Can't understand very well, midterm and final were very hard and unfair. Completely unrelated to assignments or anything given before, avoid at all costs!
One of the better CMPT 354 instructors I've had. Clear, well-structured lectures and a very kind, approachable prof. Highly recommend.
He give good feedback and thought me the history MangoDB.
Doesn't know how to teach. doesn't know how to reply to emails. Doesn't deserve to be a professor.

He does not use body language to teach AT ALL. I mean buddy, Please use blackboard, please draw something so that students can grasp, his voice is TOO low that all the time he sticks mike near his mouth. May he read this and be more active in teaching! i am shocked where are the BASIC teaching skills gone.
the professor is NOT confident at teaching at all. He is a LOT accented, cannot understand most times and not at all a good teacher!
I was so inspired so i took it twice

monotone + soft spoken + mumbly + accent = not a great lecturer. has good slides but rushes through important examples while drawing out the backgroundy/less important content. so the lectures were kind of painful. but exams were fair and he was helpful in office hours.
Must take!

Dr. Miao is very generous with both his grading scheme and giving tons of part marks. Exams are open book meaning your grade comes down to how well you are familiar with the material. Homework assignment do take a lot of time to complete. I found it difficult to understand Dr. Miao in lecture but his slides are very straightforwards.
assignments are heavy but fun. there's one coding assignment, no project.

Not sure why my review was taken down but Mr. Miao is a great professor and tries very hard. He cares a lot about his students and wants you to do well. Very kind grading scheme and high class average. Would highly recommend.

He clearly wants his students to do well and had a very kind grading scheme. Reasonable number of assignments and usually provided a program to check your answers for correctness. Recorded audio lectures. 3 midterms but dropped your worst. No final. Would definitely recommend!

Not sure why SFU decided to let him teach an upper division course, because we learned nothing from him. His lecture is from other professor, and it is extremely difficult to understand what he is saying in the lecture. I feel like all of the knowledge I got from this course is from self-learning.
The first assignment was unnecessarily difficult, after that they are doable and you actually learn something from them. The midterms are open book and difficult.

He seems to be a nice guy but is completely out of touch with his students. He has taken the complexity of all topics to the extreme and I feel as though my foundation of SQL is poor as difficulty went from 0-100 instantly. The first assignment we had to learn complex syntax that is completely useless as it is not used anywhere in the real world.

Assignments use code with poor style. Has little regard for teaching syntax or really any useful programming practices. Spends most of his time going after students that have similar work and virtual signaling, never teaching. Class uses a completely flawed model of instruction.

One of the best profs for CS. Can't go wrong with him. He is extremely knowledgeable and respectful. Makes very clear points and tells you the potential of things you do making the content intriguing. The course is an easy A+. I loved coding myself so the labs are easy to me even that I didn't know any C before.
The labs get difficult quickly, but Richard and the TAs are extremely helpful. Keep up or you'll fall behind and have to work extra hard as he gives tips on the latest labs.
Don't leave the labs till the last minute. If you have solid foundation on C, you should be fine.
Great prof. Coursework is meant to be challenging and vague to prepare you for programming in the real world.

This course counts towards your GPA now. We only got through 9 out of 11 labs, and the automatic grading for the last one took so long to be released that the deadline for the labs (there's only one deadline!) was delayed by a week. Paying attention to the in-lab lectures and asking questions will help you a ton.
This class does not count in your GPA but is a required class for CMPT major. The professor is really helpful and motivating.

He's a fantastic professor, wish he taught CMPT 125 as well. He's very helpful and the labs are very interesting. There are quite a few to finish but you only need to do 75% of them to pass the course.

He is a little intimidating at first because he obviously expects a lot from his students, but his one on one help is amazing. He was very patient and even went to a white board to explain a concept to me that I didn't get, I owe getting an A on my 125 midterm entirely to him. There should be more courses like this, a lot of pressure but it helps!
Too many labs to do. Cannot finish it and received a Fail

Doesn't know his own slides. Frequently misspeaks in class. Was so slow releasing the assignment two guidelines that he cancelled it altogether and shifted that weight to the other two assignments (one which was already complete). He seems like a very intelligent man, but he definitely needs to sort himself out.
Great professor, very interested in distributed systems and aims to teach you all he knows.
Great Prof, would learn again. A++

Excellent professor. He teaches this course like no other course is taught at SFU. I highly recommend it, with him, if you are smart, hard working, and willing to learn. He doesn't hold your hand, and you will have to learn some things on your own), but we're not here to learn online tutorial material. Theory and experience is why we're here.

Very decent. Passionate about the subject he is teaching. Concise explanations and nice British accent. Posts slides online. Midterm was 100% textbook questions, final was 40% textbook questions; difficulty is fair to hard. HW can be challenging. Takes him FOREVER to mark assignments--very thorough about checking for copied HW.

At first he seems like a nice guy and makes funny jokes and all. However,he is totally sloppy if it comes to exams or assignments.He posts assignments late and gives ambigous instructions. His exames are the biggest joke since they often cover questions that appear once in the lecture but not in the book. Lecture slides, posted late and useless.

Basically everything that can be said about Dr. Vaughan already has been. He is an awesome prof, speaks English clearly, is not boring, is interested in the subject, gives interesting homework and his exams are not too hard. I am going to take CMPT401 with him.
I echo the sentiments of the other reviews. He's an excellent professor.

Great Prof !!!!! I love this guy. He made everything interesting and didn't go too far into details. Gave an excellent sample midterm, with questions that appeared on the actual one. EX. for 3 marks, what is an Operating System? great guy. I got an A-. I spent a lot of time on his projects, but i actually learned about operating systems. Go for it!
I failed the midterm but managed to get an A on this course. Take it with him if you are really there to learn.

If you are taking courses to learn, this is 'THE' course to take!!! He has very interesting assignments and I would recommend him to anyone that asks. If you are in it for easy marks I would seriously not consider taking this course with him.
Great prof. Relatively easy on material, lots of material though. Great for linux people. Generally a nice person.
Assignment instructions were poorly given, causing a lot of confusion. But he can be pretty helpful if you go talk to him one-on-one.
Managed to turn a dry subject into an interesting class. Highly recommended. He makes the nicest slides of any prof I've ever seen.

Vaughanator didn't turn out to be that bad of a prof afterall. His assignments were generally long but do-able, and his final was fair. He also took time to help students in the lab, possibly because the TA's for the class seemed to be inexistant :/ . Overall decent prof.

Richard rocked. He actually managed to make Operating Systems interesting, which was great. The assignments were fun, the lecturees (when he had them) were useful. Take 300 with the guy, way better than someone like Bart or RandomAsianProf2092.

The "Vaughanator" is pretty cool, but I really hate him using the black board, it's impossible to read there. My friend asked him to write on the overhead, he refused and told him to sit closer to the board. But anyways, overall, i wasted around 8-9 hours of my time working on his midterm 3....
This guy is a joke! Cancelled so many clases, had uninformative assignments and lectures, and made the class useless. Want my money back for all the classes he cancelled !!
learnt nothing but "what's slacking"!!!
An absolutely terrible prof. Cancelled many classes, provides useless lectures, and pulls all problems out of the book. Learnt absolutely nothing.
This guy loves to cancel classes but his midterms and final are easy as hell everything is straight out of the textbook exercise.
Very clear and entertaining prof; lectures were enjoyable and informative. A bit too laid back at times, and pretty slow in marking stuff. First time teaching, he'll get even better.
No Comments
Chilled Prof, takes it easy but sometimes too easy and forgets to show up for class. Classes are somewhat informative, exams are alright. He's into robotics and seems pretty bright.
Just Horrible, Horrible x 1000000000000000 Nice guy tho :) Exams are O.K. If you just read ALL of the book, you can do well.

he's a big joke! he expects students to read all chapters that he didn't even cover. What in his notes are pictures copied from the book without any explantion. Hey, anyone said he's caring about his student? say again! He only cares about his stupid enough robots or his wife's iphoto.
Not bad if he didn't cancel so many lectures
lol...he rocks ... LOL
Actually has some spirit and cares what his students think. A rookie prof but shows good promise!
Generally not good.
not bad overall
No exam, but you can learn a lot from the assignments.

You will spend a lot of time on the assignments but you will learn a lot. There are no exams which is really great. Some assignments are graded competitively which turns some people off. His lectures can be confusing at times but it is a complex topic. He is happy to answer any questions and I felt like he knew his stuff. Great course!

Magnificent lecturer. Without a doubt captivated by the subject he's teaching and tries to transmit that fascination to others. Covered a huge range of topics, from classical approaches to CV and ending with generative pre-trained transformers. Homework may be hard if you've never had experience with CV, but they will change pre-req next sem.

Hi, I took this course to learn computer vision and deep learning. But the course wasn't organized, and I wish it was better. If you want to take this course, consider that you need to dedicate a good time learning computer vision. To learn more, you need to read additional books or take online courses. If you need just a good grade, You can!

Lectures were interesting; he taught a mix of classical and modern neural network computer vision. The assignments were difficult and took a lot of time. I would have had a hard time if my semester load was heavy. Good alignment between lecture material and assignments.

Not the best CS course, but definitely not the worst either. Could have been better. The course seemed a bit unorganized. The assignments were interesting but 2 of them were competitively marked so only top students could get full marks. Overall meh course, I wish it was better. I was really looking forward to this course but I was disappointed.

This course is not hard at all. Actually, it's really easy. You don't even understand concepts completely. You can still implement algorithms in assignments without understanding them. This is an easy A course.

Assignments were not hard at all. If you spend some time on them, they are very doable. topics for the assignments are very interesting. I don't know why people would complain about the workload. If you know how to read, you should be fine.

Yas changed the course structure in this term, and it's a bit unorganized. He's trying to cover all deep learning technologies in Computer Vision, which I think is ridiculous. It's nearly impossible to understand all lectures without preliminary deep learning knowledge. Homework is more challenging than the ones in other ML courses (CMPT 419).
Explanations unclear, homework incredibly hard.
Competitively graded, with extremely tough assignments when compared to previous offerings of the course. If you really like CV, its the perfect course for you, but otherwise you'll have a bad time.

His lectures were dense and hard to follow, forcing you to learn the material on your own. His exams were also not a great test of the material, as the averages were often failing grades. I would recommend you take this course with another professor.

The professor is really nice and freakishly smart. I asked him about a few coding interview problems i got stuck on and he can just instantly find the solution For this course, the midterms are only questions from the textbook. The people who got 100% all went through each problem with chatgpt/google and ignored the slides. The final though...
prof is okay but horrible TA.

MY GOAT. This prof is awesome. Although he may not be the best at lectures, he clears any professor in terms of tests. He gives clear instruction for the midterms and finals. Questions don't deviate much from what you've already done in the homework. As long as you know how to solve homework questions and don't rely on ChatGPT, you should be fine.
Extremely fair midterms and finals, if you do the textbook questions and assignments they are a breeze
Terrible professor. Could not understand anything he said. Makes the concepts more harder than needed.

Very fair exams. Reasonable expectations. Hard to read questions if you're not used to mathematical notation. Recommendation is that you don't solely rely on his slides, also look at other resources and do a few LC easy/med questions.

A great professor. The people complaining about his accent are wild, he has an accent but he speaks clearly and is easy to understand. Assignments and exams were difficult but fair. If you do the assignments and make sure you understand them you will do well. His lectures are good and he will answer all questions thoughtfully.
So hard

Ignore what others say about his accent. He's PERFECTLY understandable, these people just want an excuse for their lack of effort. This is one of the better profs of the faculty, he's very fair and responds quickly to questions. The assignments are fantastic for preparing for the midterm. Grading distribution is great too. Very respectable prof.

The problem, first of all, is that you would not understand any word he says. This is an Algorithm class, and you'd find the class challenging even with a professor who can speak clear English. Do not believe students when they say he's the GOAT. He's not! Anything you learn will be from your own self-study. It's useless attending his class.
Very lenient marking. If you don't mind learning everything on your own, you can take this to get a good mark. Lectures are just him reading the textbook.

Gu was very lenient on grading which was helpful for such a tough class. The assignments were basically free marks. The midterms and final were graded by few questions which could be tough if you didn't understand a question. I would take 307 with him again. Sometimes he is hard to understand but he is a nice enough guy and not a bad choice.
Very bad teaching skills. Kind of hard to understand what he is saying. Teaching an important course at too early in the morning.

The most useless course I've ever taken in my academic life. I could barely understand anything he said in class. His lecture materials are harder to understand than the textbook. I had to download materials from other schools to understand concepts. What is most annoying about the course is that asides from the horrible grade, I learned NOTHING.

The course is extremely heavy and he didn't try to make it better. Writing this review 16 hours before the final exam and half of the assignments are not graded yet. 12 chapters are included in the final exam that's worth 40% of the total grade. His explanation is OK at best. Excruciatingly hard assignments that are worth only 5% each.

I didn't like CMPT 307 with him at first. But as time went by, I came to enjoy it. He answered everyone's questions immediately (literally!) during lectures, and his assignments & exams were fair. The only con is that his lectures are not that good. But I think his handling of other things really makes up for it. He is also very nice and reasonable

This professor maybe not the best professor i have met in university life, but he is one of those most caring one. He always replies email very quick, not like other professors and TAs. If you have any questions, you are welcome to ask him. He is very supportive and willing to help. I never thought of passing this course with a B-.
Pretty old school (boring) lectures. Exams and assignments were graded lieniently, but that also meant there wasn't much feedback.

I agree with the good feedback below. He makes complex concepts in algorithm matters easier. I liked his way of caring for students. His exams are fair and only asked the important part of the course, not covering all the trivial details.

Professor Gu is knowledgeable in computer algorithm. His accent gave me NO TROUBLE in understanding the course, some comments here are unfair! Gu cares about his students and gave extension on the assignments/exams to students who had health issues. Everytime I asked him questions, he could answer my questions clearly and encouraged me to think.

I really like this man. He clearly knows his material. He is caring but not pretentious at all. He doesn't seem eager for praise or recognition but just does his job well. He is often amused by students' questions but answers them clearly. Students mock him because of accent and grammar but to be honest his English is really good.

The prof defines the (admittedly complex) material clearly and adequately. He is adept at understanding answering students' questions during lecture. Test difficulty is on par with assignments and feels fair. Low amount of work required (roughly 4 hour long assignments, due every 2-3 weeks). His accent may be hard to understand for some.

No effort put into classes. Lectures just zoom by advanced topics with minimal detail. His slides are only excerpts copy & pasted from the textbook. I wish he'd at least give us time to understand the material. His accent is super confusing as well. Surprised they let these kinds of professors teach!

He looks cold and dull, but is indeed approachable and warm-hearted inside. Always reply to emails quickly and try his best to help you for any questions or requests. Language barrier may sometimes be a problem, but it is totally understandable as he actually speaks three languages(Japanese also). Don't be hard on this.
The worst prof I've ever had at SFU. His accent is hard to understand even for Chinese speakers. He doesn't know how to teach at all, all he does is reading slides.

He is the worst lecturer ever in SFU. I will suggest no one to take course with him. The quality is zero, you wont understand a word in class due to language barrier and the exam is extremely irrelevant and super hard to be done in time. He is also very tough on grade.

Accent isn't that bad once you get used to it. Slides are cryptic and he doesn't answer questions in lecture clearly. Weird terminology sometimes. He was really understanding when I had a medical issue though so thumbs up for that.

wonderful prof. explains things clearly, everything posted online so attendance is optional. exams are fair and its easy to do well if you put in some studying. The accent is not a problem. AT all. I dont know how people complaining about that survive in this city. Highly recommend.

The instructor has a slight accent, but hey we live in multi culture society, all matters is how good he is. Indeed, he is extremely good at explaining the concept, and those details you will remember forever. Midterm and finals are quite reasonable. Highly recommend him and do NOT skip his lecture. Reading book is optional, all mats is in lectures

Easy course. Prof is nice. True, he has an accent problem but since everything can be learnt from notes, it doesn't really matter. Exam are based on assignments, midterm is a bit hard due to limited time, final is extremely easy.

exams were unexpected, too much reading. cmpt371 other prof covered up to ch 6 of the same book. we did up to ch 9 + another text book. exams not closely related to homework assignment questions. take with another prof.

Would avoid. Covers two textbooks which is over 1100 pages of material, gets flustered when people ask him what to study because there is so much material. Didn't learn anything, midterm very difficult.
He is useless. And never share notes with students. Midterm is really really hard.

Poor instructor abilities. Sounds like a recording (just tries to get the lecture out of the way so he can go back to research). Doesn't prepare you for his midtermsfinals - they hit you like a train when you have them. Assignment submission system he has is annoying (drop it on a folder an NFS, what happened to coursys?)
Didnt really learn much from 471. Assignments were easy and on the same level as 371. Lectures are almost not worth going to as slides cover everything.
He is dull and sometimes hard to understand but all assignments and exams are based off notes and homework. As long as you keep up with those you will get a great mark in his course.
Notes are crystal clear. Exams are straightforward. Extremely helpful and approachable.

The worst thing about Gu is that he teaches two books, but doesn't clarify to study which chapters! you will be surprised by questions from never thought chapters in extremely difficult final exam.AVOID

Gu is reasonable, although his English makes it hard to understand him completely. Also, three-hour long lectures may put you to sleep. Nevertheless, he has very good notes and you may read the book/ use google groups. The assignments come from the book, which is somewhat confusing. If in doubt about the assignment questions go to Gu directly.

Sounds like Kermit the frog, but with HORRIBLE grammar + accent. His teaching was boring, and not helpful for the assigns. Lecture notes were very unclear, and did not even have the diagrams he used from the book. $160(new) so look for pdf or used copy. Final exam WTF it was outta the blue. Read the book and know the graphs well. Turn off your CELL

I was very excited about computer networking before I took this course with Mr.Gu. Well not anymore! While the book discusses some of the latest technologies, class discussions are monotonous, fast-paced and hard to follow. Even though some of the classes' finest minds were helping me understand the assignments, I found them to be quite hard.AVOID!
does not make class very interesting at all. weak prof at best..
No Comments

WORST prof. I have ever had at SFU. Had to complete and hand in homework before he even goes over the chapters for the assignment. Can't understand a single word coming out of his mouth, won't clarify questions on the exams (despite his atrocious grammar, which makes it hard to discern exactly *what* he wants us to answer.) Avoid him at all costs.

Lecture notes are overly simplified that you could have ahard time understanding them. His English is sub-par at most, making him difficult to understand. Assignments are straight from book, but his solutions are not helpful and asking him would not help you. His midterms and (especially) final exam will have you go, "WTF?!".

Never take 371 with Gu. His lectures were horrible and his attitude sucks! For his lectures he memorized and read word for word from the textbook, no preperation. The assignment questions were also from the book and he managed to mark them unfairly without giving much explanation. Did not synthesize material. Gave no context. Two thumbs down!

Little difficult to understand his English sometimes; Assignments are straight from the book; His lecture notes are overly simplified and does not help most of the time, so lecture attendence is a must; You really got to read the book to understand what he is talking about in the lectures; His midterm and final are okay.
very good prof...
very nice prof. well organized and work load is fine

Nice, good organization, high interest to the coures make him such a high-rating prof. Willing to listen and very helpful on assignment. The class's overall average was 82%! Although he offers this course once per year, believe me, take his 471.

Lectures are dry, but he knows his stuff. To be honest, I didn't like the content, it was very symbolic and theoretical (I think that's where he specializes in). The first 2 assignments were interesting, got to apply algo's learned in class. The second half of the sem was very theoretical, didn't enjoy it as much. Lots of extensions. Exams tough.

Jen's class got more difficult near the end of the course but he did a great job on making the midterm and final related to course material. Most of the questions were things we did on the assignments. He is also easy to contact and quick to answer questions and puts in a lot of effort to helping students. Gave lots of assignment extensions.
His assignments were average but EXAMS will drop your grade so bad! It was hard to understand his lectures as well.
Lecture notes very terse with awkward handwriting. Lacking structure in materials taught and over-expands on too many topics. However, he does present the logic of things quite clearly.

Hazra's class was a rough ride. Assignments were very challenging, with a lot of tricky edge cases. The grading was harsh, and the midterm format change caught many off guard. Still, unstuck study was a lifesaver for prep. Overall, a tough but rewarding experience.
Awesome teacher and really cares about the students. One of the best in the cs department.

You could tell that Hazra knew what she was doing, but she didn't seem too passionate about teaching this course though. When she gave us time to work on a problem, she would often circle around to see if you were working on it which made the class feel like I was in high school. I did learn a lot from this class though.

Hazra's lectures were very interactive as she would always include in-class coding activities. Most of the time her explanations are good, but sometimes very confusing. She often accommodated requests from students for extensions and responded well to feedback. Quizzes didn't match the material perfectly, but exams were fair.

Hazra is an amazing instructor. Her lectures are understandable and helpful. She is approachable and gives awesome feedback. She encourages everyone to participate in class activities and I personally loved that. I would recommend taking her class!

Hazra is a very sweet professor and puts a lot of time into responding to students questions. I barely scraped by on the exams but that was due to my own poor study habits. Read the class material, practice, ask questions and you will be fine. Hazra is fine for cmpt120 (its an intro programming class it is not that deep). grading is fair.

As a prof she's not the best at explaining things however she does her best to go over it if you ask her questions personally. She quite literally just reads from the slides so you have to make sure you're on top of everything to do well. Going to lectures is important to stay on track or you will suffer

This is a very easy course, but Hazra doesn't meet the standard that SFU should expect of their professors. She seems to put little effort into course structure or creating elegant assignments to reinforce topics. There were even a ton of grading errors. Taking harder courses with her would be awful due to her poor organization and teaching skills.
update she did not consider the 40% rule but still the assignment very heavy and there are lots of edge cases that need lots of time to discover and cover them
I won't say much cause every thing I was going to say is written by others here. This class was a pure waste of time.

The assignments were very severely marked, and the automated tests were broken. Midterm 2 was insane, we had to select all the correct choices in the mcq, and even if we miss one right answer or select wrong we will get 0 for the mcq. There were approx 15-20 mcq's worth 60% of the midterm
Hazra is great! I definitly recommend

2nd exam had multi select answers instead of single select like 1st exam so a lot of people did poorly. I didn't agree with that change. Assignments aren't easy - esp the 2nd one about threads, I think she should use two classes to demonstrate code for deadlock/synchronization/multi threading. She is caring and will support you with regrades.

I would not trust anything this prof says. The course outline changed in the last 3 weeks without warning to include a final (promised NO final on first day). The assignments change on whim sometimes a few days before due and Hazra is too lazy to amend the assignment document. Scheduling is bad too, entire course rushed in the last 3 weeks.
why doesnt rate my prof update her rating properly, she aint a 3/5 prof, please trust all the recent ratings there is a reason why she is getting 1/5 from everyone. DO NOT TAKE A CLASS W HER

Despite her "18+ years of teaching experience" and "extensive knowledge of learning pedagogies" this course was extremely disorganized, with an unexpected final. Assignment instructions are vague and often contradictory, requiring dozens of Piazza questions to clarify. Regardless of what you do, they WILL find something to penalize.

This class is like a curse, no matter what u do there is ALWAYS something wrong. messed up markings, automated tests are broken, kids don't implement functions, get mark but when I implement them I get marks off. Hazra after all backlash went missing in Piazza and does not even answer. It is like the prof is gone and I have to deal with TAs.
The only professor where following the instructions makes you lose marks. The instructions are ambiguous and contradictory.

She is a little bit lazy. Sometimes she gives inconsistent requirements and wrong hints on assignments, and she won't update the assignment instruction document even though she knows there is a mistake.

The prof herself is alright. She provides a tentative schedule of all assignment deadlines, exams, and readings for the course. I, unfortunately, wrote down the deadline of the last assignment in my calendar a month before it was due. She must've changed the deadline without announcing it, so I missed the deadline. That assignment was worth 12%.
why some people think Hazra is really good "prof" ? take 300 with Harinder

Good enough prof. I don't think the 1/5 ratings are warranted.
