Pros: - Responds quickly on Piazza - Will regrade if you feel it was unfair and explain why - No real final exam - She cares - Late days - Extensions Cons: - Vague assignment instructions resulting in lots of questions/redos - Confusing answers irl Depends: - Exams are mostly MCQ
I'm glad I am almost done with this useless class. Have fun if you are taking it with Hazra in future

This prof herself has heavily affected my mental health due to her irresponsible decisions. It is not fair to have a final exam on the last day of the class and teach new materials on 2 DAYS before the FINAL exam. It is unfortunate that she can rest easily and be selfish enough to put in a final earlier for HER sake, while students suffer

Course originally has 3 non-cumulative exams, no final. Last minute change to make third exam cumulative. Third exam is on last day of lectures, very little time to prepare. Assignment instructions are ambiguous and contradictory and Hazra changes requirements halfway. Assignments are most reading files and parsing user input.

She is really irresponsible, she does not know how to schedule the class at all, and lies too. At the beginning of the class she said that midterm 3 is not going to be cumulative and we should stay in her class cause there are no finals, but then LAST minute decided to have a cumulative exam after failing everyone for her second midterm.

examinations are ridiculous specially the Multiple Choices. need to choose all correct options otherwise get zero. need 50% in every tests to pass the course. Assignments are heavy and vague and no code demo provided. In addition of being rude, TAs are not familiar with stuff . lots of edge cases for Assignment .new edge cases pops till due time

The lectures from the prof are not bad. But the course schedule is messed up. Lots of things like assignment requirements, grading and exam schedule were changed last min. Rubrics and assignment instructions are vague. One of the TA's grading standard is absolutely non sense, and is unwilling to provide test cases and help students out.
Most unorthodox professor ever, for multiple choices, asks us to select more than one option and NO partial marks.

She stated that there would be no final exam, but then changed her mind at the last minute and made the third exam cumulative, which resulted in a final exam during the busiest time. Additionally, the marking of exams and assignments is quite harsh, with deductions of almost half of the total marks for things that were not mentioned in the rubric.

Makes false promises, first, she says the third exam is not cumulative but the last minute changes her mind to make it cumulative. You will see a LOT of inconsistencies, and weird events taking her class. The organization is awful, it seems like she has 0 idea of how to make the planning of the class smooth.
Irresponsible professor, uses her slides from last semesters but still has the same typos, this shows the professors dedication

She doesn't post her slides ahead while she has a final with lectures that get covered 2 days before the final. This professor does not provide recourses as she thinks she would lose her job. She also makes everyone's job harder by pushing deadlines earlier so she can have a longer summer break. A lot of obvious selfishness.

People are putting fake high ratings for her as they feel personally bad for her. She has messed up her class schedule a lot this semester. She has added an extra section AND has put a final on last day of her exam because she wants to be done with the semester early. The class is a complete mess.
it's week 12, almost end of the semester but I learned nothing from this class. Hazra should really reconsider the way she teaches that class. Take cmpt 300 with another professor.

The assignments are way ahead of what she is teaching. The grading is harsh for both exams and assignments, which can be demotivating. On top of that, she gives two weeks to prepare for the last exam, which will include the complete syllabus. I will not recommend her for CMPT 300 class.

Assignments were confusing, had little to do with classes, no clear defined marking guidelines with a particularly arrogant TA that is too happy to give 0 marks. Strong accent makes it hard to follow lectures. Promised no final but gives out a cumulative 50 min midterm instead, which is worse than a final.

Honestly, Hazra is really good as a professor and I really like her lectures and enjoyed her assignments but she should fire Jetic Gu as her TA as he made the grading for assignments way more difficult than it was required in the assignments.
She is really good at teaching, answers questions quickly and I learned a lot.
Avoid her, you won't learn anything about operating system. All you learn is just opening and reading files. How did this university let Hazra teach here
take her for cmpt 300. her course material actually make students learn about operating system

Took this class with zero background in computer science. I loved it, lectures were clear and easy to understand, and assignments were fairly graded. They were just challenging enough to learn. Questions asked on Piazza or in person were answered clearly and concisely. Would take any class she teaches. I would recommend 10/10.

Her lectures are really good and straight forward. Her Assignments are difficult, which actually teaches you a lot about ways to become a better programmer. It seems that some negative reviews stem from concerns about the grading of assignments 1 and 2 by the TA. Overall, she is really nice and understanding, and she cares about her students.

Her lectures are hard to follow, they're all over the place. Assignments and tests are always ambiguous and confusing. I think she's a nice person and all but her teaching just isn't up there with it.
there will be no final exam* *no exam covering whole course material during final exam time, instead it will be on the last week of classes, same day as 15% assignment is due

The class was terrible. She said there won't be any final exam instead there'll be 3 midterms. However the last exam is cumulative in week 12 which is worse than final exam. Did not learn anything related to operating system. All assignments were about reading files (not to mention her horrible assignment description). She's a good slide reader.

Reuses the same ridiculously unclear assignment instructions despite students from previous semesters complaining the instructions are too vague. Do not TA for her class, assignments are very difficult to mark because of ambiguous program behavior. Just memorize the lecture slides.
all I have to say is avoid this class, you're not going to learn anything to be honest. That's it

Really vague assignments, every time someone asks a question to clarify it adds additional requirement creep to our programs. She doesn't provide a marking rubric or guidelines for her TAs, so marking on assignments can be very harsh. Only pro is that there is no final exam. Please read the textbook, the lectures are not helpful.

Plus - no final exam, that's about it. Has an accent, reads off slides, assigns projects with ridiculously vague instructions whose scope keeps creeping up as questions flow in, & lets TA arbitrarily + harshly grade them without being on the same page with him. You're well off just skipping lectures and reading textbook. Quizzes are quite boing.

Assignments are poorly designed. It may seem that Imran has never attempted to do the assignments herself, because assignments often have nonsensical requirements or irrational design patterns. Unable to take criticism from students when we suggest assignments should clearly document program behavior.

Assignment documentation is very unclear. The professor and TAs will often give conflicting advice on how to complete the assignments. Assignment marking is very strict; failure of a single test case shall result in zero for the tested functionality. Assignments do not reflect current course content. Imran only reads off lecture slides.
She doesn't bother to give a full list of requirements for the assignment and then deducts marks for something she didn't mention in the assignment requirement...

Was a beginner coder and she explained Python really well, helped me understand programming quite fast. I was irritated about the exam grading scheme where the "predict the output" section was worth 3-4 marks per output, no partial marks, as well as MC changed to be worth 2 pts each. Lost around 15-20% on my second midterm solely on those.

Dr. Imran was an amazing professor for CMPT 120, she was kind and caring towards all students and gave extensions when needed. Midterms were fair and mostly based off assignments, however the final exam was harder and had a heavier emphasis on post midterm 2 material. She really wants all of her students to succeed. Highly Recommend Dr. Imran!
She really tries her best to explain concepts. The only problem is the coding on paper, but she gives even if the code is not 100% correct, and the lectures are a bit boring.

She seems nice, but the way she handles the course seems unfair for students. Passing was determined by a mere pass/fail exam policy despite having several graded assignments. Emphasizes welcoming introductory class despite skimming over content. Difficult assignments with topics not covered before. Also written paper exams for a computer class??
Just awful. Refuses to use email, so you need to make another third party account, and check that regularly Lectures are not understandable lots of the time.

She's a nice professor, but very hard to understand and not the best at explaining concepts. The practice problems she gives us for the midterm are way too difficult and involves concepts we never learned. The answer keys are often incorrect as well, which can be misleading for students trying to study.

Even though shes terrible at explaining anything, she refused to record lectures and her thick accent makes it extra challenging for a lot of international students to understand her but she doesnt seem to sympathize.I have no problem with a thick accent but her incredibly poor grammar is also confusing especially for INTRO comp sci. AVOID!

terrible prof. Super unclear, terrible explanations, does not care about student feedback at all. Her lectures are always super vague ppl stopped coming. I was excited to learn comp sci but taking it with her is such a waste of money and time. Be prepared to go to lecture for the absolute worst explanations of basic concepts.

She has excellent slides, and her assignments were fair. I give her a lower mark because her accent is unbearably strong, which is distracting from the lecture content. Also, written exams for a computer science class??

She is the best professor of Computing Science department. I can bet on that. Her lecture notes are amazing. She explains everything in detail. She is so good in the basics and concepts. CMPT 354 with her was worth taken and the project made you learn the entire DBMS. She is good in the gradings too. Take any course with her without blinking.

I took cmpt 300 with her as well. With Hazra you are safe as she makes everything reasonable. However, I won't take any courses with her because I don't like her reading lecture notes without enough explanation.
One of the following statements is true. A: The AI course is boring. B: Hazra has made this course boring. If I did this multiple choice question, I would choose B.

Good professor. Clear slides, good range of activities that make up your grade, and material isn't too difficult imo. Assignments are a mix of theory + coding (python). Prof is very caring and nice. Only complaint is that the final was too long for the time given, but the prof did address this in the final marks. Would take Hazra again!

Dr. Imran is a great prof who genuinely cares about student's learning and understanding of the materials. Having taken 310 and spoken with previous 310 students (not in Dr. Imran's class), unless needed as a pre-req, there are far more useful upper level CMPT courses offered in the department (i.e. avoid 310 if possible and spend money elsewhere!)
I wish she spoke english

Really good professor!!! Pace is easy to catch. Sufficient practise for quiz and homework. Textbook is not mandatory, lecture slides are very clear, detailed and cover everything. Group project in 3 people group, through the beginning to the end of the term, every milestone of the project is associated to what you are learning that time.

Hazra clearly cares about her students' learning. The lectures had a good balance of involving students in discussion and explaining concepts. Always willing to take questions as well. Lecture recordings were also made available and class was streamed over zoom for students who could not make it in.

Very clear and well put together lectures. I learned a lot in this class, everything was presented in a way that was easy to understand. There is one group project where you work in teams of 3 to implement a database system from start to finish. Lecture recordings and slides/notes were made available which was useful for review.

She is one of the very few professors left in computer science departments who actually teach the course and dont tell you to go learn it yourself. She explains everything clearly and course-load isnt heavy. Also it's easy to understand her accent. When she starts the lecture, its hard not to concentrate. I would recommend taking her in the future

Hazra is brilliant & very available outside of class to answer questions. The exams were fair. The only complaints I have is that there seemed to be a big discrepancy between the assignments and the exams. Get prepared to spend a lot of time on assignments as they're worth most of the grade. Would love to take another course with Hazra.
A very nice and responsible professor.

Hazra was an extremely fair professor and did a great job of providing the necessary lectures and resources to succeed in her class. The 4 assignments she gave us had dwelled on Linux programming, multithreading, and dealing with C processes which were awesome. Her exams were also very fair and not punishing if you hadn't done well on them.

The teaching isn't amazing or anything she expects us to read the textbook and teaches everything very slow during lectures. Low workload and it's easy to get a good grade in her class. But we're paying to learn and I didn't learn much from this course and it's basically a waste of money. I'd take a real professor as Hazra is just a temporary prof

Dr. Imran is a very passionate, engaging, and above all caring instructor. If she is such a good choice for a difficult course like CMPT 300, I'm sure she would be one for other upper-division computing courses as well.

She is not bad at managing the course but didn't make students learn something practical or interesting. To be honest, the course was a waste of time. She should work on the course curriculum rather than making over-detailed marking schemes for outdated assignments. She has a caring attitude even though her teaching skill isn't on the great side.

Two years in SFU - she is one of the only few profs that can actually teach. She actually explains every slide and waits for everyone to finish asking their questions. She cares about student learning and that shows in her teaching. She took feedback regularly and adjust the class as needed. I will certainly take other courses with her.
very helpful prof, and the course content is not too hard to study or understand. The marking is fair, and the workload is not heavy too. Recommend if this prof is teaching again

I took this class to learn AI. To be fair, a pro of Hazra is her workload is quite fair and easy. However, I really didn't learn anything in her class. Her lectures made no sense. Office hours weren't of much help either. I don't care if she marks hard or not, I just want to learn something and unfortunately I didn't (I did all the readings btw).

Hazra is a decent professor and tries to make her class relevant. It's important to do textbook readings. The final exam was challenging. It is not just memorizing definitions but being able to see a scenario and understanding the concept. My grades dropped from A to A-. I learned a lot and enjoyed the course. Hope to take more classes with her.

Hazra is a great professor and I would definitely recommend her for CMPT 310. I have never taken a course as structured as hers. She gave us five assignments. All assignments were very interesting. There were quizzes, in-class activities, polling questions, recordings. On top of it, very caring and accessible. One of the best AI profs at SFU.

Such a lovely person and a dedicated professor. Her interest in the success of her students is really evident. Those who'd say different were probably the students who are mad about not getting easy grades. I truly enjoyed her class.

Hazra is so detailed, clear and organized. Her teaching style would suit the majority of the students! Keep doing all your practices! She's also very responsive with piazza and emails. She's beyond amazing!

Hazra explains and teaches in a way that I wish all professors taught. She cares about her students and ensures that every aspect of a topic is addressed so everyone will understand. Truly a blessing!
I enjoyed her class. She knows her stuff. She teaches at such a comfortable pace. She goes through everything so thoroughly. An amazing prof who I would recommend to anyone.

Even though this semester was online, the performance was below acceptable. Most of her lectures didn't make sense and she just told everyone to read the textbook or slides beforehand (even though many ppl did...) Her final exam dropped me from an A to a C+, my lowest mark in my entire academic career. Never take a class with Hazra Imran.

Participation marks are worth a lot of marks for what they are. Her lectures are more theoretical heavy. There is a lot of repeat from previous courses. A big con is she is bad at communicating what she wants.

A decent instructor. She focuses heavily on the theory side of Ai instead of interesting things like ML. Assignments weren't very interesting. Fair marker, approachable, and she knows her stuff. Feels like sometimes she jumps around on a subject, could have better logical order. Overall a prof that I wouldnt avoid, but I wouldnt jump to either.

Poor teaching skills. Maybe she is a good researcher, but she cannot teach unfortunately. Only pro I can think of is the course workload is fair. Honestly, I feel like I learned nothing this term in this class...

Pros : relatively light courseload/ fair exams & assignments/ she tries to help students Cons : she makes easy concepts complicated (wordings in the slides should be improved)/ old and not very useful course materials/ not the great administration (lecture cancellation with short notice, couldn't finish scheduled activities and sections, etc.)

Hazra is a kind prof. She spends a lot of time explaining the material. And it's easy to get an A-range grade with her. If this's your purpose dig in 100%. But if your goal is to learn a lot about AI - FORGET. The entire semester you will be remembering CMPT225, MACM101, and STAT270. No practice at all. All assignments are theoretical.

Hazra is one of my favourite profs at SFU. She cares for student learning. Her slides are informative and present real and useful examples. The online environment is challenging for everyone. I am enjoying her course. Would love to have more courses with her.

Lots of activities in a remote class, so don't fall behind. Communication could be better. Read the textbook before class to understand the lecture It feels like she cares about her students but has a hard time putting herself in our position. Can come off as a bit rude (like when using caps lock), but that might be due to online communication

Hazra is one of the best instructors in the CS department. Lectures are clear and a lot of opportunities to learn the materials through in-class activities quizzed and try out exercises. The workload is medium. Would recommend taking an AI course with her.

She is *definitely* the worst instructor of the department. Powerpoints are jam-packed with confusing information, and she's impossible to understand. No time is left for comprehension, she just speeds by everything. Highly unethical of SFU to make us pay for such poor quality education. Will complain to department if she doesn't improve by the end

Not surprisingly, one of the best instructors in CPSC department. Learning goals are clearly laid out, lectures are clear and a lot of opportunities to learn the materials through clickers and in-class activities. Her exams ... quite fair. Would recommend to take database courses with her.

It's easy to follow lectures but sometimes handwriting gets a bit messy. Lectures recorded, post lecture notes posted, iclickers in class, and tests are fair(like assignment/lecture examples) Tests are a bit time constrained, but you should have enough time to read and process the question, just don't get stuck for > 5 min for multiple questions.

She is flexible and easy to talk to which is great. The course material is fair, though her exams are a bit challenging due to time management. She posts all her notes online, as well as lecture recordings - very convenient if you have life/work commitments. She does not curve - put the effort into the project/work and you will get a good mark.

Very easy course, and Stella makes it that much easier. The projects were very straight forward, the presentation at the end was really easy, and the midterms were pretty standard. You've got to study off of the notes, and you'll do fine. Got an A+ and I attended half the lectures. Just study the slides and notes, and you'll be fine.

Overall a pretty easy course. I managed to get an A in this class which is rare for me considering that I am not a smart student and pretty lazy. Exams are pretty easy, read through her lecture notes and you'll easily get a decent grade. Would definitely take another course with her if I got the opportunity.
A very easy class. Unfortunately, it is also tediously boring. Stella does her best to make it interesting. Her grading is fair, the workload is light, and the usefulness is rather limited.

Course involves a semester-long group project, so beware of deadweight group members. Slept through 90% of lectures and still managed to get an A. Assignments are generally fair (though difficulty may depend on what your group decides to for the project), exams are also very fair. Grades participation based on class activity handout sheets.

Very good professor who makes a boring class a bit more interesting. She is definitely interested in the material and overall it is a an easy class. It is true for an HCI professor she seems to have problems using her computer. Would take a class with her again.

I had an enjoyable time with her for summer 2007 intersession, but too bad it wasn't full semester. Tries to keep lecs interesting, and admits she's like all of us who aren't experts in all user interfaces. That's how we learn and improve. Manageable tests and asns and fairly marked. More importantly they reflect what we learned in class and text.

Very easy course and she's a good prof, minus her inability to use exactly what she's teaching (i.e. interfaces) Some things are annoying about her but marking is fair and class expectations are more than fair - at least for this course which was a breeze. The notes are decent, and lectures aren't all that boring.
Simple class, with not a whole lot of work required. I'd say it's a pretty easy credit.
exams are incredibly easy, lots of assignments but none take much work. easy credit and somewhat interesting.
one of the easiest courses I've taken in sfu. It shows that she's not an expert in UI design and HCI. But what the heck, Ive taken it because its a gpa booster, cant complain

First time teaching this course. Basically summarizes the recommended text and present them to us. Good research background, but comments regarding HCI made in class are too subjective. This is a theory class; not an art class. A lot of overlap with CMPT 275. A lot of work required; be prepared.
No Comments
Course is good itself except the tutor
No Comments
A bit spinny, but still a nice enough prof. 340 is brutal!

Mediocre lecturer at most. Unreachable other than the class day, even on piazza. The course is built around an OS designed for teaching. Whether you can gain some insights into general OSs from the assignments is debatable. No written exam, but easy marks from clickers and an oral exam. Overall a weirdly designed course. Dont think I learned much.

If this course isn't changed, it'll be an infamous killer for CPEN students. I'm not exaggerating when I say assnt4&5 will take about 40 hours, and assnt6 60+. The course is based off of the OS course taught at Harvard. Your task is essentially to complete an operating system. Resources such as videos from other schools will be helpful.
She is bad.
A lot of paper reading. But she is an awesome person. Class average was 88.

I would say this is a good quality and at the meantime challenging course for a undergraduate student. I made every effort and devote more than 8 hours a week to read assigned papers and prepare for in lecture presentation. With all these being done, I got a relatively good result as I expected.
The course content is very useful and the lecture is great. You will need to make lots of effort to keep your pace with the instructor. Digging deep into the material is necessary and rewarding.
Don't take her course unless you have to. Quiz are not useful, Lecture turns out to be Communication classes. She was not clear about most of the paper which she gave us to read.

One of my favorite profs. Assignments are long but challenging - you actually use this stuff in real life. The grading is fair, and she helps out when you need it. Interesting papers to read, and fun in class activities. Doesn't try to actively fail you, which is refreshing :)

She is unclear in her marking scheme. Half way through the semester she changes her quiz weight and gives us online "marking quizzes" without putting it on her website about the timesdates. The assignments were long, but I did learn a lot. Also, two scholar papers to read weekly that were unrelated to the assignments.

One of the best instructors at SFU. No reading 100s of pages of useless, outdated material. Everything assigned is on point and relevant. Assignments are not trivial and require thinking, but she gives you a lot of freedom. If you think they're hardunfair you're in the wrong field. Completing them properly will make you a better programmer.
Exams are not hard, just study her notes. However, the assignments are *very* time consuming - start early! I've never had an assignment as long as hers. Oh, and she's so hot so don't get distracted!

I found this class pretty hard. The exams are not too bad, as long as you study her lecture notes you should be able to do well on them. The hardest part of the class are the programming assignments. I found them to be really challenging and long.
not bad
Study her lecture notes only and participate in discussions and you should be fine with exams.
Best teacher, she is so helpful especially on Vista. You can tell you spends a good deal of time answering your questions as they are very thorough answers.
Very nice. Tries really hard to make sure everyone understands what is going on. It works. People get it.

Kimberly was a *GREAT* teacher. She took a conceptually difficult course and made it clear, accessible and fun. She genuinely wanted her students to succeed, and really worked hard to provide support on webCT and through office hours. A++++ to Kim.
The best instructor at UBC (teaches at UBC & SFU). Clear, interesting, funny, and super nice. You can tell that she really enjoys teaching and she is very good at it.
UBC summer course. Awesome prof. Clear, reasonable, helpful. Respectful towards students. Sets the bar high for other instructors. Highly recommended.
The smartest prof I had. Knows so much about so many different topics. Classes are really fun and assignments were really imaginative. She is a true inspiration!!!

She was so helpful and welcoming. I'm sure she was bogged down by thousands of emails over the semester but she quickly answered every one of mine. I hope she does well as a prof and in whatever career she is in after!!
Kim is a really great prof. She presented topics really well, the assignments midterm and final were all fair, and she made herself really available for extra help.

Lectures clearly and in a straightforward manner. Kim is readily available outside of class and is extremely helpful when students have questions about how to do an assignment or lab. She also answers e-mails quickly and thoroughly.

Good lecturer with minimal stutters and no accent. I don't know why people say the lectures are boring. Just imagine its a movie, you don't need to take any notes. You can literally show up without a pencil. The late penalties are 10% per day. I ate a lot of late penalties. The formatting of the assignments are marked harshly.

Probably one of the worst classes I have ever taken. The lectures are boring , the content even more so. The TA's are insufferable. They cut way too many marks for very tiny details while grading rubrics are way toooo vague. Do urself a favor and take this course with any other prof.

Great professor with a good attitude but the lectures were quite boring. Not too many assignments but near the middle and the end of the course there is big papers you have to write. TAs marked very poorly and took off large chunks of your mark for the tiniest things.
Marked very thought, boring lectures, you are expected way more than what is being said, TAs take away big marks for little things. Bad experience
Pretty chills, just make sure you don't mess up the informative and persuasive papers as those are worth essentially your entire grade.
great lectures, extremely boring content, insufferable marking, final was insanely random
Probably the best MACM 101 professor out there. Practice the textbook questions a lot and really understand the assignment quizzes. They help a ton.
Amazing teacher for macm. Amazing lectures, he made everything so simple and easy to understand. Definitely take him for macm 101
Horrible prof for cmpt 105w do not take him. Class average was a b- but the other class had a a- average. Do not take him for cmpt 105w
if you have the option take him
Despite being a notoriously difficult course with hard assignments, this professor managed to make it all clear and understandable that attending every class was definitely worth every second.
Goated Prof

Amazing lectures, amazing slides. Hard assignments (4 of them, 2-3 in pairs). Fair, medium-hard exams. About same difficulty as 295 but it's okay because Harinder made this one of my favourite cmpt courses so far.
Majestic speaking voice gosh dilly darn

Best prof at SFU. Very professional and he sets the standard of what a professor should be like. Exams are fair and assignments are challenging but teach you a lot. Lecture ability is amazing and he is very accessible outside of class.

I took Harinder back in Spring 2023 (those who know, know), and his class was extremely fair. Ample time for assignments, answered questions quickly on CourSys discussion forums. Provided practice quizzes that the midterms/final were based on. Assignments were tough but rewarding. Also took CMPT 310/MACM 101 with him and enjoyed thoroughly.
Great prof!
Mr. Khangura, I love you.
This is a challenging course but he made it much easier to understand. One of the best profs I've had.
Super. His lectures were easy to understand and the TAs were helpful at the tutorials.

Very clear and straight forward lectures; content itself is difficult to learn but he makes it as easy as possible to understand. Assignment quizzes and exams were pretty hard, if you don't know something you are out of luck.
Puts effort and enthusiasm into even the most simple lectures. Cant go wrong with Dr. Khangura

Many students enjoyed his course, but I didn't. He's lectures were very good, well organized, easy to understand, but mid-term and final were NOT easy. Do read his slides. However, the assignments were very time-consuming and difficult. Guidelines are vague which were hard to direct yourself! Plus, all implemented in C.

Midterms were easier than expected, final was fair. He explains things clearly and his lectures contain great examples. He really wants everyone to pass so the exams aren't outrageously hard. MACM is known to be a tough course, so I think Khangura did a good job teaching us. If you want a good mark, make sure to do practice problems every day.
Such a great prof for MACM makes everything very clear and easy to understand. Also very accessible outside of class which is nice. Cares for his students.

His lectures are very to the point and give many examples for you to understand the subject. Although the exams were quite difficult he was extremely nice in giving out half mark. I didn't have enough time to finish the midterm completely and rushed the last 3 questions but he gave me many half marks for what i wrote down. Very nice marker!

Harinder khangura is probably my favorite prof at SFU! Clear, concise, and entertaining lectures. His quizzes, assignments, tests are fair and reflective of the amazing quality of teaching he provides. If you plan on taking macm101, taking it with another prof would be a serious mistake! Can't say enough nice things about him! Highly recommend!

He's a great professor. A mature, respectful guy who speaks concisely and clearly. He does not assign too much work for marks, with most of the work outside of class being optional practice. All the info you need is in his lecture slides.

I enjoyed Harinder for CMPT 300. I think he is one of the best when it comes to preparation for lectures. His lectures/slides are great for understanding the material. No textbook was required. As others may have mentioned, the assignments are hard. If he gives the "s-talk" assignment, be prepared to spend a lot of time researching how to do it.

CMPT 300 is a notoriously hard course, however, Harinder does a really good job keeping the material interesting . Lectures were straightforward and his notes are straight to the point. Online classes gave us the opportunity to ask questions and he is quick to reply with detailed answers. Assignments are tough so do them early!

5 quizzes worth 60%, homework worth 10% and final worth 30%. If u fail the final u fail the whole course. The quizzes had been fair except induction where he pull out some hard ones. Final was pretty hard imo, and we have different versions, apparently some versions are easier too. I would recommend this class if u do not have to write exams ;)

Love him. Patient and very helpful. Lectures are clear, concise, and engaging (you'll usually practice the code alongside him). Highly recommend him because he'll give clear explanations and examples if you're struggling. A great introduction to Python.

Harinder put so much work into making sure online classes went well. He answered so many questions and was so accessible and helpful outside of class. Everything on the course website was well organized. The notes and lectures were well done.
I usually do not have many things to say about someone who does his job perfectly. That should do it.

Amazing professor. Lectures are very well organized and he is the type of professor that is so good at explaining that you end up learning a bunch in his classes and not just catching up to deadlines and not learning a thing. Very caring and very helpful professor, assignments are brutal but normal with CMPT300. Fair exams

An absolute gem of a professor. He will go above and beyond to make you have everything you need to succeed in class. He is very easy to approach and never hesitant to repeat himself for questions. He explains difficult to understand concepts in a very clear and concise manner. Exams and assignments are fair. 100% recommend

a cute and helpful prof. His lectures are clear and helps a lot w revision. If you listen to the lecture and take good notes (i.e, your own understanding along with filling the blanks he gives), you'll do well.

THe notes are pictures so take notes. The group project part gives no help or lectures. Accuses of plagiarism easily and wont reply Group project participation is graded against whole class Tricky questions like logic and codecracking and history Dont circle more than one if not needed. Final is everything way harder and more thniking questions
Good professor, very clear and helpful
A very great prof. He is nice and helpful. He explains things very clearly. As long as you attend the classes, pay attention and study the notes, it will be an easy A for you.
Exceptionally clear speaker. Reads from PowerPoint slides. I did not use my textbook.
Awesome prof

Great Prof. Very ethical person. His classes are very interactive and interesting. Crystal clear in his explanations. encourages class discussions to make it more interesting. Without a doubt, one of the best profs at SFU.
He is a good prof for sure! However, I think he demands his students in this course too much
work hard.
interesting course.fantastic prof,speaking clearly and making difficult things really easy for u to understand!!midterms are pretty easy.
good pro.

Amazing prof. He makes things so easy to understand and is very good with answering any questions or clarifying his in-class examples. His assignments can be tough but he gives very good directions as to how to do them while still being able to teach the important lesson of the assignment.
This guy kicks ass.

I think Harinder is a good prof. His lectures are very organized and if you do not understand, you can ask him after class or in office hour. He is not an easy prof. His exams are pretty tricky. If you do well in labs and assignments, you are going to be getting B+.
very good prof!! best one i've met in cmpt and he knows his stuff very well. class notes are useful so do attend class.
No Comments

He just doesnt care about class, hes just reading slides in lecture. You learn nothing. Exams are simple, you just have to memorize all the theorems and you would pass but there is no partial marks at all.

Horrible time management. Doesnt care much about the class. Heavy accent. Be ready to do alot of self learning. There r 15 mins YouTube video that taught the lectures better than Andrei's 70 mins video. Hws are VERY difficult. U will need external sources for help. He made it VERY clear about his expectations, & that ur problems r urs only, not his
I have trouble undrestanding his lectures. Cannot advice properly to the problems we face in the course.

I am not a huge math person, but Prof. Abulatov made me really enjoy a math class. He was a little intimidating in person when getting help, however, he really made me understand the material during lecture. If you get a chance take his class!

She is the worst teacher one can have for math. Gives long assignments filled with lengthy questions, then asks tough questions on the exam and refuses to elaborate on the criteria based on which she assigns students marks. She blatantly asks us to watch videos of another prof at SFU named Jamie, even during class. Nothing is covered during class.
Great
Highly recommended professor. 5 stars. Will take his course again.

Prof. Tayebi is by far the best instructor I've had. He's genuinely caring, understands what students in this program need, and gives us all the tools to succeed. He takes time after labs to connect with us and regularly brings in industry professionals to share their experience. He's definitely the best person to be leading Cybersecurity at SFU.

I took this fall course at SFU and, while he demands a lot, his cadence and expectations are truly industry level. Many who rated him poorly may not realize how much they learned. He genuinely made us better me included even though I already had substantial knowledge.

2 big group projects. 4 more projects. 11 assignments. 2 quizzes. A LOT of presentations, which take time away from learning. Terrible at teaching. Reads off slides. Dense, incoherent slides. Rude to students. Somewhat generous with grades. Assignments are useful + difficult, but worth only 2.5% each. Labs where the TAs explain stuff is useful.

The assignment grading system is frustratingly opaque. I've followed requirements and gotten same inputs/outputs, yet lost marks without clear feedback. Hard to learn from mistakes when I don't know what they are. A more transparent system would help.
Inconsistent grading criteria. Avoid him since there are many alternative profs available for this course.
His slides are erroneous.
Terrible quizzes. Terrible mistake-riddle slides. Won't learn anything in his class.

His class is him just readiing his mistake ridden slides. Additionally, there is a noticeable inconsistency in the grading criteria, which undergoes frequent changes on a weekly basis. Moreover, his teaching abilities are wanting, marked by challenges in both the articulation of the English language and logical comprehension
Great professor, terrible quizzes. If you read the slides and practice outside of your own time, you'll do just fine.

I really enjoyed his CMPT 120 class (it was my first time coding). His slides were easy to understand and follow. He did gave some challenging homework tasks, but the midterm and final exams were fairly easy to score in. I enjoyed this course so much that I decided to change my major!

He's such a kind and caring guy. The lectures weren't very understandable and sometimes legit useless, but the detailed slides make up for it. He's inexperienced with teaching, but I think you should give him a chance, he really tried his absolute best to teach. Assignments/exams were easy & fair (I had some previous programming knowledge though).
All i'm going to say is that he cares more about the class average than actual students learning experiences.

I had prior coding experience and did not need to learn too much new information. However, once I had a question and approached to ask wasn't helpful and it seemed he had limited knowledge especially if you ask out of scope questions. Other students struggled to keep up with the course pace. Generally, I recommend looking for alternative profs.

had zero coding experience and despite this course being an intro level, i really struggled. he ran a very fast paced course with heavy and difficult assignments that a lot of people struggled on BUT hes a super nice guy and really encourages attending office hours or emailing him. he's helpful and really wants everyone to succeed

Poor teaching skills and messy slides. Seemed approachable and organized at the begging of the semester so no one drop the course and afterwards he was kinda rude with monotonic and boring tone and unfair expectations. Just avoid. You learn nothing.

He is one of the nicest instructors I've ever had. He answers questions quickly on Piazza. Many complained about the difficulty of quizzes, but they were definitely doable. They were all online open book too and were only worth 10% of the course. He prepared practice exams for the midterm and final, both similar difficulties to the actual exams.

I'd say with having absolutely no prior coding experience, I found Mohammad to be pretty decent at teaching the subject. However, lectures can be boring, but can't really blame the guy since compsci is boring. He is super nice and caring only thing I would say is that assignments are a bit tricky and gradescope makes the experience even worse.

Coming from someone who's doing well in his class, avoid him (for 120 at least). He is very caring, did try his best, and gave reasonable exams, but his boring and convoluted lectures plus brutal assignments made CMPT 120 extremely painful. If you've never programmed before, good luck, because his expectations are insane for an intro course.

Great professor overall, is really caring towards his students and encourages everyone to succeed. Assignments are pretty tough but he is very helpful if you go to his office hours, and his lectures are also super useful. Final exam was pretty tough but fair, just make sure to keep up with topics and practice often!

Super nice guy and truly cares about his students. Gave multiple extensions on assignments. His exams are fair and the assignments prepare you for the exam. Provides tons of resources and practice questions, and makes an effort to get to know his students.

Prof Tayebi is a really good guy. As a first time coder, assignments were difficult but he was understanding and gave extensions. Exams were easier and are similar to his practise exams. He is really nice, tries to get to know his students, and wants to make sure students learn something and can apply it.

The class was very hard but he was a very caring prof and truly wanted his students to succeed. If you want to do well in this course with him you WILL have to actually put in the time and effort. He was very forgiving and gave extensions. Assignments are made extremely hard implementing everything we learned making the exams easier.

his exams are fair. he provides practice exams that prepare you well enough for the exams. he gave extensions to his assignments, but I feel like he wouldn't need to do that if he made the assignments a little easier. workload can be a lot if you have other classes to prioritize, other than that, he's a pretty good prof.

His assignments and quizzes are really hard, but the midterm and the final were quite fair tbh. He was really responsive and helpful if you asked. There was a free textbook but i feel lectures were better to work from as his slides are informative. Nice guy, gave lots of talks about life.

His assignments are really hard but as long as you put in the work it's possible to get 100 on a majority of them. He truly loves teaching but I think he fails to realize how difficult his assignments especially for people with no coding experience. His midterms are a breeze as long as you understand the fundamentals and review the quizzes

Honestly speaking, he's not as bad as these reviews make him out to be. He's obviously knowledgeable in the topics he's teaching and passionate about his job. He just doesn't know how to relay information well especially to first time coders. His assignments and quizzes are difficult but his exams aren't actually too bad if you studied well.
lack of enough knowledge and a low-level and disrespectful personality in interactions.

His philosophies in teaching are respectable but he is not a fitting instructor for CMPT 120. Lecture slides contain mistakes, convoluted explanations, unreasonable quizzes, and you're thrown into the fire with homework. If I'm struggling despite having prior coding experience, then I don't know how first-time programmers can survive his course.

His class is not helpful at all. For someone who has no coding experience, this class will make you stress, and it's a waste of time. The assignment is extremely hard than what he teaches in the lecture. You need to learn extra from different website to understand. Overall not worth it for someone who is beginner in coding.

I don't think his teaching style is suitable for lower level classes. His expectations are too high and he does not go over the examples enough for us to comprehend them. Quizzes worded very weirdly at first but it's improved. The assignments seem too complex for it to be intro level but it is possible to get 100 if you work on it enough.

He refuses to give partial marks for anything because "nobody wants half-working code", which is terrible for anybody who has no coding experience, and wants to try it out. Rushes through material, he's gone through about 3/4 of all of the python programming language in a month and a half. Super rude and condescending in his responses to questions.

Assignments are unnecessarily long and tougher than they should be, lectures are dry and not really helpful just read the slides. He was interactive and enthusiastic during the first 2 weeks of class and afterwards he became monotoned and extremely boring causing almost everyone to zone out.

Slides have a lot of mistakes and quizzes are hard. Assignments are tough and even more hard with an autograder. Lectures are confusing and boring. Really frustrating and not beginner friendly like the course says it is

Like the comment below said, the assignment grading system is super frustrating and doesn't let you know where the error is. You could do everything that was asked for in the assignment with identical input/outputs but still get marks deducted.
when people say he has poor english or whatever it's not true. his slides are difficult to follow but it is not due to his language skills

As someone with no coding experience whatsoever, I am struggling in this class as of right now...even though the course description made it sound like it was suitable for me. I do recall the prof saying he was rushing through the basics to give us more time to do other stuff or something like that? And it is NOT working. Take it with someone else.

Lectures are difficult to follow. Professor makes an already difficult topic even worse. His explanations are difficult to understand as he overcomplicates things, and is quite condescending with answers to questions. Goes through the material extremely quickly, and adds unnecessary exploratory topics to the lecture that are not helpful.

Doesn't know how to teach first year courses. He is poorly prepared for lectures, his slides are a mess, the questions/examples are full of errors. He does not teach the material in a clear/approachable way for beginners. The assignment grading system is frustrating and the quizzes are impossible and often include things not covered yet in class.

Super nice and well organized. Always happy to help students by all means. Assignments were relevant to the course material. The course was offered online and he handled it well. Exams were very fair and he did not seem to be tough grader. I do recommend him for this course

OMG! this prof screwed the grades at the end of the semester. obviously, people getting A,A+ would say good things but I am really disappointed that in a cmpt course at 87% I got B. and explain me how is this a higher grade for my hard work? this means people who get in range of A are the respectable ones only.

The course was well organized by Prof. Tayebi and we got good feedback from the assignments which really helped us in the group project. The final was a bit scuffed but everything else I think was well done. He is easily accessible through Piazza and will answer most if not all questions in a timely manor. Would recommend.

I understand sql stuff are relatively easy to teach in CS, but still, he's one of the best CMPT professor I've had so far. He is extremely organized and the amount of resources he gives is really appreciated. His TA management is great as well. He can sometimes be a little hard to understand, but overall I've had an amazing experience in this class

Had lots of learning resources that helped me learn/practice concepts (lectures, tutorials, mentorships, practicals, Ripple, Piazza) but only Ripple questions were for marks/mandatory. Homework and exam were exactly what I expected but obviously harder than usual but still doable. I recommend doing the practicals, they really help

His lectures are good but he is really a tough grader if the class average is high. I dont mind whatever grade scheme is being used in his class but he should specify in the starting that he is going to screw your GPA with his own decided grade scheme. I am really disappointed at the end of the semester and I am sad that I took my classes with him

Solid lecture notes, lectures themselves can run late sometimes but content is good. Semester projects and homework assignments were solid and helped you apply material learned in class well. Easy to reach and get feedback outside of class, and was flexible in adjusting assignments/projects according to student feedback. Solid prof, would take.
Reads 100 slides within an hour which is too fast for most students . Does not stop and discourages questions. Do not take unless you have no other option as a professor.
Just Awful.

Guy is based for hating this course, it is almost entirely useless information or obvious. The downside is that his exams are stuff he printed off ms word the night before, with options such as a) b) c) d) e) f) a) b) Of course its the typical read from the textbook style that 9/10 of the profs have, but that isn't surprising to you, is it?

Pro: exams didn't have mistakes. Gives clear info on what on the exam. Will replace midterm grades with final's if you get higher marks on final. Con: Lectures go through materials quickly. Unlikely to answer questions during lecture. Lecture notes are reused from previous years.

You won't get much from his teaching style as he just reads off slides. First midterm screwed me over since I did practice questions from the textbook but most of the midterm were different types of questions straight from the slides. You'll have better luck predicting what questions on the slides will be on the exams than actually studying

Hes not like any prof i have had before. He believes this is a bs course so his attitude towards teaching it isn't very good. He tried his best to teach us what he believes is actually useful though. He is entertaining but its hard to tell what we should be learning because he skips over half of his ppts. Easy class but hard to excel in it.

The lectures were not helpful at all. He read off the slides and skipped multiple of them, claiming that they were so easy that he didn't need to teach them to us. He would not explain anything and was not willing to answer questions. If you take this course, you will have to teach yourself everything.

Lectures almost useless as he neither knows how nor cares to make them actually educational. Tutorials are only Q&A. If you are new to math and need guidance, then you'll get none. You have to learn everything on your own from the slides. Otherwise the course itself is doable. 2/5 since at least he isn't actively making the course impossible

I loved his class, he makes it so fun and it is the only class that I went home knowing what he taught in class, exams are fair, group project depends on your group, he gave everyone high grades, there are to much slides but you just need to go to class as he skips a lot and tells you what to focus on for the exams. One of the best classes!

This man is all jokes and no substance. Skips entire slides because it's 'redundant', and speaking with other 276 classes, he doesn't cover what they do. Exams are weighed heavily, much of it being mc's and he won't release the grade cut-offs. He was also quite negative towards his students.If you want edgy, pessimistic commentary on SE, go for it.

He says the term project's the most important part of the course, yet makes exams worth 70% of the grade. That wouldn't be a problem if they weren't just multiple choice gambles. Skips half the slides citing uselessness, leaving us underprepared for real world corporate/industry situations. Funny, cool guy but not the right course for him to teach.

Courseload and grading scheme was easy to achieve good scores. Exams were not too difficult, mostly containing MCQ. You can select your own team members for the group project and it was quite lenient. Lectures were not that useful but he was very funny and kept me engaged. Highly recommend if you need the grades and light workload.

unless you pay him off you're not getting any kind of good grade in this class bro intentionally mislead us for every test after we scored too high on the first midterm and we ended up with the lowest class average of all time. big homie is losing it

Steve seems to know the topic well, however he doesn't know how to teach. Whenever someone asked a question, he would make remarks about how dumb the question was, or even make fun of the student. Whenever he was teaching something, he would make remarks about how it was so easy he could do it in his sleep, and so skip over half the explanation.

Just show up to lectures. He explains the necessary topics well and filters through the garbage in the textbook slides. You'll have no problem passing this class with Pearce. He also makes lectures entertaining and discusses events in history that are very interesting to learn about.

HORRIBLE dude if you have him pray that you can pass his class. He spends the entire lecture nonsensically reading the slides but getting confused about his own material. Would cancel class randomly after we were already there because he had a headache. did this like 6 times in a row right before our midterm. how this man is still a professor here
you're either gonna love or hate pearce. very interesting person

He can't teach, very hard to understand his lectures (don't expect to) but his slides are good and majority of exams is straight from the powerpoints. Go to lectures even if you don't understand a thing because he gives away which questions are on the exams. Not a good experience at all but he's a man of his word and bought the whole class pizza
Very rough class. He is a pretty funny guy, but he cannot teach. Would not reccomend taking his classes, but if you need to do so you should get ready for a lot of self studying.
AVOID AT ALL COSTS IT'S NOT WORTH IT. Completely self-taught course, only merit to going to lecture is getting exam questions that he loosely gives out randomly
Only went to his lectures to have fun watching him harass students, bro cannot teach. Do not take this class💀
He's really not that bad, he's very uhhh interesting, so I'm never bored in class. He's also a man of his word, he said he would buy pizza for us and buy us pizza he did.
How this man is still employed I do not know. He is an inspiration to us all that you can get paid to not know how to do your job.

Took in the summer sem. Lectures are very content heavy, but engaging so be ready to learn on your own. Attending lecture is absolutely necessary as he often gives away exam questions. His exams are copy paste questions from the lectures, so as long as you study those, the exams are extremely easy. He is a funny guy, just pay attention in lectures.

Avoid at all costs, doesn't have any contact info for both himself and the TA's in the class. Doesn't respond to the discussions channel on Canvas and doesn't explain absolutely any incorrections on assignments and midterms/final. Always bullies and ridicules students in class and plays it off as a joke. Never taking anything with him again.
Prof was very cool guy. I thought some of the rants were funny
He has a good sense of humor. Do not skip the lectures and you'll be fine. Best prof!
Best class ever, this was so entertaining, amazing professor, I had 60% average and got a B+

Steven Pearce gets a bad rap for his crazy antics but he doesn't make the class difficult. He literally told us exactly what would be on the midterms and finals, as in the exact questions, which you could then memorize. So if you are after a good grade I would take this prof. He is also pretty funny in class, and mostly chill. You wont be bored.

Professor Pearce keeps things interesting. The class is pretty easy, with only multiple-choice finals reused from past exams. The group project is time-consuming but manageable. Unstuckstudy AI and ChatGPT were great study aids. Overall, a solid class choice.

The professor responds to emails very well and seems very passionate about quantum computing. Not too sure why he gets all the hate and he wants his students to genuinely succeed. Don't take evening classes with him though as he gets tired easily and ends lectures early. He also tells everyone exactly what's on the exams.

I think he rolls a dice to determine your grade. It's very very easy to get at most B+ but impossible to do better except by sheer luck since the questions he asks on tests are so arbitrary and his personal opinions. The grade you get has nothing to do with how much effort you put in. Don't take if you need A or better

He's one of the best profs I've ever had. Refreshing to see a prof that actually cares about students beyond his course material. Yes he debunks some conspiracy theories in his class, he does that to show the importance of critical thinking and not following the herd. He has a unique sense of humor, and made boring topics very interesting.

Dr. Pearce is a hunk of malignant magic. He's a social sorcerer and a complete troll. I love the guy for not taking life too seriously. The group project took so many hours, worth 20%... But the final is mcq reused from past exams (easy af). He's great for a course like this where all of the information in hot dog water. Pick good ppl for group.
only mcq in final

Steven Pearce is a very agile and friendly person. There will never be biases coming into play in his exams either, so you won't have to worry about ambiguity! I love his stories of exploring the world with his two best friends Jack and Daniel, and how he would love to research waterfalls in Beijing with Dr. Marx and Prof. Zedong. Best prof!
If your actual performance in this course is a C-, you might even get an A by taking his class.
If your actual performance in this course is a C-, you might even get an A by taking his class.

Ignore the people talking about the conspiracy theories, He quite literally explores how critical thinking and fact-based argumentation negates the false claims of conspiracy theories! He reiterates and pushes the class to create dialogues and critical thinking. Does he go on tangents? Yeah, but if you read the textbook, he rants about whats in it.

Steven Pearce usually delivered great lectures that often covered not only the course material but also applications and use cases for the material. He was always available after class for Q/A. The exams were fair for the course.
Steven Pearce is extremely lazy and disorganized, but he's funny and gave me an A.

I am quite annoyed with his random grading policy. For example, he let final grade subsitute midterm if you do better in final. His final is simply ~7 questions hinted in class. They are too easy for those who memorize them but too hard for those who not. The final can take 70% percent so you will expect 10% per question haha.

Honestly not as horrible as his reputation. Pearce is disorganized and reads off slides for lectures. He's busy but lazy. For the final he helped us by giving out several "hints" in class; many questions were the same as the ones he hinted at. Great guy to have a discussion/talk with, but maybe not the best at introducting a new topic to students.
Exams are based on luck. Depends if you memorized the correct question he chose. You dont end up learning anything in this class. It's a GPA lottery based on memorization.
Don't recommend him... Extremely unprofessional, reads off of slides only, and assignments and exams are not relevant and helpful for learning the material.
SO goooood!!! easy course. This guy can be your best prof in your life

Only thing done in class is just read lecture notes and tell some jokes. Can not solve students question. My grandma also can teach this course as long as if she can speak English but without telling some jokes.

Mixed feelings about Pearce. If you take a course that Pearce is teaching because he's genuinely interested in teaching (ex: 409, 320), he'll be interesting but if you take a course that he's being forced to teach by the faculty (MACM 101), he'll be pretty bad. For 409, interesting lectures, but disorganized.

Disorganized, disrespectful and distressing. Presentation slides are excessively long with little emphasis on importance of subjects, and often include numerous errors. Quick to belittle students for asking for clarification on "trivial" topics. Lectures are completed as quickly as possible and typically end early.

I'm not too sure how this man is employed, unprofessional, abrasive, disorganized, sloppy, and inattentive. His emails look like they were sent by someone in grade 11 and he never seems to know what materials he has actually given out. Worst part is, the midterm was worth 30% but was only out of 25 marks.

One of the most bizarrely abusive professors I've ever taken. I don't remember him being half this bad before COVID. Sends out flurries of emails to the entire class, blaming students for not understanding "trivial" things when he gets several questions about the same topics. Just overall a weird and unpleasant class.

The worst professor, no doubt!! It's the first time he take over CMPT 310 and the lecture is sooooo messy without any logic. It seems that he knew nothing about AI, but just repeat what the textbook said

Does not know anything about the course material he has not been able to answer questions that students have asked him to clarify. Slides are always disorganized and have errors in them every week. Course content and assignment and projects are disconnected. The worst prof that I have had in 5 years at SFU.
Amazing prof

Pearce does not give a darn about his students. Powerpoints are 100+ slides per lecture. He only reads off his slides, doesn't explain anything, and gets mad when 90% of the class doesn't understand. I eventually didn't bother going to lectures because the textbook was a better resource than sitting there confused for 3hrs/week. Get ready to READ

Blazes through lectures -> early dismissal. Cool guy with good vibe in class, but teaching is a disaster. "Do you guys get this? Just nod and say yes". Tests are unnecessarily hard because his lectures lack substance: watch youtube instead. Grading scheme heavily emphasizes tests - which are difficult. Other than his jolly spirit, steer clear.

Dr. Pearce is the funniest guy ever. He cracks a lot of jokes in class and keeps it real. He likes to speedrun his lectures so you'd get an early dismissal every time. MACM is fast-paced and it is a full-time job, so you really have to work for it. When studying for exams, I suggest mainly going over his PowerPoint examples instead of the textbook.
Bro just speedruns powerpoints so don't even bother attending lectures. You're better off watching videos on Youtube.
his lectures were really bad... like so useless... i stopped going to them and switched to teaching myself from the textbook after the first midterm and it helped enormously

He is a terrible terrible teacher. When I say take literally any other professor I mean it. He constantly made insensitive comments and would get mad at people asking questions. His tests are highly based on his slides which he leaves mistakes in. And he will say something is on the test but then it isnt even actually. Dont believe what he says.

Seems like a good guy, knows most people takes the course for fun and tries to give minimal work. Content opened my eyes about ethics/privacy/etc. The good stops there. Tests are difficult, he keeps saying it's easy. He genuinely hates online lectures, and blames students doing poorly b/c we don't attend his lectures. Problem: slides are disorganzi

Pearce usually ends class half an hour early while vomiting out an artistic interpretation of insomnia on Powerpoint. I didn't feel safe in class as his behaviour often lacked empathy. The tests were designed for maximum student disappointment. Although he can be funny, I can't in good faith recommend a professor who destroyed my mental health.

Pearce is hard to follow, and the course is super weird. So many parts that are disconnected. And the readings have nothing to do with the course content. I didnt attend or watch any lectures. I just did the readings, memorized the main points from there and from the slides, and prayed for the best. If you want to learn, dont take.
Best prof for CMPT 320!
Very passionate about the subject matter, engaging lectures with clear expectations. Fun course but requires paying attention to details for success.

Steven Pearce has to be the worst professor i have ever had! He spends all of class reading off powerpoints and doesnt explain anything and doesnt use any examples in class. In addition to this he is very disorganised as he refuses to use canvas and is harder track class plans and content. Please think twice before taking macm 101 with this prof!

He is funny. He is also chronically tired from insomnia and gives often unclear lectures. He tells students to not ask questions and speeds through his power point slides rarely giving examples. He does hint at exam questions ahead of time, but often that means you need to memorize a section of text book and repeat verbatim. That's not learning.
doesn't teach at all, horrible prof. he makes his exams really hard. avoid this teacher !!
During his lectures he just reads off his power points word for word. Tests are much more difficult than the textbook problems and assignments. Take this class with someone else!!!

"I don't feel like reading this, so you guys can read it" about sums up lectures. He just reads the lecture slides, doesn't even process the information he's telling you. However, he pretty much gives you the exam qs if you do listen--meaning you need to come to class. Do the old exams--he reuses Qs. Easy grading, mind-numbingly frustrating guy.
Speeds through slides and has very hard tests

He's not a bad prof by any means, but you can tell he's not very passionate about this subject. To be honest, nobody is since this is class is super useless. However, hs assignments and exams and really easy so if you want a stress-free course, this is the one to take.
Funny prof but also pretty hard. Plus, the midterm class average was 33% lmao. Reflects more on the prof than the students I think.

Obviously doesn't care about teaching. He goes through hundreds of power point slides in a lecture and just reads off them. No engagement with the class or pauses of any kind. Totally worthless professor.
SFU should not let him continue teaching and waste so many students' time. He swears in class and never give clear requirements for projects. Unorganized notes and useless lectures.
Barely ever shows up to class. Gets mad at students for asking questions. Instructions are useless and he refuses to clarify. 0/5

I really appreciate Dr. Steven Pearce that he made the online version of CMPT276 easier, particular when Anne Lavergne gave me a hard CMPT295 in the same term. But each coin has two sides, Dr. Steven Pearce did not teach a lot. I agree with most of other comments on him that "if you just want grade, take his course".

This professor is extremely underrated, his course is extremely light workload( only two short assignments for 10% of final grade). However, the final exam is kind of hard to pick the best answer for the mc questions. The lectures are fun and relax, but not an easy A.

Pretty interesting lectures if you're into the stuff, I attended most of them. Lectures and readings are somewhat disconnected from the midterm/exam as you can see from my grade :'(. Our 3 assignemnts got cut down to 2. Need to buy a course pack too, couldn't pirate it. Probably better to attend this course in-person if ya interested.

This is for the first offering of the special topics class on Quantum Computing. Given the circumstances, I think he did OK. None of us had the necessary math background to understand much of QC, and this is the first offering. He did what he could to get us to understand QC concepts and his passion was apparent. Homework and exams were very easy.

Course: Intro to Quantum Computing Pearce bounces around lecture slides in a dizzying manner, while assailing the class with an absolute wall of information. There's no dedicated course website, yet tons of outside resources (multiple textbooks, papers, videos). It's the first iteration of the course and it definitely needs improved structure.

This guys nuts. There's no actual answers to any questions in the course, you have to show up to lectures so that you can listen to his rants and soak in his weird opinions. This was the lowest quality course I've ever taken at SFU, I'm appalled that SFU allows this to continue. He showed up to the midterm without enough paper.

For a designated W course CMPT 376 with Pearce has very little writing. We did 3 small assignments and one large writing assignment during the course. I would avoid taking this class with Pearce if you actually want to learn about technical writing. If you just want a laid-back class then Pearce is the way to go even though he is a bit crazy

The course itself is a combination of sociology and philosophy so don't enrol if you are not interested in those topics. Pearce is pretty much crazy which makes him interesting but also makes his lectures hard to follow.
I took 105 & 106 with Pearce. He is probably the best profs for those classes, as he did not expect anything serious from students. Be aware of his exams (very tricky MCQ).

Took in 2016. Pearce had some interesting points to make, but overall there wasn't much to learn. I wish he had put more time into making the course substantial. We spent many hours of class watching vaguely relevant movies. There were almost no tests/assignments. He's kind of a crazy prof, but that also means he's interesting to talk to.

Steven may not be the best at teaching, but, he makes the class super easy! The average for the class was a B+ and the exams were not very difficult. On top of this, he's very funny and I actually enjoyed coming to lectures. The group project can be tricky if you get a poor team, but that is completely separate from the teaching material anyway.
The worst prof I have ever had hands down. Avoid at all costs because you will not learn anything. Spend your money on a prof that you will actually learn from and wont have waste your time.
GOOD professor, funny guy, not a tough grader.
This guy is not good if you want to gain knowledge but if you only needs grades then take this guy.

Steve is not a good lecturer, just reads slides of text to you, not even covering useful material, and complains about having to be there. Makes inappropriate jokes and is very unprofessional. Midterm and final are almost all multiple choice and almost half the questions are based on his opinions. Avoid.
I loved his lectures. class was math heavy, with extra theorems.

Contrary to what a lot of students say about him, I thought he was amazingly witty during lectures. He tries to engage the students with almost a comedic approach. He's very blunt, and let's you know ahead of time what to study and lookout for. Cheat sheets are allowed for midterms, and if you visit his office hours, he's genuinely a caring guy.

discourages asking questions in class and ESPECIALLY through email. Lectures are basically useless; read the text and lecture slides. He'll hint half of material on the exams in the last few lectures leading up to an exam. Make sure you know how to answer questions on previous exams-- they will be very similar

Cheat sheet for midterms. Hints at questions that will be asked. Slides sent in email. Graded homework (10%). Class ends early. Textbook, WIkipedia, few sources for slides. Rude to students and TAs. Expects us to know terminology and material before it is covered (during lectures).

Enthusiastic guy. Seems very intelligent. But horrible lecturer and either is unwilling or unable to pass on basic information on either course material or course logistics. Worth seeing outside of lectures - he's helpful then.

I regret not taking CMPT300 with a better prof. He "accidently" sends out the answer key for old final exams but intentionally has some wrong answers on them so people don't just memorize them. More concerned about keeping the average high so he doesn't get fired rather than making sure we learn the material. Final was all MC. I didn't learn much.

Clear on what to be expected on exams. Slides are snips of Wikipedia which means he just reads off slides. Read textbooks to grasp concepts before classes. Always double check your grades. Final exams had wrong answer keys, so for the midterm exams. Otherwise, it's easy with Steven

This class was very disorganized probably because it was his first time teaching Cmpt 110. Overall his lectures were not as bad as it seems and if you read the lectures ahead of time, this class is a breeze. His final had some questions which were not in the study material but it's scaled so you'll end up with a higher grade than expected.

I took 2 courses with him back to back and he is fun. He will tell you exactly what is on the exam. You might not understand the stuff in class but reading his slides and marking important ones will do the job. It's highly likely you will end up with good grade without understanding most of the stuff. Good luck!

He was really easy on us UFV students, at least this semester. No quizzes, easy assignments, short lectures. His lectures consist of 70-90 slides but ends up being half of that as he'll tell you what to study and what not to. The midterm was almost all multiple choice, but are really hard and require you to know your stuff to choose the correct 1
He is a legend, definitely should take his course. He is inspirational, and wont let you just memorize stuff, but let you understand it and absorb it.
He is my favourite teacher at university of Fraser Valley. His way of teaching is really interesting and funny, which makes us easy to understand. Hope to get good marks in this course.

Very disorganized prof. Copies and pastes material from wikipedia onto lecture slides and tells the class to "read it". Assignments at the start were nothing like lecture material because he ripped it off the online class. Way too much theory. Lazy prof, does not help during office hours. TA is even worse.
Legend
Most fun course I ever took! Cant wait to take some other course with him!

Hilarious professor who really cares about this class. He has a strange obsession with the incompleteness theorems of Godel. If he brings them up as those results up as "the last significant result in science", mention Tarski's undefinability theorem and Turing's negative proof of the Halting Problem, and you'll gain his respect.

3 steps to get a good grade in his class: 1. Go to lectures and write down every single thing that he says is important and the hints he gives out about the midterms/final. 2. Read the textbook, do the chapter examples and problems. 3. Do the homework and keep up with the course materials.
Useless course, boring lectures but pretty much mandatory to go since he tells you what parts of the notes are important.

His programming assignments have gotten tougher over the years; do not underestimate them. Unlike in some math courses where the assignments don't really seem to count despite saying otherwise in the syllabus, in this course, assignments CAN help with your letter grade. He absolutely despises grade-grubbing, so beware of that too.
The course content is quite boring. He makes funny jokes. His lectures are unfortunately kind of difficult to follow.
Pretty straightforward prof. If he tells you something is going to come in the exam, its gonna show up. Would take another class with him in the future,
