I'm not even good at math but this course is an easy A, if you go to class. He literally, yes literally told us 5 of the 7 questions that would be on the midterm, and told us a huge portion of the questions that would be on the final. If you go to class and write down what is going to be on the exams, this will be the easiest A you will ever get.
He is a gorgeous man with a hilarious attitude. And those eyes...
Dr Steven Pearce is the funniest professor I've ever had. Makes going to lectures something to look forward to. Drops hints about the midterm and exam during lecture which is always fabulous
If he says something is important, it's important. Just study that before exams. That's the key for getting an A in this course

I took this course with him in 2013. Did no work and still got a B+ (I do have a strong background in the course material to begin with). The best part is the final was composed of questions from his previous exams and exams from other universities. Those questions and answers were available online so I pretty much knew everything before the exam.
Lectures are unclear, but i think it is necessary since he give out hints for final and midterm. Exams are very hard, but i managed to get a B- with 40% in the class.(The class is heavily scaled)

Steve goes very fast in lecture, but it's worth going to because he drops several hints on what will be on the exams there. His assignments are very easy, mostly book questions. His exams are pretty standard, and he lets you have a cheat sheet. He also doesn't cover strong induction or probability. Read the books and his lecture notes and its easy.

Steven Pearce sucks, he has no idea about the course and he doesn't know how to teach. He make fun of his students and show boring & long movies during lectures. Avoid him if you want to learn something
interesting lectures, difficult tests, nice guy

Love this guy! He is funny, seems abrasive, and that's what we need. He will bend over backwards to help you, and will never turn anyone away looking for help. Most of the students complaining about him are the ones used to getting spoon fed. You will learn a lot and have a great experience in his class.
Love this professor. Lectures are very entertaining and assignments are closed related to course topics. Shows considerate and warm personality during office hour visits.

Whether you enjoy CMPT 320 with him or not is going to be up to your personality, he is very laid back and unstructured. I found the lecture content to be interesting and entertaining. The exams however are brutal and the questions are often very abstract. Overall cool course content, but overly complex delivery.
Funny guy. He's very passionate about the topic and has very interesting lectures. Might get grumpy and pick on kids that are late and his exam questions occasionally aren't very clear
He's probably the best professor for the course. He can be very fast and unclear in lecture, but his expectations are very clear. I'd recommend taking MACM with him.
The lectures are short. The assignments are very hard. The exams are even harder.

I love the guy - he's hilarious during lectures and very accommodating and kind during office hours. Doing well in his class is not impossible: go to lectures and he will often tell you what's on the final. Also go to office hours and he gives out supplemental worksheets. He likes repeating questions so get your hands on previous exams. Study hard.
Does a great job on complicating and making the course material abstractive. Though, he is hilarious.

He's a pretty mediocre teacher. He's good in that he posts slides online, makes jokes in lecture (of varying quality), gives out practice midterms(which not all profs do) and will constantly drop hints on the midterm. Cons are: goes very fast in lecture, non responsive to questions, very unclear as to what he wants us to do.

Do not take this class with him,and if you do, remember that you should complain about him (which you will want to do!) to the department head (Dr. Esther). Just writing here will not change anything, and Pearce will be allowed to ruin more and more students.

I took MACM101, CMPT300, and CMPT320 with steve. I had trouble understanding his teaching for 101 and 300. But when it came to 320, I enjoyed his class and the materials he provided. He was funny (well, maybe a little bit rude lol?) and clever with abundant of life experience. Exams could be a harsh, but I learnt a lot from him in 320.
Wastes time in lectures. Assignments are nothing like the material covered in lecture. Purpose made the midterm extra hard to drop class average to his standards. NOT RECOMMENDED!

He is the worst prof at SFU. His questions are unclear, so you have to guess what he wants. He invents answers to his questions, no matter what the books say. Get a low grade cause you didn't guess what he thinks.

I have been tutoring cmpt120 for 4 years and Steven Pearce is the most ridiculous person I have ever met: his note is formed by some pointless Wikipedia terms and he doesn't even know what he is teaching. His humor is 10 times stronger than his programming skill, so don't expect his help for coding.

Very funny guy. Very doable class. Be prepared to memorize and PROVE binomial & pascal theorem. Pretty much PROVE anything that will be covered in lecture. Attend lecture at all cost or you will miss a lot of exam hints that will definitely be 100% be in the midterms and final exam. If you do the above, you will get an easy A like me.

I found my past-post is gone... maybe bcz I said too many "good word" about Steven so the site admin just clean me up. But I still want to comment one more time: The teacher is a fool! ???????!El profesor es un idiota!???????!L'enseignant est un imbécile!L'insegnante è un pazzo!

DONT TAKE THIS CLASS OR IT WILL RUIN YOUR LIFE! I have never failed any courses at SFU until I took this course with him. His slides are useless. Whatever he says in lecture is irrelevant. The videos he made us watch were pointless irrelevant useless. He did not show up for my appointment with him because he does not care

First of all I must say I dont usually write on these things, and i actually ended up with a decent mark. But man he is a terrible teacher.His slides are outdated with massive wikipedia quotes and he's all over the place in lecture. The class is 90% midterm and final and is WAY too difficult for the content.he asks the most specific irrelevant q's.

The most interest prof you will ever meet in a lifetime. Classes are very entertaining. It feels more like a friend to friend relationship than a teacher to student relationship. He provokes us to think. If this course sucks it probably because of the t.a. Pearce is not recommended for people that are lazy and hate being called out in public
Dreadful professor, his slides had screenshots from wikipedia...has serious temper issues and treats questions with contempt. Has absolutely zero educational ability, avoid at all costs.

This is a horrible prof, one of the worst at SFU, because he is completely chaotic. We didn't learn anything in class (slides had quotes from wikipedia), but he gave his TAs free rein to mark whenever they wanted, however they wanted, as late they wanted. We never got ONE explanation for our marks, no comments, no answers to emails from TAs.
Steven Pearce is an excellent lecturer, one of the best at SFU. He really makes an effort to clearly explain course content and keep things interesting.
Hard prof! he is one of the worst professor in sfu. The midterm was so hard and a lot people fail the course. He is lazy and very rude to student

One of the most unique teaching style that most students won't be able to get used to. Seems to be busy all the time and get annoyed easily when students trying to send him emails. Though, students are expected to be "SMART" (No stupid questions and being lazy)
This Prof is AWESOME. Best Prof in my entire undergrad years. If you want a good grade, al you need to do is to keep up with the readings and pay attention to what he says in lecture.

The course is like a philosophy and social course. It seems interesting but boring. It is true that we responds to know the staff, but it is stupid to test them. The one who has sample test has good score. Otherwise, students cannot image what will happen in the test since it is impossible to remember everything and it is useless in your career.

The course is very theoretical and useless. You just need to memorize, so it is so boring. Steven's teaching style made it a little funny. However, he is very lazy and rude to response you if you make him busy. He is not that helpful, and also his TA. The TA need 6 weeks to mark an assignment, and they rarely show up in their office, so helpless.
Very very bad. The course is so boring. The exam has nothing to do with the lecture.

Very Bad. He is rude in lectures, full of discrimination and harassment. His exam has nothing to do with his lecture notes. His notes make you more confusing than you were before his course. And his grade system is bad too.
Well, I earned D grade with everything almost hit the average. I really don't like his lecture because he is so rude. The exams is fine but the grade system is so bad.
By far one of the worst professors I have taken. He makes the material more confusing that it is, you are so much better off learning from the book.
The worst prof I ever had in sfu. He thins all the students are stupid! He does not know respect people!

Laziest prof ever. Reads from his ppt which are poorly made with screenshot from wikipedia and content from other universties with inconsistent ugly background. Assignments are hardly relevant to the course and the marking is unfair from TAs. Makes horrible jokes all day to cover his bad teaching and cancels classes when he does not want to attend

Overall, it is a useful course ruined by a terrible and lazy professor and two irresponsible TAs. Assignments are vague without clarification, and TA only give you terrible grades with no feedback nor any marking detail. He canceled the last day of class only because of in class evaluation. Honestly, You wouldn't learn much in his lecture.

He made a lot of jokes in class rather than teaching. In his class, I just feel that it is wasting time for the most part of the lecture. He is so lazy that prefer student submitting assignments through email or hard copies to the TA rather than setting an online submission. never clarify on the assignment. For the ppt, he just screenshot of wiki.

Horrible. He just reads his powerpoint. Last few week was short on time so he cover 3-4 ppts but never tested. He said he would remake the final exam but simply combined 2 old finals from the previous years. Instructions for assignments are vague and marking is strict and without feedback. AVOID AT ALL COST
This prof is the most hilarious prof I have ever seen. Not PC, very direct and in your face. He was made for CMPT320.

Not a good teacher for this course. Disappointed in how the course was taught. Basically read powerpoint slides the whole semester, i felt like i was taking a distance ed class as I had to learn and understand everything on my own. Envious of others who took this course with other instructors who actually were taught important things in OS theory.

his class is interesting, you can't get bored, he is helpful and very understanding, his teaching abilities beats the hell out of Brad Bart useless classes full of sarcastic annoying jokes, at least this guy actually has a PHD to be qualified for the job plus Dr.Pearce doesn't give you attitude when you approach him with a question

Gives out midterms before hand, and I found the final online. If not for all this, I would have failed. Use the textbook to clarify what he says, listen in class for 'hints' and without studying or trying, you will get a B+ like me. He is entertaining! The class itself is hard, but he is an EASY marker. Wants his students to do well

Teaches the way courses should be taught, bantering with students and answering questions. Took two courses with him in the same term wish I could take more. Pay attention when he tells you something will be on the exam.

Pearce is very smart, but his class is really hard. He expects you know to everything already. His notes online and in class is poorly presented and difficult to understand. He makes occasional jokes sometimes, which makes the class sorta interesting.(not really...) It is is expected to do all odd problems from the textbook on your own.
Not heavy on the programming (Which was a good thing). I learned quite a bit. Could be a bit more organized though. I definitely would recommend.

Steven is a smart guy. That is the only positive think I have to say about him. He rolls through his notes way to fast in class. The notes he displays online are hard to understand. He does not respond to email and he's annoyed when you go see him during office hours. And his exams are extremely difficult. Don't take his class. Only for math wizes.
Easy class.I didn't learn much. Felt like I wasted my tuition $. Take it with someone else if you want to learn.
good class, lots movies, exams not hard. overall good class

I hated him so much initially but by the end of the course, I got used to his style and now, he's among my favs. You need to study what he teaches in the class from the textbook and you'll do great. Go to his office hours. He's THE most hilarious prof at SFU.

horrible horrible marker. I cannot believe that he is proud of announcing the average is below 50%. He is not helpful at all. He complains about the noise outside the classroom every class. He should know the noises he make is the most bothering thing
He is fun,but not helpful~~~The midterm is hard, and he did not test what he said about the reiewing. Don't take class with him.
I learn this course more from the textbook and friends than coming to his lecture.... he is a funny but not suitable to teach! I highly recommend u DONT TAKE THIS CLASS!
The contents covered are indeed easy but he is very skilled to complicate everything.
Great class! No programming required.The professor is extremely helpful is you go to his office hour.
Very interesting and fun class! The professor is crystal clear!One of the best class I ever had in SFU.

Overall, I think Steven is definitely the best person to take 320 with if you want your money well spent. Although I got an A in this class, I was interested in the material. The class is fun if your not some religious freak or someone who cannot be open to new (and realistic) ideas. Steve talks the truth and is not afraid to do so. Much respect.

I learned more from Steve than most classes I've taken, despite my final grade (my fault entirely, not his). He really knows his content, develops interesting assignments to test your knowledge, and has a great sense of humor. He will teach you a lot about life itself and to think critically in addition to the course content.
he is a complete nutcase must be on .,..

He's a person who really knows his stuff, his specialty is scientific modelling. His approach is unorthodix and challenges students to keep up, speak up and as long as you go in with eyes wide open you will be fine. Dogmatics will be challenged to defend their position his way of getting student to consider facts. Not for weak minded or spirits.
such a piece of ..

MACM 101 isn't easy to begin with and Stephen Pearce made it so much harder. He was terrible at explaining things, assumed we knew much more than we did and regularly mocked students. I didn't learn anything in his class at all - absolutely horrible prof.
Spending 15 mins reading the textbook is a better choice than going to his lecture. Messy and unclear notes. BUT if you can understand what the textbook says you will do fine in the final.

he knows his stuff. intelligent prof. the course had no pre reqs but he assumed that we all knew the basics of physics and chem. homework was HARD, but the final was on the easy side. if you get offended easily, then you wont like him. but his humor is dark and that`s his style so be that. he could`ve been better but overall, not TOO bad...

It's hard to start somewhere since the man has a lot of flaws. Entertaining lecturer and guy since he can be an **** BUT knows it and admits it. But try asking him for help and he'l' rip you to shreds. If your going to take astronomy, take the phys 190, not this one. baaaaad mistake

Don't take his class, CMPT 320 if you are politically correct, easily offended, like thinking within the zeitgeist and won't challenge dogma. However, if you are a critical thinker and like to be intellectually challenged you will have a great time in his class.

The biggest **** I have ever had the misfortune to take a class from. Rude and condescending to students. He's a good lecturer, but is completely unhelpful, doesn't want people to email him, and acts like you're stupid if you don't know (or have forgotten) basic chemistry which was NOT a pre-req. AVOID, AVOID, AVOID.

man that guy is weird. he's really smart and knows his stuff. and he is a real scientist but then when it comes to teaching, he just expects the humanity to be within the same level of intelligence as him.
He is the worst prof ever!The material taught in class mostly is irrevelant to what you actually need to know. He makes you feel dumb when you do not understand his homework and ask for assistance.

He is a horrible Prof! Try to avoid taking class with him, unless you really need this class. Worse breadth-science class to take. He does not help you if you ask for help and expects you to know your physics/chemistry even though they are not required for the course. Also, he makes you feel dumb for no reason!
He's a smart guy and he defiantly knows his stuff. He has very little patience for the ignorance of most first years though... Over all he's pretty funny and very.... high energy. Good prof.

University is meant to teach you how to think for yourself instead of how to parrot the material back; Pearce takes that job very seriously. *Don't* hand homework in late. Learn to defend your point of view with logic. Abrasive, opinionated and refreshingly direct. Not for the faint of heart or the excessively politically correct.

Absolutely terrible prof. Seems to concern himself more with making a funny joke than actually helping anyone learn. Assignments are overly difficult with absolutely no help provided whatsoever, and prof gets upset when people email him for any reason. Expects people to know chemistry/physics even though they're not prerequisites. Definitely avoid.
Intelligent, entertaining, and total BAMF. Not for the weak. Looking forward to another class with Pearce, perhaps CMPT 320?
Hilarious prof and easy midterm. Don't take his classes if you get butthurt easily.

3 assignments (60%) and 1 final (40%) which you must pass to pass the course. The assignments and final were extremely easy, not many references outside of the slides are needed. Awful prof, doesn't put in any time to prepare for lectures, all his slides are plagiarised from online, end class project worth 30% of grade was not part of the class curriculum (clearly chosen by TA and related to his research not relevant to class)

Excellent professor. Knows the content of this course very well, as he's been teaching it for a while. His accent may distract a few people, but overall he's pretty clear and concise. Would take another class with him.

HIGHLY RECOMMEND! Very good prof. Only had quizzes and final exam. Explained the material in a very clear and simple way. He encourages questions and ensures students understand the material. He is best prof I had so far at SFU. His teaching is crystal. He always encourages audience raise questions and always answered them. As the result, the whole class is so much engaged. Advice: please attend lectures, do the exercise he asked.

awsome awsome prof

I took XD's class for CMPT 469/863, interaction methods in HCI, which was a paper-reading class. He was really flexible with the format and the final project was fun to do. Recommend if you want to do research in the future to take the class.
Great professor. Very approachable and caring. Lectures are great even though attendance is not mandatory.

His teachings are very high quality, the course is very good at applying knowledge to practical applications and he's always eager to clarify any confusing material. He is one of the only two good cs professor I had during my whole time in SFU. He's super great, clear crystal lectures and cares about students. I'll miss this class

This course is highly valuable, but it requires a significant time commitment to complete the assignments. Lectures were great. He frequently demos code in class which we can apply to our assignments. Biggest problem was the curving was insane with most people going down a full letter grade.

He's nice and accessible, but the final grade curved is just unbelievable. He curved down the whole class either 1 grade or 2 grades down! Many people's grades are D or F because of that. It was incredibly disrespectful that he would post a recorded lecture if his class fell on a holiday. This happened for both Rememberance Day and National Truth and Reconciliation day. I liked the lectures and how he streamed+recorded them. But the amount he scaled down was insane for the amount of time the course requires. Went down 3 grades because of it.

Honestly he is good at explaining concepts, however, my grade was scaled down from A- to a B. I spent so many hours for this course and I am so fraustrated with this grading. Although the professor's teaching quality is very good, but I don't understand why the scale down grade in the end. Anything between 60 to 70 was a D. 11 people got D 7 people F
Even though lectures were great and learnt a lot but after all this hard work B- after scoring more than 80 and B after scoring almost 85.
Course load was higher than my 400 level courses and in the end he scaled down my A- to a B. Such a waste of time.

The prof was great! I like his style of teaching. He sits and codes. Explains concepts thoroughly. Heavy load course TBH. But it is understandable. This is a third-year CMPT course. I would take other courses with him in the future for sure.

Be ready for the greatest sleep of your life during his lectures. It's like listening to a quantum mechanics documentary but you are a dance student. So boring and no engagement. I slept through most of my lectures in the first half of the semester, and didn't attend at all for the rest. Trev Tutor on youtube is your best friend. Class's curved. W
Okay but homework sometimes gets really hard.
read textbook + self study > going to lecture. save your time transitting and spending 1 hour in lecture

This prof is very unorganized, there is miscommunication between the prof and the TA's, we didn't get our first assignment until a month and a half into the semester. Shermer just reads off the slides and barely shows any coding. Avoid this prof if you actually want to learn something in this course.

Tom is a pretty understanding and good prof, as he understants the struggle of remote learning and mental health. However, the assignments for his class are very difficult (theres 6) and requires the ENTIRE week to work on if you dont have a strong understanding in C++. His midterm was terrible but he made the final fairly easier for students.

Tom is very approachable for any questions over email/piazza and I appreciate that he has a sense of humour and understands that we have lives outside of school (reasonable deadlines + difficulty, etc.) Also, changing the grading criteria to favour assignments (60%) helped with the online format. The final (30%) was of a fair difficulty. Great guy!

Not sure why he pre-recorded everything. If there's no incentive to be in class, then students will cram instead of watching when they should. I couldn't bring myself to watch them all because he talks so slow and boring. Makes exam questions out of 10-20 marks but gives either full marks or a zero. Although I got an A- I self-studied a lot.
Extremely disinterested in online courses, practically reads verbatim off the slides. Gives no information about tests

Lectures are extremely dull but are useful. Assignments are good in length and difficulty. However, he does try to cover a lot of materials. Exams contain no surprises. He really knows the subject matter, but I feel he doesn't really care too much whether students could understand his delivery. Overall not the best teacher, but far from the worst.

I have never experienced a teacher this bad in my life. How do you teach computer science without ever demonstrating a single program? No source code or example programs, only bits of pseudo code on the slides. His lectures are so dull I can barely get through the videos. His teaching leaves me more confused than I was before this class.

Covid sucks, but online lectures are worse. Dry content and reading off slides for the entire semester is brutal. Thank goodness for 3x speed(no joke) as Tom is a slow talker. Proj. Itr. were brutal for amount of time req. and minimal resources available. This class will be ur life for 4 whole months. Exams were decent, But are only 20% of grade :(
some assignments have online solutions, and people get around 38/40 average for these assignments. final is fair

Worst prof. Do you really want to pay hella expansive tuition fee just to hear a dude reads off his terrible slides word by word with absolutely no extra explanation nor context then gives unreasonable hard assignments????? SFU don't increase the tuition fee when you are just gonna hire someone to just read off the slides.

He exhibits zero passion when teaching. If you ACTUALLY have a passion for learning DON'T take his class -.- his super boring lectures provide zero value to your understanding and you have to watch them at 2x speed cause he talks so s l o w l y ! ! ! Skip taking class with him, he is a waste of your parents money lol.
He will teach you what you need to know the hard way. But you will learn A LOT

I think Tom is very passionate about computer science as a subject, CMPT 225 just doesn't quite scratch the itch for him in terms of its content richness so he didn't seem very lively at lectures. He's happy to explain topics to you that you don't understand, and an overall good professor. I'm sure he's great for higher level courses.
Decent Prof. Lectures are somewhat boring. Assignments and tests are fair. Nothing crazy.
He was okay. Most of the coursework was manageable.

Tom is a strong believer that the best way to learn how something works is by implementing it. In this course, we started with a setPixel() method, and ended it with an implementation of a 3D model renderer (subset of .obj files) and a graphics specification language for composing scenes. It was a lot of work, but was well worth it.

3 assignments, 2 week deadlines that required working 20 hours per day over both weekends. Somewhat disorganized lectures. Textbook makes no sense for the course. But, would 100% recommend. You'll learn a ton, and lectures are really entertaining. If you want to jump into coding a game, take with someone else though - very low-level concepts.

Tom is probably one of the most accessible profs outside of class, you can stop him in the hall if you have a question and he'll help you. You need to attend lectures in order to get notes and information about assignments. Biggest downside is there is too much work (around 40-50 hours a week). Wish he would go more in depth with the theory though.

Take this class with him if you want your life to ONLY revolve around this 1 course. If you want to take more than 1 course, or work a job, or have any life outside this course, DO NOT even think about it. Only for people who live and breathe code.
Except to be spending 40 hours a week on homework. Ridiculously hard and is sometimes unclear on the board. Do not take the course with him.

CMPT379 Compiler is not easy. Prof. Shermer is good at explaining theoretical concepts in lecture. But before exams, practice how to do NFADFA, productions, and optimizations by hand. Later assignments build on your own code of previous, so bad practice hurts yourself. If possible, try not to take 379 with other heavy programming or group course.

Intense, but highly rewarding assignments. Very enjoyable and I learned a lot. I highly recommend taking this class with Dr. Shermer. If you're not a strong coder, however, it might be best to take less than a full course load with this class.

Most rewarding and challenging course I've ever taken at SFU. Hours of intensive programming assignments, but you learn very valuable skills. Tom is an excellent prof and is always willing to help or go out of his way to make office hours for you. Do not take if you can't dedicate 30+ hours a week to assignments.
no comments
Tom Shermer is also known as Professor "Mind Fuck!" he sucks at teaching and always talks about purple pants and if its raining outside.
Don't take this class with him, unless you are interested in the subject. Great prof otherwise. Assignments are not easy, but FUN!
half of the class got 0 on the final (3rd) assignment

I have learned less in this course than any other because of this professor. He enjoys leaving exams till the last minute (I think he writes them an hour or two before time). His exams and assignments are riddled with error.
Assignments are difficult but you learn a lot from them. Helps if you already know C++ fairly well. Excellent lecturer... you won't want to miss them.

If you want to take a class where you don't really have to do much work, just study for the exams, take his. Assignments are really easy and take almost no time to do. Lectures follow the book, so skip if you want to.
Great porf....You learn a lot
No Comments
dun take him unless u love doing hardcore research on ur own
No Comments
He is just the best prof in SFU CS.

His lectures were straightforward and his lecture notes were clear. However, understanding the examples given in the lectures was far not enought for doing the assignments. I used lots of online supplementary materials to understand this course and do the assignments. This was time consuming but self-study is one of the most important skills for us

Professor Jack Thomas is one of the best CMPT prof's I've had at SFU. His lectures covered a significant amount of information without it feeling overwhelming and his midterm and final were all fair without having to invest significant time into the material. His assignments are a mix between being easy if you know the material and a lot of work.
One of the best compsci profs I've encountered. Really friendly, explains topics clearly, and exams were fair. Do the work and you'll do fine.
Jack is really and actually cares about the course and helping students

So much of the material in 276 could be quite the boring grind, but if you take this class with Jack Thomas you're guaranteed a fun time. Not only are the lectures entertaining, but he will actually give you some solid advice from his own experiences. All around great guy, and fantastic prof.

Hands down the best professor I've ever got at SFU. He is an amazing teacher and is caring and diligent with his students. He will tell you the truth about the Tech-Industry and what goes in the back. He is a real gem.

Jack is really good at helping students understand the importance of the material without being boring. He also offers 'real talk' about what it's actually like to work in the field that you might not get out of other instructors. The assignments were gentle enough to prepare you for the group project in Android Studio. Video tutorials are provided

Jack is an amazing Prof. he gives people the opportunity to score more in Assignments which are already pretty easy. His Midterm is also pretty easy most of the questions are application-based, so just go through the slides thoroughly before the exam. Highly Recommended!!!!

He is an encouraging and understanding instructor. He seems to genuinely care for students and makes the lectures fun with various efforts. He tries to offer anything he could for students to learn in a better environment, which is uncommon. He is a good person or at least a great instructor. Definitely take a course with him!

Jack is a really great prof. Unlike many other profs at the school he's a recent graduate so he is much more relatable from a student perspective. 276 has very boring lecture material but Jack kept it pretty interesting. Really enjoyed the class.
10/10 prof, he really wants everyone toe succeed and do well. The material has some dry bits but Jack makes the class fun and engaging!

Probably my favourite cmpt prof so far! Super nice and funny, cares about his students a lot. Assignments and project were very fair; not super difficult. Midterm was long, but he compensated and bumped up marks. Final was very fair. Show up to lectures, or at least watch the recordings and you'll be good to go!

Lectures were fun with a few memes in some slides. Assignments were simple to do, but the group projects were a lot more time consuming and difficult. Lectures were focused on theory while assignments is through videos or own research. Midterm was lengthy but final was good. Discord office hours were nice too!

For his first time teaching, Dr. Thomas did a good job. His lectures tended to be a little on the tangential side. I really appreciated the different perspectives of software engineering he offered. The project was a lot of fun and a good introduction to software engineering.

I quite like his style for a not-so-technical course like 276. He chats and jokes a lot but still explain the concepts quite clear. My only complaint is he doesn't talk much about Java or Android but the assignment and project are all about them, but I guess that's not completely his problem

There is a fundamental mismatch in level of abstraction of lectures and the midterm. Algorithm derivations in textbook only, with no proof digestion in lectures, only functional descriptions. Then midterm expects one to derive them from first principles. Not sure why I pay tuition if rigorous learning is delegated to a 3rd party textbook. Avoid.

I took his 361 a while ago, and I think he doesn't deserve all these rage ratings. He is passionate about the topics, and I think he actually enjoys discussing the topics if you pay attention. Exams were indeed somewhat challenging but assignments were really nice tho. Watching videos before coming to class helps you prepare well, just watch it.
Using a bonus question in mid to grade students' assignment you serious??? Who gonna waste their exam time answering a question not even marked?
Yagiz spends a good 30 minutes of each lecture saying um
Do not take this course not worth it

CMPT361 is the worst CMPT class I've ever taken. The professor has no incentive to teach the course content. Lectures are just scribbles on a canvas, and the YouTube videos have almost no correlation with the midterm. The only thing helpful so far is he said, don't worry, the class is scaled. SFU needs bette profs.

No lecture slides, you'd need to watch his YouTube lectures and live lectures and take notes form them. Exams feels insanely hard not only because the exam itself is hard but also because of the he teaches without lecture slides. 1 is too high for him, I wish I could give him -2

Odd experience. His policy: Miss 2+ classes? You get an NA. No laptops in class. If using Zoom, camera on, or he'll call you out until you turn it on, or he kicks you. He answers questions during class in a way that makes you feel like you should know better. However, talking to him 1-on-1 he is keen to help and seems to genuinely care about you.

By far the laziest prof I've ever experienced. Did not provide practice exams or any help in that matter. Simply told us to watch all the lectures in 2x speed. Too lazy to use Canvas and instead uses the github that he made half a decade ago. Avoid at all costs. I wish I could give him a 0. A rating of 1 is too much for him.

Lectures don't reflect the material on the midterm, which had a mean of 40% and a very flat grade distribution. Also, the midterm was so bad that someone had a panic attack and ran out of the room crying, he didn't specify what was going to be on it and refused to give practice problems. Only take if you want to lower your GPA.

I feel like he has improved a lot as i feel like the past ratings didn't really match my experience. During lecture he gave us a lot of space to ask questions and encouraged questions and answered silly questions. he gave a practice midterm and he emphaizes what you NEED to know for exam in lecture. He was also funny sometimes!made me hehehe a few

As usual for this school he is a research professor that does not actually want to teach courses. Zero care was put into explaining any of the concepts if you dont go look for yourself in the book. Zero clue what would be on the exam because he refused to say. Just remove this course or have jason teach it by himself, he is not a good teacher.
The reviews are right

WORST PROF EVER! I mean the rating speaks for itself. I don't even know how he got his job at SFU, and it's funny with the amount of dissatisfaction from the student, SFU's still keeping him. It makes me think of my university like a joke!

this guy making associate prof will mark the death of post-secondary education what a joke of an instructor this guy deserves to stay assistant prof for 20 years one of the worst hires SFU has ever done, esp for graphics/vision faculty

One of the most IRRESPONSIBLE instructors I've ever had. Slides and homework are taken from others. Doesn't prepare for lectures at all. At times he just stares at the slides in silence like he has no idea what is on it. Still, he requires attendance. 1/3 of the term was taught by his grad students. Often late to lectures or end early.

Very bad prof who teaches the computer vision portion (roughly the first half of the semester) of the course. Midterm had a failing average and he gave us NO practice for it and didn't say any specifics of what would be on it. Overall, not a good experience taking this course so far. Do not recommend.
He is not as bad as everyone says but not good either. His teaching is so different from the exams.
my condolences if you take this course
Never seen a worse instructor. Shows zero effort to make things clear for students. Not kind either. You should avoid him at all cause. SFU needs more qualified professors.

Most accurate 1/5 I've seen. Seemed decent at first but doesn't teach anything relevant to his exam and expects you to read research papers and the two textbooks (very dense content). No sample questions, no quiz and no examples in class so you're basically going to the exam blind.

I feel like he cares about the topic a lot which is good. I don't think he deserves a one star. I wish that there were quizzes or some sort of practice tests so we had a better idea of the type of questions he would ask though. There was not a lot of connection between the textbook and test questions. He is new to teaching and will improve.

Really hard to follow what he's talking about. He makes a simple concept very complicated. The video he provided is too short to understand. No offense I think he is a nice person but as a student who pays to study here, I think we deserve better lectures. Avoid if you can...

Concepts feel incomplete and not taught well. Yagiz doesn't provide any practice materials. It took him more than a month to release our marks. Splitting this course between vision and graphics was a really bad idea. Both topics are highly dense topics and squeezing them into one semester is not practical. Avoid if you can.
Please do not take his course. You've been warned!
You can tell he has passion for this field but he is horrible at explaining
He is a nice person but horrible at explaining concepts. Nobody in the class has any idea what's going on. I'd never take him again

The worst professor ever!! He only gave you overviews of some high level concepts. The textbook has lots of complicated formulas and he never explain how to use or test in exams. This is just an INTRODUCTION to computer vision course, not PhD course, so we are not supposed to read all the complicated papers and understand them ourselves.

It has been 3 months since the semester has started, no portion of the course has been graded thus far. Course material highly detached from examination as the material in class is very high level overview whereas the exams are heavy in math. It's been 3 months since the beginning of the semester and I have no idea how good/bad I'm doing.

He's not that bad. Honestly. First things first, this course is mostly self taught. Youtube videos are good quality wise. Content wise it is very good at covering high leveled topics but a bit lackluster on the low level topics. Most of the comments here talks about his ego, but I don't see any of it so far in his course. Midterm difficulty was ok.

Professor teaches high level basic overviews of topics. Instead of explaining them he expects you to self learn all of them. Purposefully leaves out information and then tests students on this. Does not provide any sample questions whatsoever. CAP5415 lecture playlist on YouTube will save you.

Will be an unpopular opinion here but I like the prof as a person, very passionate about the material,responsible in class and willing to answer questions. His YouTube videos explain concepts clearly and vividly and will likely pass on to future generations. However, if you want to take an easy course for credit this course is probably not for you.
the worst professor i have met ever, grade my project without reading the codes and implementing it. weird accent

He gives a good high-level overview of the topics in the lectures, and you can tell he is passionate about the topics Assignments standard difficulty Midterm and final are awful, the averages were 40%. Questions are impossible to answer with just lecture content. Don't recommend this prof unless okay with self teaching everything or bad marks.
Very bad. VERYBAD

Being in his class was high-key the worst experience that I've ever had in a class at SFU. I've never seen a professor with such an attitude and sassiness towards his students. He poorly explains his material and expects a lot from his students. No wonder why class morale is so low after his vision lectures. Avoid him if you can.

Terrible. Just don't take him. Look at the other reviews, they're 100% accurate. This guy needs to seriously reevaluate his style of teaching. Or just stop teaching altogether and focus on your research. I'd like to erase this course from my memory now. Can't believe I paid money for this.

Very rude and angry professor. Puts absolutely no effort into teaching. Yagiz Aksoy's "lectures" are only around 10-15 minutes, which does not teach me anything at all. And I have to pay full tuition for this? If you want to pursue graphics, take an online course instead. Yagiz will ruin graphics & computer vision for you.
Assignments are vague, exam was unfair as we didn't have extra time for uploading and submission. Class avg for the midterm was 39%.. He doesn't seem to care about his students. Avoid if you can!
Don't you just love a professor who doesn't explain things in detail and let you read a book that is about as confusing as his lectures are.

He provides short pre-recorded lectures on YouTube that are kinda hard to follow due to his accent. He is not very helpful on piazza either. He has strict midterm guidelines that don't make sense with the time constraint. We have to take selfies with our answers on papers and then upload each image on Coursys with no extra upload time.
Really short lectures. Really hard to understand course material. Lectures skipped important details. Had to watch free lectures from udacity. Avoid him!

Disappointed by his rude behavior towards students. I do not know why he is aggressive so much? I respect the fact that you are a professor, but that does not mean you should treat everybody else as inferior to you.

The worst Instructor I have ever met, everything copied from CS188, but did not provide any help. he seldom answer question in piazza. he is lazy but extremely strict. This course itself puts a lot of pressure on us. he didn't provide any help, just give us a Berkeley link. the only thing he did is to keep adding difficulties and pressure to us

After the course was converted into online class, he disappeared. CS188 lasts for six months but we have three months and no effective help. He does't spend any time thinking about how to teach better, but making lots of complicated and strict examination rules, and he would fail people without hesitation, even under this situation. Avoid him!!!!!

Yes, like the other reviews said there wasn't enough time for the exam and he wasn't the most accommodating. By the end though, he removed a lot of restrictions and listened to student feedback on Piazza. He was also fairly active on there with answering questions. The move to online courses due to COVID-19 was not handled well by him though.

During my time at SFU this prof was the worst prof. The only thing he's good at is talking about AD Basically he doesn't do anything except copying and using UCB lectures No quizzes, no sample exams(Too lazy to write one), The only good thing about his classes is his voice(very quite and sleepy) which makes his class perfect for sleeping and gmng

The course was instructed in an unreasonably lazy manor. You are literally paying money to teach yourself everything. No problem sets, exams or quizzes were given in class, and we were directly pushed into taking a ridiculously hard final which was worth a huge chunk of the class! Steer clear!!

Do not fall for the good ratings for this prof. He basically only showed up and asked us if we had questioned, expected us to learn from a FREE set of lecture slides from Berkeley, didnt even put in effort to teach us during these hard times. The final was insanely short, with four difficult questions where you had to just read for half of the time

Too hard as an introductory course for AI. The slides were taken from UC Berkeley and he put bare minimum effort when teaching. Better off going over the material from Berkeley's website than going to class. The course was designed for a 4 credit course, but was taught as a 3 credit course. Overall horrible experience wouldnt recommend it to anyone

His lecture slides were directly copied from the UC Berkley's intro to AI class, which wouldn't be a problem except that the prereqs were different and we weren't given the full content. All the exam practise problems were from UCB, and were virtually impossible to solve and the final exam was the main deciding factor for grades.

He doesn't care about his class. Lectures and projects are taken from UC Berkeley's CS188. While other professors tried to find a way to give online lectures during COVID-19, he sent us the playlist of Berkeley's CS188 video lectures. He doesn't respect his students. Taking this course is like paying for a free online course and the credits.

He puts zero effort in his classes and doesn't care about the students at all. All the material is copied from cs188 Berkeley and even though the video lectures from cs188 Berkeley class are from 2014, they are way better than his lectures. Do not take his course with him, I repeat DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM.
His lecture slides, assignments, lectures, videos, demos and even autograders were taken from Berkeley's online free lecture. He puts minimal effort to his class

Lectures were fast, and easy to follow, sometimes difficult to understand him because of his accent, but it is a pretty easy course. Assignments are not difficult, and midterms and final all had marks taken off the total to give everyone a higher percentage! Prof is helpful to the questions you ask him, and will tell you exactly what's on the exams

Jano (Ya-Know) cannot teach for his life. He had to scale both mid-terms significantly and trimmed the total of the final exam from 100 to 75. His inability to translate his knowledge infuriates you as you pay nearly $500 to listen to a person who just arrived from Europe. Lastly, his tests are non calculator and are EXTREMELY hard.

Lets put it this way: when you have to scale both midterms because the class average was a definite fail- we know there is an issue. He tries his best- but he should really stick to what he knows. The material is easy; but he cant teach it.
He seriously needs to work on his penmanship and his english. I find it incomprehensible and I have to rely on my textbook most of the time. Also, he goes through the material in lectures too fast.
308 is as easy as upper maths get. Course pace is slow due to proofs (of which a good deal aren't really necessary). I skipped most lectures and relied mainly on textbook.

I don't know why he is teaching math even though he is in cs. I can't understand his lectures, I can't even understand his English or his writing. He seems to know the material but can't teach. Assignments are hard as well.

nice guy, however, speaks not clear enough and give lectures without interacting with students. However, he seems really care. If u ask him a question, he will explain to u in detail. I say, attitude counts too.

His lectures aren't that great, but he is fairly helpful in terms of questions/deadlines/marking schemes. Also, the material he presents in his powerpoint slides covers the course well enough to get a good grade on both assignments and the exams.
This guy needs to learn to speak english.

nice guy, horrible english, understood the notes but not him. super difficult assignments that did not concentrate on learning the synatx and structure of c++. mostly learnt all the theory of c++, which is what was on the test, (this saves you)

His lecture notes are unclear and hard to understand. He speaks unclearly and too softly during lectures (I can't hear much of what he says). He understands the material, but have a hard time putting it into words.

Sucks! Can't speak or write~I can't believe the calibre of profs that sfu can only hire with the already increased tuition. This guy can't communicate his work with students. I saw more than 1/2 the class not hand in their hwk after midterm 1!
No Comments
He's a nice guy but he can't speak or write clearly, seriously

Brian I know you see this. What goes around comes around. Clock's ticking. When it hits your door, take it on the chin like you expect everyone else to. Now since that's done with his exams are hard aswell.
Dr. Brian Fraser was the best professor I've ever had. Thank you for my B.A. in English Literature.

Brian Fraser is AMAZING! His lectures are great and he spends some time going over actual code and giving coding exercises in-lecture, and they're even recorded, so you can watch them whenever! That said, the long assignments in the latter half of the course are VERY long, so do account for that when deciding your course load!

Great lecturer for a very intensive (particularly workload-wise) course. I learnt a lot, and while the workload (sections are standardized) was extreme at times (especially with the new quizzes/labs that I felt were disjointed at times i.e. what was expected to know), Brian's a very caring, experienced, and inspirational lecturer. Take it with him.

A LOT of assignment work but it's expected for a 4 credit course, midterm and final were very reasonable, quizzes weren't too bad if you did the assignments. What I really like about this prof is that he's very accessible outside of class, along with the TA's, and has lots of online office hours, which is very beneficial when u can't go in person

Brian is a great prof, with his lectures being informative and interesting. His assignments are fun to do, though be aware of the long assignments. These take up to 30 hours to do. Even if you get 100% on one, you need a high grade on the quiz that unlocks your assignment's grade. The quizzes were obscure and shouldn't be underestimated.
Goat best prof at sfu

Makes difficult class seem not so bad. Offers lots of support through good, engaging lectures, and actively answering questions online and in person. The course does have a lot of assignments, be prepared to spend a lot of time on them, note each assignment has an accompanying quiz. Highly recommend to go to his lectures. Overall amazing prof.
NEVER TAKING HIM AGAIN

Best prof at SFU by every possible measure. Lectures are genuinely fun and useful. Assignments (while difficult) are incredibly rewarding to complete. Neovim sucks in the beginning, but it's not actually that hard (there's ~10 keybinds you genuinely need to memorize) and you get used to it quickly. Quizzes are easy if you just do the assignments.

The expectations in the course are wild! I mean you're somehow supposed to do the excessively long assignments, perform well/study for the difficult quizzes, remain caught up the heavy materials and lectures and also do the weekly labs. I mean this is easily the equivalent of 2 courses. Pair with easier classes or you'll probably suffer like i did

This guy literally needs to chill with how strict he is with people cheating like i get it your not supposed to, but he will literally write you up for nothing. Some guy he wrote up was saying hes just waiting until he graduates and I wont even say what he wants to do.
He's a good professor but makes very difficult quizzes for each assignment that can lead you together trying a much worse grade even after putting in all the time and effort.

Brian's one of the most respected and greatest profs in SFU's cs faculty. However, this sem he introduced the quizzes which cap your assignment grade. In order to get a full on assignment you need 5 or 6/6 on the quiz for that assignment. I don't think this does justice to the amount of time spent on assignments as quizzes are sometimes confusing

This was a tough course, but he included very helpful analogies and concise explanations. The exams were based on the assignments and lecture slides. He records lectures, but he brings a lot of enthusiasm and activities to his lectures so its worth going. The rumours about the long assignments are true so make sure to start those early.
One word: GOAT

Easily the best prof I've had at SFU so far. The course is dense, time-consuming and the process of learning neovim made me want to walk into the ocean and never come back. But in the end, it was worth it. The assignments are long but rewarding and now I have to stick to IDEs with neovim support otherwise it feels too slow. 10/10 would recommend.
Best CMPT prof, explains all concepts exceptionally well. All lectures are recorded so you are not required to attend. Always accessible for extra help. Provides detailed study guides for exams.

Lectures are engaging. Makes you want to go to class because you learn a lot and it isn't boring. Throws in humour, great analogies, and keeps a bright smile up. Don't think you can get a better computer science prof here at sfu.

4 for difficulty due to the crazy workload, so pair with easy classes. Pick your project group members wisely. Quizzes were trivial if you understood the assignments and lecture slides. Lectures are posted online, but they may sometimes have missing audio or examples that are relevant to assignments. Go to class, Brian's an awesome lecturer!
Goated prof, quite possibly can't get any better than this for cmpt. long assignments require alot more time so be vigilant about that. Overall one of my best experiences in cmpt courses
straightforward, do the work, get the results.

Brain is a good person and humourus but he isn't the best prof he is said to be. He is just a caring person in general, but like if you just looking at teaching concepts. There are many better people out there. Everyone just calls him best cause he is often understanding and cares for his students. In the teaching part, not really. So just be aware
Simply the GOAT. Generally has a grace period for late assignment but do note that if you miss this deadline you get a zero. Though he gives you more than enough time to work on it so it's on you.
This guy is like the Lebron of teaching computer science. He has a cool McDonalds fit that lets you know the lecture is going to be amazing.

Brian Fraser is hands down one of the most amazing professors at SFU. If you're taking CMPT 120, you're in for a treat. He genuinely cares about his students and makes every lecture engaging, clear, and approachable. His outfits are always amazing, especially the McDonalds fit which is always the best lectures.
Brian Fraser is an amazing professor. Easy and fair midterm + final, great lectures, genuinely interesting to listen to and allows for cheatsheets during exams. Definitely recommend taking him
my first programming prof, didn't realize how spoiled we were

Dr. Brian is easily one of the best profs at SFU, I took this class with him with absolutely no prior coding knowledge and understood his explanations really well. He/his TAs are accessible 7 days a week and provide amazing explanations and advice. Ignore those who gave him bad ratings I can almost guarantee they simply didn't do any work.

Very rewarding (learned a lot) but tough course due to quantity of topics. Would recommend even if not required because it mainly teaches to write good code which seems very important when it comes to actually looking for work. Felt that some of the assignments were not the most interesting, but well designed to learn what was taught in lectures.

Professor Fraser is an amazing teacher who makes the material engaging. The assignments and exams can be challenging, but he provides plenty of opportunities to boost your grade. For study prep, I found tools like unstuck ai and the free textbook really helpful.

Brian is the worst prof ive taken. He disguises the fact that hes not making sense behind his enthusiastic lecturing voice(this is why everyone rates him highly). Plus, often times he lectures on topics we needed to know to do the assignments, after we already handed it in. I would never say his class is for people with no prior coding knowledge
Brian is very difficult. His explainations were very confusing, and his tests were very difficult
absolutely amazing, what a great guy. he will go onto great places!

I found this course much more difficult than the dreaded CMPT307 which I took concurrently and got a B+ in with less effort. The homeworks are long and the tests are marked very harshly. Brian is a good lecturer, but wow he makes his classes difficult. Java is also somewhat antiquated imo, I would say don't take this course unless you have to.

Brian Fraser is a great professor, his lectures are the best. Sometimes he expects a lot from students, but it all comes from a good place. Assignments are long and tricky at times, while the midterm was very difficult for the 45 mins given. Final was more forgiving and rewarded those who went to class. This class taught me so much, it was tough.
simply the GOAT

You can tell from day one that Brian Fraser really wants to help everyone learn, understand, and master the material. He is excellent at explaining, teaching, and answering questions. The course homepage is super easy to navigate.

society if every instructor put as much effort into their teaching as brian. the course being an introductory course, is not easy. but you have almost endless resources and support. theres office hours everyday on discord and after every class during the week. the midterm was pretty hard compared to sample midterm and assignments but not impossible
he is a great professor, but you need to be extremely familiar with python. Although an intro course , its pretty hard.
Doesn't objectively really get better than this in terms of professors
Gold standard for computer science professors. Recorded lectures, well designed assignments, fair exams and overall solid guy.

Lectures are very organized, pauses in between slides to check up on class understanding to slow down or re-explains if needed, doesn't shame you for asking questions, and doesn't tolerate hate speech anywhere. GOAT. Need I say more?
Good lectures and enjoyable content
love this guy!!!!
Simon Fraser University

Arguably the best lecturer at SFU. Very passionate and attentive. Lectures = great. Assignments = well designed but a lot of work so never procrastinate. Guides / notes really provide a wealth of information. Quizzes are kind of tricky. You get to demo your group projects - a lot of strong candidates this term!

DR BRIAN THE GOAT. he is SO passionate about what he teaches. amazing lecturer, he made learning C++ so doable. midterm and final were tough, but in the end it was worth it. i learned so much from him. i wish he taught my entire degree :')

Probably the best professor in the entire CS department with lectures that makes you want to attend. (despite this he still makes them available via recordings) Course is a lot of work but also the work assigned are highly connected to course material and reasonable.
All around amazing teacher. Learned alot from just paying attention during lectures. Projects, assignments, quizzes and exams weren't difficult, but did test your knowledge.

I came into this course knowing nothing about coding. Something I would criticize is the flipped lectures. I think I preferred learning stuff while at lecture rather than watching a video. Other than that, he is an amazing lecture and is open to all questions with such care! He is very knowledgeable and you can tell he is very passionate!
Took concurrent studies with him! Engaging lectures! Great explanations

Great professor. Has a clear joy for the subject and for teaching. Records all his lectures. Interesting assignments and generous late policy. Very accessible on Discord and in person. Cares for students and tries to help them succeed.
Super nice and always finds ways to help his students, final was hard but everything else was good
Amazing professor and many opportunities to boost your grade (quizzes, assignments, reflections), however the final was very hard so make sure you study a lot for it!

He's one of the best professors I've come across, and in the 4 months that I've known him he's become someone I look up to and aspire to be. He cares about his students, really loves teaching and is just a really cheerful person in general. Exams are not easy, but not extremely hard either, you just gotta put in effort and understand concepts.
He's a really nice guy. Lectures feel very fast for people who aren't very good at coding.
I love Brian Fraser, only if I could rate him higher :(

Approachable, positive attitude. TA's and prof are accessible outside class on discord if you need help. Lecture recordings online so don't need to come to class (but do anyway because he is a great teacher). Projects every week with up to 4 days late (no late penalty which is flexible). Easy quizzes. midterm is slightly harder. Would recommend.
The GOAT
He was awesome!

Teaches everything you need to know to complete assignments, gives lots of extra examples. Lectures cover everything on the assignments and tests. Prof really tries to make sure you succeed. Best prof I've had at SFU so far.

Brian is very accessible (on Discord and in person) and cares about his students. He has a very generous late assignment policy and granted various extension options for our last assignment. He uploads video tutorials and gives away hints on assignments. Brian has a no-such-thing-as-silly-questions attitude which makes learning fun and enjoyable!

The prof was good (good lectures and lots of resources) but grading is very unfair. Basically the grading is a coin flip based on which TA you get. I have gotten 100% on all assignments graded by one TA and terrible grades on assignments graded by the other TA. To give some context, the first TA I mentioned ctrl c's out of Valgrind.
Best professor I've had so far at SFU, I'm tempted to take classes I don't really care about just to get another class with him.

Midterms and assignments were very difficult and lengthy. Midterms are very hard and even after studying everything it would be difficult to score 50%. For some reason my earlier posts were deleted (5/5 score is so misleading).

Dr. Brian is one of the best professors at SFU. He's very caring and makes the time to talk to students and understand their concerns. His lectures are fun and easy to follow. Has great demos. His instructions on assignments are very clear and he is very approachable for help. If you have the opportunity to take a class with him, do not hesitate.

Professor Fraser is a living legend. He's extremely cheerful during lectures (Which is MUCH more than can be said for the other professors I've had) and fair in his grading and expectations for you. He has that ability to make you want to learn programming and leaves you enough flexibility in assignments to devise your own solutions. 10/10.
This class requires a lot of work but was worth it. Amazing lectures that just get you motivated to code in C. 100% recommend.
Not going to take a class with him ever again. Way to much work for the homework to be worth 1%. Imagine that, an entire assignment is worth 1%. Man needs to fix his entire course.
Learn lots from this class. Although assignments may be tough, you learn a lot. Peeps below me do be salty. LMFAO

Wont take a class with him again , especially because of assignment 2. Dont take CMPT 213 with this prof. Reused work and material just like every other class he teaches and doesnt consider the timing it will take to complete such assignment. Not only does his notes make you mad, his way of teaching is annoying.

Ya if the deadline to drop this class was still there I would have dropped it. Brians assignments make no sense. His wording on it is so bad that you can barely understand it. Wasnt able to finish the recent assignment due to how unacceptable is notes are compared to them. Not connected whatsoever.
Assignment 2 is absolutely ridiculously hard. Majority of the class couldnt finish and we had less time to do it than usual. Not mentioning it was only worth 5.5% for such a hard assignment.

Brian's teaching is filled with passion and therefore highly motivating. His design of coursework is aimed at industry standards and helpful for students to kickstart an early career in computing science. Also, he takes care to suit the needs of all levels of students, and he sacrifices his private time outside of class to respond to questions

I took this class a while ago and his lectures were amazing. Well-formated and clear lecture slides (you do need to fill out some blanks). His assignments are fair in the sense that it does challenge you and yet able to finish without too much stress. His exams can be tricky.

There's a reason why he has a high rating. His recorded lectures were fantastic and great to listen to and follow along. Very enthusiastic about topics, and I genuinely enjoyed the course. Takes feedback seriously to better improve his course. Has systems in place to ensure everyone is marked fairly for projects, thus you'll get what you put in.

Dr. Brian Fraser is the best prof at SFU! My favorite thing about him is that he makes students who speak up in class who don't give the right answer still feel valued and conveys that what they have to say is considered and important. I feel this makes a big difference to students. Every prof at SFU should strive to be as enthusiastic as Dr. Brian

Brian Fraser is a God. Lively lectures, clear notes, fair quizzes, and you will learn a lot in the class. Will start you off with Java then ramp into android. By the end you will be fairly familiar with Android Programming. Project / assignments were a lot of a work, but fair. Would take a class with Brian again!

Learned a lot about git and how to make projects in a team. Assignments force you to learn Android and Java to do well in them, which is good. Only watched the first 2 weeks of lectures as content seemed a bit dry. Reviewed lecture slides 1-2 hours before quizzes and did fairly good in them. Didn't need to watch lectures as quizzes are open book.

Brian Fraser is quite literally one of the best profs at SFU. Also had him for CMPT 130 and he does a great job making the lectures engaging and the content absorbable. Projects were quite enjoyable and was very accessible for help and clarification on requirements whenever it was needed. You can just see the passion this man has when teaching.

Project-based course, and all 4 of them were pretty fun to implement. The group project difficulty depended on how good your teammates were, but Brian had measures in place to keep everything fair. Quizzes are doable if you just study the notes beforehand and attend lectures. TAs could have been better, one did not know how to use GitLab.

Class was sort of outdated. The android studio tutorials were from 2013 and many features have changed since than so it was confusing to implement such features. His quizzes were sort of difficult as if you dont go over the notes, you cant really do well. His assignment3 was more fun than the group project so Im not sure what happened there.

The group project was said to be resume worthy and something you would look forward to do. However; he has reused previous projects you can find online on github. Ruins the uniqueness of the class, His video tutorials on android are very very old and are unhelpful. His quizzes are confusing and hard. This class was for sure a let down, just boring.
He kept making a reference to "A Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy," which was very funny. Anyways, I have nothing to say that strays from the usual; Fraser is a king.

The superlatives you read below are true: Dr Fraser is a top tier instructor, and perhaps more importantly, an amazing person. Funny and engaging lectures. Listens to feedback, taking surveys and reviewing results in class. Empathetic towards students especially during the pandemic. High workload but you receive valuable and practical education.
Brian is one of the best professor in CMPT department. He is hilarious, caring and always accessible for his students. I have been with him 4 courses: 213, 276, 200 and 433 and he never let me down

I don't know what SFU did to deserve a professor as great as Dr. Brian. He's empathetic and treats every question as valid. I wish more instructors/professors worked as hard as he did to make students feel good about learning something new and being comfortable in uncomfortable knowledge spaces.

Dr. Fraser is a great prof. Lectures make sense, labs are useful, and assessments are fair. Without prior coding experience, the course has a steep learning curve, but that goes for any intro-CMPT course. I wish we had the chance to work on bigger projects instead of bi-weekly assignments, but overall Dr. Fraser is a very solid pick for CMPT130.

Dr. Fraser is an absolute gem of a professor. I did this course online, and his recorded lectures were very well put together, going over a lot of content in a straightforward manner that makes it easy to follow along and learn. He was always very welcoming and took the time to actually be invested in his student's learning.

In CMPT130, the lectures are so concise and clear that you understand everything the first time you watch. Tests and quizzes are fair. Assignments are well made, creative and enjoyable. Despite being online, this course was very organized and accommodating for the students. I cannot speak highly enough of Dr. Brian Fraser. He is a real gem at SFU!

350 characters is not enough to list all the positive things to say about this professor. He loves his job and it shows, and he knows how to effectively convey information. Only bad thing about having a prof like this early on, is that you miss this type of quality in later courses with other professors. He is a true gem.

Dr. Fraser is a great prof for online courses. Many instructors have a discrepancy between how well they know something and how well they can teach, but Fraser is great at both. He really knows how to make students understand a concept, and always takes questions and requests to explain clearer. You have to put work in, but he makes it easier.

This man is a god. He is so considerate and caring unlike most of his colleagues at SFU, and his YouTube videos are literally the best out there. Taking a class with him was an honour. you'll learn from his 276 class probably more than all the other level 2 cmpt courses combined.

One of Dr. Fraser's many great qualities is he seems to understand how students want to learn. He is really good at making as many resources available as he can so that one can do one's best as a student. From answering questions on Piazza to posting test cases, he really gave us everything we needed to be successful.
He is too bad.
Cool, nice, supportive, caring professor. It doesn't get better than Professor Fraser. He is amazing and a great educator.

Brian Fraser is a real gem in the faculty. He doesnt feed off students suffering like the other professors. He is truly there to teach you and doesnt mind giving students code to help. He will go above and beyond to help his students
Honestly, the best prof you can ask for.

Overall, Brian is not the worst prof for this class. But his project's grading was weird and ambiguous, so try to do some stupid things, like code reviews, to boost your grade. Also, you should be ready to write long responses to an outdated book about software development.
Awesome prof

Brian is generally considered the best CS professor at SFU. He is nice, funny and gives good lectures. I recommend going to his office hours when you have trouble because he is very helpful and informative. He gives a lot of homework but difficulty is moderate. Exams and quizzes are generally tough so do not sleep on them.
The best prof at SFU. He is very clear, caring and knowledgable. Love him. Definitely recommend him.
Truly the greatest intellectual of the 21st century. Engaging lectures, helpful labs/assignments, and loves the number 42.
One of the best choices you could make for a professor and course combination.

Brian is a really good professor, exams are reasonable, assignments are hard but possible to do. However, sometimes his assignments expect a lot out of you!!! He says he could do the assignment in a few hours, well yes because he has so much experience, but for students 1-2 weeks is not even enough, so expectations can get quite high.
Really good professor

Amazing professor. Well guided and prepared lectures and you learn a lot. Only thing to watch out for is that this class (CMPT433) is one of the hardest courses I have ever taken in terms of workload. You will learn a lot but I also had no life outside of school lol
Lots of fill-in-the-blanks. Can ask anonymous questions on Piazza. Definitely enthusiastic. Class average is high. Grade will be scaled down.
Easy assignment/project and tough exam. You need to remember a lot of stuff to pass the exam. The curve is applied to this course. So pay more effort on the exam and you will be succeed.

Assignments/Projects are far too easy - class avg is 90%+. You are forced to watch his Youtube videos to complete them as well. He does not post complete notes online, so if you have life/work commitments it will be a large setback. Many of his exam questions require attendance in class because they will be lecture specific. Avoid if possible.

If all my courses were with Brian, I'd be the happiest person in the world. He really has a way of delivering the content in a very meaningful and engaging way. He is able to abstract ideas and make them relatable to everyone. Furthermore, he is SUPER helpful at all times, and even stays late for his students. )

Ill say he is definitely the best prof in sfu. Extremely knowledgeable and willing to help. Assignments and project are very practical and useful in future. Take any course with him for real learning.
The best prof I have ever had in my life

He does his best to make material quite interesting during lectures. All the assignments are very practical as they are something you can actually use outside class. He is very accessible and cares a lot about his students. Be aware: heavy group projects, lectures are important as he tests something he only talks in lecture but are no in the notes.

Good guy, gives videos to help with assignments that are pretty easy for what you should know at this level. Only complaint is he scales up how much you need for a decent grade by quite a bit so be confident that you can get at least 80% if you want something that won't break your GPA. Also check you can run android studio it is a memory hog.

Brian's lectures are great and you get a lot out of his classes that can help you in future like co-op terms. Didn't had to prepare a lot extra for the exams, if you can do good in your assignments that it gets easier. If Brian is teaching any class, and you take from someone else, then it is a MISTAKE!

I've taken around 5 courses with Brian, and all have been great experiences. He makes the content easy to understand plus assignments are very much related to the topic discussed in lectures. If you can do the assignments, then you will do good in exams.

Pros: -group project is very useful (agile development method is used pretty much everywhere now) -lectures are crystal clear -kind and generous in general and very open to feedback from students Cons: -quizzes are VERY tricky
Assignments are quite fair and his lectures are very informative
nice
Great guy. He knows his stuff well. Fun lecture. Assignment are fair and related to the materials in the course.

He's keeping his enthusiasm and perfection just like 5 years ago when he started teaching at SFU. His lectures are very informative and clear. Assignments are there to help you understand the material and help you prepare for your exams. If he teaches a difficult class you haven't taken, take that class with him.
Mind blowing lectures, assignments are given crystal clear marking schemes, summaries are provided for easy studying for mid-terms and finals. Focus in class for an easy A.

Great prof, took his other classes before as well and he taught them just as good. Challenging in the beginning but everything becomes crystal clear as you approach the final exam. Make sure you understand all his notes (not too many confusing topics) and you will get a good mark.
Glad computer science department has this guy. He is great!
Truly the best prof I've ever had. In my 4th year now, I still can't forget how awesome he was.

Had him for 2 other classes prior to this. Course content is easy, but exams can be VERY tricky (he takes pride in tricky MC). He's still a good prof, and takes his job very seriously. I like the lecture format, but assignments were tedious and long, while exams were trickier than they needed to be for a 200 level course
I don't know why so many people like his, there's a few I know that really dislike his teaching. He wants everything his way. Is this really a way to teach your students!??
