However, his lectures are easily digestible and some of the best. Brad Bart is most likely one of the best professors you could take this course with.

TOO HARD PROFESSOR. Final is worth 200 marks that tests everything. You should have clear understanding of every word that is written in his notes. Not even a single word can be taken for granted. Assignments-EVERY WEEK. If you are a nerd and think you are good in chess you will do well, for average students AVOID!!!!!

Very efficient lectures and well organized. Great personality. Bart's assignments take too long to complete because we aren't explicitly given the method to do certain calculations - so lots of gaps in my understanding. His midterms test you something you may know but twists it; his exams are doable regardless. Material is not dull with him :)!
great professor, though assignments can be challenging.

Brad is one of the best professors at SFU. His lectures are very well planned, interesting, and funny. He has a very gruff bedside manner, is quite strict, and does not tolerate rudeness. Tests are hard but fair, expect to spend 6+ hours per assignment. Follow the rules, be respectful and put in the required effort, and you will learn a lot.

Yes, the course will teach you a lot, but the point is Brad once told the TAs: students need to learn assembly language in this course but they may never use it in their later life. This means you are learning a lot of things you don't need :( lecture notes fail to follow the textbook is because Brad borrowed them from previous prof

Brad is a wonderful lecturer. However, he does not like anyone coming up his behind. If you try to go behind him during class, he will roast you in front of the whole class. Amazing lectures; after taking his class, he will make you think that assembly language is a high level language. SFU is lucky to have such a hidden gem.

The course is fairly tough with a LOT of assignments which can be time consuming. The labs were pretty tricky as well and challenging to finish under the time constraints and overall forces you to stay on top of things. However I learned a ton and I would do it over again if I had to.
This course is difficult, but you can learn more than you can even expect. Brads lectures are incredible, but assignments may be hard if you start them late.
Really felt like I learnt a lot in this course. It's a difficult course with a high work load, but all the work is well designed to help learn the material.

Brad is an excellent prof and is very clear in explaining things during lecture. He is a little picky with his no electronics policy but overall a great guy. This course is difficult and the assignments due every Friday are a bit long for what they're worth (2%). Midterms and final were quite difficult but extremely fair if you study a good amount.
have to spent 8 hrs on assignments each week that are unrelated with the course material. Exams are relatively hard.

The overall course material is moderately difficult and not covered extensively in many resources. Due to this, you will be relying on the textbook, his office hours, your notes, the TA's, his website, colleagues, Intel 86-64 manuals, and the little information online for help. If you use all of these resources, you will be successful.

Brad is great at giving lectures, even though I hardly understood anything (material is insane). His assignments are well thought out, but very difficult. I felt at times I didn't have enough information from the lectures to solve the assignments. You have to fight to win in this class. Learned a TON about computer systems though!

I've never put so much effort into a course before to get the worst grade of my academic career. Doesn't match the textbook at all, the assignments take at least 7+ hours every week for only 2%. Bearly any examples, and google or the textbook doesn't help. You do learn a lot, but it doesn't show in your grade. Avoid.

Very good, clear lecturer. Pace is great. Exams are fair, and I didn't need to study too much. Assignments rather long for the mark weight, but are quite interesting. He does seem very uptight about procedure, and can be snide about latecomers, etc., but I suppose he has his reasons. Would recommend if you want to learn the material well.

Bart is very serious, disciplined and has a high standard. I agree with the others that you do learn a lot from him but It's extremely hard to get a high grade from him. You have to master everything he teaches to just get a B+. He gives a crazy final that covers basically single everything he taught and it's out of 200. Very challenging prof.
He gave final exam papers and said "GOOD LUCK HAVE FUN" -It was not fun. -Definitely, not fun. You will learn lot from Bart, but he will ask even more.

He is trying to teach this course well, but turn out to be totally a nightmare. Lectures gives extra knowledge for textbook while tonnes of assignments asks about the extra knowledge that we didn't learn(and google doesn't help at all). For exams, he will reduce all mark of a long answer question if he think your solution is not perfect. AVOID HIM!

Never had extremely baffling exams ever. Assig ments are so difficult that TAs couldn't comprehend, textbooks are useless, googling doesn't help at all. Midterms and finals are mindblowing. Questions are very conceptual and out of nowhere from his lecture note or the textbook. You will learn alot in the end. I would definitely not take him again.

Can learn a lot from his class, but lots of hard assignments. Midterms were even harder, and final covered almost every detail and was extremely hard. If you want to actually learn something then take his class. If you don't have enough time and want to save your GPA, then don't.
Class was extremely hard

Brad seems like a fun individual at first, but he doesn't know how to properly approach a student who is struggling with the subject. He's intimidating and doesn't make you feel the brightest during his office hours. Definitely doesn't know how to give his students the best support they can get. Doesn't seem very encouraging . Don't take his class

Very helpful assignments with 7/10 difficulty, though he doesn't give very specific instructions to them. You have to explore the concepts on your own. Lectures are very important and useful. You actually learn a lot more than taking with other professors teaching the same course. If you don't like that then don't take it him.

A lot of assignment !!!!!!!!! he's lecture has no relationship with his assignment and his midterm has no relationship with his notes!!! too many principles and if you read book you wont able to understand it
Doesn't give enough examples for quizzes
Awesome Prof! best

There's usually at least one midterm with lots of challenging questions (Those ones that can fail over a 100 students ) and fear of failing will constantly stress you out during the course but you can learn A LOT through the lectures. His explanations are crystal clear like you've never been learning that much for a long time.

One of the best professors ever. He can explain things very well. I think his lectures are more understandable than the text book. The average grade of this course may not high, but this is definitely not the problem of the professor but the course itself. Algorithms may be the most difficult but important subject of computing science.
9 assignments 3 quizzes, dont take this prof if you are not taking his course full time
Great prof if you work hard and go to class. Concepts are obviously difficult, dont expect easiness.
If you decide to read the textbook and do those assignments carefully, you will learn a lot. Be prepared for 9 assignments and 3 quiz in the semester with him.
Not so hard, don't get freaked out easily. Lot of concepts and examples, just review after classes and go through the textbook, I think that'll help.
Even though the assignments are posted weekly, they are not that hard. If you feel ok to the assign, then you may feel ok to the exams.

Phenomenal professor. Some of the best notes and explanation I've ever had. The course concepts are extremely tricky but if you come to every class, participate, and go to his office hours, you will have no problem. He may seem intimidating in class but is very helpful and friendly outside. Practice lots and you will do great! !

He's a great lecturer and gives good notes. My biggest gripe about him is he doesn't care about your life outside his class. Asking him for any leeway on due dates will result in him speaking down to you. As long as your schedule is clear and you never have to ask anything of him, he's great.

I think although he gives very hard exams, his lectures and ways of thinking are pretty useful for future computing science study. Also he is kind and willing to help his students during office hours. I get a very good grade after all and I do recommend students who are seeking to learn more to take his classes.

He assumes you can follow at the speed he writes and speaks. Students don't listen to what he says for the sake of getting the notes down. Have a friend take notes with you. Assignments are moderately difficult, but he gives frequent zeros to ENTIRE questions if ONE thing is wrong - esp. on exams! Sometimes funny though. Try to avoid...

I found his exams (midterms and final) unnecessarily hard for a 100 level introductory course. I am not a computer science major but I do have a strong mathematics background and I found his exams stressful and hard. Harder than it needs to be. Avoid him if GPA is your priority. He is good at teaching though and I like how he catches cheaters.

He explains things well and he is organized. However, his exams are brutal. If you want a good grade DO NOT TAKE it with him. Maybe he has the correct level of difficulty but compared to other instructors he is extremly hard. Take it with him if you are down for some challenge. Also, DO NOT CHEAT. He cares and he'll catch you.

This course was the hardest I've ever had to endure. While Bart is an amazing prof and I couldn't imagine someone else doing a better job, the concepts are very hard to grasp. Second half of the course killed me. DO NOT CHEAT, HE WILL CATCH YOU, AND YOU WILL HACVE A MARK AGAINST YOU FOR ACADEMIC DISHONESTY. Form study groups, go to office hours.
He's a good prof, always asks students if they have any questions and has plenty of office hours
Very tough course with a tough prof. However, I did learn a lot .

This class is all about THINKING! The lecture material and assignments are based on forcing you to workout and push your brain in all sorts of ways. The tests and midterms are simpler and clear-cut. Students really mess up because they focus on memorizing rather than fully comprehending the material. Advice: Selectively bring your A game.
Funny, fair teacher. Work hard, be honest and show respect.

One of the best profs I've ever had. MACM is difficult, but he makes it as clear as possible with funny examples and jokes. He's helpful, has lots of office hours and will spend as much time as needed every single time you ask him a question. One of that profs who's into teaching and wants his students to succeed.

"Bart" (thinking about Simpsons now :p) teaches well; clear and funny. Assignments are not easy. I heard he got complaint letters from the school that because his assignments were too hard, his students had crushed the server several times. But that's for senior level courses. Lol. But anyway, I enjoyed the course.

Lectures are boring and unclear, hard to follow and keep up with the prof. Have to do a ton of reading in your spare time if you want to pass your exams. Assignments and exams feels like a huge jump from what he talks about in class. Avoid taking 150 with this prof unless you have a low class load. MT was hard, final was insane.
Lecture notes are totally "helpful" in assignments and exams. Think again before u take it.

Yeah this class was hard; assignments were long, material wasn't too easy to learn, but Brad was great. He's not the nicest person to talk to but you come out of this class being a way better programmer. Props to him. Hope he continues to teach 150.

Worst prof I've ever had. Boring lectures. Hard exams. Always available during office hours, but you gain little to nothing after talking with him. Assignments are insanely hard and requires a lot of work/time. Took this the same semester I took macm + 2 electives and although I passed both courses, I've never studied so hard for an average grade.

I'd give Brad pretty decent marks as a Prof. His assignments are long, but that's how you'll learn the material. His exams are very hard if you don't have much experience. If you haven't taken MACM you're going to have problems. He's going to assume you already know the math and won't explain it. He's very helpful during his office hours.

His exams are very fair, they test you on if you really know the material. A lot of people will say his classes are hard but you need to actually do the work, he is very theory and assignment based, his exams are loosely based off his assignments. Overall good prof.
do not take the class unless you are interested in Programming. super Hard Prof. picky about very minor details. dont wanna see him ever again in my life. crazy.
Goes through material super fast, you miss one class and your done, marks super hard. Don't take the class with him.

He is actually a pretty nice prof, and helpful/responsive if you ask him questions or go to his office hours. The lectures could get pretty dry, and the level of assignments and midterms were generally pretty difficult, but overall I learned a lot in the course

CMPT 150 isn't necessarily hard, but there is a LOT of content. Brad went through this material extremely fast, and ended the semester a week early, saying, "I don't do review sessions". He doesn't help review or do anything at all, and he's not very available for office hours. Not very good at explaining either. Don't take his class.

Don't take the course with him if you don't want to work hard and learn useful things. His assignments are challenging but help you understand the topic a lot once you start thinking. If you don't study you'll fail or barely pass. But if you do study you'll learn so much more.
Lectures are boring. Some exam questions are made quite weird and hard to solve. He sometimes even tests the material that exceeds the test range!

This guy give u a very clear scope on all the prospects in computer science. his test and assign are hard(2nd midterm and final). u must be well prepared and study all his notes for the tests. if u do so, u are getting a good grade
BB is really a good guy. He can memorize almost everyone's name is his class. Really helpful. The course is hard though, actually harder than other CMPT307. But his course is worth taking
Brad is an excellent teacher and I really enjoyed his class.

He is an interesting professor and a great lecturer. However, he lacks encouragement and a real knack of trying to help students. I'm not rooting those who are unwilling to make the effort, but he does not make the cut for an excellent prof. He can inspire passion in students but his dog-eat-dog mentality is not the best way to go.

Highly recommended to take MACM 101 with him. Discrete math is a foundation course that has concepts that will be used continually throughout mathematics. Despite being a very touch professor, Brad teaches it with high clarity, thoughtfulness and care. Encourages questions and participation in class. Exams and assignments are fair but tricky.
person below me probably is the bottom 10% of the class. averages are 55-58% for both midterms. scaled to a c+

DONT TAKE HIS CLASS. That's a disaster!!!! Averages of exams are below 50%, I think 40% of student will fail the class. It just a 100 level course, he made it as a 400 level course!!!!!! And I don't think those things he taught are useful in my following study.
We were lucky that this semester brad had taught this course. Really enjoyed the course and learned a lot, I am glad that I took the course him
I would say avoid courses by Brad Bart.

Taught a 100 level math course like it was a graduate level course. A little overboard on assignments and test. Extremely hard to do well in. I'm not saying he has to spoon feed us but there is a limit to what is reasonable. And on top of that he is extremely arrogant. I feel sorry for the people who took this class without any math background.
Hard but useful

Most people will give this prof a bad rating because it's hard to get high grades on assignments, and because he won't just tell you how to do something, he'll force you to figure it out after giving you some clues. I've had him for CMPT 120 as well and for both courses he gave very hard assignments, but the tests ended up being easy in comparison

Extremely difficult course for those without a strong math background. Midterms seem easy, but are actually extremely difficult to score well in, as they are out of 20. Final exam mark, if higher than midterm marks, will replace the midterm. Assignments, 10, worth 10%. Many answers are found in the solutions manual for the textbook.

Brad Bart is a good professor. He will not cradle, or baby you and will have very reasonable expectations aimed to push you as a student. I feel these are qualities a university prof should have. When you take year 3-4 classes which assume you're a good programmer, you will think back and thank him for pushing you. Take his class.
very hard professor and very hard assignment. think twice before you select his class
He is a nice guy, but doesn't know how to teach.

It's very hard to not pay attention in class, since he's very good at explaining concepts. He makes the lecture interesting, and his examples of programs are excellent. I wish he can teach most of my courses.

Knows his stuff and presents it with wit; those who get on his bad side will be humiliated with extreme prejudice. Don't worry - this only happens if you're ignorant. I personally am not doing to well at this class; this is less the fault of Brad, whom I believe is fair, but do not underestimate the class simply because it is a 101 class.

Dry sense of humour, which you won't like unless you share in it. Expects competence and actual thought rather than regurgitation. Assignments were interesting and designed to lead you to a good solution. Actually has interesting things to say.

I do not really know what I can say..just avoid him at all the cost unless you are one of those 4.0 GPA computing science students who loves to spent hours and hours on his stuff. The course is hard, but the thing i really hated was his personality not the course. He always wants to say " I am the best" He thinks he is funny but not even clos

this course is quite challenging but is great for those who are interested in programming and have a bit of knowledge about computers. assignments are not as scary as previous comments suggest, but you should take the time to think about how to solve the questions a few days ahead of the due date. exams are hard but fair for the most part ...
try this course if you looking for a reason to hate programming. assignments was like a weekly test. final was hell.
Attend the lectures and learn what he teaches in the lectures, and you'll do just fine.
He knows the material well and does a good job of teaching it. He was using some of the material from the online Stanford course which I thought was clever. Totally recommend taking 354 with him.
you will feel confidence in databases at the end.
Good instructor and fair mareker.
If you follow the instruction well, it's easy to get an A+!
Good prof, knows the material well and extremely fair marker.

Excellent Professor! He cares students so much that although some materials in lecture might be hard, he will explain and show examples more than once until he made sure that everyone can understand. You will find the course under his teaching become interesting. Some assignments takes time, but he will give detailed instruction!
Honestly, his knowledge is not enough to teach and manage the course.

If you enjoy every piece of information taught to you right out of a text book this is your guy. He covers material way too quickly without proper explanations or examples(a chapter every few days). His assignment were tough and so were his exams. This was the first computing science class I ever got a D in.
Great prof, course is not that hard for a upper division CS course.

Great guy. This course is very interesting to me and is actually not hard. His lectures were interesting, assignments and exams were pretty fair to me although I do messed up the last assignment...but interesting enough I've got pretty high marks on his mid term and final. Be advised, START YOUR ASSIGNMENTS EARLY OR YOU'LL MESS THEM UP.
I dont understand why our final should be that hard .....assignment are unclear and he don't explane things well not recommended
Nice guy :) He explains things very clearly
realy bad- hard final he didnt even teach what he asked on final , worst TA , no respond to your email , dont take with him

Knowledgeable, personable, interesting, and clear. Better than many profs in the faculty, even though he is a sessional. For those who commented that the assignments/tests were too hard, you are clearly not ready for upper-division CS,as this was one of the easier and clearer courses I've taken.

Helpful, knowledgeable, personable. Knows his material well, and can teach it well. To the previous commenters that claim the assignments/exams were too hard, you are clearly not ready for upper division CS, as this was a relatively clear and easy course.

Easy Assignments, If your not clear with what to do go and ask him directly during his office hour, he is helpful.the last assignment is hard (given the time) limited time but tons of things to do, not if your taking 1 course.The only thing i dont like about his course is his final exam, extremely hard! From A+ to B-
Thanks a lot. Hassan. I've had a lot of fun in your class. Hope you do well in the future.
Hard assignments and exams
Interesting assignments and final.
Sometimes hard to follow the class. Assignments and exams were fair except the time for midterm. Generally recommended
Good lectures. Would repeat until everyone understands. Fair exams. Recommended.
Hassan asked a lot of questions about problems before giving out the solution which made lectures more interesting. Definitely recommended
Liked his style
This guy was significantly more competent for teaching the course.
Good prof, decent lectures and lecture notes. Overall a difficult course regardless. Most of the class did very poorly but it was graded on a curve.
Good professor. Yagiz makes it tough but Xue Bin balances it out well. Assignments are a smidge tough but nothing a few hours can't solve.

This professor made up for the first part of the course (taught by Yagiz), he was an incredible professor who cared about making course content clear and understood by students. There were many examples that were directly related to the content in assignments. The assignments were fun and interesting, and the final exam was fair and relevant
He is a bit old, which means lectures can often be a bit slow. However, he does pay a lot of attention to details, and tries to answer interesting questions students have in class.
he gave me A+ so hes my king

Lecture slides explain things badly and he just reads off of them the whole time. Assignment guidelines, sample outputs, and due dates also keep getting changed, requiring re-submissions. Midterm questions were printed weird so it was hard to find where the multiple choice answers actually were. Everything is disorganized, and wastes your time.
shows up to office hours late and ends them early. in class he just reads off slides.
Data mining is Martin's bread and butter. Must take course when he is teaching it.
Good experience
final median 55 and he did not curved
To the people commented below, exam is tomorrow. How do you know your letter grade already?

Avoid him , he's a master of jargons... He might be a "nice" guy, but at this age he's not good as prof. He can't explain materials clearly, lecture notes are full of jargons ... John edgar is your only good prof for cmpt 354

Very nice guy. Sometimes could be a little more clear on lectures but he knows what he is talking about and is happy to answer questions. I got the sense he wanted more class participation. Also some of his slides use kind of outdated SQL practices but he is fairly lenient on tests so it's fine.

to be honest, at this age he shouldn't teach anymore. I can't even understand what he's saying in lectures, mostly due to his age and accent. He's mostly read from lecture slides, and very useless lectures. I have to copy each of his slides to chatgpt to explain me what exactly this prof is saying. Avoid him, not much I learned from this course...

Genuinely just not a great professor. There was homework every single week of the semester, each time, new homework was released the day before the previous was due. Just feels like poor care for the students. I asked him to put complete lecture slides on the canvas page and he refused stating that students must come to class. Exams are 8/10 hard.
He knows his stuff and it's interesting to learn from the guy who invented DBSCAN. The average for all the midterms were < 50% though...

Dr. Ester isn't charismatic, but he is caring and knowledgeable. For a world-renowned Computer Scientist, I was surprised to hear him asking how we mere undergrads were doing every lecture. The course requires intellectual interest. If not interested in Data Mining, you'll fall asleep. Otherwise, you'll be learning from the best.
terrrible!

Always goes overtime and short 5 min breaks. Do not take with class immediately after, unless in same room. Many technical slides, and does not cover in depth. I learned nearly nothing during lectures. Many technical difficulties. Many computer issues, which distracts learning. Brutal marking on short answer on midterms.

Extremely time consuming with multiple midterms, group project and multiple assignments. Each of the assignments and group project milestones take at least a few days to finish and are grueling to finish or even get working (first "easy"ssignment code took over 300 lines of code for me and 5 days of working it on it all day) unreal expectations

Dr. Ester authored some of the seminal papers in Data Mining in the past, so he's def very knowledgeable. The class is not the most engaging however, sometimes we're just given algorithms without any theoretical motivation. Open-book quiz and final project are extremely easy - overall a grade booster as an online class.

Dr Ester is the best professor I have ever seen. He respects every student in his class. And he is willing to give students partial marks even the answer is not correct. His lecture is boring but the exam is very fair, everything is from either slides or assignments. I would take more database topic courses with him.
SOOOOOOO BORING, he is smart and know everything but just not good at teaching, bad lecture bad slices, I stopped attending the lecture after midterm, just learn by yourself to get an easy A

Lectures are hard to follow for the same reasons from post below me. A lot of homework, 1 paper assignment + 1 programming assignment alternating every week for a total of 5 each. Both are hard so start early and be ready to learn R very quickly. Midterm/Final 45% sim to paper assignments. Don't be shy to ask questions! Email/office hours are key.

I believe he knows well about data mining and he treats students with respect. But he can not teach. He does not know how to explain a topic to a novice. He tries to teach too much advanced algorithms and theories without laying a solid foundation. If the class was taught by anyone else, I would have learned so much more and better. Avoid Dr. Ester

Martin is a renowned scholar in Data mining. I think taking 459 from him is one of the best ways to fully utilize the CS education at SFU. Although his lectures are a little bit boring, and there are too many assignments, you can learn a lot of things from this course. "HE KNOWS THE SUBJECT VERY WELL" This is all I can say here.

Prof has poor teaching skills but he's a nice guy. Don't take this course if your background ain't biomedical or lack understanding of Machine learning algorithms. Prof knowledge is not so good in ML side and good in biomed side. Seminar based course. I wasn't from biomed, so I felt it was very difficult to understand prof or fellow presentations

He is a very knowledgeable professor from the School of Computing Science and he is always willing to help students to get thru the toughest part of the course. CMPT 459 (Data Mining) is a hot topic in the tech. industry right now and Dr. Ester is one of the best prof. you should take this course with.
VERY CARING AND NICE. SUPER INTELLIGENT. He even taught us some Algorithms he developed 21 years ago.

The textbook and his lecture notes sometimes dont match. He makes many mistakes. His exam/assn questions are ambiguous and marking is sometimes done on a strict basis even though there could be multiple right answers. He is very boring. On the plus side he's not too hard and exams are open book.
I find his classes boring. I rarely attended class. He is one of the easiest instructors that I have had at SFU. Exams are open book and questions were similar to the assignments.

Good prof. Very willing to help and takes student feedback into account. Posts very detailed notes online. Assignments (weekly) range from easy to fair. Exams are open book. Tough midterm (too long!), okay final. Classes always go overtime. Contrary to what others have said, I *would* take another class with him.

If you learn well from the textbook, you will do OK. His assignments and exams are fair, but his teaching is quite bad. It's unfortunate too because he's a nice guy and wants everyone to do well. And yes, he goes over time every lecture so don't take a class right after his class.

Exams are extremely long. Don't expect to finish in time. Beware of keeners in the class. They tend to get him off track for a very long time. Our review class, which was suppose to REVIEW the semester.. ended up only covering 3 questions in 1 section.. IN TWO DAYS. Thanks WM, black coat, squeaky kid, durrr kid. You know who you are.

Horrible prof ever. I am ready to quit his lecture and wait another year for someone else. Lectures extremely boring, midterm & final - never enough time to complete, assignments easy but not enough percentage on total marks. Marking schemes never fair!! Please take my advise and avoid him.

Nice guy, lectures are boring, exams open-book, assignment grades nice, but midterm fatal. listening to him is like trying to understand ****. It shouldnt be too hard to get a good grade in his class though.

Smart guy BUT, useless when it comes to teaching. Fortunatly the material is easy and the assignments aren't too bad. However, DON'T expect to learn ANYTHING from him. His notes are often incorrect so don't follow them. Oh yeah, test, can't judge the amount of time you need to write a test so you are often trying to write a 2 hour midterm in 1 hour

Knows what he is talking about, but have trouble (at certain times) in teaching it. Hands out alot of assign. but are relatively easy to do. Midterm and final exam are reasonable, but really has a tough time judging amount of time required to finish it. Overall, a decent prof that knows his stuff.
No Comments
No Comments
Most boring prof EVER, he also has attitude problem. But his assns and exams are pretty easy
He sucks, can't teach
he knows his stuff, but I found it to be a hard class even though I thought I understood everything
Unreasonably long midterm for 50 min. Reasonable final with open book, expect a lots of writing though. WILL definitely take him for 454.
No so good at explaining. Lectures are boring. Open book exam. I wouldn't take another class with him.
Easy assignment and exam, but he can't teach
OPEN BOOK exam, reasonable assign, knowledgeable!!

Paul is the most approachable professor I have ever met. You can even find him any time after class. All assignments are closely related to the course, and you need to understand the course content and assignment requirements, writing skills are also required.attend all classes. you will get a high score because the grading is very clear.

Paul is a great person, however, the course itself is subjective. It's hard to know if you're on the right track or even remotely doing the right thing. Paul is very accessible however and truly seems to care. It would be nice if this course was more objective than subjective, but I guess with the nature of the material that would be hard.

I was baited by the reviews to take this course, but be warned that you don't learn anything useful related to UI/UX design since everything that is taught is very basic and intuitive. This course is a huge time sink with no reward, assignments are mind-numbing and tedious, and weekly readings eat up time that I'd rather spend on other courses.

The teacher is very responsible, very quick to answer questions, and the materials needed to provide in class are also very thoughtful. The course content was also interesting, although I felt that the excessive writing assignments and the grading of the written assignments made it difficult to improve the grades

I have mixed feelings about this course. The content is very interesting and Paul is a good instructor, but his grading is heavily based on writing skills which I don't have. Also, there are not many opportunities to practice ui design hands-on before the group project. Will not recommend if you care about GPA and are not good at writing.

Gonna do weekly readings and summarize them. Lectures are optional, but I shown up to them anyways. Have to write a exam question each week which I was terrible at so YMMV Subjective grading for projects but it is UX so uhh yeah Overall, great course. Learnt lots about new things.
Very good professor. Read the weakly readings and you wont have to study for the final. Very easy course but requires a lot of your weekly time. I recommend taking 363 with him.

CMPT 363 (online) requires constant, but low-stress effort. You have weekly readings, which are surprisingly helpful, and make up most of the content of the course. You have enough time for the group projects, so doing a little bit every other day is an easy way to get a good mark. Getting Paul's feedback is essential for improving your marks.

Paul Hibbitts is one of the best professors I have had. This man is passionate about UX design, and applies it to every aspect of the course. He makes sure the course is interesting, he helps you in every way possible, and he asks for feedback and applied it to make sure the course is perfect. Really an amazing prof and a treat to have.

Great prof, provides good feedback and all the resources you will need to do good on his assignments and final exam. He also acts on the feedback he is given unlike other profs where it feels like you're sending your feedback straight into the trash.

Paul is one of the best CMPT professors at SFU, he gives really good feedback and makes sure you never make the same mistake twice. Other than that, he is also very clear in his lectures and his grading, the final exam also consists of student generated answers. The course overall was very fun with paul

This is how all courses should be taught. We were actually graded on our ability to think critically about a problem - rather than your ability to regurgitate information - something most SFU CS profs love to test.... In some ways I think this course should almost have a writing designation though, as you will spend a lot of time reading/writing.

Unclear grading criteria and mark assignments subjectively on what he thinks right. If you have a bad grammar, dont take it. His grading is just does not make sense. He grades based on grammar and how the answer is easy for him to understand. He does not care about his international students who have bad grammar but understands the topic.

Paul is great, he is really interested in getting student feedback on things to improve and actually applies the feedback quickly. This course with him feels like you learn useful things you can actually apply in the field. No midterms just two group projects and a final exam that comes from student weekly questions.
Tough grader, avoid at all cost. Dont have high hope on him, you wont get 90-100 on your assignment. Hard to get an A.

This teacher just wants to be respected. He does NOT LISTEN to the students opinions and mark assignments based on what he and his TA is right. He does not care about you students! UX cant be marked based on one or two person who are experienced. It just doesnt go like that sir.

Look guys, Paul is very tough in grading and so his TA's. The course seems easy but the teacher just made it tough. I do not recommend this teacher if you are looking for GPA. He is just not good at grading. His grading scheme just does not make sense for the assignments and quizzes. He makes you read every week and create questions for his final.
Paul is definitely the best prof I have ever met in my SFU life. Take this course, go to lecture, I guarantee you will change your perspective on UX design completely

Paul is honestly one of the best profs at SFU. Talking to him was like talking to a friend. Asking him for help was always super easy because he usually responds on the Canvas discussion board. If anyone is interested in Cmpt 363 or needs extra credits. Paul's class is honestly the best choice.

Paul is one of the best professors at SFU. He not only lectures, but also actively works in the industry. He has a highly modern teaching style & seems to genuinely care about his students. He is polite, professional & is always available. All readings are (free) online, group project is big but allows you to pick your own groups & grade your peers
He is one of the best Professor at SFU. Recommend!
Don't be creativity if you want to get good marks, just follow rules, laws and theorems that he gave in the class.

He marked assignments and test subjectively. He likes out dated concept from textbook and disagrees creative designs. Even you spend a lot of time on doing "REAL" user research and design a "REAL" UI that SFU students really need. He will still give you low marks due to his personal point of view. Avoid this class if you do want to be creative.
Paul's one of the best instructors who has ever taught me. He's very nice and kind to students; quite helpful too. You won't regret taking his course.

One of the best professors at SFU. Always available and eager to help out (has at least 3 ways of reaching him at any time). Helps student understand the material in depth, and is always good to stop by and chat if he has office hours. SFU needs more professors like him!

Every lecture with Hibbitts will blow your mind. The material is interesting, well organized, and teaches you valuable skills. If you can show you understand the material, even if you do bad on assignments or the midterm, you can still get an A in this course. Paul Hibbitts is a god amongst men! Do not miss your chance to take this course with him.

A++ prof. Take Paul if you want to learn excellent user centered design. SIAT students should also take this course as you learn a lot more then in other IAT courses. Assignments and marking is beyond fair and you really get to use everything that you have learned in class for the three group assignments.

Opposite to the common belief, I didn't find the marking to this course subjective. It's just a matter of learning certain concepts and applying them on practice. Very helpful prof and very interesting lectures.

I enjoyed almost every class. He knows what he's talking about, and directly linked it to real world examples. The course yields an easier work-load, which is not Paul's fault. He did a great job teaching the material. I have heard he can be non-helpful to some people though. Can't win em all.

Shows great understanding of the material (especially thru his lectures). Assignments are very well-related and requires the student to do a little more than usual. Best Prof Ever!!!

very good teacher keeps the class interesting. very good "applied" techniques. all you whiners .... well you're whiners! its a design course so DUH its subjective! he says it in the first class! u dont like it BUH-BYE

Work load is very little, but study load is a lot. The tests are pretty easy if u study
This guy does know his stuff. Fair-ish tests ...

One of the fakest prof that i know at this school. Act as if he cares about the students but all he cares about is good eval to keep his comfy/easy job, once eval is done, he won't help. Assignments and test are marked subjectively.

Absolutely amazing! He really knows his stuff & has REAL-WORLD non-academic exp!

He hates people who care about the course or want to do good in it. Don't ask questions, don't send emails, don't be inquisitive. He just wants to monologue and get out. I don't even know why he has a teaching job.

Added a midterm abruptly without consulting with the class and undergraduate committee, which resulted in a formal complaint from dozens of students. I took IAT 167 during the Fall 2024 semester, and I felt there was a significant lack of communication and support from Professor Shaw. Never respond to a single email I sent him! Never tried to help students during semester I only got help from TAs.

The only professor who I have ever heard say "The exam average was lower than expected, that means something was wrong with my teaching of the concepts and I take full accountability for that" (paraphrasing), then proceed to engage in discussions on how he can improve. I thought his teaching was great. Need more profs like him!!
I had him in the summer of 2025 for CMPT310, and he was a amazing professor. Gave amazing lectures, that were passion filled and gave very fair exams/assignments.

310 with Morteza was one of my favorite courses. He is very passionate in his lectures and provides many real world examples that he's worked on. I looked forward to attending lectures each week. The weekly assignments prepare you well for the exams. The group project is really fun if you choose something you're interested in.

Fall 2025's course closely followed UCB's CS188 course structure, however, the delivery of the lecture material was very good, and I enjoyed all my interactions with Morteza. He was very easy to approach, and the extra 'final prep' office hours (additional to his regular office hours) were very helpful to most students. Would take him again.

To prepare for exams he'd have zoom office hour sessions telling us how to solve questions that would be tested. The exams ended up being what he said they'd be like. Overall a very lenient and understanding professor. He gives you every chance he can for you to do well in his class.
Very fair and very caring. Provided multiple extra office hours sessions before both the midterm and final. 10/10

Professor Badali is an amazing professor who truly cares for his student. He provide extra hours to make sure that the material is delivered completely, gives good leads to the students who are struggling, and his explanation in class is clear and concise. He's one of the best professor I've met so far.
He is very knoledgable and passinate proffesor.

Takes in late assignments, as well as lenient grading on all assessments. Took accountability for low midterm avg applied a curve. Held 4 hour zoom meetings on the weekend to help students review for final exam. Occasionally funny, even made some jokes on midterm and final. Only gripe was that the organization of the course was messy and confusing.

Morteza's lectures were always very detailed and he clearly cares, always answering questions thoroughly. Grading has many parts (assignments, weekly work, project writeups), but all of these prepare you well for the final, and he grades leniently. We could choose any topic we wanted for the project with a presentation at the end. Highly recommend.

Awesome professor. Morteza is very caring and wants his students to do as well as possible. His workload is a little heavy at times with several assignments due in the latter half of the course but he adjusts assignments and exams slightly when he sees the class generally is struggling.
On the final u can see hard questions accounting for about 25% percent comes from topics not covered in assignments.
Lectures don't cover much material, and its very hard to prepare for the midterms and the assignments, as you have no idea what's gonna be on them
The course itself was a little hard. But that's because of the course content itself. The prof made it much easier than it should be. Definitely a star prof in SFU.

By the end of that term, our course ended up to have only 4 people registered; and professor gave 4 different grades to us; it was like ranking the students. I don't know if there were such huge differences to reflect this harsh ranking.
It was an OK course
He is absolutely very knowledgeable and cares a lot about the students. On the other hand, the way he presents the lectures is kind of messy and not very decent.

Very very great professor, very patient and helpful. without doubt a star pro in SFU

He is one of my best prof. He is very helpful. Too much reading for this course and TA has a problem communicate with students but the prof is really good. I loved this course and enjoyed this class.
easy a

This guy is probably one of the most useless professors i have ever have had the displeasure of had teach. Enlightening and inspiring. Be prepared to read, research and write. Comp sci students need this perspective!

Most of the work are done in the beginning of the semester. A lot of writing. He uses like 5 weeks to do classroom presentations which are not very helpful (didn't learn much). The TA is not good at English but marks us for English, which is quite frustrating

Art is great - he is a fantastic teacher and really knows his stuff. I am glad I didn't get him for 307 though! This writing course was a piece of cake, although I was a bit dissapointed with my grade. I had 86% and ended up with a B+, was hoping for an A range but not a big deal. Highly recommended :)

Art is a good teacher and is always available for extra help (which you WILL need). HOWEVER, he gives VERY difficult exams and is also a very strict marker. The midterm average was 20-30%, and I'm pretty sure more than half the class failed. Though I like Art, I advise you wait for a different prof unless you thought MACM 201 was an easy course.

He teaches the material well, exams are fair. Unfortunately, he seemed to have an irrational hatred for our class. Midterms were arbitrarily remarked and marked down A LOT. He then flipped out on us and said he wouldnèt scale. Final was really easy, yet his marking, again... put the average way below 50%. More than half the class failed.
Had a very negative attitude and marked the class brutally. 40 students, and the third highest mark was a C+. One of the most negative experiences I've had at SFU.
The worst teacher ever. I don't know how this guy is still allowed to teach. Avoid him at all cost.

Great at teaching a difficult subject. Any problems usually came from the complexity of the subject, no from him. Able to keep things light and entertaining even when you brain is about to explode. Pop-quizes were great in forcing you to keep up with the material. Tests/assignments were hard but fair.
Engaging prof, wry but lively sense of humour (as long as you don't ask stupid questions, you should be fine...). Midterms and quizzes were hard but fair.

One word. HARD!!!!!! Exclamation point. The class average is around 30%. NO online notes (just handwritten) brutal pop quizzes, brutal midterm, convers tons of material. However if you keep up and read the text (yeah right) you can do ok, but kiss your GPA good bye.

Disappeared for a quarter of the semester, left a grad student to teach us. The class became hopelessly lost and dwindled far behind. One of the few courses where I swear I could feel the animosity between the students and prof permeating the air around me.
6 'HARD' surprise quizes worth 20%!! The midterm was hard, he talks while he writes, and has very messy handwritting to boot! Some say he's funny, some say he's cocky...

He rocks!

Everyone mostly gets good grades on assignment and projects, so you guessed it. Your grade is determined by your final exam. I had 72% overall, and ended up with a C. Class was extremely boring and can get mathy. I only took because it was fully online. You could possibly end up with a very cool project and the prof encourages the use of AI for it.

Three quizzes worth a total of 60% of your grade, all online. It's a dense, boring and somewhat directionless look at many medical imaging techniques and equations. This course is actually terrible. While the assignments are not hard, they are EXTREMELY tedious, and can take hours to do since he wants you to HAND WRITE everything. It is so annoying. Avoid......It's not worth spending money and time on taking this class

when you bring up problems to him, he will listen and cares about students. lectures are kind of dry, but he listened to past criticism and codes more in class.

I took 128 and it was honestly one of the best first year engineering course that I took. Ghassan honestly cares about his students and his lectures are amazing, I was hooked the entire time. His explanations are thorough and the labs are just as amazing, big up to the TAs who honestly care
One of the most easiest classes i've taken, just do your labs and you'll get an A. Final exam was MC and the written component was just like the labs, SUPER EASY!!! I barely showed up and got an B+

Awesome prof. So patient and knows a lot about what he's doing.
Awesome prof. Super clear in explaining stuff.

Very knowledgeable! He knows everything and explains stuff crystal clear. He simplified concept to make sure you understand very well, then he goes to more complicated things. The project needs work. You need to spend time on the projects. You take course with him, your knowledge will boost up!

He is an excellent prof. he is humble with his vast knowledge. I found him very passionate about the topics and he is one of those smart people that can make look simple very hard and dry topics.. Great prof! Really knows his stuff and delves right into the math behind it. Definitely take if interested in the subject - its content heavy so beware - but the assignments help with understanding, as do the office hrs - very helpful.

Not a bad prof, really good at explaining concepts, our final exam mark was scaled for sure cause that final was difficult! Confusing! He does not know how to teach.

STAY AWAY FROM HIM! -- I'm sure he wrote those positive comments himself. He joked about it once during the semester. and that is exactly how he got all of us (students in this class) to register. We all trusted those few positive reviews and took his class. VERY VERY disappointed.
He completely killed my passion for computer science. I don't recommend him for any class. :(
OMG, the final was awful! I think everyone failed it. Those positive reviews down here fooled me and many other people to take this course. This guy is legit the most awful instructor that there is!
HE is the equivalent of useless! Exams are very difficult, and has nothing to do with what he teaches.

He was very unhelpful. Lectures consist of him reading from the textbook and typing out examples IN the book. Tries to avoid answering questions at all costs. He is a great prof, very nice, calm and knowledgeable. If you have interest in biomedics, definitely take his class. I have never received as much feedback or help from any professor at sfu so far.
I always get excited and energetic about my project, when I talk to him. He is very very knowledgeable, patient, super helpful and a nice person. I should say he is one of the BEST.
He is a great teacher. Very organized and patient. Make sure you do all the assignments. He likes it very much when you participate in class.
Very patient and helpful!
Great prof. Lots of work though. Really good notes and the course work is interestind.
My favorite professor. His lectures are great. If you find his cmpt 120 is hard, maybe programming is really not your thing.
good

Most patient and hilarious teacher I've ever met!I know nothing about CS before the course,even little in English,but he magically pushes u forward without noticing.Before the final,I got 95% and 3 books as a reward,which comes from his salary.I just want to mention that u can learn more if it's hard to learn,and if u work harder,everything happens
not bad but NOT GOOD.
Great Prof!

His class is great. His slides are perfectly prepared. They have many animations so it is better to save them on laptop or iPad than printing them out. He has many pop quizzes and labs+assignment, but if you attend the class every week, you will be fine. His class is well, very well prepared.

Hard lecture notes, hard assignments, hard midterm, hard final, hard project, and a bad Francophone English, but a nice guy. The final project is incredibly ridiculous --- researching on biology, and writing a 3500 word report, but only considered a bit of coding (and difficult 4 newbies). He's a good prof. Course was really hard for an intro, but he did mark fairly and provided a rubric to follow. It was kinda like a bio project. After all the struggling, it was fun after all. Not a good teacher for an intro computing class. No textbook or assignment, just very unclear lecture slides are given. All i can say about this course taken by him is "LOL". The amount of work needed for a 3 credit course as an "introduction to computer science" was extremely outrageous. Philippe is a really nice guy and stuff and he is helpful but,it's either you know the stuff or you don't. And if you are a computer geek already-you'll be fine(all the geeks did)
Course is over. Still don't know a thing about coding. No homework, no practice-which is critical for this type of course.

1st 2 assignments were not bad; bonus points reward system was poor though. 3rd project was a totally poorly explained process. In a nutshell, its a proposal paper of a hypothesis you weren't told to make + a program simulation of a bio or cognitive process u believe to be the focus the whole time + a final Thesis Paper (aka:"simulation report")...

The professor is okay. He's obviously very experienced and knowledgeable about the topic and provides very comprehensive materials for the students. Nice & funny guy, but tough especially for an intro course. Like what others said, final project was more like a Bio project (with a 3.5k-words paper to write). I wish the bonus quizzes worked better - he only gave the bonuses if you got full marks on them, so there's very little chance of pulling grades up for people already suffering low grades.

The material was tough. Most of us were forced to take that class and honestly we'd rather not be here... But the prof really worked to make entertaining lectures! He was very available for help, like he'd come to the labs to give extra help on top of TA, and he gave us a super detailed feedback for the projects.

Extremly hard teacher. Entertaining way of teaching. just too hard.project is hard. assignment is hard.It is intro to computer science and most of us are from SIAT. many people dropped in the beginning of the class. honestly. I like him. but CMPT 120 usually are easier than this. nothing personal. I'm just expecting an easier course

First half of course was well taught, well prepared lectures. Post-midterm was off from computing, more a biology research for Project-was worth a lot and only partially consisted coding. Prof was always available to help, it was the TA who was vague. Should have more assignments instead of huge final and project. Low class average, no curve.

I don't think he deserves as much hate as he is getting. He was really helpful and his slides/lectures were extremely detailed and you could tell a lot of work went into them. I do think the project was a bit ridiculous however and it was too hard for an intro level course. Other than that the hw was fair, but weekly assnmnts would have helped.

Project worth too much and irrelevant to computing science, only 1/3 coding. After midterm there was no more assignments to practice. Everything from then on was trial n error. He was always available for help, however, he still leaves you with quite alot to figure out. First half of the course was good. Project sucked because it was bio project.

He is one of the worst prof's I have ever came across. He spent loads of time on the easy stuff and for the remaining time, he gave an unbelievably hard final project for us to waste our time on. I learned absolutely nothing about programming and now am completely turned off of computing science solely because of him.

Regardless of how helpful he tries to be, he is unclear. Course slides were useful but second half focused too much on himself and his other occupations. Project was irrelevant to course and was too hard to grasp simply because there was more coding for us to figure out than he really prepared us for. I would really like to learn more cmpt.

WORST PROFF EVER!!I have never done so poorly in a course before.His lecture is completely pointless and he doesn't know how to teach at all(his accent makes it ten times worse).He doesn't realize this is an introductory comp course and thinks we are masters students.for your own sake- AVOID at all costs!!(unless you're content with receiving an F)

He spent too much time on the easy stuff, not enough on the hard stuff in his lectures, and he would just sometimes start using new code without even telling us what the code meant. I talked to him about it before, but he didn't change his teaching style at all to help us. His final project was ridiculous and very difficult.

His slides and animations are great for teaching these confusing concepts. He is very willing to help his students. Its unfair that he is receiving all these hateful comments. Maybe some of you will appreciate him once you've had a few more profs at SFU...but i forgive you.

Ridiculous. No weekly homework assignments, no textbook... Irrelvant research project. He's smart, and can be quit helpful if you know what questions to ask, but not a great teacher in general. Very unprofessional in emails. The little bit I learned and actually understood in this class was in result of helpful websites I found by myself.

We had to spend a lot of time doing the final project, which most of us found completely pointless. This isn't a bio or writing class. People would rather spend that time doing coding stuff, but instead it was wasted on that stupid project. Very unprofessional in general and extremely rude in emails.

The material is challenging but the prof supports us a lot. He always answers emails fast, and spent lots of time helping on the projects! I think I learnt a lot on the project... it wasn't easy but that was worth it.

This so-called introductory course is so effing hard. The prof should compare his stupid material with the other NORMAL cmpt120 courses. The final project is ridiculous.

Why in the world is the coding for the final project only worth 5% while the research and presentation is worth 26%. Is this a bio class!? For CMPT 120 which is suppose to be a somewhat easy introductory course to python this guy has made it HARD. If you are about to take this course with this guy be warned it's not straight up coding like most.
his lecture notes sometimes are unclear, and it's hilarious that the final project is related to biology (only 10% of cs)... overall, he is a nice guy
VERY HARD assignments/exams, but VERY INTERESTING guy!
Great prof. Responds to emails like lightning. The subject isn't all that easy though.

way his class is designed.. people who are getting higher marks will keep getting even higher because most of the bonus are impossible for normal people. He is a great guy though.. very helpful when you ask him questions and he's only like 23! You really learn in his class. I really hope he scales the grades though.. that would make him my fav prof
Really hard final exam. Have some pity on students - After all they are human beings. I beg you to scale the grade marks.
Very nice prof...extremely hard, but teaches a lot. Expects a lot from students. Overall, my grades are low, but he is very likeable.
Pretty tough syllabus but he does give lots of quizzes so you have a chance at pulling your grades up.

Overall, he is a good prof and teaches well in the class. He is very helpful and is available for extra office hours. Assignments and midterm was hard. Well, I hope he was going to curve the final grade of the class b/c barcharts show that around half of the class is failing the midterm, and I am wondering how they are going to get a grade of C & <

he was good for being a TA, but as an instructor, he might be tough (or very tough) for some students. Very helpful tho. MT and quiz are hard...need to write lots of explanation ... maybe i can't get used to his teaching style, coz he makes me really hate comp sci :(

His labs and assignments are extremely long, and often confusing. Don't worry thou, for he answers all your questions almost instantly if you email him, and he gives tons of examples and explainations to help you understand. He gives in-class quizzes to help you boost mark. Lecture notes were awesome. (animated!) And he has a great sense of humor.

tough but fair. this class requires lots of hard work and the assignments demand a lot. but really whats the point in going to university if there is no challenge. This prof tells it like it is. Very thorough in his explanations and provides helpful feedback. Overall good prof.

He is extremely helpful, with quick email responses and easy access in person (he is always at his office). he is also quite clear, always with diagrams. The guy himself is awesome. His tests, labs, and assignments are hard, but fair. If you have trouble, just talk to him to get the problems sorted out.

Assignments and labs are vigorous and extremely challenging for the student comin out of CMPT125. He replies quickly w lot of info when u email him. Mt was HARD, and instead of scaling, he gives HARD inclass quizzes that lets u raise ur mark if you get all the questions correct. No review classes to help w/ MT. lab attendance not mandatory.

Really great teacher with lots of examples. Class is never too dull. Would take any other class from him, even if wasn't all that interested in the subject because of how he can make boring stuff more interesting.
Great availability (e-mail, extra appointments, ...). Willing to help. Assignments and labs are a bit long but challenging. Lots of feedback!
Hate this guy! Won't scale even though most of the class failed the midterm.. Horrible prof, inconsiderate of student?s opinion.
She is not very organized and assignments are very hard. Avoid if you can.
Is this course important? That is a YES YES! Is she teaching this course like it is important? That is a NO NO!

Basically reads off from slides and no additional explanation is given. Has a thick Indian accent that may be hard to understand for some people. Not very open to feedback when her performance is clearly poor and lacks coherence. However, it is still possible to score higher than a B in her class if you try hard enough. (MEMORIZE EVERY BIT)

In the beginning it seems hard and challenging, but after attending few week it is fine. The way to succeed is to pay attention to her notes and prepare for the quizzes and exams. She gives fair chance to improve and learn. She is a very focused on her work even then she talks about Super Bowl in the class. (which is awesome)
She makes you learn about the topics.

Her midterm is lack of organize. She breaks our reading break with a too much long INDIVIDUAL assignment. PS. This course is basically about TEAM WORK. For the first two weeks, we even didn't get notes. She is more like a TA not an instructor.

Unprepared, knowledgeable, stubborn and does not take criticism well. It took her a week to put together something resembling a course webpage. Terrible prof to have for such an important course. Avoid her at all costs.

So far, this instructor has been extremely unprepared. She doesn't have a website to keep students updated, she doesn't fill in notes, her explanations are unclear, the first assignment is a vague, understandable mess and her English is atrocious. The announcement of the first assignment, word for word says: "It has ben uploaded".
Very strong accent that makes it difficult to understand new material in lectures. She also doesn't fill in the lecture slides, and seems very unprepared to teach in general

Extraordinarily difficult to understand. It's not her fault that her accent is bad and she has bad English, but it bothers me very very much, as it directly affects my education. Assignment specifications are impossible to understand, and she doesn't know how to explain things. Incredibly frustrating class.

Very long midterms, i thought the final was alright. he often makes mistakes on his lectures slides which he doesn't notice or bother to correct unless if someone calls it out. He doesn't post the completed lecture slides so make sure u go to lecture. He's often difficult to understand, writing is messy, and doesn't pace his lectures well.

Midterm was so easy but do not get fooled the final was insane, more of theory based rather than solving, understand the concepts fully before going in esp the material that was before the midterm. Overall lectures were so boring and he yaps a lot, just look at the slides. He is really sweet though would recommend going to his office hours :)

He's a nice and funny guy, but his teaching style really depends on you as a student. Some like it, others hate it. Tests were reasonable except the 2nd midterm on derivatives, way too many questions for 50min but he did remove 4 points off to boost us. 2025 Fall class avg, C-. Do not take if you can't teach yourself stuff. AND GO TO LECTURES!!!

Avoid Ladislav if you cannot teach yourself stuff. Midterms and final were difficult compared to the practice questions we were given, and he does not curve, even though the averages were low. I personally will avoid it in the future.
After seeing reviews on here I was scared to a course with him but he was a pretty good prof. Midterms and final were fair and based on assignments & lecture slides and he's a nice and funny guy.

If you are taking Math 154 with this prof, wait for a different prof. Not accessible outside of class and won't answer any questions regarding exams. Exams are much more difficult than what was practiced. He is a computer science prof, not a math prof, and gave coding as homework for first-year life science calc. Overall, not a good prof.
Why no grade scale Ladislav 😢😢😢 Funny guy, fix the screen next time 👍
teaching was horrible and unorganized, tests did not reflect lectures and written assignments were a waste of time. He is funny but hard to understand. He is a nice person though.
ladislav if ur seeing this make the final easy please i beg you

Ladislav is the goat. He's very funny but CANNOT teach at all. He doesn't even make his own midterms or assignments, he steals them from other professors. First and third midterm were easy but the SECOND MIDTERM? Horrible. As long as you practice doing the questions done in the lecture, you will be good.
If you can, don't take math154 with him. He doesn't post the lecture slides after the lecture. He rushes through the concepts. He has a thick accent so understanding him is also really difficult.

He struggles to simplify the concepts enough for a first time learner, so it can be really daunting to sit in class and learn what integrals are without supplementing with YouTube. The nice thing though is that the tests are quite straightforward, pretty much exactly the practice problems just with different variables.

not the best teacher, but not the worst. he's okay. just kinda reads off the slides without explaining the material. you'll have to rely heavily on self-study + the pre-lecture videos to understand the material. if math isn't your strong suit, another professor may be better suited for you.

Don't take Math 154 with Ladislov. He rushes through new topics every class, doesn't review, and ignores requests to slow down. Midterms are unfair (7 multi-part questions in 50 minutes) and the averages are soo low (51%). I had to teach myself everything because his explanations were so unclear. He's a smart person but an awful prof.

Ladislav has a thick accent and goes through his lectures too fast skipping over steps and making you do ti in your head. first midterm was okay but second was terrible, he does not give enough time during his tests. he gives a lot of homework. usually the WA are similar to the midterms. you will need to teach yourself everything.

Show up to lectures because without doing that, tests would be even harder than they are. Very little time for tests for the amount of work required and they are strict on grading. The two assignments due each week only line up with class content 1/2 the time and Matlab is required on weekly assignments but never taught in class.
He doesn't post the problems he solves in class and speeds through the slides.
His biggest problem is that he makes his tests way too long.

Not finished course yet (at ~82% rn). notes are hard to follow, hard to read/miswritten. he has a thick accent (Czech? idk) got me really confused during lecture. lot of his written hw and test questions are weirdly worded, guaranteed to lose marks. uses matlab for hw but never taught how to use. would not recommend only if u learned calc in hs.
You won't be able to understand a word he says.
You won't understand what he is saying.
Ladislav is the goat

I didn't like paying for Iclicker and webassign, but he did give Amazon gift cards to some students that did Hypatia. I liked his energy. Really helps if you took Calculus 12, because it can be hard to understand at this pace. To study just do practice problems

he always messed up in lecture but was funny. first and last midterm were okay but second was very hard. don't even get me started on how difficult that final was, thankfully it was only 25% so lock in for the midterms but yeah doesn't know how to teach. nice guy though
Often very hard to follow his lectures. He's funny but sometimes hard to understand. Exams always felt rushed because he would make them wayyy to long. He seemed nice though...

Dr. Stacho was a mid prof. Lectures were hard to follow. Midterms were based on assignments but were only an hour, and I found that there wasn't enough time to finish them. The final was very difficult. The only things that helped me pass were the Discord server and past exams, which were not provided by Stacho but I found online.

ladislav was hilarious ! he messed up a lot in class but would clarify. despite going to every lecture, i barely paid attention and taught myself outside of class. material isn't hard, attendance isn't mandatory. he's easily approachable and if u do bad on the mt there's around 30% worth of assignments which are easy 100%s. really easy class !

Stacho is a funny guy but not the best prof. His final was quite difficult, midterms were reasonable and mostly based off of the written assignments. His explanations often didn't make sense and he would have trouble pacing his lectures. I would recommend going the the math lab for extra help. Overall he is decent, not terrible and not great.

Great guy and seemed to care about students. Math isn't my best class, and sometimes his teaching was confusing and I easily got lost during his face paced lectures, but there are lots of opportunities to get help in the Math workshop. Overall would take a class with him again.
A pleasure
Very good prof, caring and easy to talk to. Lectures clear and fun. Recommend.

He was a decent math prof compared to the rest. His lectures were confusing in the beginning but I got a hang of them. His exams are decently fair as well. They are based mainly on his lectures and some online homework basic concepts. If you review the material well and understand it, you will do well even without touching the textbook.
dont fall behind in material and do the homework; the exams are mostly word problems so practice that

He's an alright prof, his lectures were understandable but not engaging- perhaps the energy given by the class is what he gave back as well?As a procrastinator, I highly suggest you review each lecture example after class and try doing them without looking at the answers; if you don't understand any part the TA's do a great job explaining them.
Honestly if I were to describe in him 1 word it would be mid. Because he is exactly that. Not great but not horrible. Lectures are alright and tests aren't to bad either.
Professor Stacho is a very clear and concise professor. His lectures were enjoyable and he cares about his students, you can tell.

He is one of the best profs I have ever had. His clarity and knowledge of graph theory was obviously immaculate but the way he brought up profs and explained interesting problems was incredible. This will probably get banned but sometimes I would legit feel aroused after class because of how exciting the lectures were.

I reccomend Prof,Stacho to anyone even if you are not so good at math. He has a clear purpose in lectures and that is to make you understand what the concepts are. He doesnt rush through the notes like many other profs. I respect him and his method of teaching. He even gives a few tips to study, they come in handy! Put in work and you will do good.

He is a real star! Linear algebra is a tough course, but Ladislav did everything to clearly explain all course material. Exams and tests were strongly connected to homework assignments and after all everyone received mark according to the work during the term! Strongly recommend him!

Ladislav does an admirable job at teaching a very difficult course. He explains things clearly, although his accent is a little hard to understand sometimes. He also goes quite quickly in lecture so you really have to focus. Midterms and final were difficult which is to be expected because the course itself is tough. Lots of concepts to remember.

I loved his class! Super funny and teaches really well! Really nice prof and tests are really fair as well.. his reviews were very helpful before the midterms and final. He's much better than what he's actually rated as...

I was reluctant to take calc with him from his ratings but after taking math with him, he has become the best prof I've ever had at SFU! His notes are clear, and his lectures are very informative and helpful! He teaches this course excellently and makes great jokes! If you put in the work, you should get a good grade in this class, and he scales!

He hates calc, but doesn't everyone? Dude's hilarious but not for his "jokes" which weren't really jokes most of the time. Accent wasn't bad, English is fine, but he said things really weird sometimes. Extremely fair exams, easy Instructor Questions. Better prof than people make him out to be. :) Super helpful if you go to his office hours. Scales.

I read these reviews before starting the course and was kind of worried but he is very clear in lecture, and provides clear notes and explanations. He does have a strong accent but it is not hard to understand, and he is always trying to get the class laughing and engaged. If you do the assignments that he assigns you'll do fine on the exams.

Don't believe the hype, his English is perfect, ain't nothing wrong with it. As far as his teaching is considered, he is crystal clear. Also, don't forget to attend tutorials, they are essential for getting an A

Bit of an accent but otherwise good. Sometimes unclear due to breadth of materials that have to be covered, and makes exams that are quite fair. Course is generally difficult due to the amount of proofs/theorems/formulas but applying them is quite straightforward.

He's a prof with good intentions. You can tell he tries to make sure you get the material but the notes he was using (not his) forced him to rush the lecture, every single time. You will need to study the notes and textbook to pass. He loves to use proofs so you're pretty much taking MATH240 as well. Tests are hard and expect to memorize proofs.

His lectures are clear and easy to follow. He does include some of the more simple proofs on his exams, but his calculation questions are not that difficult. Study his notes carefully and do the textbook problems in order to do well in this course.

Ladislav is a great lecturer and goes through everything clearly. However, his tests doesn't reflect what the course is actually supposed to be. He loves testing you with proofs because he originally teaches Macm 201. Math 240 is the course for proofs, not 232. To pass, you need to practice questions beyond what he even gives you.

Ladislav is a funny guy and teaches well. He teaches out of the book, but he explains his steps on the way and tries to give intuitions before starting. His exams are totally fair and were all questions closely related to the homework or proofs right out of class. He's a top-notch math lecturer.

Stacho is hilarious. There are plenty of bad reviews with regards to his English, but these must be old, I am guessing he has improved, as in this class his English was good and was never a problem. He is a good professor if you are just starting math, he breaks downs proofs of theorems and teaches you the intuition in thinking and using them.
Took his class a while back. I liked him. His accent is Slovak, not Russian, which was not too hard to understand. Overall, not bad. Super nice guy.

I would say the course itself is hard. His video lecture in class is useless. You won't get it. Listen to his lectures at home and take some good time to digest them. By the way, my mid term mark was boosted 20%+ , after scaling.

Dr. Stacho lacks clarity to a non-professional level. I would however say that he himself understands that, that is why he provides the class with pre-made videos. He is willing to help. The workshop is very useful. He has fairly easy midterms and exam was super easy. Questions are similar to homework. I liked him.

His accent is hilarious but it might take you a while to get used to it. His midterms were very similar to homework questions and examples he did in class, but the averages were still terrible. However he will scale the final mark. If he gives you a sample final, actually go over it!!! a few questions on our final were the exact same.
His exams are not hard, and he pulled the curve up after the final exam

at first he was really hard to understand. once you get by that, he's hilarious in class and is decent at explaining things (not the best). His exams are straight from the homework assignments and notes so study that stuff and you'll be good!
