Definitely recommended for MACM 201, as his exams are consistently easier than the sample ones, minus most graph theory questions. He also scales everyone up after an exam.

I thought stacho was a great prof! Once you got past his accent hes a good at explaining things. I would recommend his class. His exams are straight up from the homework and notes so i don't understand what people are complaining about. If you study right and pay attention during class, you will do fine.

stop crying people. I failed the 2nd midterm and the final and I still got a C+ in math 154 with stacho. I would recommend him for others just not at the time of 8:30am. His jokes are so funny times. LIke the time he said anyone have questions and held up that stick LOL

Stop blaming Stacho for his teaching methods! At least %80 of final exam questions was straight from his lecture notes and weekly assignment. He even scaled at the end. good times at high school is over guys. build a proper schedule, and study hard if you want good grades. Be competitive and don't blame others.

Very boring class; if you've taken calculus in high school, it will be painful to sit through his lectures (I skipped the last 3 weeks). If you can learn from the textbook, do it, and use the calc workshop for support. Your peers and the TA's will be the best help. Stacho though truly is a nice guy, he's just not the best teacher.

Hes not a good professor for someone who wants to succeed. He doesnt care for students success, he wants to do his job and leave asap. He skips steps in his notes, which defeats the purpose of going to class, and notes are copied from the textbook. I went to Nadia's class and learned one concept in 10 mins that would take me 2 weeks in his class.
He is a nice guy and tries to teach, but he reads off the slides which are directly from the text. Goes through difficult examples really really fast rather than explaining the ideas behind them.

Stacho's AWESOME! His exams are a cakewalk. Seriously, easiest prof ever. Questions are usually straight out of the textbook, or from examples. His communication could use some improvement, but he clearly tries hard to ensure everyone is learning. Good job Stacho :)

He is a nice guy, but his class is very boring. His overheads are straight from the text and his explanations aren't so clear. However, his midterms are pretty easy and straight forward, just do all his assignments and you'll do more than fine. His final was a lot tougher though, he ask too many specific difficult questions. But overall he is ok.

All his notes are based entirely off the textbook... i think the course could have been easier if he offered a different viewpoint from the text. If learning from the text is your thing, youll do fine. Hes a nice guy otherwise, and he tries hard.

if u can read the book and learn from the book then you will do well in the course. stacho is not good at teaching. he skips alot of steps when he solves a problem and makes a mistake at times so it makes it hard to follow. i would suggest taking 154 with nadia nosrati. she is stachos student. most people from stachos class attend nadias

Honestly, not the best prof in the world. Not because of his accent, not because he's a jerk (because I really think he is a nice man, and can be quite funny at times!), but because he tends to go through his examples way too quickly, without actually teaching them. Just remember to clarify your answers on all assignments, midterms, and the final!

I love him to pieces! He is very straight forward, yes does read off the notes, but embellishes very well. His amazing Russian accent only adds to the class, yeah? The exams were very fair, and would recommend him for any student.

Watch out people. This prof is really bad. Study the assignments, practice questions. Stacho rambles on and on in class, and doesn't know what he's saying. He makes so many mistakes in his calculations, and the second midterm we got had a FAIL average. Wonder what the final exam average will be...

This is the worst prof of my whole entire existence! I do not like to hate on people, but he is just a dissaster and a half! Once again... worst prof ever!!! He doesn't explain anything, just reads what it says on the notes. His exams were extremely hard and were not relavent to what he said to study or what he taught.The class average was a fail!

At first I thought he was an alright prof. His notes are somewhat useless and he skips important examples. The first midterm was alright, but his second midterm killed the class. It was all on concept and there was only 2 questions with a total of 10 on a 40 question test that were actually from the assignments.
Extremely hard to understand. Overhead notes are impossible to read if you dont sit in the front of the 400+ person class.
Oh my gosh. I love him. He is so adorable, I love his accent, it's so adorable, and I love how he makes jokes sometimes. Just study (like any other class) and you'll do well. :)

This is the first time that he teaches analysis, overall well done, his accent is somehow confusing,but not a big deal since we are already familiar with the material.Exams are so easy, the avg is like 70%. An easy A+ for me!
His accent makes it hard to understand the lectures at times. The midterms and final are definitely on the difficult side.
*Very* dedicated and enthusiastic prof, made the material fascinating and challenging and brought it alive. His accent didn't interfere, just added to the fun. Great prof for this course.
It takes a little while to understand his accent for a few words but it is bad. Took me about two weeks to understand him completly.
most interesting accent i've ever heard. nice person but... i think his accent is harder to understand than the course material.

His english is both unclear and hard to make out what he is saying. His assignments are alright, but mid-terms and the final exams are a little too tough to do in the allocated time. His lecture notes are ok; has some examples to guide you along.

yeah ... i think he should learn to pronouce his English before teaching ... I highly recommand him that he should find a pronoucation teacher to correct his pronouciation!!! the midterms are ok .. but the final is super hard!!!

Easy midterms, ridiculously hard final. With scaling it was possible to have 45% and get a solid C. Scaling was more for lower marks (2 letter grades) and less for higher marks (a single letter grade). Easy to pass, hard to get a high mark.
His notes are nothing like his test questions. And extremely hard final exam.
his notes are hard to follow especially when you come to prepare for exams. he should practice more on his english and simplify his speech
Yah.. he needs to learn English before teaching math (or any other courses). And his handwritting needs to improve too, somehow I can't read what he writes for most of the time. ><"
He's a nice guy, but he needs to know how to teach before being a professor.. Even one of the TA teaches better than him.. geezz!

He should learn english before teaching math...It should be manditory to take english before teaching...jeez. Examples:sin-sinus, cosin-cosinus,etc. determine-deetermine,subtract-substract,calculus-calcouluus,comparison-compilation, and the list goes on..
good lecturer but i always sleep in his class. i should have got a better grade if i didn't sleep.
not good
Boring lectures, nothing over what I'd find in the book. Stopped showing up after awhile.
Once you get used to accent and notes, he's aiight. Funny stare and Russian accent. Vvrraight?!
Just read the book and don't go on his assignments, exams are way harder.
It's pointless to go to his class... you learn NOTHING .... just read the book

The lectures were pretty good; he explained concepts very well. The assignment requires you to do a bit of research on your own. Quizzes are hard due to not enough time and a strict marking scheme. Midterm and Exam are easier because it's basically a longer quiz with reused assignments and quiz questions.

Information is kind of just thrown at you and not much practice. Many assignment questions covering concepts not discussed in lecture... meant to "inspire you to do your own research". Quizzes are random bits and pieces from lecture. Shervin's nice but you're pretty much on your own; anticipate this being a memorization course.
Good professor

The course was well-structured and the professor was kind and helpful. However, the TA support was lacking—especially Jeril, who often deducted marks unfairly on quizzes and the project. Clearer grading guidelines and more consistent evaluation would have improved the experience.
Great lectures, difficult quizzes, fair assignments and examinations, very good professor to take for 371

His lectures are great and I definitely learned a lot. He posts lecture recordings too which is great. There is a socket programming group project. Can't recommend him for CMPT 272, but for 371, he is your guy.
he gave me A+ so im happy w it

Lecutres were amazing, I simply attended them to ask more questions about the subject. He does not rush through them and takes his time to make sure everyone's on the same page. Exams were very fair, the group project was fun to build. Goated prof overall.

Night and day from 272. Shervin was extremely knowledgeable in this class, and as a result I learned the most in a single CS course so far. Invites questions and spends time re-explaining course concepts when needed. Quizzes are tough, but the exams are more than fair. If you pay attention and work hard, it will be quite rewarding.

Dont know why reviews are so low for 371, the only thing difficult in this course is the first few weeks and the quizzes since they are a time crunch. Otherwise hes a very good prof and is very knowledgeable for this course, workload not high and exams are very fair.

I once was a Shervin hater. I had failed CMPT 272 and never mastered the ability to create HTML forms and fix 2006 era websites, however Shervin swooped in and changed me with his love of Networking and incredible explanations of TCP congestion control. Packets may be lost, but my love for Shervin will never be lost again.
only advice : RUNNNNNNNNNNNN
Good lectures, and records them too which is nice.
Horrible marking.
Good at lecturing, overall a good professor.
Had to drop, He is not that great.
BADDDDD PROFFFFFFF

His lectures were pretty informative and useful. I learnt a lot about html/css/js/react. Very unclear grading criterias for assignments and tests. The assignments were overly tedious (remake a healthcare form in html without css) and tests were way to long for the time given. You really need to be able to code fast to do good in this class.

This was Shervin's first time teaching the class, and it showed in the lack of organization. Too much time was spent on HTML/CSS, leaving only 2 weeks for JavaScript/React. Assignments were okay, but exams were impossible to finish on time. He asked for a long HTML form with unclear instructions, which he hadn't even completed himself.

Shervin's alright himself, but I think if he teaches this again he should reconsider his testing strategy, and put some more thought into the questions he asks. Unacceptable for such a "distinguised prof", I mean take a look at this guys personal website... you kidding me ???

Lectures was audio recorded, quizzes on canvas. The quizzes could be time constraining at times. Homework was not of the best quality (eg. creating forms with html). Project was not difficult, you could do it on your own in 2-3 days. The midterm and final was bad, basically a homework assignment for each question. TA's marked generously on these.

Shervin, in his own words, "decided [he] could take teach this course, as how hard can web dev be?" I think he was very underprepared to teach this course, as he often struggled to see errors in his code. Course content was unbalanced; one of the assignments was just copy and paste for 3h. As a lecturer he's a bit dry but ok, so I'll see for 371.

I get that web dev is not his area, but wow, Shervin's version of this course is a waste of money. He spends way too much time on the extremely easy basics and only does 2 weeks of actual frontend frameworks. Assignments are random and useless and he crams an impossible amount of questions into the exams. Take with Bobby or just do W3C tutorials.

The lectures were kinda dry, and the slides were minimal. Assignment instructions were vague (and the solutions sucks). Exams (midterm, final, quizzes) were very tight on time. If you know the material well, you wouldn't get a bad grade. Otherwise, be prepared to retake because he does this Midterm + Final weighted average thingy
Never wrote a rmp review til this guy, learned nothing throughout the class and prof refused to dispute any final exam remarkings. Just watch a web dev tutorial instead of taking this class with him.

Bad lecturer and very badly timed tests. Questions on midterm and final were assignment level. Assignments were fine, quizzes are medium level, you need 50% average between midterm and final to pass, which is not optimal for a web dev course so even if you did well on everything, if you do slightly bad on the final, you fail. Never take with him.

Final was not what it was described as on canvas/lecture and was incredibly difficult to do time wise since questions were nearly on the level of some assignments. Since a 50% is needed on the combined midterm and final to pass there were people who did well on assignments, quizzes and projects but still unfairly failed due to the bad final exam.
Good luck to those doing his exams. In person but on canvas... fail if you dont average 50%

Shervin isn't the worst professor I've had, but passing his class comes entirely down to how well you can perform on exams. If you are somebody who needs a little extra time when you code you will fail his exams. I had a very good assignment, quiz, and project average but failed due to the final because I could not finish in the timeframe.

He is absolutely not a bad prof, for 371, he is one of the best and chillest. 272 isn't his forte so he didn't gauge correctly the exam final load so it ends up being a mess, this is his first time teaching web dev after all. In case u do take 272 with him, just watch crash course and his final should be a litteral accumulative of his assignment.

He does not deserve this rating..hard final does not mean the bad teaching. It was not even that hard, average is over 65. If you could do all the assignments without using AI, final was doable. Don't blame prof for not putting enough effort. We are all here to learn something, not to ask for high grade.

Prof. Shirmohammadi is not a great lecturer, and has very time-constraining tests. His final exam included making a very long form in only html, which ultimately was too time consuming to finish along with the other tasks of making some small webapps. If you can, take the class with someone else.

Avoid if you can. Assignments and lectures are actually OK, but quizzes, and especially exams, are atrocious. Bro expects us to complete 4 long coding questions in 2.5 hours, each of them almost a full assignment in length. Given that exams are worth over 50% and are required to pass, do not take if you want to save your GPA.

Not the best prof for web development, I'm not sure why he opted to teach this course when he's new to the topic himself. Assignments are redundant, often makes you complete trivial things like building a long html table form. His assignment criteria and requirements also changes but he won't inform you. The final was filled with long tough q's.

I am currently writing this review bald. As I sat through the final exam, it felt like my hair was slowly disappearing, one strand at a time. He somehow managed to forget to install NodeJS in class and was watching an Intro to React tutorial on YouTube instead. University of Ottawa, please reconsider taking him back...

Just plain awful. If you are planning to take this course with Shervin, get ready for a stressful semester. Assignments and quizzes are easy. But the exams are absolutely brutal and disorganized. They don't test your critical thinking skills, instead you have to copy paste text from a form for half the exam. Avoid at all costs!

He's not as bad as people say. Not great, not bad. the worst part was that there was barely enough time to complete exams, so you're always on the edge of your toes. Other than that, shervy is not bad.
Ill be honest, it's a great course! I didnt attend most classes as the slides and recorded lecture audio was great. Quizes are easy
This dude just yaps I swear, I think he knows web dev but dude does not care at all. He wants that sweet tenure.

Only reason I did well was because I already learned everything, literally learned nothing throughout the entire class. Half the time he's struggling to use visual studio code, like your a computer science professor???

The professor's lack of preparation during lectures and the vague assignment instructions only added to the frustration. The final exam was especially brutal, requiring us to write a super long HTML form, which was unreasonable and left most classmates unable to finish.
This professor was so hard, he doesn't teach web dev at all and is super lazy. Take with a different teacher, he literally does nothing and makes impossible tests.

I had a very challenging experience with this professor. The midterm and final exams were filled with exceptionally tedious assignment questions, making them nearly impossible to complete on time. Assignments and group projects were boring and didn't contribute meaningfully to learning.

No doubt this man is very smart, however he is not a very good teacher for web dev. If you are experienced in web dev you can get by, otherwise his exams will put you in the dirt. best to take this class with a different teacher
Exams were not fair in the amount of work to do and the time given. He needs to be trialed for the crime against student mental health. -100 aura to all Mohammads
Just watch a tutorial on YouTube.

Midterm and final were absolutely horrible, questions were exceptionally drawn out and tedious making them near impossible to complete on time. The assignments and group project were pretty boring. Just avoid the course with Shervin. Overall killed much of my enjoyment of web development.

I had a very challenging experience with this professor. The exams were filled with tedious assignment questions, which weren't particularly difficult but were extremely time-consuming, leaving most students unable to finish. The assignments felt unnecessarily drawn out and didn't contribute meaningfully to our learning.

The exams for this course are heavily focused on brain rot coding questions, and while documentation is provided, it's not enough to make up for the challenging nature of the tests. I would recommend considering other options, or just not doing the course- didn't learn much at all.
Horrible teacher, exams are pure coding questions granted we get documentation to look at. DO NOT TAKE
FInal Exam was totally unfair...
This guys exams are so stupid. He puts tedious assignment questions on the midterm and final that aren't even hard, but they take forever to do so no one is finishing. Tedious and useless assignments

I've never given a RMP rating since this guy. Avoid at all costs, the midterm + final are impossible to finish. One question on the finally needed minimum 300 lines of code. Makes tedious questions and just not a good prof overall. When asked how to contact your group he said there has to be some way to (pretty much figure it out yourself).

What the hell was that final? Just came out, everybody who wrote it agreed that it was super unfair. 4 massive written questions, absolutely no mercy from Shervin that was brutal. If you love HTML forms, that was for you. Else, if you hate tedious tasks- AVOID.
That final was so brutal, making us write a super long HTML form for no reason. It was unreasonable; most classmates couldn't complete it on time.

This dude gave us such a hard final and made us recreate dumb forms with HTML that required 700 lines of code. AVOID, PLEASE; He was so hard for this course that it is not supposed to be hard. During lecture, he is also never prepared.

His assignments are so hard and his tests, I used to love doing web development but he has actively made me hate doing web development. Even if your interested in web development, I beg you: please don't take!

Terrible professor/class, midterm was very hard, as barely anybody even finished the midterm and was unrealistic. Assignments are also terrible, with vague instructions and you learn nothing at all in lectures- have to learn everything on your own. AVOID!! Professor has no clue what he's doing!!

Extremely vague assignment instructions and bad exams. The test questions themselves are easy enough, but far too little time to complete them, especially the coding questions. Lectures are not that useful, you're better off learning on your own. TAs were helpful and nice though.

The professor talks a lot in lectures, goes over the material properly. So the assignments and quizzes are not hard, but be prepared to spent a hours writing the repetitive stuff and learning nothing new. The midterm was not hard but there was no way anyone could have finished on time.

The midterm was awful and terrible, gave 45 minutes to do 5 mcq and 2 long written questions. Alone, the 2 written questions would've taken like 30-40 minutes. Lectures are also terrible, doesn't really explain anything and his slides really aren't related to tests. Avoid at all cost, he will tank your grade.

Assignments are the most boring and unhelpful I've done in any course. They aren't hard they're just a bunch of copying an pasting, nothing to learn, no interesting challenges, just write some html and css and copy and paste for 5 hours. Lectures were fine but other than that terrible experience. Quizzes were also way too short. Avoid if you can.

No doubt the professor gives very good lectures But honestly do not take the class with him if youre trying to score a good grade. his assignments are never from the lectures, completely different questions than what you study in class. he does not give you any practice questions according to what he sets the exams. Brought my gpa down. AVOID HIM!!

The quizzes/exams would sometimes have questions based on information provided IN LECTURES (prerecorded lectures are not sufficient) with often a time crunch. Assignments are fairly difficult and you would need to contact TA or read the text for more information on it. Choose your partners wisely for project as it could be a huge time sink. GL!

A remarkable professor, he listens attentively, excels in teaching, and sets demanding exams. The course mandates external reading, fostering deep understanding and broadening students' knowledge. Exams challenge cognition with unique questions.

Amazing professor, clear and easy to understand explanations. Has a good sense of humor which makes lectures easier to get through. Quizzes/homework/exams are difficult, but you learn a lot from his class if you put in the work.
Insanely hard prof, puts unrelated content on all the quizzes and the final exam. Try to avoid at all costs!!!!
my question about this class is that what he was exactly thinking when making those assignments and quizzes.
The prof himself gives good lectures, but quizzes were extremely unfair. Assignments were OK, but there is nothing provided in class or materials that prepare you for them. Midterm was ok.

Lectures only cover basic concepts, but his assignments are completely unrelated and will make you bang your head against the wall for hours and hours. Quizzes are extremely difficult and have a time crunch as well. This course has assignment, quizzes, a project, a midterm and a final.

Assignments are not related at all to whatever his lectures. High course load, the course has quizzes, midterm, assignments, project, and a final well basically everything ... his lecture notes are empty as desert. Avoid him at all costs
SIMPLY THE GOAT. KOBE IN THE 4th. CR7 IN THE CHAMPIONS LEAGUE. IEEE FELLOW GOAT.

I had a great time with him. His lectures are very clear and to the point, and has great explanations and answers to all questions we ask him. Quizzes and exams are fair. The assignments are quite hard since the assignments are different from the lecture topics but you can read the textbook and find the information from there. Highly recommended.

Professor Shervin somehow managed to make an incredibly boring class manageable. The quizzes, assignments, and group projects are fair and there is no final exam. 475 with Shervin is hands-down the easiest class I have taken at SFU so far.

Hard quizzes, but very easy marking. Lots of writing assignments and intentionally vague, but easy marking too. Very chill prof with unproctored online quizzes. 2% bonus marks, and drops lowest quiz grade. Knows we're taking the course for 400-level credits and taking other courses. Deadlines announced at beginning of term. Can choose own group.

Super nice guy and tries to make your life easy. Watching lectures and a quick review of lecture notes before quizzes are enough to get 100%. The assignments and group project are (intentionally) confusing so make sure to go to office hours- he reveals a lot of information that honestly should be in the instructions.

For assignments and projects, make sure you follow the criteria that he sets out in lectures. Honestly, this class is a easy A, got this grade for the minimal amount of effort put in. Though, one note is that the Shervin reuses old lectures (they're good but would be nice to interact with him/questions). No coding involved, just lots of BS writing.

Shervin knows his stuff really well. Any questions you may have will be answered in detail with examples and definitely help your understanding. But the content was very heavy, with lots of technical definitions. The final project was multiple 40 page papers exactly to technical specifications that were not even fully marked. Good prof, bad topic.

Prof. Shirmohammadi really knows his stuff. Explains the content well and answers questions thoroughly. Teaches applications of the content in the workforce. The course was easy, no coding required. Listen carefully to the lectures and you will do great. Haven't gotten an A+ for a course in a long time.

He is a really good prof who you will feel helpful to learn from. His assignments and tutorial questions are really helpful. If you know how to do those two things you will at least get a B grade for sure. Furthermore, his lecture is closely related to his exams.

...see I noticed he kept giving away bonuses etc..I was worried if it's "too easy" to get high marks an A+ may end up being set really high.....he said "ur email was useful" but in the end guess what..my fear was realized and A+ WAS SET AT 100%....I had 99!! Anyway hes pretty bad, unclear, goes offtrack, brags abt how fast he wrote a prog..horrible

Amin's teaching was horrible the first few weeks but later he did improve. The assignments were difficult but it just needed ALOT of time to do like all cmpt courses. If ppl took the time to visit his office hours, he offered great advice and insight on ur code. If the assignments were easy, every1 would be getting 100% The midterm/final were EASY
forces students to go to class by deciding to have a pop quiz half way through the semester, and teaches material that is not on the course page nor textbook. Nobody attends his class for a reason.

His classes are totally useless, less than half the class attend his lectures regularly. I, along with many other people used our textbook as the only reference to do the assignments and study. The times that i've attended his lecture, he seemed to be afraid or unsure of what he was teaching and alot of the time he teaches useless stuff.

Amin does not have the ability to teach. He is extremely nervous, and unsure. He always asks the class how he should teach. We are 3 weeks into the course and he has not really taught us anything at all. All his instructions are extremely confusing. I am very disappointed in the computing science department at SFU for such low teaching standards.

He says he's fair and a true gem. He tries to be funny but you won't laugh when you see your grade. If you don't have the exact expected output be ready to get 0. It's pass or fail. Quoting him "excels in hardship". Be ready to be forged by the trial by FIRE.

One of the best. SFU is lucky to have him. Clear assignments. Quizzes based on slides. Go to class & you get hints. Pay attention & ace the quizzes. Scott is funny. Great personality. Ask him questions, he loves to help. 10/10 prof. Would take a class with him again, 100%.

Best so far. He's 100% into lectures and goes on a tangent to explain any question you ask . Funny, high energy, and practically begs you to come to his office hours. This guy is a machine. You can email him 24/7 for a reply within 5 minutes, and he creates entire program frameworks overnight from scratch for simple assignments. Huge Industry exp.

Awesome professor. He was very enthusiastic during lecturers, you can tell he has a passion for what he does. He is great at helping his students and is willing to take time out of his busy schedule to meet with his students. 10/10 would recommend.

Scott is the man! His lectures are informative and his tests are super clear. The assignments can get a little tough but he's always there for you if you need help. Office hours are also really great, and he's really fast on emails. He's really passionate about CS and was nice enough to move my exam to another day since I had a conflict )
The nicest cmpt professor I have ever met, and he does care a lot about his student. I really recommend to go his office hour as often as you can!

Very nice, very enthusiastic, and very helpful. He goes quite fast during lectures (it is helpful to read the textbook before the lecture) but he does repeat the most difficult concepts several times to make sure everyone understands. He gives lots and lots of hints and genuinely wants everyone in the class to succeed. An excellent prof overall.

Scott is amazing! I've never had a prof as helpful as hime in SFU. One time he literally stayed in CSIL from 4 till 9ish to help out with every student. Got tons of hints on exams and assignments. Also would reach out to students for extra help. If you could, take Java with him.

He always gives hint probably every class. He always emails you back if you have a question. During the assignments he'll email at least 5 emails giving you hints and if you show up in lecture he'll practically give you the answer. Notes are so in dept that you don't even need the book and he's always in CSIL if you wanna ask him questions.

Given it was his first term lecturing he was pretty successful. Mostly read off the slides. Encouraged questions during class, but would scoff at people who asked simple questions, pretty demeaning sometimes. The midterm and quizzes were fairly simple, and he would often tell us beforehand what specific questions to expect.

Very enthusiastic, his lectures are interesting as he shares his experiences in the field to the class. Really nice guy and helps students as much as he can. He understands that not every student has the same back ground in computer programming and throws out many hints for those less experienced. Amazing professor, looking forward to more classes!

Nice guy, super clear, and super helpful. I thought the class was surprisingly difficult so be prepared to work hard, I did well because of prior Java experience. His exams are challenging but he gives lots of hints as to their content during lectures.

Awesome professor who is very enthusiastic in lecture and keeps it interesting with the occasional joke. He also gives hints on assignments and exams during lecture so it's a good idea to go. Very respondent to emails and will stay after hours to help you on assignments. He made CMPT 125 very easy and I highly recommend him.

Not the most clear instructor, but puts his lecture slides online which helps. Very respondent to emails, and helpful in office hours. He also gives "hints" as to what will be on the exam and more or less tells you how to do the projects. I recommend him!

He doesn't have any office hours for this course, so reaching him is difficult. The course is a famous CS188 UCB course, and he is not familiar with the materials. Better lectures are available on Youtube for the identical course. However, CS188 itself is a pretty good course, so the assignments that comes with it is good practice.
The assignments and exams are very easy, and he gives you a lot of practice material to succeed. His lectures aren't great, but just watch the CS188 Berkeley lectures and you'll do fine.
Sometimes questions were unclear for the homework, but in general he was generous with grading and the examinations were relevant to the material.
The lectures were not the best but there were good alternatives online. Grading was also pretty lenient and fair.

Administration in this course was the worst I've seen in my four years as an undergrad. Two weeks wasted due to snow day and midterm lead to content overload towards the end, and this is the first final where I had no clue what to write. Did not show up to proctor his own midterm, and showed up late to his own final. Actions show a lack of care.

He and his TAs don't answer questions on Piazza often, but when they do it's only partial information. The last month and a half was extremely rushed and surface-level. Overall very disappointing quality teaching. You can use CS188 UC Berkeley material at home to get a good grade. All content is taken from there, assignments, exams, everything.

The professor seems to have left every responsibility to the TAs and the University of Berkeley. The material is taught poorly, nobody answers questions in piazza, and the sample exams have the same question copy-pasted 5 times and little resemblance to the actual exams. Can't believe I paid for the exact free online course with added stress, shame

Most Prof usually try their best to simplify complex and abstract concepts to spark students' interest and encourage active thinking. However, this Prof made the simple course extremely complicated, making me feel disgusted with the topic of AI, which I used to be very interested in. Hang Ma is not suitable for teaching a introductory courses!

RUN! The professor gave a very easy sample exam and a very, very hard real exam. You can't get any helpful information from the sample exam. The question type, number of questions, and difficulty of the questions have nothing to do with your actual exam. The level of teaching is completely out of proportion to the difficulty.

This professor doesn't know how to teach a course. The teaching in the first two months was extremely slow, but in the last month, we almost covered 3/4 of the entire semester's content. There's less than a week left until the final exam, but we still don't even know the scope of the exam! He never sends any emails or announcements to students!

Hang Ma is one of the most lazy and unaware profs I ever had. He doesn't respond to any emails/piazza posts from students and unaware of incorrect grading from TAs. The whole course is copied from UC Berkleys CS188 class, where its the same slides, assignments, and even tests. Basically, you're paying for a course that's online on the internet.

Avoid this professor and his TA! He is really overrated!! His attitude towards teaching is very negative, many uncertainties in this course. The lecture notes and assignments are completely copied from UC Berkeley CS188 (thanks UCB) without any modification. I cannot understand I paid tuition for public resources that can be fully searched online.

I am OK with using good materials for teaching. But the experience of taking this course was so bad, the TA made me feel I am a guilty person. Although many students didn't cheat, they still got 0 for assignments (the number of people who got 0 halved in a few days). This prof leaves everything to TA, and the course is really lack of transparency.

SFU should investigate this course. Why are all his lecture materials and assignments copied from other university, with no punish for him? TA frequently sends threatening emails to students, claim they have a software to check cheating, but many of the accusations are unjustified. Taking this course has caused me a lot of fear and mental stress!!!

His exams are excessively difficult! Before the exam, the TA provide a sample midterm, but the difficulty level of the sample questions was relatively easy. However, the actual exam was 100 times harder than the sample midterm! Many people thought this was a relatively easy exam based on sample exam, but the actual exam was extremely hard!

Please avoid this professor and his TA! His TA accuses students of cheating without any direct evidence. So far, more than 50 people have received 0 for assignment 1 and assignment 2 due to cheating accusations! We have around 130 students taking this course, and it is not possible for each person to invent a different algorithm.

The professor does not care about students AT ALL. Never get contact with him, Never reply to emails, Even not show up on midterm day. Never met a professor like this. All the announcements come from TAs, even when 33 out of 110 people get 0 in their homework, still the TA sent out the email. Don't know what's wrong with the professor's email0

This whole course including the assignments is copied from UC Berkeley CA188, not a single word was modified. Professor just read the slides, the original UCB lecture on youtube are way better than this professor's lecture. 1/3 of people get 0 for one assignment because there are similarities in some of their functions.

The course material is very enjoyable (tho copied from UC berkley's introduction to AI slide by slide) and I do enjoy the lectures, but I was one of MANY accused of cheating on an assignment worth 10% because my solution was similar to another students by chance. The algorithm isn't complicated and it's very easy to have similar implementations.

On some sort of mission to find academic dishonesty when it isn't there. Accused 33 students of AD when they wrote code based on the pseudocode in the slides. Complete disconnect between prof & TAs, both of whom say contradicting information about the course. Midterm is nothing like the assignments or sample exam. Avoid at all costs!!

Okay, this professor is overrated. All his slides are not his own.. If we did this with our homework, it would be considered plagiarism and would send us to academic dishonesty. However, when a professor does it, nothing happens. This professor didn't give us practice problems prior to the midterm and go through them with us.

The entire course has been ripped from UC Berkeley. Prof just reads from the UCB slides. Prof & TAs are not on the same page. A week beforehand, TAs tell us that the midterm exam is on the first two lectures and then a couple days later tell us its on all lectures. No practice or homework apart from coding assignments. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!
He taight the lecture well, very considerate and patient to each student, and we learned a lot from his classes.

Lectures were a bit dry but conveyed the material clearly. Assignments, quizzes, and exams were well-designed, instructive, and fair. The professor gave good feedback and was accessible outside of class.
The class consists of interesting topics and projects. Although the midterm had been move to the final, all the stuff had been tested from lecture notes. Have a good experience learning with him.
The topic is interesting, but really hard to learn by myself. The uncertainty before the final exam is large.
He should be a perfect research prof but really needs to learn how to teach.

Hang is a respected professor. He gives excellent feedbacks for our presentation and the final report. You will definitely learn something from him if you spend enough time for his slides or lectures.

The exam is all based on his lectures, you will be good if you study for all his slides. His assignment is very interesting. assignment description is very clear. He is the best professor in SFU CMPT courses!!!:)
The class is totally just reading the PowerPoint, and he did not even give an example, the worst thing is that the class is all recordings no interaction at all!!!!!!

None of the slides are from the same source, so notation/format changes from slide to slide. Often they have little to no information on them. Or just repeat information. Absolutely no idea what he was talking about most of the time. I could genuinely feel the lack of effort/care put into teaching this.

His slides are taken from his academic collaborators from other universities, and so is the homework and exam. The assignment was a single project, which was marked generously. The mandatory group project asked us to extend and improve the assignment solution based on his own research results. So he's very knowledgeable about the topic.
I got A in the course, so I thought I did well. Now I'm taking CMPT 125 and I understand that I am completely unprepared. Easy A but learned nothing. Thanks a lot!

He deserves all 5 stars. He clearly knows what he's talking about, he's so good at answering questions, he's a huge expert in his field, AND he's super generous and gracious to students. He clearly wants the best for all of us! And he cares about real impacts of AI. Wish he started teaching at SFU earlier so I could've taken more courses with him!

0 experience with anything programming/compsci but he made things incredibly easy. his lectures are fast paced and can be a bit confusing, but its mostly recap of the readings. he spends time going over questions in detail, and notices when theres a lull. the midterms were similar to the final, and he gave a lot of hints for the project. the goat!!

the course is very easy. maybe it's ok for someone who never programmed, but for me the course was a total waste of time, and the prof doesn't even try to make it challenging for the students who have previous experience. i had more difficult homeworks and exams in grade 11. just waste of time and money

Nicholas is a very good teacher for people with little to no coding experience. He is very clear in his lectures and spends time explaining the smallest things. The practice material he gave is very good and highly similar to the actual midterms and final. A very nice and good teacher. Would highly recommend

The amount of practice material he gives is really helpful. His lectures can sometimes be boring, but they still taught me a lot. Highly recommended if you don't have any previous coding experiences (like me). He makes the course easy to grasp. I can tell that he's passionate when teaching!
hot but slightly complicated.
he's the best prof and hot.
Amazing professor, the way he teaches makes the class easy
He's super helpful and tries to make sure he clearly explains concepts. His assignments and tests and easy.

One of the easiest classes I've ever taken. The amount of practice material he gives you is incredible and prepares you so well for the midterms and finals. He's overall a super nice guy and always quick to respond to any questions you have

He talks very fast, but records all lectures with video and audio. He's super helpful and easy to reach on Discord or by email. For the course, keep up with the weekly readings (which isn't boring and easy to comprehend), his lectures make more sense if you've done them, and going in without reading makes it harder to follow. Super Nice Guy !

Nicholas is the goat. This is a must take course for anybody interested in AI research especially with respect to HCAI & data centric AI. Good blend of practical and theory with a mix of readings (research papers), coding (ML), and a final project. Nick is also very tech savvy so he is easy to reach online unlike other profs. 100% would recommend.
practice midterms were very helpful and similar to the exams. goated prof and might i add VERY pleasant to look at (marry me).

prof vincent is a super passionate and energetic guy who genuinely loves what he teaches. super good explainer + gives access to LOTS of resources to prepare for exams. speaking of which, they are usually pretty easy. if you can take a course with prof vincent, take it.

Nothing needs to work on much. Only spent 30 mins per week for the class. If you already took highschool cmpt course, attending class would be meaningless. Got high grade cause the class itself is pretty easy, but Vincent, though hes kind and very helpful on email but honestly, his lecture is boring and pretty much no one seems paying attention.
Amazing professor. Super kind, passionate about what hes doing, and gives great feedback. Exams are fair and he provides plenty of practice material that helps you.
Saved my life
hes a great prof!!
Inspiring very understanding and easy to follow pace

LOVE him. he is the best prof out thr. no one else can compare, brain, parsa, no one else is better than him. he was my first computer prof. and i think he is the only reason, i was able to continue this degree. I feel like if it was any one else, i would have changed majors. Lowkey sad tho, caz i thought every prof was gonna be like this :(

Professor Vincent was excellent and helpful in and out of class. He would offer extra office hours and help everyone. He also allowed us to have cheat sheets for the final, which no other class had. I would take any class he's teaching.

This is for CMPT 415. He was extremely kind and consistently helpful when you didn't understand things. Very flexible and especially understanding during exam season. Overall, a great professor. If you see this I love you Nick.

Amazing prof - so chill, I'm convinced he meditates before every lecture. Homework and exams? Easier than picking a Netflix show (okay, maybe not that easy, but close). Learned a lot without crying - a rare feat. If I say ‘I approve,' you know it's legit. Take this course with him, or forever regret your life choices. 10/10 would enroll again.

He is amazing! His first test was hard but then he noticed it so made the final easier. We have many coding assignments but are straightforward, and he is always there to help inside and outside of class. He also allowed to have a cheat sheet for the final which is amazing. Honestly do not hesitate to take a class with him.
he is the best prof I've ever had

Dr. Nicholas Vincent is an amazing prof. His lectures are great but not necessaty and he posts slides and records lectures - although for fall 2024 only audio of lectures was availabile due to technical limitations. Assignment instructions and rubrics are clear and fair, and is very accesibile. Dr. Nicholas Vincent is great and I would take again.

Nick is an amazing professor. He is super caring, he responds very quickly to emails, hes got a good canvas page thats easy to understand and has great office hours. He also give students practice questions that are very helpful for the exams. He is by far the best professor I have had. He is also very smexy.

A great professor for introducing new comers to computer science! Explained at a level where everyone was able to understand and actually incorporated meaningful feedback from previous terms. Assignments and quizzes were well designed to help in learning course content. I pray I could have professors like this for higher level courses.

GOATed prof giving amazing lectures and reasonable tests. His email response time was unbelievably fast. He went out of his way to make us a website so we could practice for the final exam. I am so glad I took his class, probably the best, kindest, and most caring CMPT professor at SFU.

Nick is a really really great prof. He is super caring and clearly cares about his students success in the course, he asks for feedback on a lot of the projects and course materials to improve upon for future courses as well. He's also a really good lecturer and Id happily take a course with him as a professor again any time

Boy oh boy where do I even begin. Dr. Nicholas Vincent..honey, my pookie bear. I have loved you ever since I first laid eyes on you. I would do anything for you. I wish it were possible to freeze time so I would never have to leave your side. I would sacrifice my own life it were the only thing that could put a smile on your beautiful face.

Nick has amazing lectures and genuinely wants his students to succeed. He provides so many resources and takes any feedback from students to do well in the course. Super quick email response time as well.

Simply the GOAT prof. Lays everything out for you and describes everything that will be on tests. Responds to emails immediately, and genuinely wants to help you. Provides a variety of resources and tools to help aid your learning.

Nick makes amazing lectures and is so passionate about cs. He is willing to help students in whatever way he can to succeed and provides so many resources and ways to learn and review. highly recommend

Dr Vincent is one of the best profs I've ever had, not to mention he is a HOT HOT HOTTIE. There isn't a single reason I wouldn't recommend this man('s class). Also he has a deep love for memes and AI. He's amazing I wish I could take this class over and over again forever. I love computer science :D
He is a really nice and kind professor

Dr. Vincent was a really good prof. Although he is new, he is pretty well-organized and you can see that he is truly interested in the topic that he teaches. He is very good at answering questions and actually cares about his students. One of the best profs I've had.
Best prof I have taken so far. He even codes with you during class. Very available and helpful office hours. Also pretty clear and concise lectures

I took CMPT 419 Special Topics in AI with Nick in Spring 2024. One of the best profs in CMPT. He studies and teaches his own research so everything in the class was quite relevant to where computer science is headed. You won't learn too much technical work but he connects technical matters with concepts. Not a hard grader but fair in general.

Took CMPT 419. Easily the best prof in SFU by far. Lectures aren't mandatory but are engaging and interesting. He's extremely passionate about what he teaches and readings are up to date, the oldest being a paper from 2021. He tries to know what students are interested in to include relevant content into the course. Extremely friendly and kind.
The best prof I ever met. He is very open to change teaching style if students ask. The assignments' instructions were clear and easy to follow.

lectures are delivered enthusiastically and in a way that engages the class. all topics are explained intuitively and clearly. all lectures are recorded and easily accessible. assignments and tests contained only material that was taught and there were no surprises. everything was marked fairly generously. amazing prof and course, would recommend
great prof, super helpful and accommodating.

Great proffesor! Prioritized students with frequent office hours and quick email responses as well as offered lots of extra material that was not mandatory but very helpful. He speaks fast but you can always ask him to clarify but other than that lectures were easy to follow and were helpful. Wish I could rate this professor higher he was amazing.

Nick Vincent was a good prof overall. I had zero coding experience and struggled heavily in this course at first. Through hard work, I was able to earn an A-. Exams were fair. Coding assignments can be a bit challenging, final project was a slight test of my patience. Decent experience overall.
Great prof.
i was very scared going into this class but it was actually very manageable. he responds to messages quickly and went above and beyond to provide us with extra resources and help. best prof

Nick is such a caring prof and really does all he can to help students succeed. During his office hours, he was able to help explain why I ran into so many problems or roadblocks during assignments. Lectures are recorded and slides are posted in-advance, so there is no need to rush copying down code. Exams are fair and cheat-sheets are allowed.

He is a very caring person and genuinely wants to see everyone succeed. He had a lot of sympathy since he realized this is a course taken mostly by students in their first semester and contionusly tried to help everyone succeed.
SUPER GREAT PROF! 10/10 would recommend. open to grade appeals and replied very quickly to email. very fair marker and seems like he genuinely wants the best for all his students
best cs prof ever!
He was a nice fellow.

Prof Vincent was a great teacher. As someone who has had no experience in coding i was able to get an A- and understand all the topics. He is very understanding and helpful. The final was a bit tough but overall the material was easy to understand even for a beginner and the class was well paced. I am glad I had him as my prof
Great prof, great communication with students, the course itself is pretty simple but he makes it more convenient for students. Would take him again 100%
By far the best professor I have met this term. I just messed up my final; otherwise, all my grades were good. Getting an A- to A grade in his class is very much possible.
W prof
Great prof. Enjoyed CMPT120 with him this sem. If you already know a bit of coding, this class will be a breeze.

Very caring and always willing to help and answer questions. Friendly and approachable, since it was his first year teaching there were a couple things that were disorganized but VERY open to feedback which was great!
a very sweet teacher who cares for his students. I will say the final was a bit tough but everything else was easy. Glad i got to have him as my prof this semester

everyone ik vibes with prof vincent. he cares a lot about students and if the material is understood and always asks for input on what to improve. he is very considerate and dedicates lots of time to helping out the class and making material easier to understand. i looked forward to lectures as they were always engaging and taught at a good pace.
He was the BEST prof I ever had. the lectures were clear.
Really good CS prof, both for people who haven't and have programmed before. Midterm was easy and final was fair.
my goat
super chill guy and knows his stuff, attend lectures or watch recordings and enjoy the class
The best professor has excellent industry experience unmatched by any other staff. Unfortunately, he's leaving. I was happy that he was my professor.
BEST PROF
VERY NICE

lecture is easy to understand but the quizzes are hard and exams are harder, but he is very nice and helpful if you have questions and struggles with the course. Don't try to email him, he won't reply. you have to go to his office.
never ever take any class from that guy. No pattern of teaching. starts another topic in between the class which is completely irrelevant. Poor style of teaching.
Excellent professor. Slightly disorganized but makes himself very available in office hours. Crystal clear in lectures, highly recommend him!
I got no programming background whatsoever but I thought he was crystal clear!! and very approachable! Great lecturer and super helpful. Glad I took his class! =]

Excellent lecturer, but a poor professor. He conveys his knowledge well, but possesses poor organizational skills. I would still recommend taking a class with him though as he is very merciful on exams.

This man should not be a teacher. I've learned more from Code Academy than I have from him. The TA's were equally as useless and downright rude. Good luck sending any emails to him or the TAs - you won't hear back in a reasonable timeframe. Avoid at all cost.

took the 120 course. first two labs labeled with 125/126, no course schedule with dates on syllabus, no course website, instructor unresponsive to emails, material and course in general feels disconnected, disorganized, and thrown together.
Nice guy, but AWFUL at explaining things. Totally awful. Will avoid him for part 2 of the programming course.
Lectures were very simple but the assignments were hard and very time consuming. Exams were fine.
Easy introductory to java course. Prof was OK.

Very nice prof. Always helpful, however I did not go to the lectures, and either half the class dropped out or many other people stopped going as well. The book used in class is all I needed. The assignments and midterms were fair. Some times it wasn't clear what was being asked on the assignments, though.
Doesn't teach much in lecture, and what he does teach can be learned in much less time by reading the textbook or finding tutorials online. Seems like a nice guy but doesn't know how to teach.

Very nice prof. The lectures given are pretty clear and helpful. As an international student, my English is terrible. But he was still very patient listening and explaining to me if u have any problem. So greatful to him.
He is really helpful and a nice prof. Going through all the examples help you succeed.
Great Prof !
He is a very nice guy, very helpful if you go to his office hour. He is willing to give examples and explain to you. Not many assignment but they are time consuming, prepare them as early as you can
very good lectures,but when it comes to assignments,which are somehow much more harder than it was taught during the class,it is not so good anymore. +sometimes late for classes
he is a great prof, so patient and very kind to students
helpful and nice, willing to help, though some time late for the class. He is good anyway.
No Comments

He doesn't understand deep learning that much and he pretends that he knows it. This is terrible in my opinion. I would like to say buying a course on Udemy or Coursera is better than learning with him. Assignments are not very related to what he is teaching. The quiz is a nightmare, requiring you to read the outdated textbook.

Professor Oliver is very enjoyable to listen to even though the course content is dry and boring. His exams are multiple-choice heavy so if you don't like those then avoid taking this class with him. Otherwise, he is a great 320 teacher.

I really appreciated Dr. Schulte's enthusiasm for the material. He tried to make lectures interesting by showing different types of media. The grading criteria were a little unclear but the assignments were interesting and not that difficult.
Nice, warm, informal, error-prone, academically doubtable, 90% too easy and 10% strikingly difficult material

Dr. Schulte is one of the best lecturers I've had so far at SFU! He made this course very informative and interesting. The assignments were a little bit difficult but Oliver was always happy to help. Exams were not that hard but still required to understand the principles. The only problem was the book because it was SOO boring and overcomplicated!
Oliver is a good choice for those want to learn and enjoy the lecture. The midterm and final might be difficult or easy depends on how did you follow his schedule.
Makes assignments way too easy, so you have an 80 percent average at the end and get a B- because of the curve. Didn't really learn anything useful in this class.

Oliver Schulte is the worst professor I've had at SFU. I had him for CMPT 310 as well as 354 and both courses were unstructured, uninformed gong shows. Everything you learn will be by your own effort; do not rely on this man to teach you anything. Assignments convoluted. Exams he allows cheat sheets. Too easy. Curved hard. Material worthless.

Good lecturer, reasonable pace, and fair assignments + exams. Databases are important and this class was a really good introduction to them. One thing you have to watch out for is that this class is pretty easy, so the average marks are quite high ( low 80's to high 70's) so he scales down the class. Stay on top of everything to get a good mark.
The class average was near 90% ?!!! He scaled down the class really hard. If you just want to pass this course, take it with him. Otherwise, avoid him.

I understood his lectures, but couldn't really get into them. Sparse, not very useful powerpoints. Had to do some reading in the textbook to really get it. It's overall a pretty easy class. Should have put in more effort, but there was no fear to motivate me. If you have to take 354 with him, don't worry, but try to get someone else if you can.

Just like a comment on 882 below this course was ridiculous. 30% of it he repeated stuff from the machine learning course from an other professor, 70% simply used free resource available online and let students to learn them on their own! Can't even correct mistakes made by students . Had difficulty calculating derivatives of one-variable function.

I don't blame the lack of knowledge on what he teaches but he is dishonest. He DO NOT HAVE CompSci BACKGROUND but always pretends he knows much about it. He wrote on his website that he graduated in computer science from UT but he was actually Cognitive Science majored. In addition, he did his master and Phd in Philosophy Dept at CMU! Not CompSci!

I took his machine learning course. He was using the material from one of the other professors from the department. He did not understand the material himself; therefore the lectures were mostly there to confuse you. I only learned through reading the textbook; watching online courses. To be fair, he tries very hard; he is just not a good teacher!

Awesome prof, nothing but good things. Assignments are extremely long and easy to mess up. If you put in the time, it prepares you for the final + midterm. The problem is time (some can take up to 20 hours). Very nice and tries really hard for students to learn - SFU could take a hint from him.

He is one of the best teachers I had in this school. In order to convey the meaning of the concepts he teaches, he uses both intuition and technical explanations. He enriches the content of his course with demos and videos, which I found very helpful. He has a talent of making abstract or difficult concepts look simple and easy to understand.

Great Prof!! Highly recommend taking CMPT 354 with him! His lecture slides are crystal clear, he does an excellent job of explaining concepts, lots of examples, etc. He had a great marking scheme; if you do better on the final then the midterm (no matter what the marks are), the final will replace the midterm mark! So impressed!!

This guy is really funny, his actions are just funny. The assignments were long but not too hard. His midterm was okay, but the final.... EKKK yeah not so easy after all.. Overall, the course is interesting.

the whole class was getting B+, and I was getting an A before the final. but i was so disappointed he made the final like 70% harder than other midterms... but good thing is, his lectures are clear, organized with slides, funny, love to let u participate and a good prof. the best of it is you somehow you feel ur learning instead of feeling sleepy.

Great, helpful, funny and accommodating prof! Print out slides and take notes in class, they'll help later. Allows cheat sheets on exams. Assignments are long but you learn a lot and doing them will basically prep your the midterm. Take it with him if you can; I certainly learned a lot + it's easy so far!

A good guy, very willing to help. But if you really want to learn something, you need to do a lot by yourself. His lectures are too straight forward. FYI 31/85 students got more than 90% on MT. assignments are long but not hard. If Database not your concentration go with him you will be fine with your grade. While if not, consider about it.

Good prof who cares. Material wasn't always the most sparkling stuff but he attempts to make lectures interesting. As already mentioned, his assignments are long but really ingrain the material. Grading was fair. Final exam was about right, no surprises.

Great professor, is very willing to help in office hrs. Exams are fair and so are assignments. They're tedious but helpful in learning the material. He allowed cheat sheets for the midterm/final! Really good professor, attend the lectures you'll learn more that way than from the book. Actually cares about his students, its refreshing to see.

His teaching skill is below average,on the other hand, he is pretty laid-back.My biggest complain is that, the fnal is A LOT harder than the midterm,while he said in class the dificulty level should be similar.
He's sloppy and disorganize in his presentations. He cannot answer if anyone has a question. His grading is unfair. To be concise he is awful.

Very determined to teach & has genuine concern for students. He is happy when no one is lagging behind. His lectures were fun & the assignments I did were very practical. If you really want to learn, you can combine the lectures and textbook(naturally).The problem is that students dont like to read. He is one of the best and inspiring profs ever.

Googled some of his lecture notes and found that they were stolen with his name plastered onto them instead. Teaching ability is pretty average, but his assignments are ambiguous and he falls behind the lecture schedule. He obviously doesn't do the assignments first because the second one had an impossible question that later had to be removed.

This got to be a joke of a prof. He always "doesn't want to give too much detail on the assignments' requirements", yet expects you to guess the right solution. Granted the TA for this course is a total failure, but the prof doesn't make the situation any better. Tests are unfair since he likes to test on things that has "no solution". Avoid!
THE WORST PROF EVER

His assignments were exactly the assignments that Dr. Greg Mori used for his Machine Learning course twice. So almost everyone had already the correct solutions. Therefore by coping some words and diagrams everybody was happy. Shame for him and his cheating students.

He set 30% (or 40%) for assignments. And there were just two sets of assignments. The questions were absolutely nonsense and funny. He himself even didn't know what his questions meant. He was totally unable to teach.

The most ridiculous course and professor. In the graduate level, at Game Theory, the whole time he was just practicing games like prisoners dilemma, chicken and Rock-paper-scissors, . . . and gave chocolates to the winners or volunteers!! Such a picnic!!

Part of his test is made up of useless details but he allows a cheat sheet for his tests so take notes when reading the book so your cheatsheet will be ready when exam comes along. The midterm is supposed to take 2 hours(or so he says) but everyone finished before 25 minutes.
pretty interesting course. not much covered, and it's pretty easy too if you know the basics. the e-commerce stuff was barely tested on the final though...
No Comments
Very nice guy. Tries hard to make the lectures clear and easy to follow. The assignments really help with the exams. The exams covered stuff from the book that wasnt in the lectures - watch out.

He's a sincerely nice guy who will help out tremendously in office hours... However, his tests seem stupid, he seems to test the unimportant things. That said, his course is pretty easy, just don't expect to learn any of the material in depth.

TL;DR: Shawn is a great lecturer and an even greater person. You'll learn lots +100. Took this course Spring 2017. Shawn is very approachable. Lectures were well organized and presented. He understood how to communicate in a way that most people would understand and related topics back to relevant industry topics, making his teachings invaluable.

RECOMMEND! He did not make us get a textbook for the class. Assignments were straightforward and fair. Marking was fair. He made lectures interesting and worth attending. Main component was a group project, so the strength of your group will determine your mark. Note: He was an industry specialist with an Bachelor's degree, not a usual professor.
good

This prof makes CMPT 320 more of a phil 101 course then a com science course. the final was ridiculous, asking very detail and useless information from his very unorganized notes,like "in which year, xyz said such such such?", I mean, who cares?!it's a computer science course!not a history course. do urself a favor and avoid him

This is exactly the same as any liberal arts course. It was not a bad course but it is mandatory. He doesn't post anything online, so go to class and take all his notes. Study those and remember everything. His essay was easy. I want to take CMPT 418 with him. Although he is an old hippy with a PHD in Philosophy and has left wing ideals.

He doesn't post notes on website because then nobody would come. Well of course not, it's a completely useless class (but mandatory). As previously said, his notes are very disorganized and his exams are for very specific details. e.g. "Which of the following was the author of xyz theory?" If possible find someone else who teaches this course.

Worst teacher i ever had. Notes totally disorganized. Writes so fast that you cant even catch up. Very confusing, and he expects you to have Very specific details on a book that put me to sleep. If you need to take cmpt 320. More work but WAY better teacher.

Taught useless PHIL 100 material that added no value. Had no problems reminding the class of his political opinions - I don't necessarily disagree with him, but it got old quite fast. Ridiculous final, 30 minutes long, required NO thought - just remembering absolutely USELESS facts ie MC what was the % decline of factory workers in 1999? Who CARES?

He's a good prof for this class. His teaching style is what anyone in liberal arts courses might be used to so a bit different from CS, but it's an easy writing course. Just write properly and in English and it's at least a B- from what I remember, especially since there's no specifically right answer always.

Excellent prof for the course. Fair midterms and finals. Expect lots of discussion and be prepared to actually understand the material and not to memorize notes, but if you're up for some connectionism, great course and great prof
No Comments

This is what a cognitive science course should be! This prof is all about connectionism and this is his pet course, and I think it is where he shines. Expect lots of discussion, less implementation. Students with a real interest in AI, cogsci, and/or neural nets will love it, the rest will hate it. Don't whine because you get challenged.
he won't give you a sample midterm or final, he believes the reason why other profs give sample midterms is so that they can get higher evaluation from students! seriously what a stupid excuse.

pretty easy course, but he made it extra difficult with bad lecture notes, and ambigeous assignment where most of the time was spent wadding through e-mails correcting errors and explanations. Then he goes and makes some lame extension of the final project a couple days before it was due.

Not a nice guy, he chewed someone out in class and told them not to come back because they were "apparently" making a distracting face while he was teaching? (Unprofessional?) Project was unbelievably hard and seemed to have little or no direction. Tested material presented in lectures though.

This was the only truely cognitive science course at SFU. The custom courseware was great. Spent a good bit of time dealing with the philosophical issues. Not recommended for typical CMPT students who dream of a cubical with 8 computers to program on
Course Materials were not too difficult. Like others said, instructions on course project were main sent in email...very frustrating trying to go over each of those emails...
My god, the course project was so hard. The course material was fairly straightforward though. He seems to make easy concepts more difficult than they should be.

Never available for extra help. His office hours are useless since he doesn't really answer any of your questions. And if you have time conflicts with his office hour, then too bad, you'll have to ask him stuff through email coz he does not make apptment.
He is not available when needed and he sends instructions for assignments via email and expects you to know every detail of every one. Very unclear lecture notes.
"Write an essay in one paragraph or less..." - midterm question. Genuinely interested in course; tends to spend much longer per subject than necessary.
No Comments

He just tried to write as much as he can on overhead every class. I don't think this is the right way to teach. No question print out for Project. The info of project are on 20 e-mails. You have to go through every -mails carefully
Nice guy - he's clearly interested in the material. His lectures are good (not the best speaker, but makes good notes), but his assignments are very confusing.
He has a good heart, but he needs some teaching skills.

Hadley knew his stuff, and tried to share some of it, but was unsuccessful. He is very disorganized and does not provide clear assignment documents, preferring to send 50 vague emails instead. Midterm avg = 54%, 45% on midterm = instant course fail.

This is a Machine Learning course, but it is mostly heavy math. I was expecting more on the coding side, but there was none. The course is not helpful at all when now I am looking for ML job I have 0 coding knowledge! The final did not make any sense and was not related to its final materials.

His teaching is lackluster. At the start, over 200 students attended; by the end, only about 50 remained. He relies heavily on mental math and moves too fast for students to follow. His favorite phrase is “hopefully it makes sense” before moving on. In the end, he became Santa Claus and was generous with grades.

I hate him! The course is academically dense but practically hollow. It substitutes deep mathematical derivation for actual conceptual understanding, resulting in a curriculum that is disconnected from modern ML engineering.
Extremely difficult final; avoid at all costs!

Mo is highly intelligent and knowledgeable. He sometimes makes one mistake and that is assuming everyone has the same level of intelligence as him. Therefore, the level of his courses might be to tough for some people at SFU. But he is an amazing and kind person. It was a pleasure for me to get to know and work and take courses with him.
Avoid this class.
Awful professor. He made the final exam so challenging and didn't bother to scale up the course...
The lectures are not helpful. He reads off from the slides, without explaining why or how. Avoid this class.
Mo's homework often requires readings and material not explicitly taught in class. This is further exacerbated by the lack of a requirement of STAT 270 and MATH 251.
Avoid him, he can't teach machine learning at all.. the lectures are waste of time
The course itself may not be that interesting (lots of math) but Professor Chen explains everything well and clearly. Also, he is patient during the office hour.

He explained mathematic in a very clear way and this course is really useful if you'd like to take the other 700-level courses, e.g. NLP, CG. However, this course is heavy of maths and includes lots of quizzes. If you are not confident in math skill, I would suggest to audit instead of enrolment. Good luck for those also interested in ML. :)
Awful
He shows pre-recorded lectures in which he dispassionately reads math off slides. Does not explain tough concepts properly. Will never take it again.

I suspect that the quizzes are made by TAs. The questions are unclear and usually have mistakes, which are sources of disputes and arguments. Hope that professor can spend more time to improve the quizzes and logistics of the course, so that students won't feel confused with no timely clarifications. The course is easy if you are good at math.

This has got to be the worst course I have ever taken in my university career. I would estimate that the professor spent less than 2 hours per week of effort. All assignments, quizzes, and even the final exam were made by the TAs, Mo did nothing - that's how lazy he is. All were littered with typos, errors, and unclear questions. Absolutely awful.

Kind, decent lecturer; toxic course environment. Professor/course staff largely inaccessible with inconsistent/confusing office hours schedule, and largely ignored Piazza. Unclear homeworks weren't always connected to course material, quizzes were unclear/imprecise and usually had incorrect answer keys, and grades were released late/never.

CMPT419 is really hard. I've done ML, now I'm in Robotic Autonomy. Mo knows what he is doing and doesn't dumb down the material. I can see classes like this making me get paid more in the future, thats why I took it, while a lot of people dropped. The class is hard! high risk, high reward. My only issue is that questions on piazza got ignored

The lectures were really boring but he knew what he was teaching, and the notes are good. But the assignments were really hard + weekly, so there was a large workload every week. The TAs marked the assignments, and it was a huge mess, wouldve liked this class more if not for that. The final exam was really hard with not enough time to finish.

Even though Mo knows his stuff, he's a really tough prof. He mostly reads off the slides, which don't even help when you look back at them, and leads to the assignments being impossible toDo. Literally EVERYONE failed the midterms and final exams, to where I think he threw the final exams away. I mostly slept in his class due to his monotone voice.
Good Pro.

This is probably one of the hardest classes i have taken at SFU. Assignments were very hard and time consuming and grades would not be given back until a month or two later, and when they were there was no good feedback on what went wrong with the code. Lecture slides wont make sense unless you attend class and tests were VERY hard.
Damn this was a hard class. Assignments were difficult and tests were pretty hard. Not for the faint of heart but if you can properly understand all materials you can do somewhat well.

Mo Chen CMPT 125 Spring 2020 was a bit of a mess for me. Not only were the assignments brutally difficult ( for example a missing space will cause you to get the whole question wrong) but the tests were also incredibly difficult and had material much more difficult than in lectures slide and oddly worded test questions.

Entire class is a joke. Abysmally long time to get final grades, assignment feedback, or exams. His teaching style will be hard for lots of people and hard to understand. Exams will be hard and have failing averages <50%. I advise not to take this course with this prof if you want to actually have a quality learning experience.

Final was extremely difficult and shouldn't be expected out of a CMPT125 student. The allotted time for the final was 2hrs for the exam, 1 hr for submission but the exam still probably failed 50% of students. Mo Chen is good at lecturing, but the TAs are severely lacking in communication and feedback. Assignments are hit/miss as are the exams

The final is CRAZY HARD, he asked us to implement some bst functions but the functions take no parameter (you cannot use root to initialize your code) so I have zero clues what to program.
