run while u can

Good person with zero skills in teaching. Assignments and labs were hard and intricate, while exams were a little bit complicatedf. The course is boring by itself, but Golnar makes every lecture the most boring event on the Earth.

To do the labs you have to understand her weird logic, taking you 40 minutes, and then write 5 lines of code in the right place The assignments were brutal, as not only you had to understand her logic, but also make your code compatible with hers The exams were ridiculous, as she only said "All questions have equal probability to be on the exam"

Yes, she's a new professor with a few hiccups along the way, but she always fixed them and seemed to really care about the class. Final class avg was B-, and a reasonable grade distribution, so read the emotional reviews here with a grain of salt. If you paid attention in class and read the text book, the tutorials and assignments were good.

Final is unreasonable. Too many coding question, I even don't know whether TAs and prof could give grade fair. There's too many codes to read and everyone's answer are completely different. The assignments are super hard. we usually spent over 20 hours in lab to finish one assignment. However, Prof is very nice and friendly in office hour.

Final exam had 5-6 C++ coding questions and it was super hard and very unrelated to what we've been studying. Not related to labs or assignments or lectures. They were just random coding questions that you cannot prepare for.

Apparently people say it's her first time teaching. She's improved over the semester, but she's still got many problems. Her assignments are difficult in both completing them, and understanding what she wants. The final exam was much harder than her midterms. She likes to use Microsoft Paint or a sheet of paper at a 45 degree angle for messy notes.

You'll get used to it but that's not necessarily a good thing. I felt like she went easy on us this semester since she was pretty new. Anyone after us should expect to be decently challenged, since the course content isn't all that hard you guy'll probably do fine. Put a little effort in to the course and you could pass with a rock teaching you.
4 or 5 written code for Final Exam.

She is not good, her lectures are not useful for learning stuffs and those are just copy of textbook, there is few thing we can learn from the materials of her lecture. In her mind, she thinks almost everyone of us are really good at c++, actually we are not. Her assignments are heavy. Try to avoid her course!
not even once
Avoid her...

CMPT 225 is basic C++ and Golnar teaches and tests nothing more. I dont understand why everybody on here has given her a bad review. Labs done in 5 lines of code? gives out completed notes? openly answering questions in and out of class? extra practice questions before the midterm? WHAT MORE COULD YOU ASK FOR?
horrible.

I transferred over, so im not sure whether I just dont know enough, but judging from what everybody else is saying, Golnar expects the class to already know how to do everything in C++, leaving assignements and labs vague, and having hard to follow lectures, constantly drawing over things in mspaint which makes no sense.

Golnar isn't the best at delivering material to a class, she sometimes asks questions on why or when we need to do something, which is very helpful. During office hours she is very nice and approachable, she always helps us lead to the answer. Very accessible through emails. You have to work really hard in this class.

Started off unprepared during lectures and giving no details on assignments/labs, but has greatly improved as the semester continued. First midterm was easy compared to past courses with a 74% average.

She is easily accessible during office hours, can be funny during lectures and is nice when you ask her questions but everything else about her teaching style is terrible. Her Labs and Assignments make absolutely no sense and appear to be written by a child. It is physically impossible to learn anything from her lectures. Still a really easy class.

Cons :( - Convoluted lectures that often cover information not used elsewhere in course - Lectures lack substance. Most slides included towers of code - Assignments/labs poorly explained and very time consuming - Drawn explanations of concepts impossible to decipher Pros :) + Responds to your frustrated emails quickly + Uphill from here

Good person, just bad a teaching. Shes very accessible through email, and replies within an hour or so (shes a lot better answering questions in email than during a lecture). Shes not too good at explaining assignments and labs, making them more difficult and leaving a lot of us confused.

Tough grader only because she cant explain her labs and assignments properly. Ive had to study using the textbook because I cant follow her lecture notes, but dont skip lectures because shell say one tiny little thing about a midterm or assignment that you must have. Her labs are graded, not just for participation marks.

the most uncommon prof, her assignments are hard, time-consuming and useless. I find no interest doing her assignment, I think she believes we have to study this course only. Her labs are as hard as the assignments. She is definitely in love with puzzles, recursive function, and vectors which is going to make your life hell. AVOID HER at any cost!!

The worst part about this class isn't that she makes the assignments hard in nature. The difficulty lies in trying to make my code compatible with hers and trying to understand methods beyond our level in an entry level C++ course. Her labs are as long as real assignments and her real assignments incorporate way too much. I have other courses too.

This is a lecturer who really has no clue how to teach. She goes through her lecture slides at a pace that assume that the students are inside her mind and able to keep up with a fast skim of material. She also makes loud childish groaning sounds when there are errors with using paint or closing paint, which is very weird and surprisingly annoying.

Horrible lecturer that doesn't explain her though process, just does the calculation/tells you what happens on a slide and rushes through her notes like there's no tomorrow. Doesn't know how to even use Paint, and lets out aggravated mumble noises every time something goes wrong. Doesn't bother giving proper explanations for labs and assignments.
Gives no details on assignments leaving you to figure out what to do. In an "Intro to C++ course" we were expected to have an advanced knowledge of C++ as she explained nothing in lecture about it.

Hes teaching at UBC this summer for software construction course. Easy going, covers important concepts. His exams are somewhat on easy side as he understands that summer is fast paced. Also he scaled grade up so thats a plus. Thus, he tried to help students by using familiar codes from notes and practice exams.

He seems like a good guy, but his lectures are unstructured and he tends to get off topic. He goes often from Topic A - B and then halfway through B - Z and then halfway through Z - E and so on. Easily loses students. Get ready to read in your own time. Is often unprepared for assignments, etc.
Dr. Oveisi was very helpful, if you make the attempt to do well, then this course will not be difficult. Go to lectures and his office hours, he is very willing to help his students succeed.

Mehrdad gave me a lot of intuitions throughout this course. He explained things clearly using good examples and analogies. Assignments and exams are easy, as long as you understand concepts. Mehrdad's office hour (three times a week) and mail list are the most helpful. If you really want to learn, you can learn lots.
Mehrdad is the very nice CMPT prof!He is willing to help everyone, also he try to help people get high mark! Very nice prof!!!!! cheers!!!

Is extremely helpful. Very willing to email out a lot of help. The course is entirely taught with powerpoint slides, which means that you don't really have to be there. The material is EXTREMELY boring. Very disappointing content. If you think AI is going to be about being bots and stuff get read to be underwhelmed. Mostly about search algorithms.

She recently came to SFU to teach a few classes. This particular class was adapted from Dan Boneh's highly-rated online cryptography class you can find on Coursera, using his slide set with modifications. Quizzes are online, and exams are based on quizzes, and only done after the quiz solutions are discussed in class. Overall her delivery is good.
He is truly the GOAT. He's super intelligent, knowledgeable, and one of the most caring professors I've ever seen. Highly recommended.
a nice guy. very clear lecture. patient with every question.

Lawrence's grading scheme isn't reasonable at all for midterm. The Grading is extremely harsh and it feels like they'll take a huge ton of points away if you don't write definitions and explanations word for word and letter for letter.
Solid choice for CMPT 363. Assignments and tests as well as their markings was fair for the course content.
One of the better professors at SFU. Lectures were a bit boring at times but were usually engaging. Assignments were good and fair. Exams were fair. No major complaints, you can do a lot worse.

Needlessly over-complicated course materials with no bearing on practical interface design, marks get docked randomly for things not on the rubric, and going to lectures is completely useless as they cover nothing that isn't just on the slides. Easy enough in hindsight, but needlessly stressful, arbitrary, useless, and (worst of all) boring.

very boring, can't even complete a sentence without stuttering, really really specific with his grading and takes away marks for tiny mistakes, doesn't have interest in his own content, and did i say very boring? but he's a really nice guy

This was perhaps the most difficult class I have taken in 5 years of university. Expect to fully read the textbook plus watch tons of online courses on the material. Exams were very difficult. I personally really liked Dr Tofiloski and think he made the most of a very tricky subject. Part marks given if you went to him and explained your solution.

Lectures can be disorganized, but Milan is flexible with deadlines. Textbook is unclear, so use Unstuck study and other resources to supplement. Overall, a challenging but doable course with a generous curve.
Cancelled many classes and able to explain the basic outlines of course to the students.

Cancels lectures randomly and does the bare minimum in lectures. If you don't mind teaching yourself he's alright because the midterms weren't horrible and the curve was alright, but as a professor he is pretty bad.
Would prefer any student to teach rather than this professor.

Horrible at teaching as everyone else has said. You are better off watching Youtube videos than going to his lectures. He cancels classes last minute which apparently is a common thing for him to do in other classes he teaches as well.

Classes got cancelled randomly, the homework felt nothing like what we learned in lecture, and leads to just using Youtube videos to learn the concepts. Tested on things that he had never fully covered in class, and marking was inconsistent. Though, the curve was generous and he did seem to want to prevent people from failing.
Dont takeit

Disorganized. Classes got cancelled on short notice. Homework took ages to be graded. Unhelpful exam outlines. The textbook is almost as unclear as Milan, be ready to teach yourself. Lectures are a drag, examples are just copied from the textbook. Milan somehow manages to come across as even more unenthusiastic about this course than the students.
I got curve but due to his messy nature course was a hassle

As the other reviews say Milan is definitely not the most organized nor a great teacher. However, his flexibility with deadlines throughout the course as well as a relatively free final made up for it. Not to mention the curve was pretty generous!
L PROF
Professor lacks basic teaching skills, fails to return assignments, and ignores emails. Zero engagement, disappointing experience.
This class easily bombed my GPA because of his midterm2 outline being so vague, and then using the hardest q's from a chapter he didnt teach. Thanks Milan!

If you have the chance to take the course w a diff prof, do so. We were ghosted the entire sem, MTs were delayed, classes constantly cancelled, and as a result our final mark is delayed. Someone who lacks this much should not be a professor. AVOID.

this prof is so incompetent! our hw didnt get marked for months on end, mt2 outline was disgustingly vague (and resulted in a 40avg), and now he rushed hw marks and doesn't have our final grade back despite td being the grade deadline. to future takers, PLEASE AVOID. he cant teach, and he read off tb the entire sem. he also doesnt know d/dx x^2. :P

this "instructor" shouldn't be allowed to teach at SFU. assignments were assigned and *NO* feedback was given whatsoever, as well as releasing all the homework grades on the last day.. His lectures are monotone including his "lecture videos" and he CANNOT explain any topic at any level. He is inaccessible outside of class, and ghosts emails. AVOID

This professor shouldn't even be a professor. His lecture slides are copied/pasted from the textbook. You will never get anything out of the lecture. His exams are also a curveball. He has made plenty of mistakes on his exams, and due dates always are pushed back at a very late notice. Very unorganized and unprofessional. Avoid at all costs.
Literally the worst professor, you are better off watching youtube instead of his lectures

Milan in Spring 2023 was not prepared to teach this course. Material covered was tangential at best and did not touch on core concepts/algorithms. He taught similarly to CMPT 105W in Fall 2021. Tofiloski was replaced by Harinder Khangura for the remaining half of the course. Great for a free grade, not so much for an educational experience.

Milan the goat of SFU Computing Science. Professors come and go but god only comes and leaves once. Salute to the best professor you'll ever come across. Really miss him since they kicked him out for some absurd reason. Brilliant professor, actually understand his students and knows the meaning of learning
inspirational

Lacks a basic understanding of computer science and has no ability to teach. He makes constant mistakes in lectures, on his recorded material, and even on tests. In all seriousness students are worse off after a course with him. I am an A student and could teach the course and even I often can't figure out what point he is trying to make.

Lectures are rambling and pointless, thinks frustrating students by giving them assignments that are beyond the scope of the course and not in the texts is a good way to learn. Frequently makes mistakes in his teaching. Has commented that he doesn't understand parts of the text book. Doesn't even know the prerequisites for the course.

I was hesitant in taking this course due to the bad reviews. However, one review said that do what you think is right which is what I did. You have to go to the class, and ask questions, he is a very nice professor. the amount of work is very difficult. if you do everything and ask anything you do not know multiple times he will answer.
The GOAT. Just do what you think is right and he will give u good marks. Highly recommend him for 105.
Promised interesting practical projects, but man forgot about them and released only 10% of the planned coursework within half of the semester before getting replaced

Honestly Milan is great! I have learned a lot until now even though we did not have full 3 hrs boring lectures compare to other classes, we had some amazing discussion with my group, and I got familiar with Unity environment which is much better than bunch of random quizzes.
He doesn't know what the course is supposed to be. The lectures are about his random thoughts not related to the syllabus. Take Milan if you want an easy A.

In the kindest way possible, Mr. Tofiloski is not fit to be a professor as of right now. Three weeks into class and no material has been covered. We are seemingly almost a month in material behind the other sections of 125. I don't think I've ever seen a professor less inspired to be there. He drawls on for 2 hours, or just doesn't show up. Run...
RUN.
Learning absolutely nothing

Milan is a professor who obviously does not want to lecture. His lecturing style involves speaking sweet nothings about the most broad topics he can think of. His idea of a 3 hour lecture block is spending an hour on an in class discussion, then lecturing for another 20-30 minutes, then ending. I thought I was paying for 3 hours of lecture...

Milan is one of those people who should not be teaching. A month has passed and we've learned nothing, compared to the other class we are on week 1 of what they're doing. He spends lecs repeating the same intro level material. He makes so many mistakes in his examples that I'm convinced he can't code, and he never prepares anything for lecs.

Never had a professor quite like this. "Lectures" (can you really call them that?) seem to have almost zero preparation/thought put into them, and the class itself is terribly disorganized. It really feels like Milan is figuring the material out with us (especially given that we are 3 weeks in and about 2.5 weeks behind the other class).

I really owe my computer science degree to YouTube and runestone textbook. We are 8 lectures in so far, with two cancelled already, one 2 hour lecture was wasted signing up for runestone which took simply 5 mins and the rest of the other lectures was Milan reading off slides and doing python review. Please do not take with Milan if you wish to lear
This course is all self-learning and the prof is way behind from the other class. Woke up at 6am for an 8:30 lecture and 15 mins in, got an announcement that lecture is cancelled.

Cares about what's important, and that's adding value to your learning experience by teaching you instead of expecting you to memorize. Was a little more disorganized then I'd like, but if you did the assignments, it didn't affect your learning. I would take another course with him because I agree with his teaching philosophy.

There is no grading criteria, you're basically just told to do things. The TA's seem to pull grades out of thin air. Milan has also not shown up to lectures a few times with no prior announcement. I get that life happens, but profs should still be respectful of students' time. Also, we received all of our grades at the end of the semester.

A good and caring man, but he is so disorganized and unprepared. The lectures seem to be made up on the spot. Grading criteria are non existent. Takes weeks (or in our case 2 months) to return assignment grades. Truly sad to receive this quality of education at a top university. Milan, if you are reading this, we need a lot more from you.

Milan is the one prof I would advise to avoid at all costs. He is not an IAT professor, he is a CMPT prof, but honestly, I don't know how he even teaches CMPT courses. I never learned anything in his lectures, he was unaware of the coding syntax, and his code always errored. We never wrote a single functioning coding program in the entire semester.
Avoid. Maybe you'll actually learn something with someone else. This guy doesn't even announce that he is not showing up to class and wastes our time.

All his tests had typos that he would catch midtest, homework was heavy, and lectures were rambles. Extremely disorganized, would always say that he would prepare examples for next class but never did. Lecture notes were just scribbles on his projecter. His exams were reasonable but were made difficult by the fact that everything was self taught.

One of the nicest profs that genuinely cares about students and is very humble. Lectures are good but he needs to teach more in general rather than posting short lecture videos. 5 star for his personality and kind behaivour.

Honestly a goated prof. It's Tofiloski's first time teaching MACM 101 so I'm gonna cut him some slack as he cut us some slack with his fair and reasonable exams. Lectures aren't the best but he tries his best to explain concepts and answer questions. Very active on Discord. Definitely an extremely hard course though so be ready to study for hours.

A lot of people are hard on Milan, even though it's his first time even teaching this course, so people need to cut him some slack. The textbook isn't that great either, making it even harder to teach. The tests are very straightforward, and he's very accessible in the class discord. He really cares, and he really values the feedback given to him.

Many of the lectures were disorganized and didn't teach much. He likes to give vague instructions for assignments to "teach critical thinking". It made grading pretty unfair since you could lose marks for things you had no way of knowing were nessecary (ie in-person attendance when watching pre-recorded presentations).
Prof was nice but is disorganized. Many assignments to do with vague instructions, but just do what you think is right and you will get full marks. Not too difficult, just lots of work.

Grading is a mess and inconsistent, nitpicky in some areas and generous in others. Lectures have absolutely zero structure to them and information regarding the final is patchy. Do not take this prof for MACM101, it is incredibly difficult without proper direction, and Milan does not direct

I've previously rated him a one star before mt1 and felt bad since hes a nice and caring prof. The tests are surprisingly way easier than assignments, and the avgs are usually 70+, meaning half of the class will get B- or higher. On discord hes also very active and humorous. However, u are required to self-study most of the material which is a down

apart from being accessible outside of class, nothing abt him is good. said chapter 5.4 wasn't gonna be in the test and he included it. It was really unfair, i spent long doing the questions that weren't meant to be in the test while i could have spend more time on other questions that i knew. just AVOID him. now gonna drop from A+ to D.

Milan's ready presence outside of class via his discord makes him super accessible, but that unfortunately doesn't make up for his lack of preparation for his lectures which are glorified Q&As / tutorials. This course has been mostly self taught on my behalf and his teaching abilities are lacking in a course that is 90% new concepts for students.

I have been at this school for three years. This guy is hands down the worst professor I've ever had. He is disorganized, he doesn't "teach" anything in lectures, and he has rescheduled due dates homework and midterms like five times. Sure, he cares about his students, but that doesn't make up for the quality of education he gives.

Readily accessible outside of class and also listens to student feedback, which is great. Tough homework, but exam questions are straightforward enough. The course material is extremely difficult so don't be afraid to ask for help
Would give it a one star because it's so hard to understand him especially when the course is already super hard enough. But since he moved the mt to next week, 2 stars from me!

Milan is nice and caring, but it's his first time teaching. Lectures were literally tutorials where he just shows you a bunch of very basic examples from past chapters, and he made you read textbook instead of teaching you in class. It's three days away from midterm 1 and I have no idea what's going on.

this sem is milan's first time teaching macm so imma give him some slack..... HOWEVER the lectures are extremely easy compared to the confusing asf hw, plus sometimes he says stuff like "i don't have a good example rn" during class lmao. he's pretty active on the official class discord tho which is nice
Nice guy, but doesn't know AI. The course was a complete joke. I got A, but didn't learn anything. I want my money back.
Great professor. Do not understand where those negative reviews come from. Everything was fair in my opinion. He's a legend.
Worst proof I've ever had, doesn't teach anything. You never do anything in class.

Milan is nice. But the course content was so useless, that if you've ever watched a YouTube video on AI, you pretty much learn everything that's taught in this class. The course was also super disorganized. We had no idea what we were learning, or why. Nothing practical either. The assignments were extremely rushed, time consuming and confusing.

Honestly not as bad of a professor as people made him out to be. His lecture is kinda useless but he do post very concise videos that you can watch instead. His quizzes are very hard but they usually don't worth much. The projects are actually quite fun after you get the hang of it and they have a lot of real life applicability.

Indisputably one of the worst prof at SFU. He does not teach anything in class, rather he assigns a ton of assignments and you will be on your own to learn the class material yourself. The assignments are tedious and difficult, especially since the students lack the knowledge to complete them due to lack of teaching. 100% avoid him.

The man does not teach, and assignments are insanely difficult. Example: spends a single lecture on natural language processing not even teaching, but showing students how to set up Jupyter Notebooks. Then assigns a quiz AND assignment on coding machine learning language models. I am teaching myself here, what am I paying for. I am speechless.

His lectures are useless. Instead of teaching the material during class time (which is the entire point of a lecture), he uses it as a Q&A where he usually spends more than 10 minutes answering a simple question. He also spends more time telling us what to read and watch instead of just teaching it to us in person. It's ridiculous and insufferable

Every lecture feels like he was making it up on the spot. Lots of rambling and unorganized thoughts. There are a lot of mistakes on his slides during lectures as well. For our assignments, he didn't even finish the rubric and deliverable requirements before posting them online. I've never seen such a disorganized class and prof.

Worst prof I have ever had. Disorganized, didn't lecture, had in-class group work so you had to show up to hear him discuss the course syllabus every lecture the entire term. Assignments were bad and uninspired. The "midterm" was given in the final two weeks of class along with the two coding assignments. Wait for anyone else to teach 310.

Worst CMPT prof that I've had to date. Tons of micro assignments with vague requirements and very little guidance, grading is inconsistent and the average is usually very low. The prof is not helpful when asked and would give confusing answers. Grades would take forever to return. Do not take at all cost, waste of time and money.

It's better to take a semester off rather than taking this or any class with him. It's a total waste of the precious money. In zoom sessions he just reads the questions posted in the chat and doesn't teach anything coz he actually has nothing prepared for the course. He is putting students future at stake by giving false marks in major assessments.

No matter what course you take with this guy, he'll be unfair to you and will trick you. He is an unfair marker who takes grade appeals but doesn't reply to any. You will keep emailing him about grade changes and errors but in vain of no reply from him. He doesn't bother what's going in his class and will make you cry for genuine marks. Don't take!

Worst Professor you will ever take in your life. Doesn't checks assessments fairly. I really don't get how SFU has still kept him as a professor there. He will mark all the assessments with the highest percentage at the last minute before releasing the final grade and will give you a very low score to decrease class avg. Won't reply to emails later

The only problem with this professor is; he will give assignments by saying that feel free to write whatever you think, there is no specific structure or guidelines you need to follow. But when the grades are out, marks are cut. Sometimes he will mark one assignment easily on certain criteria and mark the second one really tough.

WORST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER HAD Midterm essay grades didn't come out until the very END of the semester&had a 50% average for the whole class. Don't take this class unless you have to, you learn NOTHING. Unclear instructions and a lazy marker as well. The conclusion was worth more than the rest of the paper entirely. Save your money, worst prof.

At the start of the semester, the prof seemed really good, but we did not get a single grade till the semester over. And when we did get a grade there was a clear sign of the professor unfairly giving low grades in order to keep the average low. In the end I would recommend not to take a class with him.

Lectures are only for questions regarding assignments as many would be too ambiguous otherwise. Encourages appealing grades and there being multiple approaches to writing, but grading is done without much commenting and it is infuriatingly slow (only started grading at end of term). Overall very lazy professor that only pretends to teach.

The kindest professor at SFU hands down. He doesn't teach you boring stuff and throw tons of useless homework at you. Instead he teaches you to reflect on your writing practices via critical thinking. Professors without heavy accents are rare at SFU, and fortunately Milan is one of those who can speak perfect English.

Milan is the worst instructor Ive had in my entire life. Its amazing SFU keeps letting him teach. He teaches absolutely nothing all semester and just assigns work that is checked for completion then near the end of semester assigns toughly graded work and does not release grades from that work until after finals.

Lectures are basically Q&A sessions. Most assignments are marked for completion while some are marked harshly for writing style (after receiving no feedback to improve your writing style). He is kind but the course is not useful and marking is haphazardly done. Final project was released late.

Journal entries assigned 3 times per week but I doubt they are even read; do them and get 100%. Quizzes and midterm were most useful content of the course. Almost no lecture content (the course is a waste of time). Group projects are easy if uninspiring.

Makes you read a variety of topics, most of which are CS related. His lectures are quick and not informative. Gives random assignments as he pleases instead of following syllabus. He is kind and approachable but just cant teach well. Doesnt give good feedback on assignments and gives vague requirements. Quizzes have questions not from the lectures.

Extremely lazy, assignments are vague with no criteria and no feedback after. He says all the lack of criteria is "by design" which is fine if we actually got feedback after instead of just a grade. Everything he has taught is stuff you learn from any 1st year first year writing course. I don't know what these reviews are saying but he doesn't care

He is one of a few professors that I have met that actually cares about and listens to the students. There is not much traditional teaching in this course and we need to do most of the work on our own, but the prof is quite accessable and helpful.

He would be a rare prof who genuinely cares for students and eagers to make students think and learn from a communication course. He is a very inspirational and sweet person. It was quite an impressive learning experience. He needs to improve on organizing assignments, sometimes his flexibility works for students, though.

Took CMPT376 with him in Spring 2020. Have to say, he is one of the most humble professors I have ever had. Teaches you to think outside the box, and his class has a very calm atmosphere. I wish he taught AI courses as well, as his explanations are really intuitive and easy to understand. Assignments & homework are very reasonable & fair as well.

He is a very understand instructor who tries his best to explain difficult concepts to students. Really passionate about what he teaches and it shows. He made his lectures interesting and is always available to lend a helping hand. Great instructor, great person.
Lectures were more like a conversational meetup, we would talk about technical writing and do rewrites together in class. Assignments and Midterms were easy, only thing is that he is sometimes vague.

Not even the first day of class and he wants us to write an ESSAY due the third day! Extremely LAZY. He says there is no criteria, instructions etc. for assignments by "design". He says it is supposed to be confusing by "design". Who are you fooling? You are lazy and a poor instructor. I've been here 4 years. He is the worst faculty member of SFU!

Milan grew on me a lot as the semester went on. I didn't agree with everything he said, but that wasn't the point. The point was to get you to think critically while writing. Milan cares about your learning. He's very generous giving marks back if you explain your reasoning. There are a lot of small, mundane assignments, but you can do them quickly

Very Engaging lectures, and assignments push you to think critically. He is also a very fair marker and wants students to learn rather than be penalized for minuscule mistakes. One of the best professors at SFU. Assignments can be challenging but he offers alot of options to help students. I would highly recommend, it made me interested in CMPT.

For 376W, he really wasn't that hard of a marker. I found the peer marking in the course to be way harsher (probably to torpedo your competitor's marks). His notes are pretty lacking, unless you know what the material is about, its pretty much encrypted text. If you've taken an English class before, you'll do just fine even if you skip every class.

Milan said he doesn't focus on codes and what he wanted just naive ideas, but for all his assignments, we need to code for lots of things that according to him "no one can solve now". And he'll check every single line of the codes and gave low marks and x signs instead of real feedbacks despite he said what he want is just naive ideas!!

This is a required class that nobody wants to teach - its not that Milan is bad. Lectures are a waste of time. There is lots of assignments but most of them are only worth a few percent. This is a good course to take if you don't want to go to class. Once you get to know him, he is approachable.

I think Milan had his own way of teaching the course. He was very funny sometimes. I felt I learnt a lot to improve my writing skills. My first language is not English btw. If you schedule your time wisely, you won't suffer from this course,tbh. As a third year CS student, you should be able to handle it.

Many assignments. Assignments are "Google it yourself". Criteria for everything (journals, midterm, reports, in-class exercises) unclear. Rubric given after being graded. Deadlines keep getting postponed (midterm was postponed twice on the day before). Monotonous voice (want to sleep? go to class). CS analogies to writing ok. Avoid. Avoid. Avoid.

Milan is way too strict of a marker. He wants us to be clear in our writing, but he does not make his notes, or his assignment criteria clear at all. His exams are ridiculously stupid and the questions are really bad. This class should be easy, but the professor does not clearly explain how he wants us to write.
avoid him.

I think his classes are a good preparation for life outside of academia. Other people mention that his grading criteria is unclear, but so is real life. Treat his lectures as stories from your wise grandpa and you'll learn a lot.

DO NOT take this course with this guy. He does not have a clear criteria in any of his course material and always rambling about irrelevant stuff in lecture which is confusing. Assignment are ambiguous since he does not provide any sort of marking rubric until AFTER we got the grade. Exams are brutally marked and expected 100% error free
Worst presentation skills I have seen at SFU. Doesn't have consistent criteria, and doesn't seem to understand how to teach.
Milan has is own way of approaching the course. A very inspirational way of teaching a class that is meant to be boring. Assignments were not boring at all.
Although the prof may ramble a bit, I've still learned some tips in improving my writing. Midterms and tests were marked pretty hard, but assignments were fairly marked. Overall not a bad prof
Basically, all the assignments were asking you to google stuff. Barely learned anything in this class.

You need to learn many programming languages by yourself. E.g. Haskell, Scala Lots of programming homeworks. He won't teach those languages but he expect students can use those languages to implement matrix operators and use concurrent programming. For the lecture, you will have no idea what he is talking about.
This professor is very inconsistent in his teaching and has a tremendous amount of grammar mistakes on his slides which is ironic and pathetic and shows he does not really care about his teaching.
Never take this course with this professor. He doesnt know how to teach students and he doesnt have clear idea what he is saying
don't know much about the lesson he is teaching. arrogantly oppose textbook with no convincing reasons.

Milan's lectures were essentially drawn out ramble sessions with the occasional mention of technical writing. I received high grades on all assignments submitted until the last week of the semester where Milan overhauled the grading criteria (without notice) with the seemingly casual decision to begin marking strictly. I got nothing from this class

Milan is the most humble and unique professor I have ever met. This is a man who inspires students to think for themselves, rather than force them to conform to the set of criteria our education system has created. "Its the teachers fault. If you don't understand the content, you're not a bad student. It's my fault for not explaining it clearly."

Milan is a great guy and best fit for this course. Writing for CS student is always tough, but he analogies coding to writing with a brilliant strategy. I think SFU has to be proud to have such bright and enthusiastic professor.

Jacqueline Nelsen is an okay lecturer. Her quizzes are unfair and her assignments are dry and uninteresting. She teaches you what you need to know but you probably won't enjoy the process. I'd recommend taking CMPT105W with a different prof if you can, and leaving extra time in your schedule for essay work if you can't. No final exam for the course

Where to start? Nelsen is horrible in every way possible. She treats university students like high school kids, routinely yelling at people in class because they were 5 minutes late. Throws tantrums. Docks marks unnecessarily. Changed assignment requirements after due dates. Overreaches while she is a lousy teacher unqualified for her position.

This has nothing to do with writing skills. It's mostly on social issues related to female inequality in stem. My female friend went to her office hours, and Nelsen told her not to worry about her grade, as she gives A's to female students. Nelsen has no idea in computer sciences. Also her grammar is bad, and she makes A LOT of spelling mistakes.

- Mandatory lectures - High work load - Two 48 hour essays over the weekend - A ton of quizzes at the beginning of the semester - Nothing is related to technical writing - Feels like an English and Social Justice bootcamp - Everything is micromanaged - Changes grading criteria after assignments are due - Constantly contradicts herself AVOID
Jacquie is one of the worst profs at SFU. She isn't qualified to teach CMPT courses, especially upper-div ones.
This course with Nelsen is basically Social Justice Warrior boot camp masquerading as Technical Writing
Course content is does nothing to prepare you for actual technical writing. Feels like I am redoing Grade 11 English.
All of the aforementioned 1 star reviews are accurate.

She doesn't understand her own material, frequently gives contradicting instructions, most of the assignments are redundant. The final paper for this class was split into 3 parts except every part was essentially the same thing.

This might be the worst class I've ever taken, and that's saying a lot. Every singly assignment is completely micromanaged with 3 page tables that detail everything there could possibly be. Instructions are inconsistent. Lectures are incredibly boring. Sometimes has mandatory in-class activities so you still have to show up.

Thanks to her, this course takes up double time than an usual CS course does. I have to say that every single day for a third year CS student is important. But she is able to waste your valuable time on those numerous and useless quizs and assignment. Should she make none decisions that make you happy, don't be suprise because this is typically her

To conclude, this course by Nelsen is heavy-loaded. You will not only expect 80-100 small quizzes and assignments but also two intensive 48-hour projects, which stress you out. (And also an individual essay) Very very limited knowledge you can gain from this course.I won't expect any potential SDE would want to spend such an amount of time on this.

Too many boring quizzes. I guess they are only 5% in the final grade. I ever spent more than 6 hours working on 14 quizzes on weekends. Too many group assignments and individual assignments, asking about teamwork again and over again. It's definitely the worst professor I met at SFU.

Jacquie genuinely cares about her students. She has a lot of experience as a lecturer and an engineer and loves to share her experiences with her class. The information she presents is actually useful and can be easily applied. She does assign a lot of homework and isn't lenient about extensions or anything. Her papers are hard but doable

Take if you want an easy pass but annoying class. She relies to heavily on the textbook and rote memorization, rather than actually teaching students useful writing skills through consistent practice. There are many tedious and pointless quizzes/participation work that will make you hate school. 2nd worst class I took at SFU.

She is a genuinely kind person. She didnt give to much schoolwork and was always available to help and was understanding. That said the major drawbacks were her quizzes that were based on her slides and the textbook and were essentially just memorization. And her TAs were kind of mean. But she rounded my high A- to an A and I didnt even ask.

At the beginning of the term, there's 20 or so quizzes which is basically reading comprehension. After that, small stuff like participation (by attending via sign up sheet) and peer reviews account for your mark. No final, only two papers worth a majority of your mark. Four quizzes, with content pulled from lectures, which is from the textbook. EZ

Definitely not as bad as her score would suggest. Her lectures are really boring but necessary as questions for quizzes are pulled straight from her presentations. Lots of short assignments/quizzes on Canvas. Wrote two short essays for the course. Textbook is not necessary. Pretty easy course if you know how to write and read the rubrics correctly.

Dr. Nelsen really cares about her students and puts lots of effort into making lectures as engaging as possible (many in-class activities). She is very open to feedback about the course and communicates frequently to make sure everyone is kept up to date. There are many assignments (do not take very long), but she marks fairly and quickly.

Professor Nelsen is really nice and genuinely cares about her students, she is very approachable. However, I hate her teaching. Quizzes sucked, no feedback was given and I was unsure what they wanted. Rubrics were very vague and left many of us unsure of what to do for assignments. Comments were only occasionally left on graded material.
Easy materials but lots of assignments.
Need to pay a lot of effort for a high score, but once you paid, then can receive an ideal grade.
She is ok... She is one of the few instructors who really care about the students.
Worst prof ever... Useless course.

After seeing Dr. Nelsen's ratings before taking CMPT105 W I definitely was not looking forward to this class. However, Nelsen cared for her students and was a decent teacher. I barely studied for this class and still did well. Also didn't need to read the textbook at all, just go over the powerpoint slides before quizzes. No final, only 2 essays.

Jacqueline Neslen really doesn't deserve all these bad reviews. Sure, the class was pretty pointless but she obviously does really care about her students. The quizzes in her class all memorization of very specific class details, however they really aren't hard if you just pay attention to every lecture. Seriously pretty good and caring prof.

the quizzes are kinda bad lol - you'd assume you'd get graded more on WRITING than MEMORIZATION in a legit WRITING course but nooooo. 20% of your grade is based on pure memory and luck lmfao. also she's very against feedback/suggestions/criticism, like she just refuses to change anything despite giving us this giant feedback survey???? like ok lmao

She's really not that bad. It's a lot of quizzes in the first week or so but then you only have a few bigger projects like essays. Plus there's only 4 quizzes, no final or midterms. The quizzes can be tough with questions that definitely require you to read the textbook, but you can get an ok grade on those and just go hard on the papers. She cares

I literally cannot believe this is an accredited course. We had to write: -How to get more women in stem -One social issues vaguely related to CS. -Describing an Oreo and legos. Course was taught at a grade 10-11 level, with persuasive writing as the focus, and you get spammed constant assignments. This is the lowest SFU has ever been.

Unlike other classes that make you buy a textbook, this class forces you to buy legos. At least smashing your head with a textbook would give you a concussion that would hopefully let you forget about the terrible experiences of this class...

The class was disorganized and overwhelming at times, but given the circumstances at the time, I can understand why. Jacquie cares a lot and she gives really good feedback when you go to office hour (go, it's helpful). The class was not easy, but if you put the effort in, you'll do great. Read the textbook thoroughly. Tests were tough & specific.

Dr. Nelsen knows her stuff. Yes, this is a CMPT course, but it is primarily a writing and research course. Dr. Nelsen makes use of Canvas liberally and adds many assignments and quizzes. Most are quick to do, so don't be put off by this. Do the library quizzes right away and make use of what you learn, like Zotero for references; a real time saver!

Dr. Nelsen was a wonderful prof for this course as she seemed extremely passionate of the content. I warn that this course is philosophy and writing (i.e., no coding) but if you like the prior then you'll definitely enjoy the course. Give Dr. Nelsen a chance and don't rely on the RMP echo chamber, she's awesome and gives good grades!

The workload is ridiculously heavy and completely irrelevant to technical writing in CS. Take this class with Pearce/Milan for a higher grade and 10x less work. She also gets very defensive anytime someone brings up a concern or suggestion. She improved slightly throughout the semester, but still wasn't great.

- Says she would check zoom logs for attendance, but does not - Says she would check canvas logs to prevent cheating during tests, but does not - Loo-ng and typo-filled requirements for assignments - Spams your email (sometimes multiple times for one thing) throughout the term - Terrible workload that surpasses most if not all of my CMPT courses.
Probably one of the worst pieces of content I have ever had for this excuse of a "course." Did I learn anything? No. Is the format conducive to learning? No. Avoid her like the plague.

Went to office hours to appeal my grade. She didn't even read my paper. Just found a random typo and said I should proof-read next time because it leaves bad impression. I said she has lots of typos in her assignments and emails, and she became defensive, and said English is not my mother tongue, so I don't know. I grew up in Burnaby since I was 3

After hearing all the bad reviews, 105W this semester (Spring 2021) was not as bad as I was expecting. You can easily get a good grade on the essays if you follow the rubric, and the quizzes are fair if you studied. Jacquie is mindful that we're in a pandemic, and offers good advice during office hours.

Yes, this course can be disorganized and a lot of her content looks copied from 105. Yes, you will do the work of two classes. Yes, this class did not closely follow the outline for the course. However, it's not difficult content and she was tasked with creating a new curriculum during a global trauma. She teaches well and cares. Figure it out.

Jacqueline is great. This course can be pretty dry, but she tries to get through the material and made sure we got it. The essay grading criteria is really clear, and if you follow the course structure (do the essay outline, peer review, write final draft), you'll do fine.

The TAs seem to be choosing random student's outlines and essays to criticize and give them low grades. Quizzes seem to require a good memory as compared to a good understanding of the material. Overall, one of the worst classes that I have taken.

The WORST prof ever! The positive reviews for her, she probably wrote herself from fake accounts. Material she teaches is completely unrelated to syllabus or to CS. Her 376W is mostly the same course as 105W because she doesn't know what to teach. Ignores students needs. I'm from Asia, had to stay up until 2am for mandatory attendance. SFU FIRE HER

I have learned many valuable research skills in this course, which can help me start my graduate study later this year. Although someone argues that the teaching materials are not CS-related, people should understand that this is a writing course. Writing is a transferrable skill. I recommend people who want to continue graduate study take this.

Jacquie's 376W is a complete waste of time! For a computer science technical writing course, course material and assignments certainly feel like an ESL class at times! Even though the course is a disgrace to the faculty and students, do yourself a favour and take this course with anyone other than Jacquie!

Want 11 quizzes in one day? Want 70 assignments/quizzes (not joking) by the end of the term including 2, 48-hour weekend group projects that are meaningless? Does not teach the curriculum stated in the outline. Often does not answer questions properly because she gets defensive and won't actually read what you're asking. Horrendous prof, avoid!!!

Extremely disorganized class, with material copy-pasted from CMPT 105W. Only half of the material that is copy-pasted is double-checked, so instructions become unclear, with conflicting word counts for the same assignment. If you miss the word count, you get 0. She says she doesn't scale. Uses TA's outside of the faculty.

This class isn't really a gpa booster, it required more work than I anticipated. Got above average in most of the essays/tests and final grade got scaled in my favour. First years couldn't handle this course and constantly complained about every little mistake. It was a bit unorganized but the profs rough drafts/essays helped improve my writing.

Class average ended up as a B+ when I took this course in Fall 2020. The course isn't extremely difficult, however the reasons why you lose marks in the first place are infuriating and non-sensical. Read the other posts as they are completely valid complaints.

My writing abilities have gone down since taking this class. She constantly contradicts herself about what good writing is. I went to her office hours asking for help, shed tell me what to write then the day after would call the part she told me to write is garbage. Her exams are not related to studying its just pure luck on what you will get.

The prof expects you to memorize almost every little detail of your textbook, and every detail and spoken word from her. On the final, one question was What is my favorite video of all time? She lacks transparency in her rubrics and cant tell you everything shes grading you on.

She is very textboo0k heavy. She doesn't give many details. On the Final for Fall 2020 she asked "What was the instructor's favourite video". I did not know we had to study personal details about her. She also does not know a thing about computer science and teaches us ENGINEERING material. She does not give relevant information at all.

We never had projects based on the material. We had two essays where she had us peer review after we submitted rendering the feedback useless. She graded outlines two days before the essays were due making the feedback useless. The final had questions about her and required memorization not comprehension. This class should not be this hard to pass.

Pros: Tries to be relatable and uplifting Cons: Everything else lol, tests you on personal information (her dog's names, favourite youtube video, etc), tests/quizes are extremely difficult because you're required to memorize specific examples shown/said in class + specific lines from the textbook. You don't know which ones, so memorize it all lol

The course was disorganized since some rubrics for essays were copypasted and not elaborated on. Tests are very difficult, unrewarding those who studied course material (e.g. questions such as: "what is the instructor's favourite video" may appear on a test) requiring inane amount of memorization. A link between TAs and the prof. didn't exist.

pros about this prof: she is energetic, and she tries to relate with her students and be more interesting. The downsides are that she is very disorganized, and her tests are extremely difficult due to the need to memorize an entire textbook and items mentioned in lectures. Assignment criteria was all over the place and often not clear.

Fall 2020 CMPT105W with Nelsen was am an absolute nightmare. Unrealistic expectations from students, and probably one of the worst final exams ever made. 35 mins to do a 50 MC question exam with no ability to review questions. Do not take this class with her.
Final exam for Fall 2020 was absolutely scuffed. If you want to do well in this class, be prepared to literally absorb the textbook into your brain.

Try to avoid registering for a class taught by Jacqueline. Her cmpt 105w is a very heavy class with a ton of textbook readings, assignments, and pointless peer views. The peer review for the first essay was done after we submitted it so the feedback was useless. The second peer review our feedback was locked on canvas so we again did it for nothing

Imagine being a computing science student, taking quizzes and midterms with questions like: "as an engineer, what is the most important..." and memorizing numerous engineering-exclusive concepts. Imagine reading an entire textbook that constantly preaches how important the textbook is to ENGINEERS, while full of engineering jargon. I DON'T CARE.

Prof doesn't let people go back on quizzes or final, 35 min final, less time than our midterm and she didn't even tell us the format until very late and kept flip flopping about what the final is. Avoid this prof. Terrible prof.

+cheerful mood, organized course page -heavily textbook reliant if you want to pass the course(tests and exams) participation counts for marks as well, extra marks for students who participate a lot in the discussion pages tries to ensure you're always up-to-date(check your email!)

This class is truly horrible. The class requires you to read a textbook and memorize it completely. question on the midterm, final and quizzes include questions like, "What page is table 2.3b on?" and "What page are the rhetorical pattern methods talked about?". Be prepared to memorize the whole textbook if you want to get a good mark.

A miracle that she has a PhD, the level of grammar in her emails are that of an ESL student. Teaches straight out of an engineering textbook for comp sci students. The other reviews will tell you just how bad her quizzes and midterms are. Just don't take this prof. Completely incompetent.

Does multiple-choice quizzes except the questions are about absurdly specific details in the textbook. It's a very poor way to test knowledge and it seems as though there's no way to properly prepare unless you memorize every word in the textbook.

No need to scroll any further, I'll make it simple for you. If you don't have to take this prof, don't. If you do have to take a course with this prof, I wish you the best of luck, prepare for anything and everything.

I like her as a person but that does not change the fact that she's simply a bad professor. The midterm and quizzes are multiple-choice and test for memorization, not ability. Essays are graded harshly but not unreasonable. Read the other reviews. Not much more I can say except she is really really bad.
Assigns peer reviews after essays are due and does not allow the newly received feedback to be used. Also read all the other reviews.

the content she teaches is completely useless. why am i being forced to memorize an entire textbook that has no correlation to comp sci. essays are marked harshly and the quizzes are not fair. expect to memorize literally every single sentence of a 350 pg textbook when u take this course. as a person shes chill but this isnt rate my person

Horribly unfair teacher. Suspected kids of cheating on quizzes, and so she decided to no longer allow reviewing questions on Canvas, and reduced quiz times from 10 mins to 7 mins, making them almost impossible to finish. If this is the "quality education" SFU is talking about, I don't want it.

Memorize all 300+ pages of the textbook or fail. Quiz/Exam all based on memorization - down to the letter/graph/table on specific pages. Essays have very specific and unorganized rubrics, hard to get a good mark. Utterly disastrous and annoying professor. Terrible in every aspect, shouldn't be allowed to teach. Read the other reviews.

The incompetence of this professor is beyond what I believed was ever possible: the course material was littered with contraditions, and her emails reeked of unprofessionalism as they were full of typos. While the course went on, this litter spread like wildfire, and soon the entire course reeked of the rancid aroma radiating from her bad attitude.

Where do I even begin LOL. She's very disorganized and does not know how to test students well. For a technical writing course in computer science, we learned how to write as an engineer? The quizzes and midterms are completely mc and ask for random obscure sentences in the textbook. Essays are marked rather harshly; very few people get over 80%

This might be the most annoying course I've ever been in. It's like two courses in one, with essays and tests. Essays are marked by a specific rubric, and any small mistake results in a lost mark. Tests are done by memorizing the textbook. Don't know the example on pg 43? Guess that mark is gone now. Easy to get a B, almost impossible get an A.

Before this class, be sure to bring a pillow because you're gonna be falling asleep 2 min into lecture. Don't forget to bring a pillow for your pillow because your pillow is gonna fall asleep as well.

I'm not even sure where to start, this prof is far from fit to teach this course, let alone teach at all. She speeds through lecture content, which I doubt she understands herself. The rubrics for every assignment are utterly disorganized and information is always contradicting. She also expects you to memorize the entire textbook for exams.

Terrible Prof. She taught this class in 2 sections, and she gave a lot of information to one that she didn't to the other, we heard about it FROM those students in the previous class. She gave a crazy amount of quizzes in the beginning and her quizzes are the most random, precise sentences from a an engineering book. Not a good fit for this course!

Do I even need to explain why? Read the reviews in her Langara profile on this site. This class is useless. Nothing computer science related, all memorization and essays. She doesnt even have a PHD in CS. Avoid at all costs. Maybe shes different in person? Change my mind after Covid is gone.
hard and tough

For CMPT 201, it doesn't really matter what prof you take it with, as the entire course is designed to be completely standardized. Online lecture notes are good if you miss class, and be sure to keep up with assignments as they're worth more than half your grade.

He is a good teacher overall, but he gave many students (nearly half the class) a zero on one assignment after accusing them of cheating. Unfortunately, he did not give those students an opportunity to explain their side, and it feels as though he had already made up his mind.
Steve is a solid prof, but wasn't a fan of his super strict assignment policies. It's not hard to do well in his class though and I would still recommend him to most people.

Really good professor, lectures are worth attending, has good notes and you'll do great in his exams as long as you study properly. He does seem very harsh on his cheating policy, as I have a friend who did the assignment without AI or help and got 0 for a academic integrity violation.

Steve Ko is a very good professor. with extremely fair exams and a strict cheating policy. Although, I doubt every he caught did cheat as it was a lot of students; I would suggest to write your code as uniquely as possible to avoid his detection system. His github repo notes are great, as his in class notes are messy.

He gave 58 students (Almost half the class) 0 on a assignment accusing them of using AI since they have simillar patterns/functions in their code. It's also pointless to convince him otherwise, as he's quite confident in his thinking. As long as you don't write industry standard code, its a good class as exams are very fair.
Best professor! Genuinely care for students, and I love his way of teaching. It really does make you go into classroom and pay attention to his lectures. Also, pls DO YOUR OWN WORK. Don't cheat

Pros: Cares a lot Good lectures Fair tests Cons: Didn't particularly like the assignments Labs/in class assignments do take time but are worth very little Quiz marks affect assignment marks, so u ca mess up a quiz and it'll mess up assignment mark. It'd would have been better to separate the two and weight quizzes less.

Professor Ko is the best CS prof I've had so far. :) His teaching is super clear. The assignments are tough and take a lot of time, but they're totally worth it. Exams are fair. I'm sorry to hear a lot of students cheated on assignments. Please don't do that. You will definitely regret it when you get caught.

100/10 go to his lectures. Lectures are compact but clear and organized; the textbook info is not hard to read but his lectures deliver the information better. There are 5 long assignments (20-30hr) but they really help cement concepts taught in class. I can't think of a better prof to teach 201 than Stevey :)

great prof hes rlly good at explaining concepts. there were some in class participation activities but overall easy to reach outside of class either in office hours or on piazza. super hard class but hes a great prof

Best professor I've had at SFU. His lecture style is incredibly straightforward and simple; never felt lost. Provided notes are well written and comprehensive. Assignments (later ones) are challenging but very rewarding. No surprises on the exams; what is taught is tested. Will remember 201 as a formative course in my education.

He makes a lot of sense in lectures and is very willing to help students understand. In class activies and labs were really helpful towards assignments. This class was assignment HEAVY. and they are extremely difficult. Plus the environment that we have to code on is really strict and not enjoyable. Great prof, hard course.

Initially disliked Steven for being strict, with no late submission policy. Homework is heavy but essential for CMPT201 and helpful. Exams are fair, covering only class topics. Lectures are excellent; he's passionate, with clear explanations and great notes. Tough class, great professor! Beware there are pop up class activities!

Steven is an excellent teacher! His lectures are engaging, and he explains concepts clearly, so don't miss class. The assignments are the hardest I've done in first 2 years but very rewarding—they teach you the concepts and improve your skills. Exams are fair, and if you complete the work yourself, you'll do well. Be ready to put in a lot of time!

He is very good at teaching!! The long assignments are heavy but that's just CMPT 201. Steve does a great job making sure everyone understands the concepts and he's fairly active on Piazza if you have questions about assignments. Definitely one of the better profs I've had so far.

Steve really cares about his students' learning. He provides lots of examples (code and conceptual) in lectures and is very engaging. The assignments are tough but definitely doable, exams are extremely fair. This course is quite dense (hence 4 credits) but it will completely level up your understanding of everything. So kind and patient, 11/10.

Good stuff, pretty chill guy and assignments are long but fair. also to whoever the yapper was in class that kept talking over him and all that PLEASE CONSIDER LEARNING TO RAISE YOUR HAND!!!!! and shower too buddy

Great professor, you can really tell he loves teaching. If the class didn't understand a concept, he would go over it again with a different example until mostly everybody gets it. Lectures were really engaging and Steve was very enthusiatic. The assignments were challenging, but they weren't impossible to figure out.
CMPT 201: Amazing prof. Very clear and gives candy during lectures. No surprises, your grade will be almost EXACTLY what you'll expect through autograders and very fair exams. Also hi Steve.
he gets the point across in his teaching but his lecture notes are disorganized 4/5 but if he gives me an A+ 5/5

Definitely don't miss lectures. The assignments were very time consuming but prof Ko was so good at teaching the course. I feel like my programming abilities went from a beginner to an intermediate because of all the practice and knowledge I gained from this course. The TAs were also very easy to talk to especially when asking for a regrade or help

The course is tough if you're coming straight from 125, but the workload is fair for 4 credits. Assignments align with lectures, and labs give a good amount of the code. Super nice guy who cares about the material and makes it easy to understand. If you put in the work and manage your time, it'll be easy to get a good grade. Would recommend 100%
he's just a chill guy
Never mess with this guy. The course was assignment-heavy. His teaching seems fine but assignments are just soo much tougher going beyond the lecture material.

Amazing professor. The lectures are simple and engaging. I liked that he used the white board. The notes on Github are comprehensive and well written. The later assignments are hard, yes, but they are incredibly well designed and DO drill the topics into your head. Was an amazing learning experience completing them.
Bro drops a bomb just after/before every long assignment.

Honeslty, lecutres are good I cannot lie, but holly the assignments. They are the worst thing I have experienecd in this degree so far. They are a full time job that you cannot complete. They can take up to 40 hours easily then if you do not figure out one seg fault you just spent 40 hours in your week to get a 0 on a 10% assignemnt.

His long assignments are VERY difficult. You only get 2 weeks to work on each of them but some can take 30-40 hours or even more to complete. Besides that, his lectures and labs are alright. His tests are kinda annoying but aren't worth as much compared to the assignments altogether. Would not recommend 201 with him if you're fresh out of CMPT 125.

Steven Ko is extremely harsh and unforgiving when it comes to minor mistakes. I've never taken a class before where a misclick on an assignment could mean an F and possible academic dishonesty penalties. I just cant support a professor who is so dogmatic that he would punish even honest students for simple mistakes with his scorched earth policies.

The assignments are really difficult and time-consuming but he's a very good lecturer and thorough with his explanations. He knows what he's talking about and takes the time to answer any questions w/o making you feel dumb. He seems passionate about the topic but wanted to challenge students so the assignments are very hard :(. Exams were good tho!

CMPT201 in Spring 2024 is/was not a fun time. The large assignments are extremely difficult for a 2nd year course that can be taken by anyone fresh out of CMPT125. The grade distributions of the 3 large assignments (so far) have had an extreme disparity. Around ~40% of the class failed each assignment with grades almost like an inverse bell curve.
He give me lolipop
He's really accessible outside of class, lectures are really good. Overall Ko is very respected professor. Cmpt201 is worth taking, you'll learn a lot.
Amazing prof, I learned much more in 218 than 300 which none of the assignments in 300 are related to its lecture. He's a really good prof, clear explanations.

Literally biggest mistake... took it to replace cmpt 300 but honestly i should've just taken cmpt 300 instead. If you miss one lecture you will be lost most of the time and the amount of the assignment you get one month before the assignment is insane. No slides, no practice question nothing given just go to the lecture if you wanna pass.

He gives large assignments in Nov that are unreasonable for the expectation they have for the course. This expectation for this course is to be doable for students who have just completed CMPT 125. The deadlines are too short such that people doing the course have said that he gives assignments as if we don't have other courses to worry about.
not fan of his teaching style. So many assignments ... he gave almost most of big assignments in Nov. Avoid him

"Assignment 11 is going to be easier than assignment 10". Assignments are heavy, his lectures are not enough to deal with assignments. He thrown most of long assignments on us in November... avoid him

No pop quizzes but initially planned to, may do so in the future though so beware. All I can say is he was okay. Lectures were okay but enough to get the idea (lots of self-studying to fully get the content), assignments were tedious but easy so it was okay, quizzes were basically T/F with some short answer but they were okay. Average was a B.
Teaches very well. This class is easier than I thought.

No pop quizzes this term because of COVID. Changed grading scheme during semester (3 "quizzes" of 10%). Unclear assignment grading. Using Nick Sumner's slides. Own slides have mistakes and sounds unsure when teaching. Not accessible during office hours. Recommendation: avoid.

The lecture notes were of little help with the exams compared to the sample exams. A large number of students could not even submit a solution for the assignment because he refused to extend deadlines and he only provided cryptic, general support.

I'm a good student (4.0 GPA), but this guy was something else. Tests were heavy on critical-thinking, but the notes didn't prepare for that at all. Assignments had inconsistent difficulties (unlike what he promised). On many occasions, I was on the verge of a nervous breakdown because of his apathetic attitude to students. Take at your own risk.

Brian's a good guy and it's clear he wants his students to succeed. His assignments are brutal though. Lectures are focused just on theory so you are expected to learn a lot of C on your own. I imagine the course being much easier with Pearce but if you want to actually learn I strongly recommend taking it with Brian.

Brian is a great lecturer and EXTREMELY helpful in office hours, especially email. Kudos, very refreshing! That being said, his assignments were brutal and extremely time consuming. Be prepared to WORK A LOT. Exams were tough, you really need to know the material well. Blind memorization will get you nowhere. Take it if you want a challenge.

I dropped this course early in the semester because of poor teaching and irrelevant therefore hard assignments. You will have to learn openGL by yourself because the lectures only talks about concepts. His teaching ability is poor and unorganized. I hope there is another professor for this course since I am interested on the course subject.

This prof is a joke. He doesn't care about what he teaches, how he teaches it, or how it is graded. TA is always right with this prof, even when proven wrong...he just doesn't care. Takes 2 weeks to reply to emails...only 5 people passed final! Guaranteed F for 80% of the class, this is only CMPT class I failed in Univ. Avoid at all cost!

No unfavorable profs are this irresponsible. Lec mostly dictionary graphic terms, interesting but totally irrelevant to asns/tests. DIY Math and Program. Unclear asn. Don't even ask he won't help. 20/22 students fail MT. Tests w/ stuff not in lec or text. And plenty of F. This isn't even a course. Only wastes your time, $, GPA, graphic interest :(

I had unfavorable profs, but none are even close to this completely irresponsible one. Lec mostly graphic terms out of the dictionary, interesting but totally irrelevant to his asns and tests. Asn outline unclear and he offers almost no help. Around 20/22 students
Tough Course. Not so enjoyful
Thinks before he says something. Knows the subject well.

Does a good job teaching a very tough course, this should not be a 3 credit course! Has an extreme interest in the course material, thinks about what he says and can respond to difficult questions. Coming out of this course, I felt comfortable enough to reimplement most of the algorithms discussed
Worst. Lectures. Evar.
The project had nothing to do with the class. He was just re-reading the book to us off his slides. Didn't learn any practical skills in animation just howto use maya which we taught ourselves.
No Comments
He's by far the hottest prof in computing science.
TOUGH course, he teaches it as if it was 2 courses instead of 1!
i wouldn't complain if it were a 5 credit course

MACM 101 with Dr. Ternovska was a good experience. We got feedback on assignments *before* resubmission for grading which guaranteed high marks.
