(I took it as a distance ed) Expect the midterm and final to be hard(multiple questions were tricky). Overall it is an interesting course.
Great Prof, knows his stuff.

A very useful course if you want to gain some more practical skills, as web app work is in high demand. Useful material, well taught, as usual, by Greg. Obviously, it's a group project, so pick your partners and the actual project well!

Third time with distance ed course, based on first impression of the textbook, it seemed fun and well organized. The latter is true. Figuring out hwk on my own is fun but time consuming but ONLY worth 50%; you get a cheatsheet for mt, but you're racing every second of it; above all, Baker, finally seeing him at the exams, is condescending and rude.
Great prof, definitely worth taking this course for a easy grade(if you are into computers/put in a little effort).

Pretty easy course if you download and use the study guide. Also he provides all the stuff you need on the website, so no real reason to buy the books. Clear, concise and pretty funny too. Only annoying thing was the TA din't speak English too well.
good professor~

Lectures were alright and not too bad. I don't like how he throws his notepad on the counter as he moves from overhead to another. He's also quite impatient that he even yelled at somebody just because he was talking to someone. Who says we can't talk in the lecture hall? It's university, not high school
The course itself is not that bad. His notes is clear, the text book is also easy to read. However, I think he gets angry so easily...
Excellent course. Excellent prof. The assignments were interesting and fun. The exams were fair. He is a good lecturer. Classes are easy to follow and the energy level is high.

One of the best teachers in the CS department. Greg is actually motivated and interested in the subject and in his students. He is charismatic and organized. His semi-weekly labs really help you learn the material, and his assignments are actually interesting. In addition, he does them first, so you know you can get the help you need. Recommended

Easy course. His study guide really helped. I never listened in lecture and still got an A- just from reading the book. HTML was relatively easy. Programming got a bit tricky so be sure to attend labs and get help from the TA if you're stuck. Midterm and Final are allowed one handwritten cheat sheet!

Great text written by himself. His very thin and concise text is ideal for people who get restless while reading/studying. The prof did not command the lecture hall well. He was at times impatient and too easily frustrated, though that is understandable given the amount of people who talked in class and all the people who walked in late.

May as well be the first to review Spring 2010. All in all, Greg did a great job teaching this course. The TB is written by him and follows the lectures closely, which is very helpful. The assignments are totally unambiguous - he tells you exactly what he wants and you go to it. Very accessible for help and he personally attended my lab section.

This course is easy for people who have a smidgen of knowledge on HTML. The programming at the later half of the course was very introductory and not too complicated. His lectures are pretty much the same as the text (written by him) so lectures weren't mandatory. He teaches some tricks in class, but eh. Exams were not hard if you read the book.

Took this course by DE. Not too bad actually. The new formatted course is quite manageable. Just do your labs and assignments. Most of the Midterm and Final written is based off the labs/assignments. Read the textbook, then you'll get an A.

Took this class for the bsci requirement. Lets just says this was not as easy as I had originally thought. Assignments clear. Cool prof. Keeps you engaged throughout lecture. Midterm was a killer but final was fair.

I'm currently taking the DE class. I have never done any website building prior to this class, and I'm finding this class very easy to do well in. Simply do all the homework yourself, mt/final shouldn't be anything new. While I can't comment much about the prof, the textbook is enough to deal with any problems.Prof has nothing todo with difficulty.

The course itself is not hard, but the guy doesn't know how to teach. he makes everything seems really complicated. Assignments were easy. midterm was fair, but the final was little hard. because of the python (programing language) tht we only covered basic in the class. you can get above average if you study the text book, but don;t hope for A.

SO I took this in DE too and what can i say, the instructions are clear, and the labs and assignments weren't that hard to do. But the midterm! Man the class average was low. Making a css given the time limit and the stuff on the diagram =_____= and the TAs never answers anything.

People who complain about this guy are absolutely out of their mind. While I can appreciate the difficulty of 165 and 120 for new programming students, Baker is the ideal instructor for the would-be programmer and his courses are warmer and more inviting than any class you will get from the faculty. Your shortcomings are your own, not his.
The best CMPT prof. Lectures, assignments and labs are well structured and clear. Exams are not too difficult.Assignments are fun to make (especially Pac-Man).

The text he wrote for his intro course is excellent. His teaching style is solid, though he seemed just a little cold. But I thought his little quips were hilarious, for a teacher. Assignments were adequately challenging, as were exams. I was pretty impressed all around. Not too easy though, gotta work for your mark.

I took CMPT 165 by DE thinking it was a GPA booster...ha...ha...ha.HAHAHAHAHAHA. This was one of the worst courses I have taken ever. Like many of the other people here, the first half was easy, I had 100%, then the midterm and second half hit. His practice midterm was USELESS! Also, a riot was started in the cafe, when we got no help. AWFUL!
He makes for an enjoyable class.
No Comments
Teaching style is great, only need notes to be more neat.

WAY TOO DIFFICULT. not at all suitable for students with no background in programming. i took this course online and the forums would be filled with confused students. assignments took me at least a day to complete and are not worth that many marks. dont recommend taking this course AT ALL.
Hard assignments

THIS COURSE IS NOT A GPA BOOSTER. The apparent struggle and despair on webCT "cafe" reveals clarity of material and helpfulness the TA's. Cheatsheets for exams will help for 1 question...there will be questions you have no clue about. And writing perfect CSS/HTML on exams for full marks??? In the real world, we use validators.
He's an alright teacher. The assignments get really difficult towards the end. Lectures are straight copying notes off overhead and quite boring.

WOW! Whatever you do, DO NOT take this course through distance education. First half alright, second half brutal. You get no help, questions that are answered through email, a week later are vague. Take this course on campus, or avoid taking it.
Great guy, definitely top 5 at SFU. Tells it like it is, and actually cares about students.
Funny guy...i like the random side comments
This is not a mandatory course, so don't take it if u have a choice. Greg is OK, but too much work.

unlike what the course description said, this course is NOT suitable for non-cmptsci majors with no programming background. the first half was fine (a breeze even)...but once the programming stuff kicks in, ur on ur own. prog asgnments were hard considering it was a dist.ed class...if ur gonna take 165, i suggest the on-campus lecture section
Worst prof at SFU EVER ! Doesn't reply to e-mail (if he does it'll take him about a week). Overall horrible prof for this course ! If you want to take CMPT 165 then take it with the other prof.

Be wary of him! He has one of the worst qualities a prof could have:favouritism! If you are in the little circle of hist friends and favourite student you are fine. Otherwise, don't try to get on his bad side. And that's not just what I say. Also, he is very subjective as opposed to being objective.

Beginning was hard. But once you get used to it , it's very easy. Make sure read notes and attend every lecture. If you have any questions, email him, he will reply within a day or so. And he's very helpful

Good prof, a bit boring, easy to follow, seems intimidated by the class (never looks up), cute jokes. Breaks the course down into baby steps which seem easy at the time but are impressive when you look back at the end. A very good course for your first semester.

This prof is totally like a high school teacher, with all the straight copying notes from overhead. I suppose this style is a good thing if you like slow paced learning. Another + side to this course is, reading materials are free. Like someone said earlier, assignments begin dead easy and progress to very challenging. This prof is very clear.

An okay prof. Even though I had no prior programming experience, the assignments and exams were doable. It's really important to practice and ask for help when you don't understand something. Labs and assignments start out as laughably easy, but the difficulty really picks up by the middle of the course.
nice guy, pretty helpful. tries to make jokes and it's pretty cute. it's funny cause it's not funny. awwww
Friendly and quite helpful, especially if you go talk to him one on one.

Similar to another student said, this was the worst class I have taken at SFU. The TA's were the ones that seemed to be teaching the course! Even at that, the TA's and the prof were very condescending and quite often there was lots of chatter between the students for help. The prof always asked us to email the TA's for help and not him. HORRIBLE.
easy class, and his notes are really detailed. he explain all the concept very clearly. Great Prof!

He's an EASY prof. Read the text, go to class and ask question and you will get an A- with ease. The assignments and labs given to you aren't hard if you take the time to work on it. The lectures are a bit boring, but it's worth going to class because it helps. I got a 85% on the final and I only studied for 3-4 hours at most! EASY prof!!!

He isn't that easy, but as you do some preview, it will be crystal clear what is going on. Do practices and examples and think through the logic. He is willing to tell you how everything happen and how they come together, very helpful~
Real nice guy. Exams a bit hard, but it might be because of my lack of programming background.

The WORST class i've taken at SFU. I have programming background and this course was unreal for a first year programming course. There was no help from the prof or TAs, I had to rely on other students to help me out. Midterm and Final were extremely hard, concentrates on the tiniest details in the texts which are both 250 pages of tiny writing.

Unhelpful. Unresponsive to emails. Obviously disconnected from the students. These comments apply to both Mr. Baker and all of the TA's. CS department should filter out those who have programming exp. Otherwise, they set the curve. Astoundingly inappropriate course for non-CS people. Scattered, disorganized, incoherent, and incohesive. All of i
easy teacher. the assignments were interesting... tic-tac-toe and robot maze.... like, what teacher actually gives game-like projects as assignments... certainly not other profs
No Comments

He's such a jerk, said we were having lecture this day then cancels the morning of lecture and there's like 30 people that show up. Two days til the final and he still hasnt sent out that outline that he promised.

His tests are a little harder than expected... Sometimes I am not sure he knows what he is talking about because he uses "I guess" quite a bit when someone asks him a question.. I wouldn't really recommend him.

i took the course with him 2 years ago. his lectures were boring but the materials were helpful. i would recommend the course to others if you want to have some fun (or easy credit) during the semester. i would not recommend this instructor though.
has the most roundabout way of explaining things; lots of pieces that didn't tie together, and he taught subjects in the most painfully illogical way possible

he's not a bad prof. His homework is pretty much taught in class as examples. His midterms and finals are related to the homework, as long as you go to the lectures and do the homework you will get an A for sure.

Boring lectures and difficult material + assignments! Things like python are not suitable for beginner CMPT at all! However, he seems to believe that the course is easy for everyone and that everyone should do well (if not, he assumes you are not trying)Tests are pretty fair though
Extreme unclear and he didn't talk to students but himself in the lecture.

For some reasons, on the midterm and the finals, there are obscure little questions even the most hawk-eyed student can miss (unless you have a comp sci brain). Nevertheless, read the study guide carefully and do the assignments, you'll be fine. (If you fail the midterm, you can still redeem yourself through assignments and the final exam!)

Good prof. if u dont get it..go ask him. he gives u answer (assume that u show him that u understand the simple codes) the codes arent hard.. directly from the book... except for a few... but i mean.. USE YOUR INTUITION!!!! the style is the same... just diff. properties... In class he shows how to get THE ANSWER to the assignments.
mid a bit hard, final very hard, hard python assignment
Irresponsible lecturer. Degrades the quality of education in SFU CS.

Well, to me, the assignments were pretty easy, but they don't help that much for the midterms or the finals. The midterms were okay and so was the final. You need to work abit (at least do your assignments and read the course book) to get a good mark.
Talks fast. Notes are alright. Often states the obvious.
lecture is not very helpful for the assignment and project. You are on your own
he's a nice prof

To G baker, Please stop writing gd feedback for yourslef. I told you, your courses are all hard Hard assignments. His classes will be always available even in september. Ask your friends for advise they will tell you how hard is this courses

I'm not even close to a computer genius and I find this course SUPER EASY, easy MT/Final and no problem on all assignments (except last one). Got an A and I almost forgot I took this course. Read the textbook and write some program according to the book and you will be fine.

if you have absolutely no progamming background, this course will be fruity tooty hard, as it was for me, but i passed sort of.....dang. i didn't pass the fruity assignments though...and guess what, he actually reads these comments!! so immature
HAHA this guy makes the course so easy. If you know a little bit of programing, go to his course, it will be like a joke.
:( Poor instructor
I want to refund this course
He is not bad when you go to his office to find him. However, he makes the introduction courses extremly difficult and the TA for the course is not helpful also
Has great style of lecture notes and provides many examples. However, the midterm and final are both a tad difficult.

Lecture material is not bad. However, his assignments and mid terms are freak. He caugth around 30 students academic dishonesty but his assignments are damn hard. Do no choose it as elective, if you are comp sci major, Business is much better, change your major
Becareful dude ! Avoid
Hard assignment, Hard Mid term. He is unhelpful but with hard assignments. You need to spend a heck of time to do. This courses is even harder that second yr Calculus. Be careful
His assignments are hard. If you are first year, his class are always available even in AUg 30.
His courses are difficult. Never take it. Better ask your friend that took his courses before.Unfriendly,unhelpfl, hard assignment.

very interesting class... very very hard to fall asleep... his notes are very good and clear... he even teaches you how to do the exercises/assignments... like demonstrating how to do it. i honestly dont know how ppl can actually do bad in his class... especially in the xhtml section...its so clear
understood jack
his lecture was well orgnized, best porf I met so far.

he's horrible at teaching but he's easygoing if you talk to him in person. His lectures are too broad and general, whereas assignments are detailed and hard...and oh..he thinks he's hot..maybe others do too..do u?

He knew what he was doing but no one else really understood. He offered help but I never went. He's got the hot funny geek thing going for him. I enjoyed watching him teach even though I had no clue what he was talking about and failed his class. Too bad he's married? (ring) LOL.
No Comments
I didn't do so well in this class, but he gave us a chance to redeem our marks by scaling the midterm and final. Overall, a good teacher.
Enjoyed his class. Very clear in his lectures and extremely nice and funny :) Tests aren't too bad and assignments are sometimes long but not overly impossible to solve.

I dunno, is it just me but I observed that he keeps on doing hand gestures and stuff which makes him look so graceful. I sometimes get a mental image that he's wearing a tutu and dancing ballet or wearing a cabaret costume with colourful feathers on his head while he's doing the lecture. hehehe..
GOOOOD
Poor

u don't want to take this if you don't have any basics about programming. I basically thought the course was horrible, probably because it's a new course and this is the first time that he teaches this course.But he doesn't give u enough examples to understand.TOO YOUNG,NO EXPERIENCE ABOUT TEACHING.
really nice prof. but not enough examples and doesn't teach deep enough to make students understand. but he's very nice in general. easy-going.
what should i say?? this course really sucked. i totally agree with the person below me.
Pretty good for explanations, but assignments can be challenging.

John Edgar is one of the more personable profs. Attendance for lectures are not neccessary and you probably shouldnt attend them because he mostly just reads off slides. You can just do all the assignments and studying at home and show up on test days. The tests and assignments are fair if you put in effort to study and practice programming
Very nice person, but has no idea how to teach anything.

Although John's course is in-person, you probably shouldn't attend his lectures. He reads off of slides and goes on long tangents, so his lectures are empty (< 5% attendance). Midterm average was 42%, which he bragged to his CMPT130 class about. Final was pretty okay, granted you memorized his posted notes, but the average on it was still 48%.

John is a great professor and responds to online questions extremely quickly. The content covered is difficult, and without proper studying or review it is easy to get behind. Lectures can feel bland, but if you take the time to ask him questions he is super knowledgeable and willing to help explain. Expect a hard midterm and fair final.

Fun guy. If you come into this class with little to no C++ experience it might be a little bit more challenging. I managed to not pay attention during lectures and pull a B+ on the midterm so it's not too bad. His grading criteria for assignments is very clear. Do the labs. Tests were easier than expected.

Great professor, he does read off of slides but he explains and expands in detail so I would recommend attending lectures and not skipping. Labs are very useful for his assignments, so I would attend those as well (but completely optional). His grading scheme is VERY clear as he lists out everything he wants to see in extreme detail. Good teacher.

John is super nice and is great at teaching programming. I never went to the labs and went to 2/3 lectures every week. The midterm was easy, but the final was worth 50%, I think, so be prepared. Overall, an amazing experience.

Johns course was pretty difficult but you learn alot from it, the exams were very legthy but were reasonable difficulty wise, same for assignments. What I like about John, is that hes actually really nice, and accessible outside of class, not just for questions, but other things, since hes a member of the undergrad chair for comp sci.

John's a really nice professor, if you actually talk to him about assignments or grading, he is usually fairly generous, his lectures were boring, but they were also really informative, quizzes and assignments were pretty fair, mostly adt implementations. Midterm and final both fair difficulty wise, but too long. TA's were really harsh markers tbh.

His lectures are really boring and the midterm was pretty bad, BUT the assignments, quizzes and final were all extremely fair. Considering that the midterm was only worth 15%, the class overall wasn't difficult. He's a nice guy too and generally pretty forgiving in my experience
Lost his will to teach about 10 years ago. Loves reading slides and making Starcraft references as much as possible. Highly recommend this class for a good nap at 8:30am.

The class itself is easy, it just has a pretty high workload. John's lectures were alright, I found that I often had to go through the recordings to connect the concepts, and this took hours. But the quizzes aren't hard, the final was fair too.

Edgar's a solid prof, fair assignments (basic ADT implementations), weekly quizzes are pretty easy, and final was straightforward. The midterm was a bit tough, but only worth a small portion of the grade, and he did scale a bit at the end. Solid choice for this course, gives a good foundation.

Pretty big yapper. Lectures are a little boring but informative. Really try your best to understand the material. Tests are just leetcode easy so you should be doing leetcode as much as possible. Assignments are were pretty easy and just ADT implementations.

The final was very fair and representative of the material and thoroughly tested your understanding. It was comprehensive yet balanced, allowing us to demonstrate our understanding and apply our knowledge meaningfully.

Professor John Edgar is a great professor. Contents on his slides are clear and strightly related to assignment/mid-term/final. No assignments or quiz are off the scope. Go start writing some C++ code before taking this course (if you don't, you will also be fine). PS: Final wieghts 50%!!!!!!!

John Edgar is an alright prof. He explains the concepts well but is pretty dull most of the time, lectures are kinda boring. Class difficulty was ok but TAs are very harsh on assignment grading. Also, the Canvas quizzes are false hope in regards to test difficulty, the tests are much harder. Also, final exam worth 50%, ouch

CMPT225 is a hard LD course, so many people will bash him for harsh exams; he was decent/fair. Exams had tricky MCQ, but doable written. Quizzes from slides, assignments were fairly straightforward. Quiet/monotone during lecture but provided great insights to material. Strongly suggest showing up to class to grasp a better understanding of DSA.
John's a chill guy.

You know, I actually really liked John as a prof, despite not liking him all that much at first. His midterm was very difficult, but only worth 15%. The assignments, quizzes, and final were all very fair and worth most of the grade. His lectures were enthusiastic and he often went on funny tangents in class lol. Very responsive to emails too

Lectures are a little boring but are recorded and slides decent. You will have to learn a good bit of implementation on your own but thats expected. Assignments (for the most part) and quizzes are fair. Prof is quite nice and replies to emails quickly. Only issue is no sample exams which made studying for them really hard.

Honestly John is a really enjoyable lecturer and in the end his grading was quite fair. The midterm was pretty tough but as it was only 15% compared to a 50% final, so it's not that hard to bounce back. Assignments were a lot of work which is a given considering the complexity of the topic. Be prepared to do a lot of coding practice.

grading criteria is clear, and he is very organized, but thats about it. HORRIBLE exams and an extremely tough grader! Quiz questions rarely even show up on actual exam material, Assignments are extremely difficult and will leave u to learn concepts that he hasn't even gone over in class with the same difficulty, he sucks, better luck next time ig

John is a great professor, he definitely likes to go on tangents though during lecture. I found his assignments and quizzes easy. His exams are fair but without example examinations can leave a lot students putting time into the wrong elements of the material. John is very approachable and ensures that grade corrections are addressed.
50% final with insanely low avg, slides are confusing af. Had to learn on my own
Good lectures, good assignments, final was very fair, midterm was bad, but other than that he was great. His grading scheme sucked, that's why I gave a 4 not a 5

His multiple choice questions on the actual exam are way more difficult than the quiz questions he assigns. He claims they're of similar difficulty, but that is not true at all. the final exam being worth 50% of your grade means no matter how well you've done throughout the semester, one exam ends up deciding whether you pass or fail.
Very vague about the final, and gives no practice leading up to the final- when students ask questions, he is very unhelpful. Do not recommend, you won't learn much.

His lecture slides are okay, but the lectures are a bit dull—he needs to make them more engaging and stop reading off slides. The tests are very hard and not really related to the material. The class average was 42%, John made the test unnecessarily long for the time given. Grind out the leetcode for this class and understand the data structures.

I would say that John is very nice and approachable, and does a decent job explaining most concepts. His assignments are mostly fair and based on the data structures we learned in class. His midterm was killer tho, but other than that, it's a good experience. Many will complain, but that's just 225, it's a hard class.

His lectures are actually pretty good, and his weekly quizzes are very fair. The assignments are also not too bad, but require some problem solving. One major con is that his midterm was way too long, causing many people to do poorly. He's approachable outside of class and during office hours.

I like the course, assignments are fun(to me) and lectures are good. But, I wish he was more invested in our success and kinder. The talk he gave us after the midterm made it seem like our fault and he basically spent the whole time trying to defend himself saying that the midterm was reasonable when it clearly was not considering everyone failed.
He couldn't do his own midterm in class, something is clearly not right with him. I BEG YOU, PLEASE AVOID:

He just yaps at lectures and tries to avoid the disappointment that is his midterm, it's like the elephant in the room, but he refuses to talk about how bad it is. His assignments and midterms suck, not a good professor tbh I'd rather take Janice.

John Edgar is an expert at making students burn out. Avoid at all costs. His assignments are way too time consuming for what little weight they contribute. Majority of people scored below 50% on the midterm. Pretty bad overall

Lectures, slides and quizzes make you think the class is easy. The assignments are terrible and are way harder + longer than they need to be. They are WAY too hard comapred to what he teaches. Some midterm question have nothing to do what he teaches and it was way longer than it shouldve been. No practice midterm made this a lot worse.

Our midterm average was brutal compared to other years—42% versus 80%, solely because of John. Please, AVOID this professor. There is no practice midterm, which is pretty unrelated to the lecture slides. Overall, the slides are pretty useless and only discuss the basics. Assignments are also complicated with few resources. AVOID!

Solid prof. Lectures are enthusiastic, he's nice and funny. His assignments are fair and doable, but require some problem solving skills. Weekly Canvas quizzes are quite straightforward and good practice. His midterm was really difficult though, mainly due to time constraints. Be prepared to write quickly. Nonetheless, he's decent.

Midterm was super unfair, completely unrelated to most of the lectures and material + 50 minutes for 15mcq and 2 hard written answers is just unfair. In addition, a lot of the assignments are unrelated to the material, and pretty hard with little guidance. John is nice, but that is pretty much the only pro.

John edgar's midterm was very unreasonable. Considering we had no cheatsheet, no sample paper and tough coding questions which require you to think critically, I would not recommend John to anybody. I know I failed miserably even though i studied quite a lot. 15 mcqs + 2 difficult coding problems in 50 minutes is unfair.

Pros: Nice guy, cracks a few jokes during lectures. Has weekly quizzes on Canvas that are pretty fair (if you are keeping up, you should get at least 7/10). Assignments are pretty fair and requirements are well-organized. Cons: Midterm was just BAD. 10 page exam in 50 mins. 15 theory heavy MCQ (trick questions) and HARD written section.

John is a nice guy. But good god he needs to start learning how to use a microphone properly. You literally can't hear him if you are at any row other than the front few. His exams are also the dumbest thing ever - way too long and hard for a 50 minute exam. Reads off of slides during lecture in the most monotonous voice. AVOID!!

Honestly he was fine professor I thought, but his midterm was so far beyond unreasonable I'm genuinely shocked. Everyone I've talked to has said it was extremely hard and not proportional at all to lectures and assignments. There was so much on there that we didn't learn and had no idea what to study.

I would say he doesn't even care if you are listening to him or not. His mic is always turned off or doesn't work for some reason. He doesn't go much in depth compared to how difficult his exams and assignments are.

He taught this course with Tara and seemed to defer all assignments to her. Given Tara's incompetence, this was a disaster. While the TAs are undoubtably smart people, it's bizarre to have people who speak English as a second language be the markers for an English writing course. Predictably, there were all sorts of erroneous marking decisions.
Nice person but failed to explain many concepts clearly

John is a great lecturer, however, the assignments are rather difficult in comparison to what's covered in class. Good thing he responds to emails very quickly, even on weekends! Studying for exams is fairly challenging as there is so much content. The cumulative final has more than 600 slides worth of information. The class average was C+.

Excels at teaching concepts simply, makes everything accessible online, and has weekly quizzes that are useful to track your progress in the class. Midterm was straightforward however the final was unfortunately all multiple choice due to the TA strike. Overall a great prof though, one of the best Ive had so far no doubt.

During the first half of the term, he seems to be managing the course well. However, as the difficulty level increases in the second half, he appears to struggle to keep up. he hasn't adequately prepared for his lectures. when encountering challenging concepts he is not familiar with, he just quickly glosses over them.

Start assignments early as they are much more difficult than the examples provided in the slides. John provides pretty good lectures and the best part of the class was how responsive he is on Canvas. Exams are pretty fair, just make sure to read instructions carefully as they are similar to assignments, but there is a twist that can throw you off.

Thought this was going to be an easier given CMPT 225 reviews, but it seemed like a completely different prof. Decent guy, but as a prof he just reads off slides for the most part. This class requires a decent amount of review and the final had more NOSQL than expected. I couldn't take advantage of office hours, but seems available.

John was a decent prof, although the lectures did get quite boring at times. The weekly quizzes were basically free marks since they were open book. The assignments were doable. The midterm was not too hard but the final was very challenging. To John's credit, he was very accessible on Canvas discussion boards.

Low class averages on quizzes, many times a failing average. Average on the midterm was a fail, 8 pages in 50 minutes. If you went wrong on assignments it says you failed a test but does not specify how and where you went wrong so you can't improve your programming later on.

Workload was balanced, exam was really fair, responds to emails quickly, lectures are okay (could be more engaging and kind of just reads from slides), in-class activities for participation marks so you have to attend unless someone can get you the code for the day but really liked the course overall because John is super chill and friendly
He's a chill guy. Even a morning class never felt sleepy. Really help students.

The ppt slides are impressively well made. Assignments and quizzes help students learn the material. Sometimes felt the lecture covered too many details, but the exams were fair without trick questions. He's very responsive and helpful to student questions. I couldn't put much effort but could see he's a knowledgable and great prof!

John's lectures notes are already filled up; ready for you to write down anything you think is important. If you have time, pre-read the lecture material the day before class. Workload for me was about 12 - 15 hr/week (+/- 3 hr), so YMMV. Exams are fair. Just make sure you understand all the algorithms and can draw out their diagrams on paper.

Do NOT take his course. Weekly quizzes were brutal. I don't know if I really learned something in his class. Had spent so much time to review all his lectures, to practice everything he gave, plus some online practice, to ask questions in office hour... It turned out assignments, quizzes, exam, project grades were jokes to you.
Make sure you spell 14 as "fourteen" instead of "forteen" on your assignment. You will thank me later.
This professor is one of the best professors ever.
One of the best profs I had in SFU. Although the course had lots of stuff to do almost every week like quizzes and exercises but it was fun learning from his lectures.

John has a calming voice. sometimes he moans when he makes a mistake coding and cant find a solution, which catches me off guard but wakes me up during those 8:30 classes. He sometimes fake cries if someone leaves the room during the lecture but it shows he cares

John was probably my best prof this semester. Replies to emails within the hour. You should know at least a little bit of code before coming into this class. The final exam was fair, easier than I expected.

Perfectly paced lectures that are easy to follow and give a good understanding of the material. Four assignments are fun and reasonable, and the project was enjoyable with a month to do it. Weekly quizzes were annoying, they started off very difficult but he listened to requests to ease the difficulty and gave more time. I would recommend him.

Lecturer were super boring. Final was oddly difficult with some weird questions like query customer lastName "Trump" or Trigger a data attack on competitor. If you study hard, problem solve all the time, but you feel like your work still cannot be valued.

Good choice for 225. Failed my first attempt w/ another prof. John is better than that other prof. Responds quickly when you need clarification on asmt, even on weekends! Before John, none of my 225 asmt ever compiled. My prog. skills improved after taking his class :) Join the CSSS discord group, students there are helpful when you're stuck.

He is a cheerful professor but he gives a lot of work. His weekly quizzes were hard with a low average and assignments require a ton of work. His final was confusing for me and he doesn't teach much. You must be fluent in c++ before taking this class with him. I DO NOT recommend this class with him. It was a nightmare for me.

John is a solid option for CMPT 225. Responds quickly to questions, has fair assignments, and is a chill/funny guy. Weekly quizzes on the hard side sometimes, but are good for reinforcing concepts. Overall had a good experience - John did very well in the transition to online learning. Would take again!

John is one of those profs with which you get out what you put in. Weekly quizzes and exercises force you to be up to date with the material, which was great for a chronic procrastinator like me. Assignments are long and often difficult, but very thoroughly explained and fair (Start them early). Course was also overall really well-organized.

Let me start off by saying people did say Edgar was a good prof and he was difficult. But this class was just incredibly hard. Averages on quizzes were roughly 8/15. Exercises were not that hard but some were difficult. The first 2 assignments were easy. The red black tree took me 5 days to implement. Average was low on it. Incredibly high workload

His exercises are way to hard and time consuming. The quizzes we barely have time to finish and the average comes back to be 9/15. Like Edgar really needs to reconsider his grading and tests. Assignments arent bad. Lecture heavy and harsh grader for sure. I dont recommend taking a class with him

Took Cmpt 225 with him and it was actual hell. He had weekly quizzes that had close to failing averages every single week not to mention the insane time constraints. The assignments that you get from this class will take you at least a few days of grinding if you are comfortable with c++. Start them asap and make sure you test extensively

Doesnt teach the class. Most of the grading was for quizzes assignments and exercises. They were very time consuming. Took weeks for me as they seemed easy but werent. This class has a take home final not worth much but it was sort of difficult. The overall class is challenging if you arent on top of your c++ skills.

Edgar has many flaws in his teaching. Incredibly time consuming assignments (takes days) to his quizzes that are always on the new topic he teaches. You must keep up and understand everything of c++. Binary search trees, hash tables etc. If you didnt learn this in 125 or dont know much, dont take it yet. You will fail.

Crazy amount of hours put into a few assignments. He makes sure that if you want to take this class, you need to put in the work. Quizzes are a tad difficult as if you fall behind you will fail them for sure. You NEED to know c++ fluently before this class. Make sure if you can take it later with someone else, DO IT.

Quizzes every week. Assignments that will take many days. Final exam was a tad tough. Entirely this prof made this course much harder than its supposed to be. If you want to take CMPT 225, take it when hes not the prof. You will do better and have more fun. Edgar is a massive No. read the other reviews below and youll know what I mean.

Horrible prof. Will give u a 0 for the smallest mistake. Make sure to work on the assignments early, took me 4-5 days to complete them. The quizzes were annoying as you needed to keep up with all the material every week. He just read the notes , wasnt really teaching. Take it with a diff prof.

Harsh marker. I added an extra a in my name and it gave a compiling error and got 0. This isnt where u can email Edgar and say oh I only forgot this. U get 0 no matter what. Extremely high workload. Keep up ! Start weeks before as it will take that long to do. Those who know c++ should take 2-3 days to finish them.

Very very harsh grader. Lots of work. Lots lots of work. If you have a tight schedule, I recommend taking only 2 other classes with this one. The workload is a 5/5 for sure. Its like cmpt 125+127 all in one. Edgar doesnt even provide much details on the assignments in c++. He expects u to know it. MAKE SURE TO GO OVER UR C++!!!

Way to many quizzes. Need to stay on top as there is a quiz every week. Many assignments which will take 20+ hours for those who know there stuff. Maybe 35+ for those who dont know c++. Make sure if you take this class, that u always ask questions as he doesnt clarify anything. And know theres a lot of work.

Barely passed this class. Everyone in the other section had a blast doing this course while Edgar made it impossible to actually have fun. Way to many assignments . I highly recommend to not take any classes with Edgar. Save ur self the pain.

Lots of work. Get ready to learn the advanced algorithms of hash tables. If you are not 100% on top of C++, dont take this class. He doesnt go over it and our first assignment was creating a game with binary trees.

Lectures are quite boring and it just feels like he hates teaching. Does not explain material very well and doesn't answer questions clearly. Only took this class because of how difficult it is to get enrolled. Would recommend choosing a different prof if you had a choice.
His lecture presentation is terrible and it made you feel like you had a astriction

I learned a lot from this class! The exercises and assignments were straight forward and exams only being worth 30% made things less stressful. Be careful of the marking policies though. You can get a 0 over a typo in your filename causing a compiler error.

I really liked the structure of the class, you watch the video lectures, and complete a weekly quiz/exercise, which helped with the understanding of the content. The assignments were also really straightforward and clear. Some of the material was already taught in CMPT 125, but I felt like he explained the concepts better..

I was not even moderately prepared for his expectations. Each weekend had a quiz and a HARD exercise that you had to complete. His assignments are NOVEL length long and take forever. You basically have to interview him during office hours. Marking is dependent on TA, but never reconsidered by John. Red black trees still haunt my dreams.

I have had him for CMPT 354 and CMPT 225 with 354 being online. If you are good at CMPT and enjoy learning about the more niche parts of the course, you'll love it. However if you are only mediocre at it, you'll have an exponentially harder time compare to other profs. His exams and assignments really wordy/tricky with lots of trick questions.

He is the best professor that I've had so far at SFU. He is really clear about tasks you have to do each week, and also really helpful and accessible during the whole semester. We had an online class this semester and I hope I can take more classes with him in the future.
His videos are very vogue, almost everything in his assignments, you can't find it in his notes, and there are very few resources you can find by online or reading the textbook.

Taking CMPT 225 online with him. Really good professor. The lectures are well organized and clear. There's a short quiz and exercise each week, which covers the basic concept of the course materials and can be really good practice. Keep up with the course materials and you are good to go! Highly recommended.

Requirements of each of his assiments like a short story. I feel like I read a novel. It is easy to miss something. I also want to say he should pay more attention to each line of students' codes rather than file's name. You can get zero easily because of adding library even the whole code is fine.

I don't know there wasn't anything special from this prof, he was kind of all over the place and when you would ask him questions he would never give a clear answer and leave you more confused. No extra practice is available so it's hard what to expect is examined.

His pre-recorded videos are terrible since he sometimes speaks very quietly and sometimes very loud.. and tends to speak fast when the materials are harder to understand. His assignments are even worse, we had one exercise, one quiz, one report, and one assignment all due within a week.

Really nice professor overall, he's always willing to help through emails and in person. Lectures can be a bit dry but if you pay attention he explains everything well and can be funny. Labs are non mandatory, assignments are big but doable if you start early.

His lecture is amazing with perfect-organized slides. He shows everything step by step using a nice example(personly think an example is much more helpful than theorem), so you could understand even if you have no base.

You can tell by the way he presents himself in class and speaks with enthusiasm that he loves what he does. His method of delivering lectures is phenomenal and encourages questions in order to be crystal clear. His jokes lighten up the class and maintains a very welcoming atmosphere. I wish he taught every CMPT class offered at burnaby and surrey!

went into 125 knowing nothing extra and came out very confident with the material. His lectures get boring really quickly, and it's easy to miss details if you don't pay attention. I found his assignments very meaningful, so take the time to do them and don't rush them. Exams were very fair and based on the material and assignments.
Can't say enough good things about him. Awesome teacher, and just an awesome person in general.

He is good at explaining things and his notes are crystal clear and covers everything you need (you don't even need to come to class as long as you read his slides). However the assignments and midterm are quite tricky and he gives quite a lot of assignments compared to other profs.
He is on average not very clear. Of course, this is a beginners' class.
Had him for the joint CMPT 125 and 127 class, really straight forward and fair exams, just make sure you know how the all the algorithms work (visualization) and the rest is easy.

If perfection were human and had a British accent, John would be it. He is a fantastic lecturer who makes the class truly enjoyable. John's explanations make total sense and he is dedicated to helping students.

Easy final exam (the average was crazzzyyy high). His posted notes covered everything on the exams. Assignment were straightforward. Labs were never for any marks, yay! Would totally like to have this guy again.

John is an awesome prof with a great sense of humour! the funny thing about him is he never adds your name after the "hi" when he emails you back. 166 is hard for me since i have no interest in this material and this is just mandatory if joint major. anyhow John is funny and very willing to help. highly recommended lecturer!
Great Prof! His lectures are excellent.
He is an excellent professor... very helpful and his notes are just awesome! No need to go through any book, though he recommends one! He explains really well in class as well as his office hours.

He is very nice and funny person. keeps the lectures not boring. study the lectures and assignments for midterms and finals. Final is easier than midterm. He SCALES alot and teaches well. 225 is a hard course so make sure you take it with John Edgar.

One of the best teacher at SFU. His class is well organized: great lectures (you are learning great programming skill), good notes (but don't miss lectures!), good labs (content of labs are rich). Assignments and exams are challenging but if you follow lectures+labs and spend enough time, no need to worry to much about them. Just enjoy learning!!

He has interest in the course. Helps students, read his slides thoroughly if you want to get an A+. His slides are easy to understand but can be misleading if you dont attend the lectures. For midterms, many questions with little time to answer so start with those that you know. Assignments take so much time to complete, so start early.
A easy and useful course in CS.
His slide are f**king good, and he is the best teacher around!!! Top funny guy in SFU ~

In all my four years at SFU, John Edgar is honestly the BEST teacher of any class I've ever taken. He has a genuine interest in the subject, a great sense of humour and his lectures are extremely helpful. I was abysmal at all things computer-science, and now it all makes perfect sense. Do yourself a favour and take his a class with John Edgar.

this guy is the BOMB! Seriously, he's great. Lectures are never boring. Assignments are long and challenging, but well worth the work. The last part of the assignment (hardest part) is usually worth very little, only for the students that go for the A+'s
funny and extremely helpful~good prof...but honestly,i do not recommend people who have no programming background to take this course!

Amazing Prof...Very funny guy..will keep you interested by making fun of him self and bringing in mods of games he has coded. Very Helpful and material is not too tough...one of the best cmpt courses i have taken for sure
the course material was quite challenging, and the prof wasn't that good in explaining & teaching
Such a nice guy, extremely helpful. good sense of humor and of course, a good teacher.
His slide are crazie good, and he is the best teacheer around.......!!!!!!!!!!!!!
The man, even though he screws up, makes fun of the fact he screwed up and keeps going. got to enjoy it!

Wacky humour is good. Accent is fun. With previous experience in programming, I find his labs a snap, but people around me are floundering. He should be teaching higher level students- he doesn't quite know how to handle people who are clueless about programming.
The best professor I have ever had. Do take classes with him you won't regret it.
Summer semester, Surrey campus. Extremely helpful - writes lengthy documents to help us understand, beneficial to those who actually ask for help. Got my first A+ in programming!
I took this course a while back. His lectures are rather boring and uninformative. When asked questions, he would be like "i dont know" etc. Format of midterm vs final threw me off.
A very good teaching style. Agreed that this class is more difficult that Comp Sci majors will have you believe, though. Does he remind anyone else of Pete Townsend?

He dishes out far too much work that takes forever to complete. His classes are boring. He is constantly going off topic witterng about stuff that's happening in his life.I found him pompous. He never gave a straight answer to a question. Hated the class.
His homework is hard and takes forever to do, and very little people get full marks on them. The tests are fair
Gotta love that A-
Meh. He was alright as a prof. Must like droll British humour! Class was a little dry.

He is nice, but expects too high for the students and apprently he doesn't realize that what he's teaching is INTRODUCTION to VB. If you don't have any VB background, be prepared to drop the course. Notes are useless....and assignments are EXTREMELY MEAN!

vb should be an easy course!! but not w/him... notes and lectures are basically useless, recommend having some background knowledge in vb first, and READ the book. first assignment starts out super easy, but then.......

This course is way too hard thought it used to be easy. THIS SHOULD BE AN INTRODUCTION TO VB... NOTE: you have to spend at least a week, 4 hours a day, to do his assignment.. he's teaching cmpt254, he thinks that all of us are genius~
Does he even know VB? Anytime he was asked a question his answer was a laugh and I don't know ... useless lectures, long exams, hard assignments, bleh
No Comments
No Comments
Reading straight from the lecture notes was very interesting. :P
very unclear during lectures and jokes are not funny.
Knows his stuff, funny, approachable. Makes lectures interesting.
Good teacher and he is very approachable and helpful to his students
SFU shud hire more prof like John. He knows his DB and can teach!
Very humorous, and knowledgeable prof.
Very good notes. If evening class lectures become painfully long and boring. :)
Just read the book, he's British
Good prof, best exam marking time ever!
Terrible experience. Hate algorithm from now onwards.
Excellent Class and Excellent Prof

He is the best prof that fits in what I believe to be the best way to learn and be tested. He gives clear lectures which his exams are all based on them and the homework. Even he was old school using paper and pen in class, but he is the only one prof that I have taken who uses the recording system for us to review his lectures online. Take him!!!

CMPT 307 is hard, but he makes it as simple as possible. I am surprised that his explanations for Master Formula and SELECT are way better than the ones in the textbook. The weekly assignments roughly took 15 hours each, but they are the core for getting good marks on exams. Memorizing solutions usually doesn't work, but understanding logic does.
lots of homework; but still easy to get a high grade, and there's always a good curve.

Very knowledgeable and smart prof, so it's worth to attend and listen to him. The biggest problem of this prof is you won't be able to get complete answers for your assignments. He shows solutions for SOME questions but not all. So, you don't know if your answers are correct for the other questions (TA marks the questions the prof showed answers).

I took Combinatorics/Graph Theory from Prof. Hell 40 years ago, and it was (really!) life-changing. What I learned, esp. from programming assignments, made things easy in my 1st job as a database professional. More important, I learned teaching techniques I use all the time, and I ran business process imprvmt workshops on five continents last year.
He know his stuff. The course is very difficult and when you ask questions, he is definitely not encouraging. If the TA didnt help me understand some stuff, then probably would of failed.

Great proof. Killer final but very nice curve. As long as you attend all the lecture and take notes, you should be okay. most of exam questions are from the lecture notes and homework. overall great prof.

Hard course in general, and he's well-experienced teaching it. However I wish there'd been more feedbacks for students during the semester. Seeing my grades for assignments and midterm, it was not clear to me if I'd pass the course, which embarrassed me the whole time. But at the end I'm happy with my grade.

very hard course, one of the hardest among CMPT courses.his teaching skill is above average.The assignments are very time consuming (one assignment per week).exams seem intimidating, but actually fair after u take a closer look

Very good prof, The course is taught like a math course, weekly assignments and such (some of which are hard) but thats how you learn the material. He is also crystal clear in his lectures and very approachable for help during office hours.

Very good professor, but difficult course content. He explained everything very well. The homework was not to long but it was difficult. His midterm and final exam seemed intimidating at first but they were manageable.

Good teacher, hard topic, he did well to help most of the students pass and his class always has more people passing, so i guess that means he's easier or a better teacher than other people for this class
too many assignments! one assignment per week, and each assignment takes a average student about 8 hours to finish.Do u the math.avoid him unless u don't have any other choices.
He's sometimes kind of short-tempered. Besides he's like always in a rush, seems to have more serious commitments/affiliations somewhere else.
Knows the course material very well, and teaches the stuff better than anyone at SFU.
not quite an encouraging professor
Take him should you be hardworking.

He's a famous prof and a good teacher. Although he's moody and his presentation is a bit boring. There must be a problem with his ears or his english because he didn't get some of the questions during the lectures. Very hard homework questions but he solves them right after the deadline. Too much strict for homework deadline which is the very begin

3 hours lecture. dry but understandable... Requires alot of thinking and creativity. It always seems like hes jumping around topics and hard to follow if you are working through the textbook. Midterm consists of 50%+ homework questions... good for some, bad for others that don't like memorizing...
Great prof, the course is hard if you've never seen any of this stuff before but he does a good job with explaining it.

He's an awesome prof. Heaven is a good word to describe him. In what was always talked as the hardest CS class, he made it seem pretty understandable with simple concepts, etc. Everyone did well in his class. So take him!
A very good prof. Course is not bad, if you spent time do/understand all the assignments. I think it's good that he made the noisy people be quite.
lectures with clarity! easy to understand, great prof
He is not "HELL" at all!!!
Good prof good lectures
He is the best MACM prof ever!! The stuff is hard, be he teaches it well
easy course, well taught

He so was the Count. Good prof, fair assignments and exams. Take 201 with him.
Looks like a German composer, sounds like the Count. Good prof!

you have to really work hard!
best
Ruiwen is a good prof. He can sometimes be unclear at time but overall he's good at explaining concepts. He's also a very easy prof!

This class is better if you have previous knowledge of programming in Java. I took this class as a refresher course , and compared to other Intro to Programming classes I've taken at other schools, you're mostly on your own here, and the lectures don't go as in depth as they should. But I suppose for such a large class, its difficult.
Lecture is useless
So easy and fun class. Absolutely loved the way he used google docs for assignments with comments and discussions. Got an A and never used a textbook, all from his slides and some google =)

aw he's such a sweetie! explains, posts helpful hints and answers to assignments. only thing is that some assignments were extremely hard compared to the final. not doable for a new programmer (they were what brought my mark down). final was fair unlike other cmpt courses. 3 page double sided cheatsheet, YES. STUDY LOTS.

Sessional instructor, originally from China. English is poor, but he does his best to explain everything in a clear manner. Very good for first time teaching a course. Only 1 or 2 classes were taught poorly. Extremely prompt in replying to emails and good office hours.
very clear, very helpful
Gives you interesting problems to solve for assignments. Always willing to help and answers emails very fast.

Overall I enjoyed this course and learned a lot about basic programming concepts from Dr. Havens. The assignments were interesting and challenging. However this isn't a course you can leave until the last minute and cram for. If you study diligently and attend classes regularly, it's not too hard to succeed in this course.

He's such a terrible prof. Seriously save your money and time by taking this course with another prof. Assignments are really difficult and his exams are tough. If you plan on taking this course with him you should take a light course load or drop it before it's too late.

His midterms are difficult considering the amount of time given and his assignments are beyond the scope of completion. Amongst all that, he schedules his office hours during our lab time. Basically all he does is reiterate what is already in the book. He'll just basically read everything off of the screen. Avoid him and take it with someone else.
He's a very nice prof. We had two programming projects and both were very interesting. You'll enjoy his lecture even if you are not really into the materials.

Excellent Prof. If you are interested in a masters this is the guy to be a student for. He treats his masters students like friends and works hard to help them achieve. All his students go on to do great things....

Havens flip flops more than John Kerry. If you ask him how he grades an assignment, get it in writing, because its likely he'll forget what he said to you. Also, he's an american who wants to be a canadian.
Excellent professor. Very enjoyable class.
hard assignments, easy exams given you have a very very good understanding of his notes.

Bill is a great teacher for this course and actually has tried to teach programming, not just copying code samples. Yes, the course isn't easy, but at least unlike every other course I've taken, he teaches it in a fun way with interesting assignments
Too Hard. An assignment was given about Constraints Satisfaction Problem (CSP) which is a upper division AI's problem.
Great Prof. Was able to communicate quite well. Lectures were usually well structured..
He's pretty good, his final was okay I guess... except that I wish I could stay awake in his classes though zZz :)
No Comments
Great lectures, unclear assignments. Doesn't put enough time into his assignments/prep.
So far so good. I enjoy the class, he makes it interesting
good prof~~
one of the best CS prof i've had in SFU

Knows his stuff, can't convey the knowledge. Beware of Harold Hophozophen.
The dude looks like President Sinclair from Babylon5....'nuff said, great prof
GOOD TEACHER but very hard better avoid him
AAAHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH
Explains concepts very well
good prof.. just focus on recursion
good teacher

For fall 2025, the final project was worth 45% of the total grade, but the class mean/median was only 30/45 and the HIGHEST score was just 36, with no curve applied. Grading felt extremely harsh, see many solid projects presentations yet ended up as mediocre grades.

Good professor, easy understanding lectures, but TOO MUCH MATERIALS! There are almost 100 pages slides to read every week, though the assignments and final exam were ok but still too heavy workload. You might not use all of the materials in your 4 assignments but they might be in your final exam.

Great lectures, she knows what she's doing. You'll have to self-study a lot of the math because she usually skims over it. The course is inherently difficult and covers what feels like 1.5 or 2x the material of other 300 level courses but this prof is good.

Based on my experience, she's the only professor who offered us computational resources for our projects. She expected us to deliver excellent work, yet she's considerate about each students' background. Overall, I believe that she cares about students.

She is a good lecturer, however, I did find myself needing to go to the textbook and slides to truly understand the concepts covered. She is very fair with regards to due dates and it is obvious she cares about making sure the class is a good experience for the students. We had three assignments (cut down from 4) and a final exam.
A very fair workload and puts in the effort to lecture well. Lecture recordings were not made available.
Quite good

The course was quite fun, and challenging, a good combination. The assignments always took longer than I expected, graphics are hard, so start early. They were cool though (tetris, golden bunny). She also nicely spaces them, and the lectures/notes and extra tutorial help alot.

Basically a good prof. A lot of programming and writing assignments during the semester, but she will intentionally make the final exam easy . She will organize a field trip too. Taking 3D graphics with her is not a bad choice.

Pros: Helpful if you go to office hours. Nice person, polite and caring. Cons: Accent makes it difficult to understand. Often conveys information in a roundabout and confusing manner. Self-study is pretty much necessary.

Hard to understand but not too too bad. Used another teacher's Powerpoint notes for every lecture, expanded upon them, but not in any really enlightening ways. Constantly made mistakes. Expect to learn everything on your own. I'd really really recommend studying in advance of the course. 25% of last year's students got D or lower. 27% got C- to C+
WHAT A SHAME! She copied everything from an equivalent course in UBC CPSC304!! EXACTLY SAME FINAL EXAM QUESTIONS! SAME TYPOS!!

Incredibly inconsistent marking. I did homework with a few friends, had the same answers and each person obtained a different mark with substantial deviation. Tests are easy, but this was due to studying via Googling everything that was taught in the curriculum. You could potentially attend zero classes and do well in this course.
Not a very good instructor, but the class is easy and you can self-study. Her marking is too nitpicky for my liking. I wouldn't recommend.

She teaches the basics and throws a challenging assignment to finish. The exams were okay, but I had to study hard for them. She also got my mark wrong several times, so I had to visit her office often. Would be a good TA not a lecturer. Looks like it's either Jane or Bobby that teaches 125 so take it with Bobby and avoid Jane at all cost.

Her accent is fine. But she does not know how to teach. Focusing more on the concepts which can be be explained in a few mins. I think I will fail on this course. I understand the concepts but don't know how to programming. But 125 is not a hard course, just don't take this with her. She assumes that you guys have already known everything.

The almost worst Prof u can get at SFU. Very vague grading criteria, explaining concept really unclearly. Her accent is not as bad as people say, but she always make grammatical mistakes when she is talking. Assignments are extremely hard while midterms are ok, the whole semester is a joke with such an instructor. Strongly suggest avoiding her!

She seemed nervous that's all. At the end she started loosening up and making jokes. Her accent is way better than people are saying! I do think the lectures need restructuring, I liked your overhead lectures better (should be posted with explain) because the ppnts are too layered and cluttered. Focus more on explaining detailshard concepts.

Jane is even worse in 354 than 125. Relational databases and mySQL should be taught by native or near-native English speakers. Her expectations are unclear but her marking is diabolical. The textbook is an easy read. I agree that Langara CS faculty are better; I honestly think FAS cares more about number of publications than English proficiency.

1-All the people giving bad reviews are ina first year compsci course. Guys computer science is hard you have to learn how to manage these university classes. 2-Yes she has an accent but I did not struggle in understanding her I think major exaggeration is being shown I had her for 354 and gave her an ok review cant write more

Apologies everyone for not being a very good instructor. Being an ESL and first time managing a huge class like this is difficult. I just wanna say "sorry" to all the haters and the good news is I am moving to industry. After all, it is still a good experience and I will continue working on the rest of the term.
Can't understand what she's saying.
Worst prof ever, you can't even understand what she's saying. Trust me when I say this, don't take this class with her
Boring and useless class. Better study by yourself than going to her lectures.
AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Absolutely horrendous professor; worst I've had in my 4 years of university. She has an elementary understanding of the English language and does not teach the topics well. You will need to Google topics to learn anything as her lectures are absolutely useless; including the provided lecture slides. Most of her 'help' is telling you to look it up.

Not being a jerk or anything, but honestly the single worst prof in my life as being a student. She does not speak English well enough to teach at a elementary level, let alone a University level. Also, she is unclear and gets flustered easily, often leading her to skim over topics. There is no way she should be teaching at any level. Avoid!
I've had much much better CS instructors at Langara. She's awful at teaching.

Her command of English is inadequate for such an important course and she's teaching CMPT 125 in the Spring. I've complained to the department chair Dr. Ester as well as FAS, but they don't care that their faculty cannot teach or speak English. Let the Peak and CJSF know about this, and consider taking this course at Douglas or online via TRU.

Very poor English skills make her class very hard. She is so hard to understand and it causes her to not always make sense. Not sure why she's teaching in an English speaking school. You have to learn from the text book to make it make sense. Probably the worst prof I've had.
Pros: She posts lecture notes online, which are all you need. Cons: Hard to understand in lecture. She marks pretty brutally on the assignments too. Very unclear on her expectations.

It will be easier if she could save same words in notes and solutions. But overall, the lecture is clear if you keep following her. Notes are covered all the staff that you need to know. The tutorial questions and assignments are relevant to the topics in class and are helpful for exams. Make an appointment, she is there to help.

Not very good at teaching. She doesn't give good explanation about the material. I found that the videos I can watch online on the topics are way easier to understand than her lecture. Textbook is required since she doesn't give good explanations. Her assignments are not too bad. AVOID IF POSSIBLE.

Pros: Posts lecture notes online, posts sample midterm, provides solutions to homework and midterm. Cons: Words questions in unclear manner; unresponsive to questions; very unclear in explaining class material; poor English skills; difficult assignments; unclear on what she expects from you.

Overall Jane is not a very good professor. Her pros are: She makes some effort to make lecture entertaining, posts her notes online, and gives a sample exam. Her cons: Very unclear speaker, class structure is horrendously disorganized (made us read in an order explicitly advised against by the book), and her homework is obscenely difficult.
Has a bad accent making her tough to follow in lecture. She isn't very clear on what to study for the midterm and final. I'd avoid if you can.

Really good professor and he knows his stuff really well. The class homework and assignemnts are not hard. The final was very fair and you can do well if you can understand the homework. The midterm was a little challenging. The course is very interesting and Professor Wang is really good. Highly recommend.

Haskell changed my life. Before: miserable, sad, depressed. After: enlightenment, free from the cycles of suffering. He (in my opinion) has a very deep understanding of functional programming/Haskell and really cares about communicating abstract topics in ways that are easier to understand. Who wouldda thought that abstract topics are abstract.

Prof. Wang's lectures are a bit dry and abstract. It is hard to understand everything in the lecture, but the homework helps with understanding. Homework and midterm are reasonably challenging, and the final projects are graded very generously. The course is interesting and provides a very important foundation for advanced software engineering.

Dr. Wang is a very good professor. He understands the course material and he can explain it clearly. He is very willing to help if you have questions on your assignments. You should expect a relatively heavy workload in this course. The material is kind of abstract. Don't take it if you don't have a solid background.
Informative notes. Timely responses. Always ready to provide feedbacks and help people. Great professor that you don't want to miss at SFU!

This is a challenging course with tons of assignments and hard quizzes, but if you survive you will get a lot out of it. Unlike the other sessions (which are basically functional programming bootcamps), Professor Wang diverts to covering abstract topics in the latter half of the course, such as Semantics Verification. Overall, great experience!

Unlike previous 383 classes, this class with Yuepeng is purely Haskell. The lectures are very abstract and hard to follow, there is weekly homework, as well as extremely difficult programming assignments. Quizzes were very unreasonable, both had a failing average. Overall, Yuepeng is nice and is trying to improve. He takes class feedback seriously

Haskell only. Very reasonable individual assignments that only require concepts from the slides/lectures. Lots of homework assignments, but each one can be finished quickly. Can skip class and just read slides for all information. Does not answer Piazza questions well.
Great person, really helpful and easy to reach anytime

Ben is passionate about what he teaches. His explanations during lectures can be hard to follow, you will have to watch YouTube videos. The course closely follows slides provided by Brad Bart, another prof at SFU. Ben is very approachable for office hours and even post-lecture. Asking Ben questions will help you a lot. Poorly organized final exam.

He loves what he teaches and has a genuine passion to teach. He tries his best to ensure everyone gets the content. Will make extra effort in the office hrs to make you understand everything. He enjoys algorithms and will make you enjoy learning them too. Very caring and kind. Puts a lot of effort to make the class interesting.

This was his first time teaching his own class, and while he did have some trouble at the beginning of the year due to his inexperience, he has gotten a lot better at teaching. He cares very much about the class and tries his best to make sure we understand the content His assignments are also very fair, and correspond with what was taught in class
She was great. Long time ago!
Wow this was ages ago. I remember it was a great course and a great prof.

She is a great instructor, her notes are clear and easy to follow. As long as you attend class and follow the examples she solves in class you should expect at least a B. Many of the questions she includes on her quizzes and tests are taken directly from class notes.
Awesome prof.
I actually remember this course quite well. She was obviously knowledgable. And I think this was her 1st time teaching so she wasn't 100% ready every time but good overall.
I just stumbled upon this page and remembered her. Good course!

I took this class ages ago! I was just looking at some of my SFU prof's pages on RMP. As most of people mentioned here, the prof was very nice. Fair amount of assignments and I remember exams were similar to the problems in the assignments.
I just stumbled upon this page. Don't remember much about the course (few years ago). She was nice and the exams were fair. A lot of assignments but it was worth it. Overall no complaints.
Don't remember much but a fairly easy course.
Good course.
Took this course some years ago while in Simon Fraser University. Got an A (or A+, can't really remember). Overall, an easy course.
I took this course a couple years ago. It was easy and the professor was nice :-)
She was a very kind woman. Needs more practice in teaching, but overall a good course.
Very nice professor. A+!
She is very nice. Overall this was a very easy and useful course.
She is a good teacher. And she is very kind. Also she gives good marks!
So glad i knew half of the material before the course, HORRID PROF.
a good woman, dedicated pro, but relatively not good at teaching
Awful professor, hardly teaches, can't make the course matter interesting to save her soul.

She can't teach but the assignments and labs are pretty easy. Stutters a lot during lectures and because of it, less than 50 out of 120 students bother attending her lectures. Only reason to attend is for possible quizzes for bonus marks otherwise you're better off just reading the lecture notes she provides, at home.

Quite a terrible professor. What these other commenter's don't realize is that while the power point slides (not her own) that she reads out in class may be well be well made. However, she JUST reads them.. thats it.. she knows very little on the subject and she has a poor reading voice to top it off. You basically have to run off you own steam.
If you just read the notes, and understand them (not a difficult task, if you're not stupid) and attend her lectures for possible quizes you can easily get an A.
Good overall. Good for your GPA if you know DS. There are bonus marks if you attend the class (bonus quizzes) which make it easy to get an A.

I got an A- by attending the class less than 10 times (as it was boring). Ppt notes were quite well (not done by her). She basically just reads off the powerpoint for the entire lecture. Her assignments were fair. She's quite helpful. Recommend for students who have good cs basics and don't mind doing some independent studying.
one word horrible.my gpa is dropped down.
i don't like the way her taught the class. her class is a good sleeping pill. i would rate the hardness of her exams: 7.5/10 (not that easy but fair)
her lectures are very monotonous. the powerpoint slides come straight from the textbook. I'd say her exams are fair and the assignments are tedious but not that bad.
I cannot understand a word she says and is always going "um" and "ah" and "euh." Her lectures are totally boring and she shows no creativity or interest whatsoever.

Dr.Cameron's lectures are really boring and hes very unclear about his criterias. The grading feels random and not based on actual criteras he mentions in class. Quizzes every week. Heavy work course. He's very helpful on technical problems however. Great person, not a good prof.

Always held a grudge against this thing but writing a review now to critique him on his poor planning skills. Delayed an exam without permission and let Janice make unallowed changes to grading scheme. He's a reminder that some people can bs their way through parts of life by negatively affecting other's lives and no consequences will follow.

Actual fraud. I'm not even joking. Its the equivalent to taking an advanced calculus class only for the prof to go over addition and subtraction. He's also just a bad human being overall. I used to wonder why so many students say terrible things about him or talk behind his back but I now know why.
Janice and Robert! The doom duo! The world has never seen anything like these two!

The course involves an android app group project. Teammates are random. Prof is a nice guy but he's not clear about what he wants in each iteration of the project. His lectures are horrifically dry. Would highly suggest you take CMPT 213 with this course, because if you don't know Java you'll have to learn both Java and Android on your own.

I truly wonder why he chose to become a professor even though he doesn't like it. I learned absolutely nothing about software engineering. His criteria is ambiguous for the group project and he doesnt help out at all. Dont take the course with him, save your money from this fraud of a prof. Ive never disliked a prof until now.

Rob does not respect your money or time, in this course you're expected to write an entire android app with 3 other students while Rob shows you ted talks about how AI driven cars should run straight into a wall if there are pedestrians. He also will just leave half way through to China and not plan lectures around that. Seriously don't take.
