Honestly dont take tis class with him. i have nothing against him but u honestly wont learn alot

The worst prof ever. He just 'assumes' that every student in his class is an expert in python. He doesn't go over the basics of programming and doesn't hesitate to screw you over with those hard tests, labs, assignments. He just can't teach.
Do yourself a favor and stay away. If they don't offer CMPT 120 in Burnaby with any other professor, it is seriously worth it to take CMPT 120 at SFU Surrey with someone who knows how to teach.

Chris is the worst prof I've ever encountered. He has no knowledge of the material covered in this course. He is very unclear when explaining things. He has no teaching skills whatsoever. He simply makes powerpoint slides from a studyguide that another professor wrote for the same course. He makes no effort to help students who have questions.

The prof doesn't know how to teach, he just assume all other students are expert. this course is just an introductory course to programming, and he never explain through examples, only main theories which we can just read off the textbook... the assignments are extremely hard, tests are so hard too.. avg was very low~ OVERALL WORST PROF I HAVE HAD!

Went to the first few lectures and couldn't read the handwriting. The assignments were marked very easily and the average was always ~95%. However... the 3 quizzes (mini-midterms) and the final are the hardest things I've ever done in my life. Try your best to understand the tutorials and assignments for them. Discrete Math II on steroids.

Math/proof-heavy, so it's challenging, but it's also very rewarding. If you put in the effort, you'll learn a ton and your fundamentals will get a lot stronger. Sharan is super knowledgeable and explains things clearly, grading is transparent with solid feedback, and there are usually a few extra credit opportunities too.

The instructor is generally caring with quick email responses. Grading is not tough, and the course is curved at 1.05 for a better grade. All lecture slides include detailed notes are uploaded to Canvas, so it's easy to review even if you miss a class. However, both the final exam and quizzes are quite challenging, plz be very well prepared.

Very difficult midterms(1,2,3) and an equally difficult final. Avg for all testable was around 50% and final was 42%. Very difficult class you really got too put in the time. His notes are summarized notes of the textbook, textbook overall has better explanations imo.

Sharan was kind enough to drop the grade scale so that more people could pass. He also scaled final grades by 1.05x. However, this was due to impossible exams that no one could comprehend. He responded fast on piazza, had decent homework, tutorials were helpful, but the final exam was impossible. It was scaled and still had an average of 42%.
Honestly a goated prof. The textbook he uses is great so you can learn quite a bit off of that, in addition to the lectures. Tests are challenging but not unreasonable.
The structure is easy to follow. The notes will be posted so you don't need to worry if you miss the lectures. BTW his hand-writing is awful.

He basically uses the textbook questions on his exams by tweaking their wording to an unreasonable level, hence the averages of the exams stay at 50%. He's a good lecturer and responds on Piazza often, but he needs to improve his skills on writing exams and ensuring other people could comprehend too. This is the reason his exams take long time.

Nothing against this professor, but his exams are unreasonably difficult/unforgiving with a severe time constraint. His lecture slides are mathematically rigorous, yet often lacks cohesion with tutorial/test material. Easily the most difficult course I have taken.

Hes a reasonable person but exams are hard like why not just test people from what they learned. What is the point in making the exams hard atleast make it fair. The TAs dont give any part marks either.

His lectures are good but the problem is that he teaches you questions that are way easier than what he expects you to solve. Intuition for solving problems is also something he didnt really give much of. Brutal final exam avg was around 40%

Hardest class I've taken so far. His slides are too cluttered, and homework was tough but doable, exams were brutal (midterm avg: 50%, final avg: 40%). He pulls questions from MIT-level sources. Also, he can be rude if you give an “interesting” answer. Even scoring above avg, I got a B-. Not a GPA booster! Curve was just 1.02x.

Sharan explains topics very well, and he tries to make the lectures engaging. He also replies really quickly to questions on piazza. But the exams were tough, especially the finals. Maybe don't make 30% of the final about a topic that we've learned 3 days ago on the last day of class. Overall, good prof but hard class

He cares for the students and listens to our feedback. He posts answers to hws and tuts. All material in lectures relate to what we need to know and he goes through many examples. This class was hw heavy but the hw was not bad. He knows what he's doing and also enjoys it. Too bad the class was at 830 and I found the material difficult.

He's literally so smart. His lectures are basically repeating the textbook but he makes it very understandable. Assignments were difficult but graded easily. Go to office hours as much as possible for assignments since they give big hints towards the answers. Test questions came from the textbook and very difficult. Do the textbook questions.

Sharan Vaswani is pretty ehhh. Assignments make up a lot of the mark and are really difficult especially if you dont have friends to chat with or use ChatGPT to get an idea of wtf to do. The lectures are meh, explanation of concepts is ok but could be much better imo. Overall kinda meh course with this prof

Chill prof who responds to messages very quickly. Slides are very neat and he shares annotated versions of them. Allows a cheat sheet for the final, which was really nice, as the exams were pretty difficult. The assignments, worth 45% total, were pretty lenient in marking, resulting in extremely averages (which were brought down by exams marks).

The lectures are pretty good and course policy in general is quite lenient (lecture/tutorial participation not mandatory, weighted passing average on exams not needed to pass the course, detailed solutions to assignments/tutorials posted consistently), but the sheer difficulty for a lower division course makes me unwilling to recommend to take

I think he's great, I like him a lot. Knowledgable and clear lecturer. The only downside is that his confidence in us is a little bit high, definitely not an easy course by any stretch of imagination and the midterm was also hard given the time constraint. Hope he makes the final a little more reasonable. But overall, not bad! Thanks prof!

Great professor, his lectures are pretty good as he provide the theory and then does some application with them, which is appreciated versus just spitting out theory like other profs. Assignments and tutorials are hard, but set you up well for tests, which are VERY HARD. But otherwise, he is a good lecturer and I'd recommend taking him for 210.
Great Prof! One of the best ones at sfu.His lectures are thorough. He focus on explaining concepts rather than providing formulas to solve problems. Exams and assignments are tough.

His lectures are some of the best, the concepts were explained clearly. But, the tests are just too difficult. Expect similar question difficulty to the assignments, i.e. don't expect questions where you only have to apply one theorem, or even know which to apply. Overall you learn a lot, because you are forced to understand everything well.

Genuinely nice guy. Very accessible through office hours and piazza. Lectures are very thorough but do little to build intuition so can be hard to follow. You'll have to do lots of self-study to fully understand concepts. Homework and exams are very difficult.

Very knowledgeable, you will learn a lot. Yet unprofessional, mean and condescending to students, meanwhile like to use gaslighting techniques to ensure itself to look innocent. Unclear and ad hoc grading criteria, and able to make very good use of its roles' administrative power to make students suffer. Recommend to avoid at all cost.

The course covers optimization in a modern ML setting, with an emphasis on proving why things happen. You'll learn a lot and get better at math, but will spend more time than expected doing it. Grading is very fair. Overall strong recommend!
Difficult, but you'll learn a lot. It's worth taking.

Course material: very good. A combination of basics and frontier research topics. You can't find a textbook to learn these by yourself. Workload: much heavier than normal 3-credit courses. It requires extra motivation and commitment, but you did learn a lot. Logistics: A little bit disorganized. Hope for improvements in following iterations.

The problem sets are fairly difficult but were very useful for grasping the material. There's a final group project where you improve on a recent optimization paper. Overall a lot of fun, but probably more work than three credits.

Took his class in his first teaching term and I though he did pretty good. He had a piazza and office hours where he would always answer question quickly and his website included all the lectures filled in and posted along with free textbook pdfs and the chapters + the chapters we should review. Tough tests, but good class overall, learned a lot.
Good prof. learned much and very well related cmpt125 to 225
I took his cmpt125 java class! His exams are relatively easy. He follows the textbook very well. He is very generous on grading.
A very good instructor!! definitely recommended!

If you've done at least a bit of VB, you'll find this course easy. It's intended for non-computing students and assumes no prior computing knowledge. I was actually somewhat surprised at getting an A+, but I think the class was scaled (to make it fair, as many computing majors took the course). Final exam average was over 100%.

Lectures are very boring and filled with a lot of material we didn't need to know for tests. Lectures are not recorded so i would recommend going. Coding projects were not bad and not harshly marked. Exams were similar to homework questions and he told us mostly exactly what would be on it. Overall a nice prof but bad lectures
Very boring and monotone lectures. And if the difficulty of the lectures was 2, then the exams would be 12. Not fun. Very tough grader.

This class was my first 300 level course, it was really bad. Can't stay focused for more than 10 minutes really boring. Get ready to study everything by yourself, exams are hard, the grading is crazy tough, but it isn't that hard to learn on your own, don't expect to learn that much. The way he replies to emails is unprofessional & annoying.

He is very knowledgeable in his area of research, and the topics taught are interesting. Thanks to the circumstances at SFU the difficulty was SIGNIFICANTLY lower - a lot of extensions and watered down quizzes. Textbook did a poor job at explaining things; slides might be better but not by a lot. Had to constantly refer to external sources.

He was a great professor. He really cares about the content and wants you to do as well. He is clear about his expectations and is fair at the end with grading. He is pretty strict with presentation and has high standard. You will be highly tested on what is said in class, so get ready to attend every session if you want a good result.

Pretty low effort prof. Bad lecture notes that are organized terribly. Very silly assignment deadlines and other course component deadlines at the same time in the same day (6pm lol). Intentionally feels like he makes easy content harder by doing a bad job of explaining it or assuming that the people he is teaching are knowledgeable on said topic.

Lectures are pretty dry and due to a lack of recordings, you have to go in-person to them. Assignments were pretty easy and exams were based off lectures + written assignments making them feel very fair. Overall, the class was informative and I enjoyed the assignments because they applied the topics we learned to real world applications.
Professor JC is really nice person. Hard work, then you can get what you expect.

Very informative. Course itself can be dry, but comprehensible. Read textbooks to get more background knowledge. He is clear on what he expects on exams. Overall, I enjoyed his lectures and his programming assignments were interesting too. Defitely doable!

First, take math 242 before this. It's not listed as a prereq but it should be. JC teaches the course with a focus on how to design multimedia applications rather than using them, which is really interesting. Lots of material but for exams you only need to remember what he stresses. The projects can be challenging. You get back what you put in.

JC is a quite helpful and nice prof. The two projects are related to the course content and you can learn a lot if you really put effort on them. The exam is fair and not difficult if you know concepts well.

He's wonderful. His midterms and finals are pretty easy. Almost two thirds students finished the final exam in just one hour. If you always attend his lectures, you will find that several final questions that look difficult have been discussed during lectures. You cannot benefit from that if you only attend few lectures.

All the material is covered in his lectures and slides. Exams are based on the assignment and the projects are not very difficult. The prof is helpful and can always reply your emails very soon. A quite nice and interesting Chinese guy:) I'd love to take his courses in the future.

Most things covered in lectures are not difficult. Midterms are based on the homework and his lecture slides. DO NEED to attend lectures as he will tell you what you need to memorize and how the exam will be organized. The projects are interesting and not that difficult.
Very good professor. I am now very interested in networking related works.

one of the best professors I have met. attending class is important since he will tell you what will be covered in the exam . also, he is very glad to answer your questions and always replies your e-mail in 24 hours.
he's cute. anyway u never have 2 listen 2 his lectures, they r jus helpless. better spend time on the textbook and exams become so easy.

This class could be good. Making JC teach it makes it horrible. One of the worst profs I've had in my 4 years here. He actually knows his material, but his assignments are so old that most people copy them, and his projects are useless and also mostly copied. This means those of us who bust our asses to do it without cheating get penalized.

this prof is very smart,but he has hard time explain to students things himself understands well.And sometimes pronounce words wrong, like he pronounce "genuine" like "genius" .The exams are fair, but assignments and projects could be very time consuming I think if you r his graduate student, he will be more helpful.

Smart prof. but have a hard time explaining things. You're better off reading the book. Extremely easy assignments and relatively easy exam. You need not memorize or understand everything to pass. Though grading is low. Average and median grade of class is 85/86.
Good professor, and very easy course, seems that almost all students get 80+
Beware - 80% overall = C-
Wonderful professor! You should choose his courses without any hesitation!

The midterm was too long and I wasn't even close to finished. He gave an assignment due 2 days before the final, and a group project due after all the final exams. He was fair on the final grade though. If you plan on taking him, just be careful of this poor scheduling.

Somehow I thought he was going to teach another easy class. I had him for MACM 101 and it was fine. For CMPT 371, for some reason the class was just so ridiculously hard. His exams are nothing like questions in the book or asked on the assignment. I want to cry from his final.

Not so much an easy course, made especially tough by him. Lectures get kind of boring. Exams are extremely difficult, but he ends up bell-curving it a lot. He taught us materials that were not in MACM101 curriculum. However, I actually appreciated that he did so when I took MACM 201 (had an even worse prof). He's one of the more decent prof in SFU

he's well prepared for the lectures. no class before exams. the three assignments worth a lot. attending his lecture will make his powerpoint notes more clear. he gives in-lecture examples that appear in final. he scales the class quite a lot. he does not intend to fail anyone. final exam class average is 30% but he bell curves it to a B.
Worst possible person to have for MACM 101.

Lectures are all done in powerpoint, with the professor reading them off. Midterms and exams are very hard. This professor also taught us graph theory, which is not a part of the course, but in MACM201. This was not covered in the final exam, and was a giant waste of time. Before assignments are due, TAs would show students the answer book.

Very nice man. The average is around B. This course should be at an elementary level but JC sometimes didnot make himself quite understandable. Maybe slightly difficult for freshmen. But overall good prof and easy credit.

Lectures are very organized,notes are extremely helpful. Mid terms and assignments are so easy but the final is a killer,hard for most of the students, class avg around 35%, luckily I aced it and an easy A+ for me.
Very boring professor. Sometimes its really hard to stay awake in his class. He claims that that most difficult questions are easy. dont take MACM as long as he is teaching.

Very poor lecturer. Extremely boring. Heavy chinese accent. It is not fair that students marks should suffer due to a bad professor. Apparently high quality education is a thing of the past. Faculty in the Computing Science at SFU is a joke.

If JC is teching MACM in the semester you want to take it, wait until the next semester when someone else teaches it. Or even try taking it by distance education. What I am trying to say is that JC Liu is a horrible, horrible professor.

His heavy accent is a bit distracting when you are constantly trying to figure out what he is saying throughout the whole lecture. There are profs that make sure the material they teach is clear and care if there student get it. JC is not one of those professors. Heads up.. Stay away.
Very boring lectures. The class content was actually supposed to be interesting, but he made it exceptionally bland!

His accent takes getting used to, but you'll be glad your in his macm101 class! His power points are very good and in lecture he actually does go into detail into the points which he tests you on. So study the slides, listen to his lectures, do the assignments(practice/marked) and you should do well! it takes practice, but once u get it its easy.

It takes awhile to get used to his accent but overall, it's a fairly easy course. Powerpoint lecture notes are extremely helpful. Sometimes it's hard to stay awake. Make sure to go to class before midterms and finals. He gave us some really good sample questions for the final which happened to pop up in some from on the final.

It's possible to do very well in this class without ever attending a lecture. (Lectures are incredibly boring; bring a pillow if you plan on attending.) PowerPoint notes are usually very clear and easy to understand. An good prof, if you have the will-power to actually listen to him during class.
Very good professor if you pay attention in class. Takes a while to get used to his accent, but the powerpoint notes more than makes up for that.
He is a great prof compared to other profs in CS...
Hard to pay attention in class.

His own cell phone rings during class so he can't fault students when their phones ring. Rubbish as a prof. though. Only reason did well in the course is because I was taking Math 242 concurrently. Otherwise would have lost interest. Relatively ok exams, but harsh marking. Exceptionally smart but unable to bring himself down to 1st yr stud level.
He is obviously very intelligent but his english is poor and he is a poor orator as well.
This guy is so boring. I hate his voice. Falling asleep in class
Great Prof., very smart, very helpful, I recommend taking his graduate course on multimedia networking...
Unbelieveably easy!!!

Jim did not put in much effort for the class. The notes were poor, and many things were not explained. His assignments were not sufficiently explained, and he did not give good feedback. He packed in too much content for the course, as it should be an introduction to AI.

The only instructor who are not willing to help students during my 4 years university life. However, he can't even explain his own slides and textbook questions properly in class. Get ready to teach yourself.

His lectures are average. Biggest problem is he doesn't like answering questions. Often times, he gives non-answers, shrugs, walks away, and tells us "that should explain enough", which it clearly logically doesn't. I sense a hint of animosity or condescendence from him, or perhaps it's just a manifestation of his lack of passion towards teaching.

1. Not accessible after class. Gives non-answers and will walk away from you. Quite rude 2 Lectures are theoretical with little examples. The examples given are a mess 3 Marking is terrible 4 Unresponsive to email 5 Changes assignments after they were given without notifying 6 Struggled to provide solutions in class to his own test AVOID HIM

Jim is paid 6 figures to do squat. His lectures are boring ; reads from his slides and gives no examples. He covers concepts and not applications but his assignments and exams are all about applications. The guy could barely solve his own midterm in class; man's not helpful. For those wanting to take 310, don't take it with Jim.

Lectures were dry and unorganized. Easy concepts were made difficult due to overly complex explanations, and some of the terns used seemed specific to the course as it was impossible to find other definitions. TA marked very hard and did not provide any feedback. There was a very low class average. Would not take another class with him.

Lectures were extremely dry. Examples were very few and far between making the material difficult to grasp right away. Did not seem interested in teaching by any stretch of the imagination. On the last day before the final, cancelled his office hours without notice and responded to my coming there with questions with "Too bad"

this is more like an english class, you learned nothing eventually because your main responsibility is to hard memorize 1000 pages of logically unrelated notes and hope that he won't ask you something outside the notes.
This is more like a PHD/Master level course, plus Jim is not a good lecturer

Ah, Jim Delgrande. Makes simple concepts appear much more difficult because of his lack of explanations and practical examples. He's a bit like a terrible technical writer doesn't understand his audience's level of knowledge, and doesn't care to. Don't expect to get more than a "check the textbook" answer to in-class questions. Avoid him.

Jim's lectures in general are pretty chill and exams are straightforward as long as you do all the assignments. He might not be the best person you would want to ask for help right after class since he usually wants to leave and go back to his office probably. But when I had questions and emailed him, he always replied me within the same day.
Cannot explain properly. Dont understand anything what he is teaching. Doesnt teach properly.

well, i d say that his materials are easy. but the way he taught us is very confusing and hard. he maybe a good problem solver, but not a good teacher. he can be very harsh on a sem and then very nice on the next. and unluckily i was on the harsh one

Dr. Delgrande is a good teacher and the class was quite interesting. I learned a lot about AI (something I knew nothing about before) and got introduced to functional programming too. The lectures in his class are very valuable. Definitely worth going to.

He uses his hypnotising voice to read his slides, and make you sleep like a baby. Which, all the slides you can just read them by yourself! I had been studying myself and stopped going to his lectures. And i got all perfect on my assignments, with help from my expert friend. Lecture is boring as an English course or even worse. STAY AWAY FROM HIM!

An average professor. This class was pretty straightforward, nothing too difficult other than getting used to Racket which isn't a big deal. Nice guy too. He can be a bit scattered in his thoughts but he does know the subject. Would recommend.

The best class was when he was sick and a TA covered for him. This prof cannot explain anything. But he marks harshly! For assignments, if you make it perfectly you get 8/10, and you only get 10/10 if you make it more than is required. How can students guess what he wants??? His midterm was too long for the time, also.

He teaches from the textbook and uses slides from the textbook, which is fair. The problem is, the assignments are all about functional programming in Scheme which has nothing to do with the course topic and isn't covered in the textbook at all! He also barely covered this in the lecture. He's made an interesting course agonizing by doing this.
Not clear!. He may be good for research and MAY know his material but not at getting in front of a class to explain it properly.
poor at teaching. assignments are fair but exams are irrelevant to the teaching materials. dont take his 310 course.

Quite weak prof. He just rambles off his slides, and his explanations are very weak (if he has any). Good luck trying to ask for more help if you don't understand what he is trying to say. I would stay away from his classes. If you happen to fall behind, the book and friends are the only one who can help. Exams were not very straight forward either

Assignments were very fair granted you kept up with readings and attended lectures. I got above 90% on all without too much effort. He knows the material very well, I honestly loved his class and thought he was a great prof. Don't expect an easy A though, lots of work.
poor prof
Jim might be good at his research, but not this class. The assignment is hard and exam is not relevant to the class material.
Jim doesn't know the material all that well and was not willing to help with clarifying on the assignments. The course itself is very useful however.
great prof
Very explanatory slides; enjoys helping students get high grades.

Jim falls short as a prof. His explanations to a very technical material are often messy and with a lack of direction. Not to mention, his chicken scratch writing is just impossible to make out so good luck taking notes. Written assignments are straight-forward but don't be surprised to get a couple of coding assignments that will kick your a$$.
Do not take this course with him. Grad students in the combined class but graded the same as undergrads.

I wish I had never taken a course with him. He had errors throughout his exams. Didn't come to class for at least 2 full weeks of the semester and tested on material he didn't cover, even though he promised not to.
yes, his cmpt310 is pretty enjoyable for anyone who deserves to be in cmpt sci... but i dun understand why anyone would rate him "HOT"
Overall, he is a nice professor.
Assignments are fair. Exams are fair. In general a not too useful but enjoyable course.
very good course. Clear and patient to explain my question. I got A+ and my final exam is over 100%. Very easy.
Worse professor with all fake accent, very confused and don't know what to do during lectures.

The worst part about this class isn't that she makes the assignments hard in nature. The difficulty lies in trying to make my code compatible with hers and trying to understand methods beyond our level in an entry level C++ course. Her labs are as long as real assignments and her real assignments incorporate way too much. I have other courses too.

The lecture talks nonsense, and you can learn nothing from that. Term project is very hard if your team don't have any related background. Besides, the grading standard for term project is not aligned with the requirements written in term project instrutions. Moreover, you would never get his reply when you email him to argue about grades.

Readings for weekly quiz but every time the quiz has little things to do with the reading. Can't refer to any textbook nor online resources. Homework is step by step setup but almost no explanation or theory lectured. Lectures are full of superfluous content. I'm with zero cloud computing background and truly devoted, but still I learned nothing.
Halfway through the term, I look back and realise that I did not learn anything useful. Would not recommend the course as long as it is with the same content and structure.

Lecture is boring, talk nonsense, no keypoints, talk hours for simple concept. Expect paper long redundant answer for the assignments (his audience must be non-technical people). Cannot google this background; cannot find his degree.

kinda boring, group projects are challenging, understand protocols well and you will do fine on them. Test are open book which is great. reading the textbook to supplement lectures helped me. Not difficult, lots of memorization but overall nothing will catch off gaurd.

Prof is very knowledgeable and caring. The course is well-structured. Materials aren't always interesting, be proactive in class to get the most out of it. Workload is HEAVY. Projects offer bonus points. Designing an RTP in project #2 was rewarding. Open-book exams with high averages and no scale.
As a graduate-level course, the instructor should avoid putting too much shit on the slides that she does not fully understand herself.

Test/quiz are open book. The class average was high. Though I didn't particularly benefit from lectures as I find that she mostly read off slides. I believe the 1 star reviews are too harsh. She seemed like she cared and was organized with her course. Group project is a bit vague, takes time. HW finish in few hours.
Avoid this professor at all. Bad grader and teacher.

If confusion were a teaching strategy, this would be a masterclass. Disorganized lectures, no clear focus, and passive-aggressive responses to questions. Projects graded unfairly. The "clear pass" rule punishes students despite effort. Just a very frustrating and discouraging experience. Good luck for future MPCS students RIP

An EXTREMELY BAD instructor. Recommend avoiding her courses. Gives very poor explanations of concepts. Slides are vague and lack examples. Quiz questions are odd. Unclear homework and project expectations. She lacks knowledge (eg. doesn't really get Spark). Her course was the WORST uni course I've ever taken. Many in my MPCS cohort dislike her.

This course is like playing Dark Souls—you step in full of hope, armed with what little knowledge you have, only to be mercilessly struck down by something seemingly insignificant. Every concept feels like a relentless enemy, each problem set is a boss fight in disguise.

The professor just reads off the lecture slides and just throws a mass amount of new vocabulary at you. You're better off just reading and doing question from the textbook + watching the author's videos online, it's the same content. The mini-projects req a partner and was tough due to the limited instructions. I didn't feel like I learned much...

The professor is average at best, she spends time reading off lecture slides and you don't really learn much in class. The textbook author's videos on YouTube are a better watch along with the textbook questions and text itself. Mini-projects were kinda tough but there's only 2 of them. I just don't feel like I learned a lot in this class...
Really nice professor. Even tho her lectures may not be the best, she definitely is really caring and helpful. I would recommend.

!!!AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!! My previous rating was removed for some reason, but try your best to avoid her classes. Passive-aggressive, the lecutre was EXTREMELY BORING. If you do take any of her classes, get ready to see concepts and stuff you have NEVER SEEN in any lecures. Don't delete this comment again

The lectures and layout of the course content aren't great, and you will have to do a lot of self-learning. The term project has vague requirements but is thankfully marked leniently, and she is very helpful if you ask her for clarification. The active learning sessions are great for understanding the material, I wish there were more of those.

I don't usually write RMP reviews but this is easily one of the worst teachers I've had in my whole life. You constantly have no idea what is going on in cIass. I basically had to make all of my own notes to help prepare for the midterm as her slides were just copy-pasted odds and ends from random websites and research papers. Waste of time.

She is just presenting the concept at a very high level; requirements are very vague and not marked lightly, so you never know what's important to know. Group project was a waste of time. Optional reading is not optional. Just study the papers and skip the class. Distributed systems is interesting, though.

Really poorly executed. Lecture content didn't map well onto other coursework, so there is constant uncertainty about what is important to know. Group project is probably the worst I have experienced at the school; requirements are very vague yet not marked lightly. The material is interesting, so I wish the class wasn't such a mess.

90% of coursework is trying to understand what a question is even asking you. Most requirements are as clear as an opaque wall, lectures are ramblings of loosely connected streams of thoughts, and explanations usually come with a circular definition. Course content is interesting, don't expect to learn or understand any of it though.

Writing this comment while watching her lecture recording. She basically cannot explain any complicated concepts. I am going to search her lecture notes on google and Chat for better explanation with examples.

My biggest advice is to do practice problems from the textbook. The networking textbook also comes with lectures on youtube that are very helpful to supplement learning. Ouldooz has her own set of yt vids too. Assignments are hard, but quizzes/exams are easy/medium difficulty.

It's a yawn fest to put it bluntly. Recycles all the materials from Kurose / Ross textbook and basically reads off them verbatim. Didn't even try to make this course more interesting. Requires minimum weekly input. Little programming required. Open book everything.

As a student, I was let down. Ouldooz lacks subject knowledge, making lectures tedious. She often dismissed or belittled students, creating an unwelcoming and hostile environment. Emails and in-class questions went unanswered, showing little interest in our concerns. Sadly, a true disappointment. Would avoid at all cost.

The instructor's articulation was poor, with too many filler words like "basically" and "um" which made her already ambiguous explanations painful to comprehend. Additionally, the way she organized the course content was very poor, the slides lacked any logical flow, containing only lines and images cut from other textbooks and papers.

The course I took didn't provide me with enough knowledge on cloud and distributed systems; instead, it focused more on technical jargon and confusing language. Although the course was geared towards engineering, the instructor failed to explain the practical applications of each concept, leaving me unsure about how they could be applied.

A lot of work to get an A, you need to remember a ton of specific details. 4 quizzes, a midterm, a final, 4 hw, a mini-project with partner, and 6 small wireshark assignments. That said if you put in the effort you can get a good grade. Reading the book was helpful but time consuming. Exams and quizzes are easy if you know your stuff. Caring prof.

Class was really organized. She listed everything on main page date by date including recommended and mandatory readings, practice questions and all other course related things. I highly recommend doing readings and putting time into homework. She also responds discussion questions and emails in very reasonable time frame.

Class was organized and grading was fair. Course materials were hard but she explained them clearly. Reading textbook also helped me understand this course. I highly recommend you take CMPT371 with Dr. Ouldooz Karimi. Negative comments about her in previous year did not match my impressions on her.
She is a good prof and knowledgeable. If you keep up with the class, you will get a good grade. She responds back to emails quickly if you have any questions. Organized class. I would recommend her

I don't understand the negative reviews here. In my experience prof was extremely organized. Her lectures were alright and straight out of the textbook but slightly more abstract. If you do her readings before exams and submit assignments on time its very easy to get a reasonable grade that corresponds to the amount of effort you put in.

Prof Ouldooz is a nice human being, but a bad professor. She explains like a closed book. Zero guided practice, so it means there is no way for students to realize if they understood the concepts until they face exams. Confusing explanations and unpredictable grading. You'd *really* better not taking any course with her. You'll regret it deeply.

Ouldooz was often late to class (including the last day) and made very last-minute changes to the course and deadlines without notifying students. Assignments are not related to lecture material and graded without any rubric/consistency. Although she tries to be consistent, she fails to communicate course content effectively. Avoid her at all costs

Professor is conscientious and holds a high standard. Positive side: Transparent grading standard; Can learn a lot from the reading materials. Negative side: Problematic accent; Many topics left for self-learning; course quality not as good as some university's equivalent courses; Professor is busy; late feedback; grade too quick to be accurate

I would like to give updated feedback on professor Karimi this semester. I found her attitude in teaching much better this semester. This time she was far more friendly and caring of her students throughout the course. There are still areas for improvement such as her slides, and presentation. However I wouldn't hesitate to take a class with her.

Her lectures are dry and confusing - slides aren't detailed and don't have accompanying visual aids. Assignments, quizzes, and exams have nothing to do with the lectures and aren't connected. Her wording is very unclear as well and she cannot articulate correctly. For the group project, you are required to teach yourself because she does not help.

Least favourite professor in my academic career. While course content is well structured and organized, the negatives were overwhelming: Constant typos in lecture material, tests/quizzes, and assignments, lecture videos provided no value, and somewhat inconsistent marking on quizzes. I would highly recommend avoiding her classes if you can.

She's a very accomplished and intelligent network engineer. However, she tends to assume that students are also aware of her domain-specific knowledge and enterprise technologies. You must read the book, even if it is stated as recommended.

Lecture/Lecture notes are completely useless, don't bother studying them. Just study the textbook. Test/midterm questions are copy and paste/multiple-choice, but then she gave us only 75 minutes for 45 questions for the final. Exams, homework and lectures felt incredibly disconnected.

Ouldooz is a bad prof. Lectures are dry, she avoids using visual aids, and never uses examples to clarify concepts. She is very stubborn and doesn't change course content between semesters. Online quizzes that contain broken questions (literally broken) are not removed or changed for future offerings. Assignments do not relate to material.

Her attitude appears to be better than previous courses since we moved online. Only tweaks content when we get higher ups involved and she can't teach complex concepts. Her quizzes require in depth understanding of what she glosses over in class. So if you're forced into her course like me, prepare to take very detailed notes. Otherwise avoid her

Her slides and lecture pre-recorded lecture videos are not informative. The content in them is very barebones, and expects the student to retain the information with just the slides. If you want to do well in this course, skip her lectures and read the textbook, and take notes from that instead of her slides. Midterm easy, final was difficult.
Lectures were dry and not explained in depth, you will need to read the textbook to get a real understanding. Quiz questions are worded poorly and not entirely related to lecture content.

Copy paste textbook questions, reword sentence structure and replace variable (abc) with (xyz) - plagiarism of the highest degree. Horrible pre-recorded lectures, made the content extremely dry and uninteresting. Reading the textbook by yourself is more engaging than listening to her speak. Profs like her do nothing but tank SFU CS' reputation.

horrible course. only theories, no demos in class. Docker, Kubernetes. containers, images. What are those things? She is very shy, does not reply emails. If you do not choose your group, she will not put you in a formed group. I think it would even be better to take CMPT 470.

Her exams are extremely unfair, she will give you 45 minutes for 45 question. IN A 4th YEAR CMPT COURSE!! She claims it reduces "cheating" but I don't think anyone can solve math questions and theory question in such a time limit. Assignments are graded based on "keywords". If you don't specify a keyword, you lose marks.

Lectures are just reading definitions of network terms. Quizzes/Assignments are just copy-pasting textbook problems. Worst part is, she can't even do that right, as there are many times where she forgot to put the correct answer as a selectable option. Should've listened to the reviews, but how she is still teaching is beyond me.

This is an online class SERIOUS review. She posted 15 lecture videos / week for you to watch. The lecture video HAS A VERY LOUD EDM BACKGROUND while she is talking. Students dislike this very much and asked her to remove. Instead, she said RESEARCH SAYS IT IS GOOD TO HAVE BACKGROUND MUSIC. She doesnt remove it, and doesnt lower the volume. AVOID

Took this class at the start of the Pandemic. The prof went from not the greatest to the worst prof ive ever had. When classes switched to online she gave us a 15 min video per week instead of online classes. She deducted marks from people for asking questions about her poor wording. She refused to answer her email and refused to accommodate anyone

- gets mad at students for writing quiz at home during a pandemic (code quiz code from friend who attended class) - docks marks for not informing her of who your group partner is by email IN ADDITION to Canvas - threatens to dock marks for asking questions about the project This seems to be the underlying attitude of this prof. Avoid!

During 354, Dr. Karimi has threatened to dock marks for asking questions, dock marks for choosing to do a group project individually, for not attending class during a pandemic, and given inadequate instruction and support. Her apathetic attitude is not only disrespectful to her students, but also to her colleagues, and the name of the university.

I took 371 with her last year. She hasn't improved one bit. When you ask a question, her answer is mostly useless and leaves you more confused. The class is almost always half-empty because her lectures are boring, uninspired, and sometimes don't cover things in the homework assignment. I wish I could say more. A nice lady but a horrible teacher.

It was not an exciting learning environment. Sometimes she seems just as confused as you are and does not give the best explanation/ illustration for important topics that you need to know from front to back.
TA is unhelpful, teaching insufficient.
My biggest regret in Ouldooz's 354, other than enrolling, is that I did not skip every single one of her class and just read the textbook instead. I would have learnt much more.
Quiz expects you to memorize meaningless details of the history of SQL; She does not write examples on board, so she struggles to make students understand even the simplest concept like a Join.

I don't know why people don't like her. I think she's nice and helpful. She even had bonus point for pointing out her mistakes in slides and she will reply to every one of them. 471 was better than 371 though, there were interactive sessions added, where you can analyze real network traffic and exams were also worth 35% instead of 40%.

Homework and exam difficulty are on the easier side compared to other CMPT courses. She is very nice but her lectures are very boring. Her notes are decent but you will need to google stuff if you want a deeper understanding of certain concepts.
Read the textbook and do all the practice questions in the end of the chapters.

Exam and midterm are like assignments, but she does not know how to gauge how much she is asking for in a question vs how much space and time is needed. Questions often have multiple parts or compare/contrast but only given a small space worth 10 marks. If you have test anxiety, slow writer do not take her! Says exam is 6-9 but only gives 90 mins

Ouldooz did a good job teaching for the first time. Assignment criteria was unclear at first, but she did her best to clear things up. Lectures weren't the best due to her accent and she frequently mixed up words or had spelling errors in her slides. Overall, she did her best and she definitely has potential to be a better prof in the future.
Easy courses. Some assignments criteria were unclear at first. Is a fair and caring instructor though.
Not a bad prof. Tries to teach but not good yet. I can definitly see some good potential in her

If you ever think that a professor is terrible, well you haven't seen this one yet. She one of a kind. Terrible lectures, give unreasonably difficult assignments, does not reply to student's requests. I am just happy that i got through this course and never have to take a class with her again.
Literally all of her slides were just ripped from the textbook, you're basically paying tuition just to read the textbook.

Dr. Karimi seems to be not that experienced in teaching. But I could tell that she is really caring to her students. In the last class she guided us go over some questions and they similarly occurred in our final. As long as you focus on what she is teaching, you will be fine though. Exams are fair. Assignments are kind of annoying but fine.
My mediocre midterm mark was replaced with a nice final mark! Prof needs to work on her lectures and notes, but clearly shows effort and willingness to improve, and is nice as a person.
her lectures are good sleeping aids
The final exam was just a mess. Do not take with her. SFU better do something.

I have noticed all of her materials from lecture slides to practice exam questions are all taken from other websites. There is no effort being put into the materials at all. She also threw a project at us which we only had not even three days to complete when exams started. Oh yea, and you only get 90 minutes for a 3H finals. Gotta love SFU LOL.

The final worth a huge chunk of our mark was decided in less time than the midterm... Unfair assignments, examinations, and notes/lectures of questionable quality. Take a course with this prof if you really need the credit, but other professors and the textbook are your best friend. SAD.
SHE IS VERY BAD INSTRUCTOR.

avoid at all cost. this prof does not know how to operate a course at all. project requirements are extremely ambiguous and vague. Final exam was 10 questions and 90 minutes. giving 9 minutes for each question when each question has multiple parts and requires a stupid amount of writing just doesnt make any sense!why does sfu even hire such a prof?

biweekly cumulative T/F, MC quiz w/ vague questions that are kinda true, kinda false. every assignment, project, exam question taken from google or textbook. announced 3h final was 90 minutes 3 days before final. student-sourced spellcheck. added unrealistic project requirements a few days before deadline. "it can't be that bad right?" it really is

The professor is extremely unclear when explaining important concepts. She does go through more examples in class but only after receiving feedback from students. Overall, she is just a terrible professor. Avoid at all costs!

By far the worst cmpt science prof at SFU. Barely can explain the material she is teaching in English. The difficulty of HW assignments have huge transition gaps. One assignment is easy and the next is absolutely hell. Well, we don't care about that shes teaching for first time because there are so many professors who still can do better than her.

It seems that she does want us to really explore the course material. However, she fails to give clear instructions, lectures, and materials. As the result, the course really put toll on students' time and efforts. I know that she improve teaching in time since she really does care about students learning, but for now it is best to avoid her class.
The projects are alot of work for little value to your overall mark, and the midterm destroyed everyone due to the incoherent lecture and quality of material we get.
its not possible to do the homework because you dont learn the material nor is it in any provided material. all this work and youll still be left wondering how and what to study for the final exam...
As the course progress, her lecture and slides become more and more over simplified and unclear to the point where you have to learn everything by yourself.
Assignments and notes copied from the internet/textbook. She does not teach how to solve any question.
Need to learn everything on your own. Any skills necessary for the assignments not taught in class.

It is very difficult to understand her lecture due to her bad public speaking skills. And her slides are filled with horrible wording that make the content very difficult to understand. Not recommended.
Nice person but get ready to learn everything on your own...

The professor definitely makes an effort and responds to emails quickly. However, the pacing of the course is somewhat inconsistent and the course is disorganized making it somewhat confusing to study for. The material isn't the hardest but it certainly becomes more of a challenge when the lecture slides occasionally seem fragmented. Be advised.
I learned everything during the lectures and as others have said, shes caring and likes to help. responds really fast to her emails.

I am taking this course with her in summer 2019, and I gotta say that she is good. Her slides are amazing. Yes, it gets really hard to understand her, but I would totally take more classes with her. She cares alot about her students and wil not hesitate to re-answer questions for you.
I appreciate profs who have good lecture notes, and she's one of them. I missed a few classes but studied the slides real good and did good on the midterm. She's available for extra help

This is likely second worst prof that I've had that ruined a typically medium difficulty course. Notes are bad, quizzes and exams questions are vague and will cost you time and marks. Would not recommend! However, would recommend taking CMPT354 next time it's offered if Ouldooz is teaching.

Some of the criticisms about criticisms on this page is really unfair. We pay just as much as any other section of 354, only to get a much lower quality learning experience (and grade). The notes are unclear and lack context, the assignments take too much time for what they're worth and don't help for exams, and the exam questions are unclear.

A lot of the criticism here are unfair. "Quiz is hard". The quiz averages are like 80%. Midterm 68%. She is a new lecturer, that is a clear. But she is CLEARLY trying her best, stays after lecture to answer questions and also provides bonus marks. I think in time she will be a great prof, she just needs to be given the time to prepare for the class

Quizzes are unclear. More time is spent trying to understand questions than answering it. Midterm covers material that has just been covered/is still being covered (optimization and parse trees). Repeats information many times but is unclear each time. Quiz once every two weeks. No one is able to get 100% on quiz. Class always ends early. Avoid.

Dr Karimi seriously cares about students' learning and understanding and goes over lecture materials if needed (haven't had many profs doing that in engineering school). Biweekly quizzes are a great way to stay on top of lecture materials (if you actually study for them!) Will definitely take other classes again with this prof in future.

Not hard midterm. I appreciate she does not want to fail all our us. The exam basically comes from her lecture slides, nothing more. Just the lecture itself is kind of tedious. You may want to learn by yourself.

Hardest prof I have ever meet. She is really bad at teaching. In her class, she only read the lecture but not explain the reason or give an example. She need you to read the textbook and the lecture notes for the exam which is really hard, tricky and come form details in the textbook. She has quiz that no one get full marks in class every two weeks
Really not a good lecturer. Cannot explain things in a clear way. For most of the students they were stumped trying to figure out what she saying.

One of the worst lecturers in SFU. You might as well skip the lectures because she doesn't have public speaking skills (mumbling, stuttering, long awkward pause,...) Just read the book, you'll get more info from it than sitting in the 3 hrs long lecture with her. Other than that, her hw assignments and quizes are ok if you read the book.

Avoid. If you take a class with her, you're better off just reading the notes + textbook, and doing as many problems from the back of the book as possible instead of showing up to class. She does not provide clarification when homework instructions aren't clear. Quizzes are cumulative from ALL previous material. Spelling errors everywhere.
Worst prof I had at SFU. You have to learn most of the content yourself as her teaching is not helpful, just goes over the powerpoint slides.

Still early in the semester. Lecture slides contain many minor typos (mostly spelling). Assignments are sometimes ambiguous. She fixes the mistakes/clarifies if a student asks. Anonymous feedback can be given any time. Considering that this is her first time teaching this course, I would say her material is excellent. Recommended.

Delgrande is a solid prof with a very dry sense of humor. I found myself laughing several times in his classes. Overall I enjoyed his lectures though they could be a bit boring at times. The assignments and midterm/final were fair and not too difficult.

In my opinion he's fine at best. I actually liked the class a lot. But, at least for me, his answers to any questions (during lectures of office hours) were useless because he just repeats himself. Assignments (especially programming assignments) were really fun. Exams were fair.
First class I failed in 4 years at SFU. This is probably my fault, although I did attend every lecture. Got an A- in the other class I was taking that semester so...

Really nice, really kind lady, with a good teaching skill and clear lecture note which she even print for students. Moreover, she is really patient for answering questions. She is the best calculus professor I have ener seen
take with him! He is knowledgable and caring! + his notes are enough!
No way you fail his class if you do all his practices. Final was pretty hard though. However, as long as you do his practices every week, you'll pass.

I repeated this course with Yonas only, first time I did not study at all but second time around I never skipped a class did all Lab work questions did all assignments and quizzes on my own rather than use chat gpt. overall Awesome professor and clear grading criteria
He's a very good professor—you should definitely take his class, even though the final is a bit tough and the lab assignments are quite demanding.
Honestly the ones that are saying he tough grader or his lectures are boring haven't seen SFU 2nd lecturers and how bad they are.
His exams are very difficult and you need to study more than what you learn in class to pass them. The impact of quizzes and homework on the overall grade is minimal.

Great lectures. I just attended the classes and did the assignments myself without any external help and was able to do well in his course. He is always willing to help students during the lab session so take full advantage of the lab session to clear your doubts.

Hate this Prof, very lecture heavy and they are boring, even the minutest mistakes are punished and Most Importantly has a wrong approach in teaching Computer Science, liking using the most outdated software, hand written exams etc. (coming from a 3.45+ GPA holder)
He is patient and willing to help students. If you do not work hard, your grade will be hard. He is a nice teacher but the material needs lots of practice.

Teaches everything clearly. Everything you need is in his slides which he posts so it's easy to review. Seems intimidating at first but he's really more like a dad who gives tough love. He'll help you with any problem you have. Tough exams, but as long as you practice you'll do great.
He is a very good teacher who tries his best that the students can understand the concept . Course is super easy if you do all the lab work as exams are related to it . Also he is ean easy grader .

Although his exams are pretty tough, he teaches the best. The flow and the way he teaches will make you the best programmer. Also, his lab works are super tough which makes it easier. So, in short, this course is for people who are keen and desperate on learning programming.

Yonas really helped me understand the course material, I also found him very understanding. Make sure to do the lab exercises as it is good practice for the exams. The final and midterm exam was hard though, so make sure to study hard for those. Plus, he's a cutie.
Cool course that makes u learn abt c++. Some parts are fairly interesting and overall i'd say these 2 courses are fairly easy. Yonas uploads useful materials that helps u get a good grade.

Other teachers at FIC told me he purposely fails students to earn money. He will fail you no matter how hard you try so you retake the course and he can make money. He is greedy for money. His marking is unfair. I am at SFU now, no upper-year course has a harsh marking, and no one deducts 25% or 50 % for a small error as he does.

Yonas probably has one of the worst course outlines in FIC. For example, you can do well during the entire course and a single midterm or final exam can destroy you. Do not take this course, i'd even say avoid this school at all costs. I dont know exactly what happened but apparently my finals failed and I got an F.

The course is very disorganized. I've spent ~90 hours (I've been tracking) working on this course and yet only got a C+. The next semester I took the same class with professor Jiang Ye and got an A. He does not inspire you to work harder and mocks you when you have questions. Also he gives looooots of work and expects you to complete all of them.

His quizzes are ten times harder than his lectures and practice material and you will be struggling each week just to barely pass the course. No clear grading criteria either so you may get 75% and still fail.

He is the worst professor I encountered at FIC. Incredibly disorganized and expects us to spend all our time studying this course. I got better marks in higher-level courses at SFU without having to spend as much time and frustration on them. He definitely fakes the 4/5 rating. More than 60% of students fail the course on the first try and have to re-attempt it to transfer. Each week is cruel and soul-wrecking.

He is a very good professor he always teach the material very clearly and if you don't have any basics he is a good professor. I will take his class again.

terrible he treated everyone like those kids from india who have already learned coding before. his questions are long and tough, too hard for anyone who first learn coding. 4hour lecture just sucks. i can do all the quizzes from another instructor doing this course but i can't even pass his course

He really inspires students to learn more about coding and problem solving. Although he does send many optional lab questions, they are very useful for practicing for the quizzes. He is a great professor!!! and has office hours every Saturday to clear any doubt.

He is the worst ever, he thought everyone in his class is in CS he doesn't care engineering students, he has 50 questions per week asking you to type the code. If I'm in CS yes cmpt is the only thing I need to take care of. But no, besides this course engineering also need to do calculus physics etc. I would rather learn this in college

One of the best professors in FIC. Dr. Weldeselassie is really caring when you have problems with lab works or quiz and he makes sure that your problem is solved during the office hours. The lecture notes and videos were awesome.

WORST PROFF EVER, he doesn't understand that his course isn't the only thing that matters. His lectures videos are sometimes over 5 hours long per week. His lecture was very clear, but his quiz was a disaster. In particular, the quiz score can only be passed or not, either you get a full score, or you can only get 10% or 20%.
Yonas is a good instructor but he is a very tough grader.

His lectures are sub par, he'll do the bare minimum but nothing more. His grading is unreasonably hard and he's mostly non cooperative. His email replies are quick. Try to avoid taking his class. I loved computing BEFORE I took his class...
hes the reason why I am still doing computing science
Best Professor for computing science. Very inspirational. Marks well for the amount of work you show. Office hours and labworks are a must to ace this course.

Best professor for someone who is new to coding. Clears all the basics before starting the course. Explains all the topics rally well. His grading can never go wrong. Do Lab-works regularly to ace this course. Attend his office hours for regular updates and to clear your doubts. Video lectures are very informative and are sufficient for the topic.

He is absolutely great Prof and has outstanding teaching skills. His video lectures and notes covers every topic with a great information that each and every doubt of yours will be explained in there. He is passionate about the quality of education to be delivered to students. The more you interact with him, the more you learn from him. Awesome!!
He is the best teacher, I've met for computer programming.

Great prof!! Super friendly and approachable, he may make fun of you just a little bit (attending office hour last minute :p) and your silly problems but it's all in good faith! His slides are very informative but make sure to watch his videos since it covers a lot more than what he has in the slides. Highly recommended prof!

Great and easy to understand slide. The class I had was online, yet I had no problem following and learning the course material solely with slides(didn't watch his recorded lecture as recording was poor). Super strict

He's an amazing teacher with great concept clarity. He clears every doubt and actually can sense the doubt that arises in your mind through a mere eye contact during lecture. He gives lot's of homework and solves some of it in class. However, he seems to not like people who answer a lot or give alternate solutions. Sometimes gets rude holds grudges
really dedicated teacher

Best teacher if you are starting to learn coding and he will guide you through everything in detail. Amazing professor, will guide you through the logic required for this course. His lectures and slides perfectly grasp the concept of the course. Gives a lot of fun excercises and tests, which can be quite difficult if you are not on top your game. Definitely would recommend.
The most amazing teacher. I took both 130 and 135 with him. He is a tough grader but you understand the logic quite well if u carefully pay attention in his class.

He is a really good instructor. Read all his lecture notes carefully and do all his lab works, you are guaranteed at least an A-. He does answer every question carefully. Please try answer to answer and check for every possible mistake before asking him.

Yonas is truly an amazing prof. He's one of those profs that really make you love the course and actually make you excited to attend his lectures. If you're taking CMPT 130 he can be a tough grader. pros + explains in details + asks for feedback (in the class) cons - doesn't have active office-hours - rude sometimes

He gets strong accent and sometimes it seems that he is speaking his homeland language rather than English. Moreover, he is really irresponsible. THIS IS THE PROF THAT MADE ME INTERESTED IN CODING AGAIN AND MADE ME WORK ON MY OWN RATHER THAN SPOON FEEDING. He is the best. You'll say the same after the end of your semester with him.
Great teacher

He gets strong accent and sometimes it seems that he is speaking his homeland language rather than English. He can make us understand some of the classes but mostly his lecture is really confusing. HE IS THE BEST. I took CMPT130/135 with him last year and because of him I have very strong foundations in C++. There is no prof who can teach C++ better than him. You just need to understand his slides and you can do all assignments, midterms and final without touching the book. His programming assignments really help in understanding too.
would't take his class again

He will clear all your doubts. Amazing prof
Friendly
Amazing Lecture
Helpful. willing to share and help.
Amazing Lecture, Caring, Participation Matters.
Amazing
Awesome teacher who makes coding easy. No need for the textbook. Gives lots of good (optional) practice. Will definitely take him again.
Participation Matters. Skip Class? You won't pass. Amazing Lecture.

I enjoyed working in my group (for once, everyone carried themselves) but the content felt really dry and i don't think it really set me up for a job (unlike what we were told in class). I heard from my friends that the 276 was their highlight course but ig their prof was different. i'd avoid kasra if you can

chill guy but oh man was the course disorganized... I love Kasra but I don't think he's cut out to be a prof. Get ready to do most of the learning on your own and carry team members in the project. We didn't have assignment grades until the very end of the semester.

He's chill but a little disorganized. He'd lose sight of macro concepts mid-lecture and assignments weren't graded until the end of the semester. Lectures were mostly not relevant for the group project, so get ready to teach yourself A LOT. Also pray you get assigned good teammates — I lost a lot of marks from things outside of my control

This prof is the nicest person/prof that you could meet at SFU. He is very helpful, caring, approachable, and whatnot (man, I wish this guy the best). The only thing I could complain about is that the course is pretty vague; you have to learn most framework by yourself. Get yourself to self-learn about Docker, Alembic, api, deployment, CICD, etc.

Great chill W prof. Just focus on the team project and know the basics of class content from a general perspctive and u also good to go for quizzes as well. Even gives a lesson after final grades released lol.

Kasra is a great prof for 276 since he actively works as a software engineer. He is able to give insight into what its like working as a software engineer currently because of this. The class is mostly just a big group project, and the difficulty will depend on the group members you get (random). His lectures are useful and not dry.

Kasra himself is a very chill, friendly and approachable guy. However I feel like he is not ready to teach cmpt 276 yet due to the lack of structure in the course. Frankly, I felt like learning nothing after a semester (or dont get taught to know where to apply what I have learned). I got stressed out so much after taking this course with him.

Kasra is the worst prof for 276. He doesn't have enough skill to teach in 276. His lecture material is so random. As an introductory software development course, students didn't understand what he was saying. Lack of experience, the course was so tough, although 276 is one of the easiest courses. It was my biggest mistake of year to take this.

Known as one of the best CS profs at SFU, and I can see why. Marking is very fair, and assignments are relevant to the course content, although can be a little time consuming. Overall, a solid choice!
Arguably one of the goats.

Lectures are clear also recorded, so don't need other learning materials. This is a big time saving! Homework isn't difficult as he provides step-by-step pesudocode. Exams include about 20% difficult questions, but you can easily get the rest by following his lectures and doing slight review before exams. He answers our questions in Piazza quickly.
best candidate for this course

Dr. Alameldeen excels in clear, organized lectures and makes complex subjects accessible. Known for his approachability, he quickly responds to inquiries, enhancing learning. Highly recommend his classes for a rewarding experience. Early review course material and basic C++ knowledge help with assignments.

Probably one of the best profs I have had at SFU. He's the only prof I've had where I feel 100% comfortable asking for clarification on anything because he will happily re-explain if necessary. He is also very accessible outside of class and will answer any question you have. Exams are difficult but very fair. Wish he taught more courses :')

Course itself and assignments are pretty good, but not a fan of his lecture. He just reads off slides which are ~100% based on textbooks so lectures are basically skippable. Open book quizzes and no final.
The man, the myth, the legend! He is so caring and willing to review the material again to ensure we understand it.

Seriously, 5/5 is not an understatement. He cares so much. A whole week before finals, he was answering 10s of piazza questions each day in under 30 minutes (night and day,literally). If he notices the class isn't following him, he is not hesitant to explain everything again even more slowly, yet with the same friendliness and enthusiasm.
Better than Arrvindh

Goated prof. Answers all questions clearly and very accessible outside of class for help on material and assignments via piazza. Lectures can be a little dry due to the nature of the material going over formulas and their derivation. 4 tests, one every 3 weeks but open book/internet and pretty straight forward if you generally know all the content
Top G of a professor, he's probably the only good prof we have in the cs department. If we taught more classes I would take them.

Having Alaa with Arrvindh was an experience. Its like you dip your body in hot water and then you get the relief of getting your body cooled down with fresh mineral spring water from the swiss alps. With Arrvindh being the hot water and Alaa the cool water. Alaa Cares a lot, he stays up late to answer questions and is generally quite nice.

Alaa is one of the realest, most caring professors around the block. The content in 295 is not easy and Alaa is the reason why I made it through. He is always very patient with all of us and will do anything to help us understand the material! I, myself, might even call him the GOAT alongside Michael Jordan and Lebron James.

* THIS IS FOR CMPT 450 BUT IT'S NOT AN OPTION AND I CAN'T ADD IT. "Caring" wouldn't describe how much he is always willing to help and how much he prioritizes students. The Goat actually cares about your success and will work overtime to help you
The GOAT.
Alaa made a good effort at explaining material although gets monotone and fails at emphasizing what is important. Very responsive to student questions

Alaa is one of the most considerate professors ever! He actually cares about his students and will patiently respond to all of our questions. His exams are quite fair and he is very lenient with his grading.CMPT295 is a pretty tough course imo and Alaa made it a lot easier to get through. Will take again.

This course is usually split in half. First half is done with a different prof , latter half is done with this prof. This professor does seem to care about the student. During the finals season, he was even seen staying awake till 2 AM to answer questions.
Great professor all around. This guy truly cares and will go out of his way to assist you. Definitely would recommend taking courses with him.
Great prof that really cares about his students, very open and responsive to questions

He's humane and genuinely cares about his student's well-being. The last half of the material was difficult, however, he made sure that you understood it if you asked questions. Madlad stayed up super late just to answer Piazza questions about the practice finals. He's a bit monotone during lectures, but other than that, he's a respectable guy!

431 with Alaa was really fun. Assignments were reasonable and interesting and if you're into distributed systems he's definitely very knowledgeable on the topic in a non-condescending way conducive to learning. Awesome professor.
Very knowledgeable and looks out for students falling behind. Takes the extra steps to help during class and in off-hours. Really appreciate it!

Professor Alaa is very knowledgeable and knows his material super well. Very organized. There are 4 exams, so the load is quite high. Assignments are not as easy as others say since I am not too comfortable with c++. But he answered your questions promptly, that made it a bit easier for me. It would never go wrong taking any of his classes I think.

I really enjoyed Alaa's teaching style. His lectures were really succinct and straightfoward. The assignments were really easy and straightfoward if you are comfortable with C/C++ and thread stuff from CMPT 300. Also very accessible and respectful learning environment and timely answers. Could not recommend this prof more.
Useful concepts and kind professor! There are 8 assignments and are based on C++. You will have a hard time if you feel uncomfortable with writing C/C++ stuff.

An excellent professor that genuinely cares about his students. Held additional office hours on a holiday. Responded to an e-mail at 1 am in the morning. He's also extremely knowledgeable -- I don't recall a question he couldn't answer in depth. His quizzes are extremely easy for a 300 level course. Our class average was A-

Good prof, very responsive to questions, good lectures, useful slides, and lots of examples. Tough class and prof but you learn a lot from him!
Uwe is very knowledgeable about cybersecurity, but what is taught in class is more the theoretical side of cybersecurity. Uwe is probably cybersecurity Fortinet HHM anomalies haha

Phenomenal professor who adeptly carries out an effective and cohesive approach in conveying a productive and coherent lecture during every class. Despite limited availability due to conference engagements and research commitments, it does not undermine his capacity to impart a sense of enjoyment in an advanced computer science course.
hes the man
bro reset his rate my prof bruhhhh
The course covers a lot of content and the assignments are very long. But the prof is great, he explains things very thoroughly in the lectures. I learned a lot.

Numerous students are dissatisfied with this course, but perhaps we should direct our anger towards the TA, not the professor. The word "harsh" is not enough to describe the TA.
