First, I need to indicate that this class is about OpenGL and is very hard. His labs are extremely long and hard, and he only provides very basic stuff in his class. If you'd like to take this class with him, be sure to prepare a handy OpenGL primer guide because the textbook he uses is not suitable for OpenGL beginners.
This is the COMP SCI professor not the english one, don't be tricked. DO NOT, I REPEAT DO NOT, once again... DO NOT take this course with him.
Great Prof, start your labs early. Got EXTREMELY high mark from him

What's wrong with you stupid arts people, this is the CMPT Torsten ! OK!? Torsten is one of the worst profs in cmpt at sfu. His lecture is not informative at all, labs are brutally long and hard. His exams are hard in the sense that you never know what he's gonna test you on. It could be anything that he said in the lecture but not on the slides.

Overall, Torsten is a great professor. Very laid back and easy to talk to. He seems really interested in what he's teaching. However, he's very easily distracted and somewhat disorganized during lectures. He always seems prepared to deliver an amazing lecture, but he gets so distracted by the class that sometimes he doesn't get to the good stuff.

Torsten ROCKS!!!! He is incredibly laid back and you feel it in his lectures. They are enjoyable AND you leave learning a few things too!!! Super understanding and picks some GREAT material to read. I loved him sooo much Im taking 342 with him in the fall. He's awesome. You will think so too! Best English prof I've encountered at SFU!!!
Such a great professor I've enrolled in his class 3 times...great class to take to gain credits with ease..he made my 4 course load feel like 3.

One of the most easy-going, helpful, engaging profs I've ever had. He knows his stuff, and he actually cares that his students learn it, too. He is beyond reasonable, understanding, and witty. Anyone who says otherwise is just afraid to be out-classed. Attend just ONE day--just the first class, please, and judge for yourself.

HE is by far the best prof i've had a st sfu. Not only does he give a simple quiz at the end of the term as his final, but he is extremly passionate about his work and is always evoking laughter. You are fortunate if you can get into one of his classes, he'll make your semmester

Torsten is FANTASTIC. Lectures are painless, assignments are explained clearly, NO FINAL!, fun in class group play, nice guy and generally picks good material. Thumbs up! SFU better give him tenture before he gets a better offer.

Although he is constantly joking and thinks he is hilarious, he isn't. His three hour lectures were monotonous and full of rambling. He was also very disorganized.However, the lectures often had really good points to consider, and papers were very relevant with laid-back guidelines. Not too bad, but probably wouldn't take another with Torsten.
Just a really nice guy
Excellent prof!!! Very nice, very funny (at least I think so), and shows that he is passionate about what he's teaching. Definitely recommend him, one of the best at SFU.

Awesome. Makes learning this subject interesting and fun. This is the way teachers were meant to teach. His lectures are funny and he managed to make a boring topic really interesting... Best Proff to have... I would recommend all of his classes whether they are interesting or not...

Torsten is an awesome prof! He's super nice, understanding of student's stressful schedules & very knowledgeable about course material. He loves teaching, esp. Shakespeare, and it shows. Definitely take a class with him if you can! Best Eng. prof I've taken at SFU. Another awesome one: Steve Collis. Good Luck!!
Very nice guy, easy-going and empathetic towards student's needs. Funny sense of humour and really into his Shakespeare. Made the material understandable.

Chaucer! well Torsten spends more time talking about "things" than actually learning Chaucer. No syllabus given out for what, when things are expected. Not someone who follows time-lines or structure. Nice guy tho'. But, the question remains to be seen: can he teach Chaucer?

Awesome teacher. Made Shakespeare fun! You're missing out if you don't take an English course with him. One of the best!
Torsten's great. 'Nuff said.

Torsten was a great prof, he is very intellignet and knows his Shakespeare. He made class interesting and you were never bored. Reading Shakespeare was made easier and alot more fun. Highly recommended that people take his classes. He never gave out too much work.
He was a really funny prof and a good teacher, but id have to say i hate early shakespeare...talk about dull and boring! No offence Thorsten..you were a great prof!

Torsten is an amazing prof! Great, engaging discussions, and open class plan - makes for a great teaching environment. his quirky attitude and chocolate addiction makes him a great person to hang around with and have class with. highly highly recommended! and he really knows his shakespeare stuff!!!
Great prof.! Creates an open class environment condusive to intelluctual discourse; funny, friendly, knowledgable on subject matter; intrested in seeing students succeed. Recommended, wholeheartedly.

Kind, funny, engaging prof. Class is self guided, especially since sometimes Torston seems scattered and unable to be critically analytical about material. Sometimes his lectures can come across as plot summary rather than helpful lit discussions. So unorganized that it hinders student's abilities

Torsten is my favorite Engl prof. However, this is probably the worst class I've taken with him, and though it was still good, I prefer his Shakespeare courses, which everybody should take. Some advice: if you like structured courses with specific dates then Torsten is not the prof for you.

torsten is the nicest guy ever. EVER. he's really engaging, i always listen in class and he's hilarious too. totally understanding with extensions and stuff. but don't take advantage of him, he's too good a person for that! :)

Torsten is by far my favourite prof, in any faculty! Very nice, knowledgeable, un-intimidating, and funny to boot! If structure is your thing...what are you doing in the arts? Keeps a light environment and understands the pressures of being an undergrad. Take as much as you can with him!
Torsten is an awesome prof. Very understanding in assignments. Very approachable. A chocolate addict who loves his Shakespeare. If you can't decide which course to take, take his!
Torsten is interesting, kind, honest, and genuine, and supportive. Absolutely loved his classes!

I'm never intimidated in Torsten's classes. I was never one to participate that much in seminars but in his classes he puts you at ease and responds well to even dumb questions! Makes you feel like you know stuff and always motivates students.
Awesome prof.
Torsten is my favorite English prof. However, if you dislike profs that tend to go off on tangents he may not be for you.

Torsten was a great prof. He really knows what he's talking about and his lectures are always interesting and funny. He makes reading Shakspeares plays really enjoyable, and helps students understand the deeper meaning in the plays.

Torsten is the best english professor at sfu. He is incredibly intelligent and offers great insight into the text. He makes Shakespeare fun to learn and his lectures are always interesting. Very understanding and a very nice person.
Torsten is a great prof. Sometimes goes off on very hilarious tangents. The most understanding prof I've had when it comes to assignments.
Mixes philosophy with Shakespeare. A caring person who loves teaching. A very worthwhile professor. Highly recommended!

Best prof in English Dept(not tenured yet to boot). Actually believes the theory should inform the text, a rare novelty at SFU's usually theory driven lackies. Take him for as much as you can. Can't believe someone thinks he's hot!!
Great prof, great class. Very enjoyable. Very easy going in regards to assignments. Makes the class come alive.
extraordinarily empathetic prof who is flexible with respect to assignments, deadlines, etc.; fun, enthusiastic, and well-read
Laid back, interesting, able to fill seminar voids with engaging thought. Also very funny, makes class enjoyable.
No Comments
Wonderful annecdotes and casual style... knows his subject.
Torsten is a really good, funny and interesting prof. Good job, Torsten!
Easy tests, super long homework - helpful and easy to approach guy
No Comments
No Comments
Interesting course.
Good Prof, Hard Assignments, Math Intensive, Should have more code in lectures.

He extends the course content to include mobile security, so there's a lot of material to cover for the final. His lectures can be dry and he didn't explain concepts very well imo. His exams are not fair at all… a lot of overly specific and unrelated questions. Assignments are really cool but require lots of time and self-teaching.
Exams: Hell on earth Homework: Actually interesting

Tao Wang's CMPT 403 materials + mobile security from other school, basically 1.5 terms of material in 1 term. Lectures are dry, no practice exam questions, and need at least 50% on exams to pass (midterm avg was 49). Final may included some topics/questions that are not covered in the slides and class.

Lectures were just him reading borrowed slides, often pausing because he didn't understand the material. No exam prep was provided the exams were much harder than the slides. Assignments required implementing Bluetooth protocols with no guidance, you need to figure it out on our own. Avoid if possible, you'll be teaching yourself the entire course.
Took 371 and 300 with Ryan. He's very engaging with his discussions and live demos. Definitely recommend for either or both of these courses!

Interesting/entertaining lectures. Randomly busts out kali and aircrack for live demos. Actually discusses slides rather than just reading them to you. Slides/textbook available online. Attendance not mandatory but definitely helpful. Not an easy grader but not a random trivia master either. Midterm/final stuck to the slides. 75% exam average

Prof has outstanding fundamental knowledge in Computing sciences. Tough assignments. covers 2hrs of topics in an hour. Grad seminar course, so students talk more than prof and students cant emulate prof. mostly self learning in the course and project. I feel they should rethink about course structure and limit student seminars to final presentation

Only criticism I have is some exam questions concepts were repeat from assignments. If you struggled on the assignments, it mirrored on the exams. Otherwise, Dr. Shea is a hero. He really knows the course material and how to teach it. Fast and efficient in office hours. Gave homework extensions based on class feedback. No regrets taking his class.

This prof is the reason people wouldn't wanna go to school. He lacks originality with copying all of his assignments from other universities which take up WAY too much time. The assignments should be worth 70% of the course instead of 50%. We should be focused on learning EFFICIENTLY, not having a lot of time consumed for the purpose of learning.

3 years at SFU and he's the best CMPT prof I've had. He knows what he is talking about AND he knows how to explain it clearly to students. He postpones assignments when requested so students can learn. Ryan Shea MAKES your job as a student easier by providing you with the necessary resources.
Hard course with tough prof. Assignments are Copied from graduate course ones. Exams ok.

Great Prof. Had him a year ago, don't know if he still teaches here. Notes crystal clear, simple and goes over things in lecture very well. Assignments easy but projects may need a little bit of effort. Marks & tests very fairly
Funny prof. But you need to know python well and prepare to code and debug. Best prof to be recommanded for CS students. May not be good if you don't know python.

Great prof! His work load is a bit much but nothing too bad. Definitely doable and worth doing since the concepts find their way in the quizzes and definitely the final exam. He's more than reasonable and always willing to help! His notes are amazing and he programs during lectures so the lectures are definitely worth attending! Overall, amazing!!!

Simply amazing! This guy is as close to the text-book definition of "perfect" as far as professors go. Passionate, helpful, reasonable and his notes are gold. I didn't have prior programming experience, but I still received an A+ in his class! Just study his notes, attend the lectures, ask for help, do his assignments, and you'll be fine. =]
AWESOME PROF! very clear. lecture notes are really useful. Reasonable tests.
Really cool, funny guy. This class was easy for me, since I had experience with the content already, but he's a great instructor.
this guy is amazing,he is nice and super helpful just go to his office hrs and you will be fine. his project is ok.. if you dont know how to do the things you can go through the ppt that he sent out.

I like him!!! He is a good teacher! But you need to work a little hard on his course.. He does give hard project, but he is a reasonable guy. The notes are there and he is helpful. If you try you will have fun time with him~ Do attend to his class, cuz he's class is funny.

AWESOME PROF! Young, energetic and helpful. Clear and complete notes. Too bad, he'll leave, just after two weeks. I don't know how well he knows about computing science but he definitely knows how to teach a first year CS class which most of the other profs at SFU don't.

Amazing Prof. I'm a student that skips a lot of classes, but I will go to this guy's EVERY class. His notes are 9/10 but his lectures are 10/10 (EVERYTHING is clear after the lecture!). Now onto the bad parts, his labs are extremely LONG, but not difficult, but at the same time FUN. Crazy right? I didn't believe it myself.

One of my favour profs in SFU!!!An awesome, funny guy. Course content is really useful. Just try to do all the labs and pay attention to his notes, you will be find. Have fun with his quiz bonus quesions!!lol~

I took this course over a year ago and still talk about Colin. Such a young, funny lecturer, but 95% of the class was international so I suspect no one got his sense of humour. Hard class if you've never programmed before but Colin is always available for help. Super funny/easy bonus questions

Discourage students and arrogant. Sometimes goes off topic and end up not finishing the lecture. Can get easy mark with the TA. But do not show your work that is for mark to him he is very pick. If finds he does not find the variable name likable will deduct huge mark.
Awesome Prof! Colin programs during his lecture, which is terrific. Go to class, do the labs and you will ACE this course. It boosted my GPA :)
The final exam was pass or fail for the whole couse no matter if you did well before. You are expect to work super hard in his class. forget about having fun if you don't already enjoy programming.

Overall, I would say hes a good teacher. He posts lecture notes and goes through them in class and does examples. Lab work relates to his lecture notes, 2 projects, 1 MT, 4 quizzes and 50% final. Material isn't super hard but is workable. I found his class okay enough though I have no prior programing knowledge.

Gives you a penny and expects a million dollars. Course is VERY difficult. Would not recommend to students who are not planning to major in comp sci. This course is a gpa killer. Prof's grading schemes are utterly ridiculous and unfair as well. In hindsight, wouldn't go near this course with a 50 foot stick.

I took this course as a GPA booster and indeed my GPA went up (I had previous experience with xhtml/css). His final exam however was a KILLER (for most students). The final question required full understanding of python: we were required to build an online shopping cart. The material was covered... but the last question was too damn long!
Nice teacher~ and easy mark~ but you need to really understand your exercises and do your exercises and projects~! Final was not hard as projects~ and He curves the final,so you can get better grade!
The amount of work you have to put in his class is too much. lots of projects, exercises and quizes. you can learn things from him but this requires lots of time to spend on this course..

His assignment requires lots of work but definitely doable, and he's always available for help. For those complaining about the length of his projects, take a class with Diana. If you wanna actually learn stuff and willing to put effort into this course, take it with him. And he scales everyone up at the end, so not that hard to ace it at all.
he is going to fail your plan of everything!!!!!!Having free time?!!no project and exercises and quizes everyweek.difficult final. Its not a GPA booster. bad choice to take class with him !!!
extremely difficult
he is hard, swears a lot. shouldve taken ths course with an easier prof. bad luck for me.

The course requires a lot of work and time. The assignments were a joke and took less then 3 hours each, scored perfect on everything. Midterm & Final on the other hand were VERY difficult. The MC messes with your brain and the written portions are not even close to the ease of the assignments. Letter grades were pulled out of his back pocket.

Tok this course distance ed! Wouldn't suggest you do if you cant handle elarning by yourself and guess/check type methods. Programming requires a lot of testing and re-testing. The webct forums were littered with "im lsot, help", "i have no clue what to do". And I guess thats why when I got a 77 it turned out to be an A.

If you're thinking of taking this course as GPA booster then think again. You will regret taking this course with this prof. His last two projects are extremely difficult and the text book was pretty useless. I'm warning you avoid taking this course with this prof.

I took CMPT 120 with this prof.(he said he didn't like to call himself as 'prof'). Anyway, his note is very clear and easy to understand, but lab and projects are needed time to finish. midterm and quizzes are getting to difficult and then final exam is extremely hard. Be aware that 1/3 ppl will be fail!

I took this online as a C/GPA booster course. Instead, it's one of the courses dragging me down. It's much harder than it sounds. The textbook doesn't give enough information for the assignments and the midterm was ridiculously hard. I'm not sure about in class, but definitely don't take this online!

He's a cool guy. Extremely helpful, especially outside of lecture. But, the course load is way too much for a level 100 course and his assignment difficulty is way too hard for the scope of this course. He stacks up projects and assignments so be wary if you;re going to have him. He also goes over class time.
Overall a very amusing lecturer. He brings a new sense of energy to the class and still focuses on his lectures.
Great prof. If you keep up with the material and and start your assignments early, you will do well. Colin is a very clear, entertaining, and funny lecturer.
I WOULD ADVISE YOU NOT TO TAKE THIS PROF IF YOU ARE TRYING TO BRING YOUR GPA UP BECAUSE HE WILL DEFINETELY CRASH IT.....DONT TAKE HIM

HE IS A VERY INTERESTING GUY AND KNOWS WHAT HE IS TALKING ABOUT ALTHOUGH HE READS THROUGH HIS NOTES. HIS NOTES ARE VERY CLEAR BUT DONT BE DECIEVED .....WHEN THE GUY SAYS HE IS GOING TO FAIL ONE THIRD OF THE CLASS HE WILL FAIL IT. SO I HIGHLY RECOMMEND TAKE ANOTHER PROFF BUT HAVE HIS NOTES. HIS MARKING IS ALLOVER THE PLACE. DONT TAKE THIS PROFF.

Great prof. For me, he really opened wide the world of computing science. Marking was fair and transparent. He encouraged questions and had a genuine interest in promoting understanding of the content. Outside of class, he was always available during his office hours and provided all the tools and explanations necessary to complete any assignment.

One of the best profs ever. funny and uses a lot of cultural refrences (telling us to not bit torrent to download the VB program cus SFU has a download archive area). swears in his lectures. Course was A LOT better than the surrey version of CMPT 110. quizzes are open book, open notes. MT and Final, you're allowed to bring 4 page cheatsheet!

I can't believe the way he scale the grade. He fails 1/3 of class students. In order to get C+ is above 78, and class average is 70 %. I strongly recomment to take with other prof and not with such a mean teacher. However, his notes are very clear. Get his notes and take with other prof!

His notes are very clear but in lecture he just read over the lecture note. He is very helpful when you asking not hw and projects related question. However, his exams are extremely hard and he expects more than what he taught. Lastly, he says he will fail 1/3 of class of students, he means it! I suggest, obtains his note and take with other prof.

A "language processing in Prolog" class much more than it is an AI class. Doesn't explain things well in lecture and the overall "flow" of the course seems disorganized. I recommend seeing her outside of class or sending e-mails, she's very helpful and thorough there. Overall, not as bad as some of the reviews here suggest, but not great either.

First of all she is so mean, and she has such a cheap character! Second, she does not understand that she's teaching undergrad not some grad students w/ tons of AI background! Exams do not make sense and they are so abstract. She is so unorganized. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE W/ HER!

Do not take this course with her if you are actually interested in AI. She is very knowledgeable in AI but her teaching abilities are terrible and she is not friendly to students. A terrible prof. and not a nice person.

She is very knowledgeable, but a bit disorganized with her lectures. it is better if you know prolog pretty well before you take the class otherwise be prepare to master it. Her marking is pretty tough for the exams and midterms so study hard for them and you can easily get an A in the course. I would recomend taking her class!!

The absolute worst prof at SFU. Not helpful and mean to students. If you ask her to clarify on a point made during lecture she will glare at you and accuse you of not paying attention in class. During class she threatens to fail half the students then indulges herself by boasting about the things that she has done with her life.

This was the worst teacher I have ever had. This was suppose to be an AI course but instead she made it into a course on the only thing she knows (Prolog). The only problems is she doesn't know how to teach that either. Rearranges the syllabus.

She never seemed prepared to teach. Made the class up as she went along. Changed the date of the final exam half way through the course which threw everyone. I felt like I didn't learn anything in the course and was glad when it ended.
A bit flakey
No midterm = no idea what final will be like. Lectures didn't prepare us well for assignments. Moved final 2 weeks earlier "for our benefit".

so so. not helpful outside of class.
atrocious speaking/teaching abilities, arrogant, multiple complaints filed
Very considerate of students time and workload.

Amazing professor, great at explaining and very passionate about teaching! It's impressive to see a professor balancing all his research alongside being an excellent teacher. You will learn a lot from him and he speaks a ton about his industry experiences as well which I love!

Great professor, his biggest issue is yapping, but he's a good professor, people who gave bad reviews should switch majors because the quizzes were reasonable and the assignments were moderate, it doesn't get easier from here on out.

5 tough, assignment that build on top of each other (don't mess up the first few) + final exam. Vague feedback, hard to debug old assignments, but instructor/TAs are very responsive. The instructor genuinely cares, but has high expectations for students. VERY coding heavy, prerequisites don't do it justice. Don't take with other heavy classes.

He was very fair and a decent prof for CMPT 300. No final exam, only two quizzes, 2nd quiz was not cumulative and covered material past the first quiz only. Majority of grade came from the 4 assignments we had to do, which were pretty doable. I believe 454 is his passion class hence the higher difficulty, but would take again for a class like 300

Great prof. 4 assignments + 2 MT for the grading, no finals which is awesome. Very knowledgeable and slides are detailed and easy to comprehend. Only issue was that the class was way too early in the morning, but that's more of a scheduling issue. Would take a course with Tianzheng again!

Honestly one of the best lecturers I have encountered in my degree so far. Dr. Wang was engaging, open to questions and very approachable. He made a notoriously hard course a breeze. Even though CMPT 300 will no longer be offered I would recommended taking any course with him as a professor.

I wish that we had homework or quizzes so that we can check how well we absorbed the materials before exams. The professor and TA really save their time by making no homework/quizzes/feedbacks, but it causes confusion for us students. It's reaally an awful learning experience for me.

Seems to me that the course has too many aims to achieve: both introductory ones and advanced ones. It's like one course trying to achieve two courses' goals. The professor just crams everything into the course without adjusting the workloads according to the limited time and students' levels.

He set high standards for students, but he didn't seem to spend enough time or make enough efforts to guarantee good logistics or fair grading. It left me the impression that our work was graded arbitrarily, in a hasty manner, and by few things.

While the professor said that he "really" wants to give us marks, the truth is rather the opposite. Minor mistakes in the projects, each can lead to deduction of 2-3 points in the final grade. So, if you have several minor mistakes, you might receive much lower-than-expected grade (for example, A- becomes B+ or B).

Some professors hold high expectations for students. However, they usually show a clear path of how to be successful in their courses by giving clear instructions. Wang's problem is not tough grading, but not giving useful instructions. You'll be surprised by the reasons why the marks are deducted.
Prof Wang is a tough grader. In fact, I haven't met any other professor at SFU who is so harsh in grading.
For knowledge: he didn't explain things well, and he skipped key concepts. Online resources can offer better learning experience. For grade: Wang is an especially harsh grader to be avoided.

The course was not extremely bad, but the logistics was toxic. He changed his mind/policy many times without formal announcements, and certain policies were made just before the final deadline. I am not a fan of such lazy and disorganized manner.

Professor Wang never give us clear and up-front instructions so that we have a guideline of what's expected. The feedbacks are released too late. Without an idea of what are his actual requirements, you'll find his grading arbitrary. Seems that he graded at the last minute, and it's graded by few things.

Learning from a demanding, irresponsible, and unpredictable professor can be frustrating. If you really can't avoid him, you still can try to get the best scores from all other courses, and that will demonstrate to the world that you did excellent work, and the poor grading is his problem.

On average, you are expected to get B- or C+ from him, while other CS professors would give you an average of A-. It's not just some students are falling behind, it impacts everybody. The exceedingly low average grade clearly indicates that he can't teach well and he's just not responsible enough for teaching matters.

Some project topics require us to use high performance computers for experiments. No one tell you this, but you lose marks if you don't do it. I don't think it's fair that we got lower marks because of having no access to a good computer. I'm not satisfied that prof never make instructions clear and fair.

Surprised by the number of negative reviews. There is a lot of content covered but the lectures, projects and instructions are well organized and fair. Project builds on top of other modules so it can be difficult to catch up if something goes wrong early. Overall I enjoyed building the toy database and learnt a lot from the course.
Life is hard. Don't make it harder by learning with him. "Why is the score so low? What did you do?" "I worked hard but the professor is Tianzheng Wang. He's impossible."
Horrible. Maybe he's good at research, but he definitely doesn't care about teaching and students. Avoid if you can.

I'm grateful that I don't find a second GPA destroyer like this one at SFU. For those who want to learn database: he's not the only professor who teaches this course. You have better alternative options.
Prof Wang successfully minimizes his efforts and maximizes students' frustrations.

I strongly discourage you from taking his course. I can't think of another professor at SFU who gave me such bad learning experience. Our work was graded carelessly. Hardworking was not rewarded and minor mistakes can be punished harshly. Since there's no homework/instructions/grading criteria, we are punished for his laziness and awful logistics!

I've been at SFU for 4 yrs and I've overcome many difficult courses. I have confidence tackling difficult courses, not difficult professors. tzwang is definitely the most difficult professor (not one of) I've ever seen. He just made everything unnecessarily hard because he didn't do his job properly. He's second to none in creating low morale.

Taking course with him you'll get much heavier workload and much lower grade. But he did this for your good, right? Even if he doesn't explain things clearly and doesn't check details for fair grading, you did learn a lot and you'll have something funny on your transcript. Isn't that great? Isn't that the perfect course to take?

He didn't care if students absorb the lecture stuff. He didn't give useful, clear instructions. Some feedbacks were released too late, and grade was based on few things. I doubt if he put even minimal efforts in teaching.

He made the course unnecessarily hard by giving vague instruction, few feedback and non-sense grading. He has constantly proved that he's failing at teaching yet he's proud of that. Don't let this guy ruin your GPA or your confidence in learning database. Database is not difficult, he is. He would punish students for his own faults. JUST AVOID!!

Some graders are tough cuz they care about ur success in future. This one just grade by few things and pick out minor mistakes to deduct marks heavily. He doesn't care about students, just want to spend no time in teaching.

I have a rubric for prof wang. Being absent for 2 lectures: -2; Dismiss class before scheduled time >2 times: -2; No clear instructions before assignments: -4; Give low average grade without good reason: -4. Overall: -7/5. There's no rescale because that's not fair to other professors. I'd give a high score but this is the rightful thing to do.
This is the WORST prof at SFU. Giving him 1/5 rating is unfair to other professors receiving 1/5. His rating should be something like - 1/5.

Only those who have already learned the stuff before taking the course can get a merely fine grade. He's not teaching. He just wants everyone to be an expert and contribute to his own projects. Indeed he's lazy and irresponsible.

After midterm, he suggested those who earned 20%-30% in the exam to "work harder". He said that if you put everything there in the report it will be fine. It is funny and unrealistic. But it is also EVIL because he knew crystal clear in his mind that he won't let them pass and he still gave them false hope and tortured them.

Giving students extremely low scores is not a way to prove that "I am the best, I am too good to be at SFU". No clear instructions, few feedbacks, unrealistic syllabus/learning goals, requiring expertise as an intermediate course... these all indicate poor design of the course and neglect of students. It is so pathetic and pathological.

Difficulty should be 6 out of 5. Don't care to improve teaching, provide feedbacks, improve logistics, or make policies clear. Average grade is much lower than other courses at SFU. There are way better database courses online from really top professors. Work harder and get a lower grade from him is the last choice you might want.
First course that I failed in 4 years.
Disappointed
Terrible
One of the worst prof I've taken so far. His assignments are really difficult. This course also had a negative impact on my other courses. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM.
I am just astonished how misleading the reviews can be ... Look at those reviews below with full marks, then think about quite the opposite, which might be closer to reality.
Prof wang is good and helping but lectures are low quality which confused me many times. There is a lot of coding in this course and unrealistic difficult grading standards.

He's great. Lectures are efficient and very coherent. Most importantly, attendance is not required but participation is rewarded. Responsive and good choice of TAs. Assignments and quizzes are challenging and educational. Rare to find profs as good as he in SFU CS department.

Awesome prof. He explains the concepts quite well and if you have more questions he is approachable to ask. The database project is very hard and he warns us to be prepared and to drop if we find the first milestone really hard. (many people dropped and complained because they literally started milestones 1-2 days it was due and we had 3 weeks...)
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. THE DATABASE PROJECT IS DAMN NEAR IMPOSSIBLE. OVER 40% HAVE ALREADY DROPPED THE COURSE. IT'S ALL IN C AND GUESS WHAT THAT'S WHAT YOUR GRADE WILL BE.
he's the best prof you could take 300 with. He's very good at explaining the concepts. The assignments and exams were fair imo

Sometimes the lecture content can be a little too vague, but Dr. Wang is helpful if you ask further questions about stuff you didn't understand in office hours. No final exam, however the quizzes weren't easy. Reasonable assignments -- everything you implement is discussed in lectures. Despite earlier reviews, he was not very active on Piazza.

I liked the project where we built a database engine - it was good to see how to apply some of the theory (its a lot of locks). I do wish we implemented query parsers/optimizers. The lectures were long and a touch on the dry side (and i find many of the topics interesting).

Very project heavy, but you learn a lot. You implement a database and it's worth 85% of your mark, he also gives chance for bonus marks and if you do well he gives you a good mark, no scaling (his words). Quizzes weren't hard if you attended lectures and studied a tiny bit, no final. Learned a lot
Hard projects, quiz beyond expected difficulty

To be honest, this class was not for me. I am not a fan of low level programming or using C++. However, if this is what you do like, then you will love this class. The assignments are really tough if you do not know C++. The profs instructions were not extremely helpful. The lecture notes are also very dense, almost unreasonably so.

This is without a doubt THE best course currently offered that I have had the pleasure to take at SFU. The group project is well structured, but start early! tzwang has proven lecture after lecture that he really understands the content and can _easily_ answer any/all questions. His content is very well structured - one of the best profs at SFU!

You get to learn many programming techniques while doing the project, the class material is not hard but the challenge is to memorize all of them. as for the project, he literally said "if you can do well in this project, you can theoretically do well in any other programming project." So if you can survive, very interesting and useful class.

Incredibly fast replies on Piazza: 30 min after posting. His replies help you through the issue you're facing and assignment criteria is clear. Textbook is unnecessary. He provides his lecture notes and they're amazing! They go through everything you need for the tests, which accurately reflect course material. 10/10 would take his classes.
Just going to say one thing, he replies piazza questions faaaaaaastt :)
Easily one of the best professors I have had at SFU. He is super approachable, his lecture notes are amazing, and his midterms are more than fair.

Great prof, really clear up front what the grading scheme is and the schedule that he sets is pretty much bang on. He posts lectures notes online beforehand and posts the video lectures after. He is very approachable for questions. Only criticism is that assignments are marked too slowly. Course material is hard, but he is the best prof to take.

Very friendly and approachable professor, his lectures are relatively easy to follow. Exams are fairly easy. Assignts are highly related to the materials and are interesting to do (I really mean it), it's just too much work for only 6% . Take with him if you can handle 5 harder assignments and like easier exams.
this is the best professor I have ever taken in SFU his lecture is very clear and the assignment is highly related to the lecture, if you have a chance just take cmpt300 with him.
Makes the class significantly harder than it needs to be. Assigns very hard assignments and doesn't curve the class when everyone bombs the exams.

The grading schemes kept shift according to the pace of the course. Lectures are recorded. Assignments are detailed. Communicate if you have questions or issues with assignment. Would also give an extension, if needed. You can easily get an B+/A if you follow instructions and do the submissions on time. Enjoyed the class
Never take this professors class. Awful classes and simply awful grading practices
Worst professor I have ever come across. He acts really nice and screws the student. Lot of favoritism. Worst class i have been to. Teaching is really poor as well. Wish I had not taken his class
easy stuff but the prof's very disorganized and not very good at teaching in general. i'd recommend to take this course tho as a gpa booster.
Most disorganized prof ever! He's a great prof tho, really knows what he is talking about. Almost like a student as he procrastinates so much . Final was hard.
Amazingly funny and very knowledgeable. Great at explaining concepts and ideas and is very patient. Really helpful outside of class as well. An amazing teacher in every way.

Generally Ted is a very good professor however he's very disorganize. He procrastinates at the same level as a normal university student, so our group project was being written up while we're doing it. Also because of the time crunch a lot of the project was done by Ted and we wrote very little code. Great experience overall.

Ted seemed to always be behind schedule in terms of topics and assignments. This made the first few weeks fairly slow and lacking in content and the latter half was basically a rush to get things done on time. To be fair, he tries his best to answer any questions and is always open to feedback.

He's big on C++, so if you don't know it, then get ready for lots of work. He tried hard to make the class do-able for non-C++ students, but I still found it difficult. It was his first time teaching 225 (Fall 2015). 225 shouldn't be difficult if you study well. Textbook is your friend. And GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS! He'll help you.

A great instructor - keeps class interested, hilarious if you share his sense of humour. Assignments are a bit long, but not at all difficult if you start early. Negatives: goes on tangents when students ask questions, could give more review material. Assignments took forever to mark, but fast exam marking more than compensated.

cmpt474 is still in course design stage. Assignments were not defined until very late in the semester. The prof and TA had to program more than all the students in order to setup assignment environments. Group processes consumed 23 of assignment time while most of the other 13 in his office asking for assignment requirement clarification.

He's very disorganized, and doesn't really know where the course is headed. Extremely unclear instructions. Scarce feedback. Irrelevant lectures. Save your money, and learn AWS on your own (unless you need this course for grad).
Chipper, helpful but quite disorganized. I spent a lot of time with the textbook on my own to get through the course.

Very good at clarifying questions. The first half of the course goes by too slowly and the second half is done very quickly so if you have a lot of trouble at the beginning this may not be the course for you.

Prof is helpful during his office hour, really wants you to figure out for yourself how to solve your problem while giving little hints. Lectures are a little boring but informative, bring a coffee to keep awake. Nice professor, will miss him.
He's a good, fair professor. A bit disorganized but lectures are still interesting and he's clearly very knowledgeable in the material.

Very accessible and very generous with his time in office hours. He's well-spoken and also more well-rounded in his knowledge than most cmpt profs. Interesting fellow who made the most of fairly dull material. Doesn't work off slides, takes criticism well--it was his first time teaching the class. Would definitely take another class with him.

Fun/interesting prof, although he was unorganized at times. It was also annoying to not have a textbook, not very used to HAVING to attend lectures and take notes these days. I actually feel like I learned some very important principles pertaining to user interfaces - but for the amount of content in this course,it could have been taught in a month

This class is easy as long as you keep you and know what you're doing. His lectures are boring. He gave only 1 assignment before the midterm and 5 assignments due before the final. Unfair marking. Gave 5 questions on the midterm that he DID NOT cover in lectures, then only gave 2.6 more marks. UNFAIR FINAL GRADE! I deserved WAY BETTER.
Great lectures, no textbook needed.
lecture is ok, but the final grade is really unfair!!! For example, he gives B when you actually can get A. Do not expect a high grade from him.
nice guy, easy course

unbelievable! how can students recommend this lecturer for this course? Yes, he may be intrested in the course, but he lacks ability to transfer knowledge: unprepared & boring lectures, difficult exams on easy content; taking this course with him is a GREAT mistake for a serious student cos it does not matter if you work hard or not.Take my advice!
Nice guy, very easy course, but very slow. The only imperfect thing is that he never gives feedback and comments on homework.
Interesting class. Very easy for someone who can speak english. Oh yeah and he is a SUPER slowwww marker!

A nice guy, however the class was very disorganized. The marking is EXTREMELY slow. Don't count on your drafts being used for the final papers. Feedback is little to none. The marks seem like they were picked out of a hat and given. It's like "hey this paper looks good you can have an A, but this paper sucks so you can have a D".

It's "Writing for Computer Scientists." The class is going to be painful for any computing student. That being said, he does a good job to make it tolerable and hey, no tests. However.. SLOWEST MARKER EVER. The last assignment was due 2 weeks ago, and as of this point we have 2 out of 8 assignments marked.

This prof is poorly organized. More than a couple of times he showed up to lecture saying "there's no lecture 'cause I haven't prepared". Assts and tests are given back very slowly (got first assignment more than halfway into the semester). Marking seems random and he can't even explain what's going to be on the test.
there have to be better than this guy for this course

went to class once every other week just studied notes from first 2 weeks and got an A. hes boring to listen to very repetetive not prepared, and his example lack imaginaiton. whole course spent criticizing websites. lame but good for those who need gpa boost they give him lot of good reviews here!

This was the easiest course I've ever taken at SFU. But mostly that was because I was already pretty interested in interface design and Ted's passion for the subject made me even more interested. If you're going to take this class you couldn't find anyone better to teach it.

Glad I didn't take a REAL cmpt course with this guy, he's very low on talent - not even close to the esteem of other profs. I dreaded every moment of class with him because he just doesn't seem to say anything of meaning. The course was very easy but unfairly marked. Work hard or don't work at all and you'll still get the same grade.

He makes sure you really understand concepts through assignments that make you apply things you've learnt in class. This is not a course where you memorise and spit out. It's one you digest. His style of teaching may not be pleasing to some because he seems scattered at times, but he sure bent on you learning!

This class is mostly writing and a lot of imagination. No programming... if you can write a decent essay then you shouldn't have problems in this class. Textbook is boring... prof makes it a bit more interesting with real world examples and an interesting group project.

Sometimes unorganized, but a good prof. Lots of in-class practise, which is the best way to learn this material (practice, review, evaluate eachothers' work). Nice guy, great UI anecdotes. Always concerned about the 'UI' of the classroom! ("Can you see that there? Is that too bright/blurry/dark/small?") :)
I'd agree with previous comments that he is unorganised. He kept on changing the evaluation criteria which was the most frustrating part of the course. Kind of picky on things!
No Comments

Very unorganized. Frequently came to class unprepared. Class average was very high up until the final exam. Did not follow his initial schedule. Assignments were not graded in a timely fashion. Good personality but bad lecturing skills!!!
Easy contents but hard exams.
Gotta be the worst class in Cmpt Sci.
Gotta be the easiest class in Cmpt Sci
He is very into this subject and explains really well. He is also ready to explain as many times as students need. This professor is straightforward and sometimes sarcastic.
It's fun watching the undergrads fails, but it still really tough . I feel so sorry for all the undergrads taking this course with us.
Class is very difficult
He is so smart!
No Comments
Very nice guy and patient when helping you during the lab. The lectures, however, are quite dry and I have a hard time staying focused for the entire 2 hours.
Very nice and approachable prof. Class is very relevant and final project is a good thing to have in interviews. Be prepared to write tonnes!

He is nice and teaches well. Exams are fair. Only thing is you will get a LOT of homework, every week actually. Be prepared to put in the time and don't take too many other classes. Also, he often isn't clear about what he expects for the HW.
Great prof, brings a lot of real world perspective into a class that could easily be very boring otherwise. Examples are good but should only be looked at as samples.
The best part of this course is that you will feel computer science is so funny to learn and easy to pass! I am so glad I can take my first CMPT 165 with this professor. Good luck guys.
Best prof for macm 101 at sfu. Absolute legend.
GOAT that's all I have to say

Im very close to graduating, and I can confidently tell you that David Chou is the best professor i've had at SFU. I think the school would highly benefit from having professor Chou teach other professors how they should run a class. Im not just saying this because I thought the course was easy, I actually learned a lot and had a great time as well
hes pretty much jesus
One of the best professors at SFU. He explains things very well and sets students up to succeed.
Super nice, because of exam pressure forgot to submit my assignment on time but he gave me one full day extension Also he explains concepts so well
"thats a good question" 🗣️🗣️🗣️
"So that's the main idea here"🔥🔥🔥

Genuinely a very friendly and caring professor who is always down to answer questions and tweak things for a better learning experience. The assignments and concepts are interesting and they don't end up being too hard or too easy and challenge your learning properly. The first midterm is a bit difficult so be ready for that. Highly recommended

An awesome instructor. The lectures were super clear, and you don't really need to read the book to get the concepts. Assignments were graded pretty generously. The exams can be a bit tricky, but everything comes straight from the book or lectures.

David was an excellent instructor. He explained concepts clearly, supported students well, and made lectures engaging. Midterm 1 and the final were tough, but he adjusts grading fairly, so there's no need to worry.
best prof, very lenient and kind

He cares about the students and tries to give them the best he can. If you have any difficulties with his course, go to his office hours. He tries to give a full understanding of the ppt slides. At first, I won't guarantee whether you will like him or not but in the end, you won't regret taking his lecture as a lot of the students in my class did.

Nicest, kindest professor you will ever meet, Such a great guy, and always so helpful and understanding, even for assignement extensions, etc. Good lectures too, although majority of the course was just set by the John Edgar, midtern and exam were fair.
Kindest and most helpful CS prof I've had yet.
Such a great guy.... def recommend him

David is a good prof, some explanations are confusing (mostly cuz of the slides). He does his best to help. The exams were quite challenging although I understand he didn't write them. Assignments are really long and difficult. Most took 10-20 hr and there's 5. 50% final so gotta lock in for it. Final grading scheme / scaling very fair overall!
He just good

This guy is very detailed. You know exactly what to study; he even explains the code implementations. He has lecture recordings from day 1 and is genuinely an awesome guy. He has a strong accent thogh, but it works in your favor. keeps you awake as he tries to be funny. in my opinion, the most goated 200 level course prof.

A really good professor, answers my questions and explains it really well so that I can understand it. He explains the slides while also giving us examples so that we understand what the slides are telling us. If he doesn't really understand what are the slides are saying, he will put the explanation in the announcement later in the day on canvas.

David is one of the BEST profs I've had. His lectures are amazing and very interesting. He is a really nice person and responds to all email fast. His exam study instructions are very clear and helpful.
Like the other commen said was help back my John's slides. However David is an amazing person and really easy to talk too. Replies to emails very quickly, some would say to quickly!

Goated prof, incredibly approachable, gives great feedback, and very helpful and understanding. Unfortunately, he was being held back by the Burnaby section's lecture and exam materials which were abysmal in quality and difficulty. If it weren't for David's input, I would have failed.

He's lecture is clear. It is important to get used to his accent. He is willing to help students. However, he always mark tests very late. For the midterm he released the grade after the course drop date(almost 3weeks after the test). There are plenty of online sources for this course. So the grade part is important when taking it in university.
THE GOATTTTTTTTTTTTTT 🐐 Lectures are easy to follow and he gives advice on how to perform well in class. Love this guy so much.

I had him as a guest lecturer in cmpt365 for two weeks. I can tell he enjoys the content he is teaching and he knows his stuff. He has good examples to improve your understanding and tries to show real life applications. He also keeps the class interesting and lighthearted with small jokes.

Dr. Chou is a great teacher, with clear instructions and very intuitive explanations;however Some of the tests are very long and need to be done very fast. Read the textbook, understand the homework completely, and really try to understand the structures and you will have an easy A+
I took CMPT225 with him. He is a good instructor who knows how to warm up the class, and he is nice to every student!

David is a great professor. He's very approachable and friendly to questions. You can tell he isn't jaded from teaching (yet) compared to some other profs and still has that young person energy. The content is interesting and structured well. Exams are fair (though mt1 was on the harder end). Would take a course with David again!

Professor David is truly an outstanding educator and one of the most impactful professors I've had the pleasure of learning from. His deep knowledge of the subject matter is evident in every lecture, where he effortlessly combines complex concepts with real-world examples, making even the most challenging material accessible and engaging.
Very good!

Professor David is a fantastic instructor who genuinely cares about his students' success. He was very responsive to emails and patiently answered questions after lectures. The course covers various media representations, including audio, images, and video, and their lossless and lossy compression methods. Highly recommended for anyone interested.
His lecture slides are not the best and are vague, he will be available for you if you have any questions. Cool, respectful, and nice guy.

Prof. David is a great professor. His lectures were quite clear and he made best efforts to listen to the students in between class to answer their questions. He replies to emails and discussions with rapid speed. There are pop up quizes at times that count towards participation so make sure to attend the class. Overall, amazing prof!
Read the textbook his slides and lectures are bad!

hope my boy is ok, the only reason you'd treat a class of students like this is if your own life is crumbling before your very eyes. unfortunately, this guy is the only one who teaches this course, you're best talking to people from past semesters as the tests reuse all questions from past semesters

No students get 100% on any assignment, all students 76% or below on midterm (43% avg). Clearly, the professor loves seeing people suffer. He isn't even a good teacher ever, don't ever go to his lectures, they don't help. Train your photographic memory!
AVOID ABSOLUTELY AVOID. Highest grade was 70% on one of the exams. Avg grade was 40% on exams. If you can take a separate class then do it and learn distributed systems on your own time!!!!
Never again, another professor abusing the ability to get away with C- averages. It's a prof problem and a school problem, it's not the students. Absolute worst experience, I hope he sleeps poorly.
Poor grading schemes that don't reflect student's work, but rather reflect a clear effort in trying to undermine students no matter how well they actually do.
C- average what can i say :sob:
If exams are easy if you attend lectures then why the C- average. Take this only if you want a challenge and a low gpa.

RUN! DON'T TAKE THIS COURSE!!!!!! prof have heavy accent. Assignments worth 40% and Exams 60%. Class final grade average C-, NO SCALE UP. Exam 1 Mean 57% Median 59% Exam 2 Mean 40% Median 40% Exam 3 Mean 42% Median 43% If "Exams are easy if you attend lectures" then why we have 86 people but only 7 people got 60% or above on Exam 3
Run! All the good review u saw were fake. Class average is c- no curves. If you ask him questions he in class he will just mock you for being dumb.

How the department approved a C- avg is beyond me. Exam avgs are 40-50% and is worth 60% of the total. Assignments are better but still ambiguous. At least with other tough courses they'd scale or curve if the class is failing. This guy doesn't do that. Never take a course with him unless absolutely necessary or if you don't care about your GPA.
Everything is coding based. Exams are easy if you attend lectures. Keval covers a lot in lectures than his slides actually.

Keval is a good teacher who makes you work hard, but we learn a lot. The coding assignments are practical but not simple. This course made me realize my previous classes were useless money wasted. I got job in cloud infrastructure company because of this course because the interviewing team knew about Keval's tough standard!!!!
Amazing lectures getting easy topics could cover advanced topics

Professor Keval is super nice and understanding. The classes were awesome and more like discussions sometimes boring. The TA grades assignments very hard though. I thought I would get B but I got A- and my friends also got higher grades so we think he curves grades. Bad is many people failed because of plagiarism.

Assignment descriptions are often vague, yet the expectations for implementation are extremely specific. Lecture slides could also be much clearer. Also lost marks on being too verbose on one assigment but for the following assigment they docked marks for not being verbose enough. Assigments didnt teach me much either, it was repetitive.

Very good professor. Knows what he is teaching and knows coding. Only problem is he expects students to know basic coding from courses taken before. He also expects students to remember topics like discrete math and operating systems which is totally unreasonable.

Quizzes, despite being online were only open Tuesdays between 2pm and 7pm, despite the lecture being on Friday. It's fine, not like people have jobs, commutes or anything important between 2pm and 7pm. Professor didn't even respond to an email asking for accommodation for this nonsensical policy.
Amazing prof in explaining and lecturing. Assignments are not difficult if you know simple C++.
He explains well but the assignments are hard. Also I'm really bad with accents but I got used to it by the second week.

I felt that I did good on assignment 0 since tests passed but I got 30%, so I dropped the course. Hear me out. He explains really well as long as you stay engaged. But the assignments are too rough for me. There is an assignment every week, and I struggle so much despite starting 4 days before due date. In interest of mental health I had to leave.

Lecturing is informative to learn a lot. Quizzes are easy if you follow material. I think the quizzes and assignments can be made more challenging. Plagiarism checking make many students get F grade. One of the few profs at SFU I respect.
Quizzes are pretty rough, make sure to pay attention on the example in the lecture. Using chatGPT is even worse, you may get worse mark. The assignments are ok, but make sure you do it earlier.

Quizzes are tough, so make sure you keep up with lecture material and ask questions. Assignments are graded very strictly as even a small seemingly small mistake (such as continuously allocating/deallocating an array in a loop) could cost you half marks of a 15% assignment. TA office hours are extremely helpful for coding assignments. Cheers.

Dr. Keval Vora is an expert in his field with a passion for his discipline. He provides concise lectures and relevant assignments that are challenging yet realistic. While he seemed quite intimidating at first, I realized I just can't read his humour. For the Tues quizzes, set a weekly alarm and remember the questions he asks in class.

Keval's CMPT431 is a must-take. He delivers complex course content in an interesting and engaging manner. He's very passionate about the content and is extremely approachable and caring outside of class. His class far outshines others at SFU by being practical, foundational, and relevant to industry. The grading is clear, fair, and reasonable.
Keval is cool and no nonsense prof. Clear grading criteria, fair and reasonable. Very good lecturing. Mentors outside class.
Lectures are good and Vora makes it conversational and interactive. Sometimes boring mainly because of silly student questions. Dad jokes are excellent quality.
Keval vora is a gem. I will work at Microsoft because of him.
Good lectures and easy course but so many assignments. Grades are ok class median is B+
He has no sense of how to grade.

Out of touch with how much coursework he is assigning. Adamant about "it should only take x hours" but none of his students agree with him. He knows its a lie. He's similar to Arrvindh. They both make the course unnecessarily difficult without contributing to learning outcome. Creates mechanical coding robots instead cultivating understanding.

He is knowledgeable. Very very strict marking. Introduced negative marking for two quiz and then removed as many students ask him to remove it. Tough quiz. Doable assignment. Overall, 431 is a really good course with Keval if you know to work with his style.
431 is good with Dr Vora. I think Dr Vora should relax and take a smoke before his lecturing. He has a hard shell but he is clearly a softy from inside. Very caring and passionate.

Take this course ASAP. I am sad Keval only offers final year course because it teached a lot about real coding and software development. It should be given to 2nd and 3rd year also. Keval is knowledgable, kind, helping.
Awesome lectures so far. Restrict assignments plz.
KEval is nice and helpful and his grading criteria is clear and strict. Do not get free grades.
Awesome prof! I learned about research and graph mining softwares. Prof vora is so kind and caring he answered all questions and encouraged to discuss during lectures.
Awesome graduate level course for undergrads for discussion of graph mining papers. Learnt so many new things. I will request to work in Keval's lab.
I enjoyed the course and the challenge. Assignments were great way to apply what I learned. I would be interested in learning more with you. Thanks Keval :)
Take his course if you want learn parallel programming and distributed programming. Best teacher in my 4 years!!!! Bigdata graph programming assignments are bonus.
Good professor and teaches nice. Coding C++ is required and prof expects us to read documentation of certain API calls. I get good practice and learnt a lot.
Professor Keval Vora is best teacher. He taught us distributed systems with good lecturing and assignments. He also helped me for a fulltime job in data processing.
This course initially made me realize I don't know so much but with some extra reading I was able to do with all assignments and got A. Vora is quite cool.
High recommendation for software job. Prof keval vora is helping and gives many bonus options to cover missing grades.
Big thumbs up for distributed systems course !!! I got high-pay fulltime job from this course.
I like Keval Vora a lot. He is only professor teaching systems course properly. Covered a lot of topics in 431 but Keval makes it easy. My coding definitely improved because this course.

Professor Vora sets the bar very high. There is a lot of assignments, and a lot of topics covered in class. So it is a difficult course. But his teaching is excellent, and I learnt much more in this course than any other course so far. Take this course only if you do not have other heavy course.

Keval is tough, and gives tough exams and assignments. Pay attention in class, the content is important. Ask questions if you don't understand or are curious, he gives good and clear answers. Spend lots of time on the assignments, they will give you a much better understanding. I learned a lot and would definitely take a class with Keval again.

Only take if you are already familiar with the course contents and want to improve your depth of knowledge. Midterms and final build upon covered topics (usually not enough time to finish). Highly recommend taking CMPT 295 before CMPT 300 (similar content).
If you have to take him, get ready to work hard and sink time for a decent mark. Exams aren't that hard but may be different from what you'd expect by studying slides.

The grading of assignments is like a joke, you did such hard work, you write thousands of code and you could successfully compile and run on your PC, but surprise, you only received 0 for all your hard working and all the time you spent. HAHA

He makes the course hard, but I think that's the way you learn the most. I personally likes his teaching style. He is very knowledgable and is always ready to help, even with questions outside of the scope of the class.

About 6 assignments throughout the term, most taking me about 10-15 hours each. Lots of content, but follows text book fairly closely. Lectures were theory heavy, while tests were much more applied. Would be better if assignments related more to tests, but that means even more course load. Helps a LOT if you have solid background in C programming.

Keval does an excellent job explaining concepts in a class that is notorious for being difficult. He says in the very first lecture that the course load is high, and boy is he right. Assignments take an incredible amount of time and are marked very strictly, but you do learn a lot. Midterms are extremely difficult but final grades are curved.
Kernal Vora is one of the most difficult people I had to compile with.
I'm disappointed in Keval.
Keval, don't promise to scale the course and then hand out C- and D grades like candy. Be more transparent with the grading.
Avoid

This course will take up all your time. This is not an exaggeration. Get ready to learn everything yourself and spend at least 8 hours per day on this course. If 8 credit undergraduate courses existed, this would be one.
Unreasonably difficult exams and projects. You're best off waiting and taking it with anyone else but him.

Keval was probably one of the best profs I've had. While many people dislike his strict teaching style, I found it very respectable and helpful. Slides were not posted before class, but that's because he wants you to engage in lecture. He really tries to ensure everyone understands before moving on. Very knowledgeable. Amazing prof!

He knows everything about OS, can answer whatever you ask. On the other side, grading is tough, exams tricky and projects heavy. Will adjust final grade, your actual grade won't be that bad. Prepare to invest tons of time in it. Stop daydreaming. Operating Systems IS THAT TOUGH with ANY teacher. You can downvote me but I found the course rewarding.

Wouldn't say the course material is particularly difficult but the slides used in class aren't the best. The homework which is about 40% of your grade will take up a lot of your time. We had 6 assignments each taking around 8 to 24 hours of actual work to complete so by the time you finished the assignments you had no time to study for the exams.

There is too much work for this course. Expect little to no free time because of the projects that this prof gives. If you have the usual course load, your semester is going to be a nightmare especially if you're a CMPT major. Get some friends and work together on the ideas for the homeworks and you might be okay.

3 bullet points: 1) You have to work very hard for this course if you take it with him 2) You will surely learn a lot from him based on his lectures and by doing the assignments. 3) Exams are very conceptual based, also tricky and hard. Do not expect to finish it just give your best. 4) In the end, he's marking is very lenient.

Very well organized and well spoken. Knows the material, can teach it well and responds very well to questions and discussion during lecture. Teaching style is very professional and adult, he does not hold your hand, be prepared to work hard. Assignments and exams are hard but fair. He is awesome if you want to learn and don't mind working for it.

2 midterms, very tough, 2nd midterm had an 35% avg... Expect to be tested on material he briefly mentions in class discussions (not in notes), also expect some trick questions. Has an hard to understand accent, but he knows what he's talking about. Overall hard class
Never ever take 300 with him! You will cry!
AWESOME PROFESSOR AND THE TA DID STRICT PLAGIARISM CHECK AND GIVE ME 0 IN PROJECT 4. RESPECT!!!
The lecture is so heavy and the assignment is so hard that made me suffer from this course for the whole semester. I think I worked so hard to get a D.

Yes, don't ever take his class. Accent really bothering. Heavy theories during class but he just repeated by the content of PPT. And his coding assignments are based on really good c coding skill. OS concept is really important for CS students, but he just kinda let us study by ourselves. Really "helpful" professor.
Please, don't take his course

Professor expect students know C coding. His lecture slide also have C code and testing also on coding questions. Assignments also programming heavy. Lecture is boring because student don't know coding.
Class is super boring and assignments are way above the level he teaches . Never take this course from him , not worth it.

Assignments are tough. Very tough. They really allow you to practice the concepts learned in class and prepare you for the difficult exams. Lectures are theory heavy. He sends tons of ridiculously lengthy emails, and assignment instructions often exceed 10 pages. Some examples he provided were from 1990. The exams included questions based on his personal beliefs.
