literally the best grading scheme i've ever had. like really 80% assignments, 10% midterm and 10% final. no need to come to class as lectures are useless (he posted notes online and he has youtube videos of him going over the notes that you can watch).
take 300 with him if you really want to learn how to code a real OS. his assignments are really hard, but you actually learnt sth. In addition, he record and put all the lecture video on youtube.

Take Steven Pearce! Arrvindh's assignments are worth 70-75% of your final grade. Sounds good? NOOO! His assignments are not supposed to be for 300 level courses!! They are too hard. Try him if you do trust me. Heavy Coding for both assignment and midterm. He is knowledgeable, but doesn't know what students needs and he is not helpful at all!!

this course is very interesting. assignments are not easy but doable and you'll get a lot of them. arrvindh is very smart, flexible with deadlines and grading but he's not willing to explain everything.

This course is very interesting, assignments are not easy but doable and you'll get a lot out of them. Arrvindh is very smart, flexible with deadlines and grading but he's not willing to explain everything.

Should not be teaching a 300 level intro class. I am sure he is intelligent, and "mad genius" is something I appreciate in professors, but Arrvindh had no idea HOW to do the assignments he himself posted. TA had to pick up way too much slack. Inconsistent and conflicting information. Would avoid at all costs. Nightmare term.

You will start off being intimidated when doing his course. His version of 300 is different and the assignments are difficult but you learn a lot! Thanks arrvindh for fun/rough semester. It was worth it in the end, though at the beginning it seemed it wouldn't be.

He is very knowledgeable in his topic, and very accessible for additional help. Will genuinely happy to help when you work on your project. However, his lectures are not very exciting, there is little use to attend them at all.

well, she is one of my favorite teacher. her notes are clear and well organized. read all the notes will guarantee u a c+ or higher. listening to her lectures and take her side notes will get an easy A!!
If you would like to actually learn, and be in a good class then take this course with her!

Great professor. You follow the slides, and listen in class you should be have a decent mark. If you ever have questions or problems, she is very kind and will help you out. She understands the situations students are in, and keeps that in mind when it comes to assignments or exams. I highly recommend her, unless you want to do programming!

Her assignments and exam are fair, but her attitudes during class are excellent. her teaching is clear. she ll teach u more if u ask her during her office hour. she makes me feel the course is interesting and fun. ofcourse, u still have to work hard and think hard to get a high mark

Best professor ever! She is inspiring when she teaches her subject. She is a very nice person, who cares about her students. She's also a fair marker. I only wish she also taught Networking II. As it is, the choices for that course are not great.

Doesn't practice slides. Lazy, skips through difficult parts. Often has no clue what she is talking about, dodges all difficult questions that students ask. May be easy but you won't learn anything in her class.
Very good professor. Extremely clear lectures.
She does not invest enough time and effort for her classes.
Funda is bar none the best professor I've had so far at SFU. Fun, helpful, and very engaging -- she genuinely cares about her students. There's no wonder why she's the graduate director!

I enjoyed Funda's class, she's got a great sense of humour. Assignments and tests are both fair, imo (plus the class is always curved). Teaches from textbook-based slides that are posted online. My only gripes are that she can be a tad disorganized and her wording on assignments can be vague at times.

This prof is very *VERY* unorganized. On assignments she'll ask you questions she hasn't taught. Flies over slides in lecture. Sometimes skips them because she has no idea what they mean (she hasn't done her slides...). Will test you on stuff she hasn't talked about in class. Is often late, cancelled 2 classes for personal reasons.

A prof whose exams seem extremely hard, but at the end of the day, she marks very fair and really tests if you know what you're doing or not. I didn't much like the popup quizes, aka attendance checks, but i guess that's her choice. Assignments were doable and marked easily.

dun wanna comment on how she teach, i think just so-so... i got all assignments perfect... mt on avg... but end up got a D... thx to her tricky, non-sense final... u will see questions u dont know how to answer at all even with ur book opened

An excellent teacher to take 371 with. I have no idea why people complain about her using the powerpoint slides that come with the textbook, they're good slides! I much happier that she used them then tried making up her own material. Her explanations aren't always clear but if you ask her to work through an example you'll get what she means.

Lectures consist of reading slides aloud. Questions are encouraged but she answers unclearly and it doesn't seem to me like she knows what she is talking about. Not recommended, better get a networking book.

she is not quite up-to-date, but knows the course content well enough to teach it. you can do just as well by reading the textbook alone, although her explanations help, but they're just so long and windy...

Very nice and knowledable prof. Will take the time (sometimes too long) to explain the finer details, and always (and I do mean always) checks to see if there are questions. Only major complaint is that 99% of the class was her reading power point notes (which she said at the beginning of the term she wouldn't do). Otherwise, very good prof.
No Comments

I think the topics are interesting and practical. Labs are alright but projects are a bit underwhelming. Usually only takes low single digit hours to finish. 2 exams and no final. If you want a light UD course this will work.

The prof will make you will fall in love with how the IT industry has evolved over the years. How it took 50 years to build the protocols that are in use today. What problems each iteration of these protocols solved. How the networks are set up in the industry today. How does money impact the optimality of internet. Hands down awesome prof.

This professor is a good professor, he explains concepts very clear in lecture. Very helpful professor with enough office hours, and very responsive (quick reply to emails). The exams are fair, as long as you study his lecture note thoughtfully, you can get a good score in exams.
Great prof. Very kind and explains in detail. We did bad in the midterms so he made the finals easier. Approachable and always ready to help.

Gives one of the best lectures I've ever seen. Explains things in great depth, giving out examples and ensures students understand the content well. Networking may be a confusing topic at first, but Hefeeda explains it extremely well.

Lecture slides are shallow overviews with no explanations. He doesn't do a good job explaining the course material. Assignments/projects are impossible but everyone googles the answers. You learn more by reading the textbook. Course itself sounded interesting but turned out boring. Would not recommend this prof.
Follows the textbook very closely. You need to read the text book, and then read it again, and you will do well. Attend lectures, because he will give explicit notes of what is on the exam.

He kept the class really interesting. He tried hard to make the material more approachable to students and gave extensions whenever needed to help everyone get a better understanding of the course. However, he isn't a great teacher and skims over the material during the lecture. It's not a big deal, since the course follows the book very closely!

I got a bad mark, so I gave him a bad review - that's how this site works right??... seriously tho, Cons: found assignments/projects time consuming and hard. Relied highly on the text (course did not go much beyond what this offered IMO) PROS: he is a nice guy, and passionate about what does (really more suited to graduate students tho IMO).
A good prof overall. He's not funny or exciting but he's clear, organized, and explains concepts well. Assignments might be a bit challenging but the grades he gives are fair.

other then not learning much, the last assignment - 3&4 grades came less then 3 days before the final and the 3rd midterm came after the final was released. expect no feedback and no responses for your questions other then a yes or no if using online discussions with her

Compiler Design is the area of research for Apala Guha. Therefore, this course was really interesting and exciting. The process of writing compiler step-by-step was the most complicated part of the course however it was really useful. Also, I like her video lectures since they were always accessible and very informed.
Didn't learn too much, but the prof was nice and the grading was nice.
I got an A but I don't really understand databases to the degree that I should. What is even normalization?

i cant speak for 354 but she was really good at teaching 379 the test were very clear and you would pass them if you watched her online lectures which explained everything the assignments were more on the difficult side but her grading criteria for them is nice so try your best on them and youll get a good mark. overall highly recomended for 379
Best prof 2018

For Apala to receive so many bad reviews in only 4 months should be a big red flag for you. She puts minimal effort into her videos and in person lectures. Wastes 10 minutes going over material covered in online lectures. The unfortunate 80 people cant get our 4 months back, but we can help everyone else to avoid tanking their gpa and wasting time

Horrible online lectures that do not teach you what you need to know for assignments/tests. Horrible marking scheme for assignments, either get 15/15 on a problem or 0/15, even if code for the assignment was not entirely incorrect. Takes far too long to mark everything. Had to self learn everything in this course.
Take CMPT354 from her if you enjoy challenges.
BEST PROF EVEEEE
Skip the classes, study at home.

The grading criteria and rubrics for assignments were unclear and the grades were released too late so while I thought I was doing well at the assignments, my grades were terrible eventually and I could do nothing to improve them because a couple of days before the 3rd assignment deadline came, the 1st assignment grades were just released.

While her online lectures are an improvement over actually going to class in the first place, even those are slow and hard to concentrate on. In class, a time for review on material covered.. .in the online lects? She "listens" to students ask questions and then responds by talking about something different, and passes it to other students to answr

She is a professor with a really good knowledge of compiler design for CMPT 379, but she just doesn't have the background knowledge and experience for Databases, so that explains why she cannot teach the course well. If you have no choice but to take her class, it is possible to pass and end up with a decent grade, but don't expect to learn much.

An absolutely frustrating experience. Consistently late, answered students' questions with questions. In person lectures seemed unorganized, and she often ended early. The assignments lacked clarity. Video lectures were of poor quality. Asked for feedback and then blamed the students. Overall, just truly unprofessional.
Great prof :)

Video lectures were a great idea, if she did them properly. They were difficult to understand, and jumped all over the place. Any questions directed at her in class she would redirect them back to the class for another student to answer. Assignments were ambiguous and hard to follow with no examples. Please avoid.

Do not take CMPT354 with her, I REPEAT, DO NOT. You pretty much learn nothing from her. You will be better off just read and learn SQL by yourself on Tutorialpoints. or other online resources. Even though the exams are seemingly easy but the instructions and questions are usually confusing and not clear. Definitely avoid.
Confusing, unstructured lectures and assignments. Many questions are unclear and left for your to interpret as she does clearly state criteria. Answers your questions with questions.
She taught us literally nothing with those 10 minutes "video lectures". I feel like I am better dumping my tuition into the toilet than taking another course with her..

She has terrible "lecture videos" that is supposed to be your lecture notes. Out of 3 lectures per week, she hosted only 2 and skipped at least 3 lectures on top of ending the lecture early occasionally because "she has nothing else to say". Exams aren't difficult but the pure lack of content to study or learn from made writing exams hard.

A very Incompetent teacher. Asked the classroom after the mid-term if they had any issues regarding the exam. Numerous students mentioned valid claims to issues with the midterm. Every single time there was any form of criticism she would immediately blame the student. Assignments and exams were all over the place in terms of information &structure

Great teacher, just be sure to go to all the lectures. She sometimes gives questions during lecture for practice which you can answer using an app. I found that part to be helpful and engaging. If you are good with programming this may be a good choice for you. Assignments are worth around 40%, so make sure your program passes provided test cases.
ez gpa booster class when she gives you double extension

Toby is a great professor. The instructions for the assignments are always explained very clearly and he is genuinely interested in what he is teaching. He provides lots of practice for his midterms and the final. The tests are fair and are a reflection of what is taught in class. I would definitely recommend taking a course with Toby.

He makes learning python for beginners super easy, beginner python is very dry overall but he is a generous marker and cares that you actually pass the class. He also gives a very important outline of what's on the final exam so make sure you do the practice exams. Take it with him!

Attended a couple lectures and couldn't even hear him so I just stopped going. The lectures notes online however are helpful and enough to get by. Just do the weekly quizzes, and then study for midterm and final by doing the samples which are very similar. Did about 1-2 hrs of work a week and still managed to get a decent grade

If you already know Python and basic computing this should be pretty light if not it will definitely take some work. The lectures are a bit dry but the assignments and quiz's are fair and good grade boosts if you don't perform on the exams which aren't super hard anyway.

Toby is a good prof. Lectures are simple and easy to follow, assignments are very helpful, and exams are generally fair. Be careful with those exams though - I underestimated the midterm and completely bombed it. It wasn't difficult by any means, just requires a but of effort.

He was kind of incompetent... He's a CMPT professor but can't set up a microphone. I sat in the 4th row and couldn't hear him at all. I stopped going to the lectures halfway through the semester and just studied and did the work myself.

Pretty mid prof. For context, I already knew python coming into this, the way he teaches python is god awful. He spent way too much time on turtle and txt files instead of more useful things with python like pandas and stats usages. On the final exam, there was a question on something he literally did not cover which definitely f'ed ppl over.

It takes months for him to return marks on quizzes. Each quiz is 10 questions, multiple choice, and online, it should not take months. The midterm was mostly multiple choice and it still took several weeks.

Got a decent grade without much effort because all the quizzes were online , assignments were easy and mid term was basically the last semester mid term with values changed . There is just one problem with the course which is a mcq based final exam , you can loose a lot of marks there . Other than that chill prof and class

Meh, dry lecturer. If you watch lecture recordings + do practice Q's on his obsidian, you'll be fine. Assignments, midterms, and quizzes were fair, but final was a bit hard. Make sure to know ur MC, as half (or maybe more) of our final was MC.

Lectures always feel productive albeit a bit dry + they're recorded. His notes are great for studying. Assignment criteria is clear and they're graded fairly imo. No surprises during exams if you listen to lectures, do the weekly quizzes and hw. Lots of multiple choice; make sure you have a good understanding of underlying concepts/terms.
Substituted for amir, was really kind and got our emails replied to

Absolutely loved him. Fair grading, excellent at explaining. He tries to be funny but he sometimes starts giggling instead LOL. I literally watched all of the lectures starting from week 3 onwards online instead of attending the lectures and I wish I did that from the first week. He is very clear at what he is teaching. TAs and assignments were OK

Toby's lectures were pretty clear, he explains things simply and straightforwardly. He also posted video recordings of the lectures which was really nice. The midterm was insanely easy, the final was tough. Make sure you can do multiple choice. Exams reflect what was taught in class, assignments were fine. Overall solid prof.

exams are pretty easy especially if you pay attention to what he says in class and know the quiz questions. also he is good at explaining concepts and mostly clear about expectations. only complaint is he's kinda weird about answering questions in class/office hours
Toby is the GOAT, amazing lectures, practical teaching, he explains very well, exams were fair, he made the course easy
Boring explanations, unfair horrible multiple choice questions on quizes and exams, very mid.
Strong english, passionate about what he is teaching, goes over crucial important material, does not waste time

Assignments are easy and posted 2 weeks before due date but are riddled with mistakes to the point that you're better off waiting a week before starting them. Exams may contain ambiguous questions. Lectures are okay, but I found myself using John Edgar's lectures over Toby's by the second month.

(Fall 2023) I think Toby was a pretty decent prof! I *did* go into this class with some existing experience with Python, but I found that his lectures were very straightforward and his personal website with lecture notes was very well organized. He explained things well and emphasized what would be testable. Pretty funny, too!

Comprehensive notes, recorded lectures, practical teaching. Average workload with 5 assignments throughout the term, weekly quizzes, 1 midterm and 1 final exam. Some issues with grading assignments but I had a positive experience overall.

I genuinely enjoyed toby's class, I am not going to lie we all pretty well initially thought that he wasn't going to be that good but her surprised us all, his lectures were decent but outside of lectures he was incredibly supportive and he provided more than enough materials for all of use to succeed which I genuinely appreciate. GO TOBY :)

Lectures are pretty dry, but technically the teaching style is effective. (Never reads from a slide deck, instead presents a problem related to the topic and solves it in real-time). Occasionally says something funny. Provided resources are really good, (recorded lectures and in-depth notes for each one.) Midterm easier than weekly quizzes.

Easy but fun. Toby gives an average of B+ though according to the average stats in canvas it should be B. Anyway the marking is generous. The only killer is the final (59%) but you can do well by simply memorzing all the stuff.
Learned a lot about functional programming in this course, and the prof definitely knew what he was talking about.

Where do I begin? Completely disorganized lectures, awkward when answering questions, and marking seems to be utter madness at best. Just to give you an idea of how insane the grading scheme was, participation was worth more than the final exam. Also, if you so much as get the capitalization wrong for your function name, the whole thing is wrong.

The class is not difficult, but the concepts learned are very engaging. To add to this, Toby has done everyone a favour by uploading a lecture recording of every class. This makes it so that you can simply stay at home, and watch the lectures videos 1.5x speed. Toby also explains concepts well. I would recommend this class to everyone in CMPT/SoSy.

Toby is an experienced profs. Pay attention to lecture and read course notes ahead. It is not easy to get a good grade if you dont spend lots of time on this course (Unless you're super familiar with C++) You may find that assignment instructions are vague. You will learn a lot by doing assignments and final project!! Lots of effort if you want A!

Toby is a really reasonable, calm, humble and knowledgeable professor. Most of reviews giving him lower grade were written at the beginning of semester and I don't think it's fair to judge him that quickly. I didn't go to his lectures that often at the end of the course, but his reading materials cover enough for me to survive through the course.
The assignment is definitely not about what you have learned in class. I have never failed any assignments in any courses but this.
I don't even know how to describe what this class was
Imagine your worst nightmarish version of a careless bad prof and you've got Toby.

Spends his classes chatting as if it was some discord chat and has no organized presentations or class material to teach like one would expect. Beyond that he leaves everything to clueless unsupervised TAs where it is very chaotic, and you can have your grades tanked simply because they don't know how to mark or test your assignment files.
Does not care at all about actually teaching. Very chaotic class. Worst experience of my live
Run, just run for your own good. Take whatever class you need to take in another semester, even if it means waiting a couple semesters. Its for your own good

If you are looking to question your sanity whenever you spend countless hours trying to understand the assignments, wanna spend countless hours self learning the entirety of C++ because the class material is absolutely useless, and wanna risk getting an F & possibly ruin your education an curse the day you decided to enroll to SFU, then this is it

I'm not sure about his teaching in person, but online was an absolute bore. Assignment requirements are hardly clear and frequently misses things that should be on there, which makes you lose a few unnecessary marks. Despite that, super easy course if you pay attention and double-check everything.
The most disrespectful professor I've ever had.

Prof Donaldson was such an amazing prof for 125. His lectures were always really clear and he'd explain concepts from the ground up which is nice because we started learning c. We didn't have any midterms or finals, just a bunch of module quizzes which were pretty easy and the questions were word for word out of the textbook. Overall a great guy!

Lectures are well organized so if you follow them and read the notes, you'll do fine on the quizzes. He delegates the marking of assignments and the final pair project to the TAs you're assigned so he will not answer any questions about marking. But he's willing to answer any other questions for hours after lecture which is great for online classes

Toby's notes were usually quite helpful for both the Module Quizzes and the Assignments. Unfortunately, I found Toby's lectures to be quite dry. He would sometimes just read off his notes or breeze through a code without much explanation. He also made us read 12 chapters in the first 2 weeks. Not the worst prof, but definitely not the best either.

The course is unfortunately too dense (C/C++). However, Tony's lectures are amazing at explaining the topics (if you listen closely he gives great insights). Design of the quizzes were harsh (30 seconds per MC max) and if he designed the assignments, one was explained very poorly and not suitable for CMPT125. It took 24+ hrs to do well for most.
Worst Prof ever. A lot of work to do and very strict. Since is coding course, you might get plagiarism under his rule. Even its all by coincident.
he's good

we had to read about half the textbook in the first two weeks and then we never needed the textbook again. his assignments are worded in a confusing manner and his notes were more helpful than his lectures.

Toby is the definition of a low effort prof, who spends most of his time rambling about his opinions on non-technical topics. For an upper-division course, he didn't share his area of expertise/research into the lecture much. Best of all, for ML section he just showed us three 30 mins youtube videos in class.
Great prof, fun to talk to!

Toby's CMPT310 wasn't much technical. The final exam was a memory test with no problem solving questions. The midterm was easy. He just changed a few words from a sample. Assignments were not easy, but marking was generous. Overall, the course is a giveaway.
Best cmpt prof ever. He provides a lot of excellent examples during lectures and provide everything you know on the course website. Exams are very fair and everything is based on his course website.
383 was an interesting class that teaches some more obscure languages but still manages to improve your overall skill in programming. Toby teaches the course well.
Great Proff
Very nice and approachable prof but he doesn't know his material. Self study.
Very nice prof, will take his course again. The assignments are not easy but really help you to understand. Notes are online and everything are base on the notes.

IMO the best prof I've had at SFU. I've taken 135 and now 383 with him and in each of those classes I've actually learned something useful. In 383 you get really into functional programming and the languages you have to learn really teaches you to think in a different way. Very fair assignments and exams. Take this course with him.

His lectures were interesting, and his assignments were well related to the material that he taught. His midterm and final were fair, completely based on the notes for his class. Didn't use the textbook much, just read his notes on his website. If you had to take this class, take it with him!

Excellent Prof. His online notes are awesome! Just study those thoroughly. His assignments are super fun. They are like puzzles and are about the right difficulty. Highly recommend this prof for CMPT 383!!

Great & helpful prof! Very glad to have taken 383 with him, he couldn't have explained concepts better. His midterm/final were extremely fair, if you studied you did fine - generally he will give outlines, follow them! Assignments/projects sometimes tedious but for the most part very manageable. Great course overall! Definitely recommend.
The best CMPT166 teacher ever!!!
The best pro I have ever seen in my life!!! High recommended!!!!

Great prof, doesn't teach with slides but rather through example-I find this better for programming courses as you can follow along easier. Despite what was previously said, my friends and I found him to be quite funny. Final wasn't too bad, practice the problem sets given!
Really takes the time to explain things in detail. And, instead of simply reading slides off a powerpoint, he actually engages himself into doing some examples in class.
Odd personality, very intelligent concerning his subject. Tends to have low expectations of students concerning their abilities to provide quality work.

His lectures are ok, they are all online. But he is a little wierd in personality. He makes jokes in class that nobody laughts. I have yet to take his exams but from the looks of the Sample exams they look ok. Point: read the notes online before going to class

Great prof! Very helpful and he doesn't make you feel stupid when you ask him a question. Midterm was veryy fair and easy, it was so much easier than i thought it was going to be. Final was very fair if you studied and know your stuff.

At least the tests are scaled and somewhat easier than the terrible textbook (why easiness is 2). Otherwise, everyone would be spending WAY too much time (if not a lot already) on a course that is too hard since both the text and the prof are VERY demanding. *Sigh* Visit the TA often plz!
Very bad, not clear at all. Does not help with anything, go to another proffs lectures.

omg! he expects us to get 90+% for the hwk, and the first Q is correcting midterm 2, out of 1 or nothing if totally correct! I only expected him to be at least 60% good, but now he's not even going to pass :P

Clarity is hindered by his long, convoluted, impossible to follow proofs. Doesn't share knowledge about hwk pbms. Slightly better than the terrible txtbook that blabs all page. Txtbook hwk is difficult. Always see the TA for help or you will be farrrr behind. Tests are also difficult. Have tons of scrap paper ready to clarify MACM concepts.
Best person to take 210 with!
Goated prof!

He does his best to explain the course material, even though it's a difficult subject. The midterm was really tough, so you absolutely need to have a strong analytical grasp of the examples in the slides to do well. Luckily the homework was worth a big chunk of our final grade and was marked fairly, which was a huge relief.

Very accommodating professor as everyone else says. Responds to questions on piazza and email quite fast. There were four assignments worth 45% which were quite time consuming and difficult. His midterm had an avg of 43% but it was scaled slightly at the end of the course. Personally, I found his lectures very hard boring and hard to bear.

Best prof for this hard class. He actually cared about us, listened to our feedback and implemented it. Like recording lectures after we asked, making the final and marking easier after a hard midterm. Curved the end grade to make up for the hard midterm. Tries his best to make the content easier to understand. Answers you on Piazza even at 1:40 am

Professor Mohammad's lectures are detailed. Given his lectures are borrowed from another professor's (whose lecture material is very difficult), Mohammad does a great job breaking down the difficulty of this course by a couple notches! If Mohammad has the time to make his own lectures, no doubt this course will be a lot easier and less stressful

Pretty good prof. He was great at explaining the slides he was using despite adopting them from another professor. Very helpful in office hours. The midterm was awful difficulty wise but he revised the difficulty and made the final quite easy in comparison, and ended up curving slightly at the end. The course is hard though, good luck D:

The class itself is not easy whtsoever. So dont think tht a good prof = easy class. The class & content is alot of work. But the prof is super nice & caring. Responds to everyone really well. Takes feedback and improves. Is clear throughout his lectures. Consistently asks if anyone has any questions. Out of everyone who teaches 210, he is the best

I have taken Tariqs class for both Math 111 and Math 112, and I am thoroughly glad I had him as an instructor. If you make the effort to seek help from him he will go out of his way to help you succeed in his class. Great professor, hilarious too!
He's funny
oh.he is very a lousy teacher.first,his accent is very srtong.second,he is not a respond teacher.third,when we need help,he can't give us the help that can make us understad.in sum,horrible!@

Really lazy and unorganized, unfair in marking, hard to understand, not enough domination on topics, makes lot of mistakes but if you do the same you'll lose your mark. When you made an appointment with him, you should not expect to see him on time. On the other hand he usually tries to be nice and show that he's confident...
Becaue of him !!! I messed up this course. Because of him!!! my GPA has got lowed. Because of him!! I've learnt not much useful things. He may be a nice person but not ready to be a good teacher yet.

Textbook is hard to follow. Marking was unfair. Lazy teacher and not really clear with what he is trying to teach. He posts the lecture notes for the first few weeks on the course website then suddenly stopped doing so. I would not want to take another class with this instructor.

He is irresponsible, mean, lazy, rude, helpless, and unclear of his teaching. He gave us a wrong email address so that no one could ask him questions. TA was not able to explain any question we asked and had no office hour

Avoid this prof. He is really unclear so you'll spend a lot of time studying yourself. Notes are very disorganized and he always tells you to look at the textbook. The textbook used in the course sucks too, it is really hard to follow and more complicated than it needs to be. Probably the hardest to follow text book that I've used before.

He is so not organize..He doesn't explain much..Gave us a taking home assignment(part of mid-term) the same day he taught the materials and needed to finish in less than 2 days of time.The TA is useless.Did only 1-2 Qs during a 50mins lab.Everything sucks in his class.
Worst prof I've ever had in my academic career so far.
bad...bad...bad...
he is not clear on what he teaches. He doesnt explain things very well either, most of the class is confused by his way of teaching

clear lectures, knows what he is talking about, will get you interested in the material and the software space. make sure you are going for the employer project. he is accessible outisde of the lecture. will help you if you ask. the TAs are also good. You need to know the code for the Final and midterm and the theology for software developement.

(from Spring 2024) Bobby is very approachable and kind. Lectures are mostly conceptual, sometimes a little dense. He gives a few coding tutorials but you'll need to do some self-learning for the project. The lecture content and structure of the group project are extremely relevant to real-world software development. I really liked his curriculum.
Assignments are engaging, but lectures can be disorganized. Bobby is helpful when asked questions. Unstuck ai was a lifesaver for exam prep. Overall, a solid course if you put in the work.

Majority of the grade is based around one group project, this was clearly laid out and communicated. The project itself depends on how ambitious you want to be, need to find the goldilocks zone between being innovative enough and having the least amount of work without losing grades. Very nice prof overall and would recommend taking if you can.

He is good at teaching lectures, and the theory knowledge is interesting. But because 276 is a course that includes way too much workload, there are situations where he feels unhelpful. The feedback for projects are also very lacking, and it's confusing on how to do them better.
He is a good teacher, available and eager to assist students beyond regular office hours.
He is a nice prof I would say
Good prof, this class is very heavy in memorization and projects.

He was very kind and nice in lectures and while conducting himself with students. His course is more practical than theory based which is good for leaning. Assignments are fun but can take a little time and there was a group project where we got to implement our own ideas. I recommend the course for others!

Kind guy. Explains things really well in class. There's a group project, he is not nit picky about things and just wants to see you actually learn in the course. Exams have stuff he's talked about/demoed in class so I highly recommend going in person.

The main focus of the class is the project and is worth 40%. Bobby bases the project on web development, although the course material isn't explicitly about web development. He does however give a few tutorials during class and has a playlist on the basics of html, css, javascript and java spring which is enough to get started. Overall hes good.
Very difficult grader.
Simple assignment and exams, you may not need to spend lots of time on this course. Remeber to take notes, read textbook and do homework.

Great Professor, probably one of the only professors who asks to write midterm and final code on computer, which is really helpful as you can check your program by running it. Gives partial credit for almost everything, so write something and don't leave any questions.

Bobby has a good attitude and is knowledgeable. He is very nice and it is easy to ask him questions and get extra help. The assignments are fun and practical plus there is a group project. Exams arent the easiest but a good professor none the less. Would recommend him!

He's a nice guy with good assignments and projects. His lectures are not that good however and his demos leave you clueless as to what's going on. He just fires off random examples of code that co-pilot writes for him and doesn't explain much about it. His slides are largely unhelpful and his exams are tough as well. Its a good course ig.

Assignments will make you learn a lot and encourage searching finding answers online or through other sources. Bobby is very nice and gives great feedback and is very helpful when asked questions. Exams are usually slide & assignment based, very manageable if your review lectures or do good on assignments

Great professor. He taught everything slowly and understandably in a very rigorous manner. The assignments were fun and not too serious, and he was very responsive to emails and questions. He does not speed through the course; rather, he stops at every part to make sure everyone understands. Amazing professor, strongly recommend.

Bobby does mostly demos in class and posts them to GitHub which I liked. He is passionate about web dev which kept lectures engaging and is always available for Q's. This course had a LOT of material and was quite overwhelming at times but I learned a lot! Def not a GPA booster course but would recommend if ur interested in web dev.

Awful at explaining topics. His lecture notes are tested on yet he does not cover them in class which makes no sense. He instead does demo's and hardly explains what hes coding since he is just looking at another monitor with the code hes gonna write. He seems nice but SFU needs another prof teaching the course.
Exams take a lot of content from the slides. He is almost always available for any questions, would take another class with him

His lecture notes are incomplete and he doesnt even go over them in class. He expects students to go over his lecture notes on their own time which is impossible since his lecture notes aren't complete. He goes over demos in class and he himself doesnt know whats going on and student help him debug his code. His lecturing abilities are poor.
Avoid her at all costs. Very negligent prof!

Don't know why people said he's good. Just because he's nice?! He expects students to know HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PostgreSQL. I am trying to learn everything by myself from YouTube and other resources. If you don't know HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PostgreSQL, learn them first, and then take his class. Otherwise, expect a very low grade.

Assignments are quite enjoyable and force you to teach yourself, but his lectures are not great. Lecture slides are disorganized and unhelpful, and the prof rushes through topics without really teaching them. Be ready, anything, even those that he barely explained, are a fair game on the exam. Exam seems to test pretty insignificant or minor info.

Bobby has great lectures, responds fast to emails, assignments were really fun to do. However, the only downside is his tests are multiple choice memorization of little facts in his powerpoints and lectures which is kind of unfair. I got like 67% on the final and still got an A though so it was ok. Overall very very good.

Clear lectures, difficult but practical and fair assignments, good lectures. Exams are on hard side, beware of MC. Really good course for web dev. Bobby is always smiling and approachable, friendly guy. Final was a lot easier than the MT imo. Would take bobby again!

Reasonably difficult assignments and exams. Very good lecturer. Explains things very well and found a very good balance between going into enough details and not going into too much details. Also, he was always in a good mood, smiling, and easy-going and kept me engaged and interested during the lectures with his positive attitude. Great teacher.

He covers everything that is tested in tests and exams in lectures, but u have to work on at least some problems in the textbook for a good grade. The exam questions are not hard, but he doesn't really give much feedback on tests and exams u've done, so it kinda sucks that u have to remember answers u gave and check it with answers he gave.
Lecture notes are lacking, lectures are less informative and helpful than just reading the textbook.

I wouldn't recommend him, he himself doesn't know what he is teaching. Quizzes are way more difficult than those examples taught in class, also midterms and quizzes are not allowed to review. Students cannot know where they are losing marks in the quizzes and midterms. He is very slow in grading. No materials are provided for preparation.

Bro don't take him!!! If you have a choice between async and in-person classes, take the async class and you will thank me later. After each midterm or quiz, he doesn't give your work back and you can't check your mistakes. Quizzes are really difficult, but if you take the async class, you will be able to cheat
Great communication in class, simple assignments but he will torment you during midterm and final exams. Level 1 difficulty in assignment but 5 in exams.

If you skip anything that help you get good grade, you may fail this course because you wont pass your exam. His inclass homework is not related to your final exam. He is a nice person but the things he taught is no use to final exam. Haha

The course seems so easy when you listen to the classes . But , when you work you will understand how hard the course is . I havent used the textbook whole semester . But, I will recommend you to use if you want to get a A grade in this course . Finally , Bobby teaches so good that you will not at all find the course difficult.
Omg he doesnt teach anything. He bring such difficult midterm and final and expect us to know everything. Avoid him at all cost. Dont be fooled by the 4.3 rating here
Take CMPT 276 with anyone other than this guy. He makes you a terrible experience for a course that its fun and important.
One of the worst profs ever. Does nothing.
Hes really nice and gives high marks on assignments, but will absolutely destroy you on exams, resulting in an "average" grade.

No sense of difficulty, expects students to know everything, spent too many weeks on materials available online instead of things that are not then tested on them. The only good thing about him is he speaks clear. I took macm101 with him thought the course was hard but then took it with another prof and realized bobby was not good at teaching it.

Very nice and sweet professor, but don't expect a good grade. Like someone else said assignments are given full marks even if they don't work properly. The midterm and final was quite unfair and they weren't curved (as far as I can tell). A lot of information in this course was from the textbook which you definitely had to read.
Bobby is a nice person, but the difficulty of the course is much more than what he teaches.

The only good thing about bobby is that he's nice. His videos are alright but his assignments take way too much time and are given full marks even if they dont work. Midterms/finals are 60 average and terrible.

Nice teacher, but attend pop quiz and weekly HWK. Don't be cheat or copy code from internet or other people, otherwise, it's may lose marks. Easy to pass if you have basic knowledge about programming.

He's great imo. His lectures are mostly demo-based and clear to follow. He could do more prep beforehand though because he sometimes makes mistake during lectures. Homeworks and exams are fair; everything should be covered during his in-class demos. He is ready to help you during office hours. It's rare to see a good CMPT prof like him at SFU.

This is the second class I've had with Bobby now. First was MACM 101. Bobby is an amazing prof and will always give you the grade you deserve, provided you work on what you need to work on. He's extremely helpful and I would 110% take any class he teaches.

He's a decent lecturer for sure, and the assignments in the class were reasonable. However, both the midterm and final exam were drastically harder than they needed to be. Students who put time and effort into studying the materials had no more advantage than someone who winged it. No prep for the final - Exams did not test knowledge of the course.

Lectures are pretty boring, they put me to sleep half the time and their is barely any study material for exams. His study material for the final is just a list of topics covered. Assignments are time consuming, but relatively easy. Bobby seems like a nice guy though.

Not sure if it was because he doesn't teach this class often, but there is no material to prepare you for his tests other than his slides. No practice questions or review to give you an idea of what to expect. Midterms and finals are hard, but on one good note he marks assignments really easily so you can get free marks from that.

Professors like this (only had him for 276) should not be allowed to "teach". Although he can speak clearly, he expects students to know html,CSS, JavaScript, and PostGreSQL. having experience with non will grant you a C- average as thats the class average. Not to mention the countless people who drop this course.
Clear , easy to understand , nice prof, easy assignment and quiz and exam, easy to get high grade

Bobby gives easy marks on assignment, however his exam are difficult as usual. It is a norm that his class avg for exams are barely passing or fail, just work hard on the assignments, and it should be an easy B.
Bobby gives tough exams but assignments we're graded that harshly. Lectures were pretty decent because he always spends enough time answering questions. Office hours at 830 am are negative.
Concepts are vague exam questions are vague assignments are vague

I don't know why he make every class he teaches more difficult than other teachers. And many knowledges he teaches are also unfamiliar to him. For midterm, 35min for 25 questions? Or 35min for 32 questions? Don't choose him except one level class.

Never take a class with bobby Chan. He makes any course extra hard for no reason. His exams were 40 questions in 35 minutes even though our lecture was 50 minutes long. He even cancels office hours and issues weekly assignments that feel like projects. Also its been months and our midterm isnt even marked yet. Avoid avoid avoid

He won't show your sheet of midterm, and he marks it really slow. The difficulty of the exam just between the question from the textbook and lecture example. So, recommend finishing the question in the textbook regularly. Overall, he teaches it easily, but have to know it's not that easy.

Good project class (similar to CMPT373) but for web applications. generally just a tutorial type class. Gets u familiar with IIS and Internet structure. I have no clue as to why this class needs a final or midterm. Just tests random trivia. The project is fun, and useful. Bobby is nice and competent, he just needs to prep his materials a bit more.

"I probably should have set this up earlier"- Bobby Chan. Its clear he's a complete amateur in terms of web development Often making mistakes, to demo basic PHP and node scripts. So the class sits in silence waiting to diagnose issue. 90 percent of questions he doesn't know, so he responds let me get back to you next class (and forgets).

Doesn't like recording lectures, so go to class. His slides are mainly pictures and don't make much sense when you go back through them. Lots of demos in class so be prepared to pay attention. Group project is a month long, pretty easy. Not much help given for the individual assignments. Nice guy, but disorganized.

He is very unorganized, unprepared for lectures, and seems busy doing researches or something. His assignments are okay except the last one which he asks us to deploy our app to GCP without any relevant info how to do so. His exams are tricky. Overall, I would not recommend taking his 470.

I remember the average score he gives is just C- . and almost every thing about for project, you need to learn by your selves. and the test maybe test everything in his notes. no example for midterm and final

Bobby is an exceptional prof with excellent knowledge of the course material. As it is mentioned many times, his exams are unnecessarily difficult on arbitrary material, some briefly mentioned in lectures, others from course appendices. Be prepared for fairly difficult tests, but a great learning experience if you take any course with Bobby!

Bobby is a nice person, but I really don't like the way he teaches 276. The project and lecture content are wildly different, which means that everything you need for the project you'll be learning yourself. The project will eat up most of your time, but the final is worth 43%, and tests you on random trivia from his slides. Lots of memorization.

Nice person but his criteria is unclear and the grading is quite harsh. Exams are weighted heavily and really expect you to be able to apply the material. Tests you on things he expects you to have self learned (never mentioned in lecture).

This course is testing your ability to google. Prof is real nice but sadly does not teach much about assignment stuff. So if you are not very familiar with html, css and javascript then be prepared to spend hell lots of time on assignments.

Bobbys lectures were can't miss. He did a great job of explaining the concepts and answering questions. When it came to grading things got noticeably worse. The turnaround for assignment grades was a month. He doesn't offer any practice questions which made exams terrifying. The coding section of the final was especially difficult. Learned a lot!
the lectures are well organized and he gives interesting examples to real life software development processes. study his slides and you'll do fine on the midterm and finals.

I LOVED Bobby! Honestly, one of the best professors I've had at SFU. He actually EXPLAINS things rather than read off word-for-word what's on the slides (which unfortunately a lot of professors do...). He's super engaging, his exams are fair IF you attend class and take notes. His questions are typically what's discussed in class, so don't skip.
Nice guy but extremely hard tests and marking. Lack of study material for exams also didnt help. Class average was a C. Dont recommend.
Bobby gives really good lectures and is more than willing to help. The only problem is his tests are really hard, and he does not give any study materials for tests.

Bobby is a really nice guy, but he taught CMPT470 in a WRONG way. I think this course should be more practical. Bobby focused too much on some theories that can be learned in other courses (ex CMPT371). Moreover, his final exam was one of the most useless exams that test memorization skills.

His is doing demo all the time. For a 50min course, he probably will typing for 20-30 minutes. You will feel sleepy in his class. You won't get a high-level understanding of Java by taking his course. If you want to see Bobby doing coding at the lecture, then take this course. He is a nice guy though.

Bobby was very disorganized this term. The notes were awful and do not explain the content. The class is not meant to have an exam, but he added some. These exams weren't fair, as they asked for trivial knowledge (like the features of IIS) that shouldn't be memorized. He also made us use PHP but did not give a reasonable justification.
Final is not that hard. Bobby is friendly, always smiling, patient. I want to take his class again. Give him 1000 out of 5, hahaha :)

I have taken Bobby's course twice. He loves testing students on obscure useless trivia that you will never apply. I took his CMPT470 class and he chose to use PHP as the server-side language. His rationale for limiting us to the language for forcing us to use this dinosaur of a language for our project was that it would allow him to test us. By the
final exam went beyond my study level and he said that exam will only cover study materials :(

Good lecturer but his class was very poorly run. 4 assignments and 2 midterms. By the last week of class, only Midterm 1 and Assignment 1 had been marked (got one assignment back AFTER the final), which doesn't give you a lot of time to find out / improve your weaknesses in time for the exam . There's no practice questions or sample exams either :(

Bobby is a well Respected Prof and is very clear when it comes to explaining,his notes were sometimes messy but it wasn't that much of a Problem, Very good professor except for the long time it took to mark assignments (a month and a half for assignments haha)
CAN I GIVE HIM 10 OUT OF 5
the best teacher ever!

The project is a hit or miss depending on your group. I unfortunately had a bad group which required me to complete the entire project practically by myself. The exams are based off the slides and you'll probably get away with not attending lectures.

Bobby is a really nice guy and cares about his students. The concepts in his class are not too complex. However, his exams are odd, and aren't out of many marks, which may be tough for some people. Also, he really does not provide much practice material, and is even lazy with giving proper answers to assignments. Overall, he's decent.
hes amazing and super nice. cares about your future and also kind of funny no suprises. gives it how it is.
Great prof, detailed notes!
Best prof ever!

Best professor I've ever had. I had him for both MACM 101 and CMPT 125. Don't take those courses with anyone else if you have the option of taking it with Bobby. He's super helpful and explains everything very clearly. Although his tests can be quite tough, he is always there for you to help you prepare. Wholesome guy.

Bobby's first time teaching MACM 101. His lectures are very clear and concise. He goes very in depth on counting(VERY IMPORTANT) and FOCUSES mostly on applications rather than theory. His two midterms are a bit too easy in comparison with other profs BUT his final exam was HARD. If you're a first year, prepare to study A LOT. Especially for finals!
A really nice prof.

Bobby is a legend among the CMPT profs at SFU. His lovely smile really compliments his teaching style! But be careful! Behind his smile is a really nasty midterm that will make sure you never took his course! xD
He is a really great teacher, yet the subject is heavy! His lectures are great, but I recommend to do problems from problem set as they are similar to those on quizzes and exams!!

Nice guy and is happy to clarify. Assignments are fair, but different TAs may mark them differently. Exams are relatively difficult/tricky even if you're already proficient in C programming. You need to really know the theory.

Bobby is clearly a good guy and teaches well. But the concepts in 125 are oversimplified relative to the exams while the labs are overly complicated. Lab 1 begins well and gradual progress is made, but then about halfway through it cranks things up to 11 and you quickly fall behind. If you have no experience coding: AVOID!
Bobby is the best! The in class assignment is very helpful.
Great prof, explains stuff well and is available outside of class too help. Would recommend.

It's very helpful to review lecture slides thoroughly and come to every class. If you miss a class, you probably will be fairly behind. Cue cards definitely help to remember theorems and remember to practice implementing the data structures and algorithms discussed.
Great prof, cares about students. Office hours extremely helpful!
Final Question is kinda hard. Make sure you work for it. His questions pattern are way different than in class notes. Unfortunately his notes not helpful.
Highly recommended, he is very kind and helpful, he explains material very clearly. Exams were hard, you really need to know the material well.

He gives assignments before we cover the material and often doesn't cover it until before it's due, which is stressful when you have other classes and a job. He doesn't post any assignment solutions or exam solutions, so I didn't learn much.

Bobby is one of my favourites! He's so nice, cracks jokes, non-aggressive, very good at answering questions and doesn't continue the lecture unless you understand his answers. Although he doesn't make his own notes, he's good at relaying material to students. The class was a pleasure to take. Almost everyone loves him.
I find it very confusing when he makes up his own terminology and never explains what he means by it

Bobby is a really nice guy who knows his stuff and will explain things to you again if you come in for office hours. The only complaint I have is the manner in which he gave assignments. All assignments were given out way before the material was covered in lecture and sometimes he only finished covering that material 2 days before it was due.

The course is meant to challenge you and you are expected to learn concepts on your own time. Fortunately, his lectures prepare you well enough to have resources to solve the assignment problems. The assignments are often vague but contacting him, he clarifies. Note that the exams are not difficult so long as you understand every piece of material.
A great teacher and really respected by students. The stuff that he teaches is not easy but the way that he teaches is so clear that he makes it easy to understand.

Bobby is super willing to help his students and explains material very clearly. But he's disorganised. Questions were sloppily written, assignments were unclear, and I wonder whether he even reviewed the slides (borrowed from another prof) before lecture. He made the exams and assignments easier as the course went on because averages were low.
You can tell this is his first time teaching the course since he is not clear about his own grading scale. Seems like a nice guy, but not very clear in what he wants.

He's really nice as a person, but studying for exams is a pain. They are not very hard, but he does not tell you what's on it or what to study. There are 4 assignments for this class and so far they harder than expected. They are worth very little for the amount of work put into it. Concepts are self taught. He is only in his office once a week :(
One of the best professor ever! Take it and you won't regret it!

Extremely helpful during office hours and lectures are clear and straight forward. Would take another Computing course with this professor. Wish the administration would make him a complete faculty lecturer instead of a limited term one.
Best prof ever, he genuinely cares about his students. Take him!
Very nice professor, best teacher that I ever met. Hope he has CMPT128 next semester.
He really knows how to explain his stuff. If you ask a question in class, he will answer it and make sure you understand it.
Just enjoy his lectures.
Nice clear prof! I really wished he'd upload his notes though.
He teaches well but misleads you concerning midterms so aspect to be surprised on midterms. HE does not make his tests based on the textbook HW, so doing text book HW is a big waist of time.

Bobby is a great professor, any help you need, he will give it. He's mostly pretty clear in lecture. The course I found to be very difficult, thankfully it's the last one of its kind I have to take to get a CMPT minor. You absolutely need to attend lecture because the textbook is useless.

Bobby is a very nice guy. His friendly demeanor makes him very approachable inside and outside of lecture. He is very organized with good notes that you print out and follow along with in lecture. His midterms are pretty easy. Overall, a very good professor. Be warned, CMPT 150 itself is not a fun course, but Bobby makes it bearable.
Best prof ever no kidding. He is very helpful, email him if you got any questions.
Very friendly, cute, nice, the best professor ever got during 3 years in University so far
great prof I had even met
The best prof in sfu!
He is cute. Lecture clearly.
Great Prof
Great teacher, nice explain,and try to give you high grade. he is your good choice.
Modest prof.

It has been a privilege taking Bobby's CMPT course. He is super helpful and encouraging, so no one hesitates to ask questions in his class! Also, I like his smile which makes him more friendly-looking and approachable. The textbook was nearly useless. I used it only for practice questions. He is definitely one of the best profs you can take in SFU.

Super young but super inspirational professor! He's working on his PhD in Theoretical Comp Sci and what he's teaching (MACM101 and CMPT150's course content) is also what he loves doing the most. He also has a very nice way of lecturing and appears to be very helpful and understanding of students' problems.
Teaches very very well, and clears doubts if a need arises. If you can take his class, please take it! And hes super cute too!
Great prof, responds to feedback, actually cares about students. Material for this course is boring and dry though.
BEST PROF HANDS DOWN! Clear, understandable and helpful. Would definitely recommend taking courses with this prof.

He is the best prof ever!I love him! he is cute too.material isn't that easy, require lots of thinking! He is so good at teaching that I can learn most from him! I went to his office hour every week, I didn't need to study too much before finals. I was very relaxed before final. Definitely recommended! We love Bobby Chan!
Best Teacher I Have Ever Studied From. Great Verbal Skills, Helps Understand The Concept With Utmost Ease. Would Recommend Everyone To Take The Course From Him If He Is An Option.

Recommended to print notes for lectures. Had a hard time following what was going on because I prefer writing notes from scratch, but once I started printing them, lectures became less painful. He goes very fast & it's difficult to decipher his notes afterwards. He's got excellent verbal explanations though. Doesn't give much practice material
best cmpt prof i met so far.in last day lecture, everyone stand up and applaud for him.strongly recommand to take everything with him.
Very friendly, caring and nice. But tests are disaster. Class average around 60% for midterm and final - midterm worth20% and final worth50%.
BEST PROF EVER
Amazing
He is a awesome prof
He's amazing, he smiles all the time, he's extremely helpful and he's lectures are actually helpful
he's so cute!O(∩_∩)O~~ also he's ready to answer ur questions all the time!!
Note is clear, you can download the powerpoint from his webpage. Attending the lecture is useful as he write down key note on the powerpoint during lecture and those note will be covered in exam
He is excellent and extremely helpful! :)
He's good and extremely helpful ! :)
He is extremely nice and helpful and his exams are fair. He's also pretty cute lol
GREAT Proff..
HE IS THE BEST
He is brilliant,calm and always willing to explain to my understanding. Keep it up Bobby.
An excellent teacher!! Notes are very helpful. Assignments are fair but he'll provide lots of help. Study his notes and you'll do fine on the exams. Yup.. he's hot too =)

If he could teach all my cmpt courses, I wouldn't hav to use ratemyprofs.com ever again!! Dont be fooled by his age, although he's a student himself I think?, he was an excellent prof who explained the topics and information well. He used slides to teach 150 and gave relevant exmples in lecture. His midterms were fair and based on exactly whatwas

Very knowledgeable. Clear and organized lectures. Very fair assignments and grading. Easy to talk to and loves discussions and questions during class. I would certainly recommend him. He is a very calm, quiet and well spoken person which can make his lectures almost soothing and relaxing to listen to. This can be a problem if youre already tired.

He's a super nice guy, very knowledgeable and passionate about the material. 5 written assignments were fairly easy (24/30 average ish), midterm and final were fairly difficult (50 average ish) but they're scaled so you'll be fine. I missed a bunch of lectures but read every textbook chapter and got a B+. Read 10 pages a day and you're good to go!

Joseph Peters was a great prof, particularly compared to my expectation of CMPT 307. His quizzes were straightforward - they directly tested your understanding. The homework was tough but marked quite easily. His choice of textbook (CLRS) was also great. Joseph's lectures simply helped us understand the content in the book.

He is nice but you just can't really focus on his lecture with his low, soft and deep voice reading the ppt in class... It always makes half class students fell asleep... Maybe Prof peters can change to other lecture style somedays...

The content of CMPT 307 requires students to develop strong problem solving abilities over the length of the course which requires you to put in a lot of effort. Joseph Peter's lectures and homework really helped me to develop these skills and succeed in the course. He is a funny and clearly a caring person. I would certainly recommend him.

307 is a tough course and I would recommend taking it with Joseph Peters. The quiz material comes from the textbook and lectures so don't miss any classes, and he is very helpful in office hours which you will need for the assignments. The final is much harder than the quizzes.

He's probably one of the toughest prof in SFU. You may be tricked into believing that he's a lenient grader at the end of the semester, so you give him a good review. Do not believe him. He failed many students in my class because he graded his final exam brutally. If you do bad in the final, he will fail you regardless of your overall performance.
Hard to follow. He is not a good lecturer. Avoid this class.

An awesome instructor. Caring, explains concepts clearly, and is very helpful in office hours. One criticism - I feel like I got away with some BS/handwaving. The material is hard, but important to learn, so I definitely cared about understanding more than marks. Beware - the final is harder than the quizzes, and is worth 50%.

Recommended for 307; course is mostly about solving word problems rather than mostly proofs so it's an interesting course in that way. Homework and quizzes can be tough but he is very helpful and friendly in office hours.
Marks were based only on quizzes and final. Tough material so expect to study a lot.

I took this class because it was a pre-requisite for Distributed Systems and I wasn't necessarily expecting to be that interesting. However, I very much enjoyed this class. Dr. Peters is a fantastic lecturer, one of the best I've had at SFU, and he was helpful during office hours and after class. I learned a lot from this class.
The prof's level of interest in the subject is very high so you will get lots of tidbits that is real world compared to other classes. Highly recommend him.
Great prof. Lectures were interesting, and he listens to students' suggestions

Dr.Peters does a good job teaching a rather difficult subject. This is perhaps the hardest CMPT course I have taken yet, yet most likely the one I have enjoyed the most so far. He teaches in a way that encourages actual learning and thinking. Coming up with a solution to problems is not easy, as you will find in this course.

He is one of the best professors I had. He rewards people for creative solutions to problems even if they don't work out in the end. He encourages discussion in class which unfortunately not many take advantage of. Exams require creative thinking so if you just want to memorize something, then you should rethink the reason why you are in university

I always seem to be running out of time on quizzes, and half of the course grade are based on those quizzes. His notes are useless, so just study from the textbook. Would not take another class with him again.

Nice guy, but avoid his class, I don't understand how he expects you to finish his 50 minute midterm in time, hard to believe he is a full professor and still doesn't understand this, how many more years until you figure this out Joe, maybe you don't really care although it seems like you do. Proof, some can do, some can teach ...

Marking is based only on many quizzes and final exam. 20 minute quizzes were given about once a week or so. I found these quizzes hard and also he doesn't give us enough time. Class average on these quizzes were low. The instructor does not give out useful notes so be prepared to study from the textbook yourself.
Really difficult prof. Would not take another course with him again.

The prof was 100% dedicated to making the learning experience best possible for the students. Even then, the material is difficult and hard to focus at, so either read carefully in advance or prepare to study on your own after the lecture.

Text is excellent. Good lecturer. Lecture notes are so mathematical and not too usefull in prep for exams. Tricky exam questions - was somewhat deceived by easy subject that turned difficult. Project can be difficult - so start early.
No Comments
No tricky question. Strict Forward Test Questions. Just read the book and you'll go okay. His lectures are very clear. Work hard on assignments.
His assignments are empty-hearted and exams are ill-fated.
Nice attitude. His lectures seems simple but assignments and exams are tough. you need to do a lot of research in order to do assignments. Final exam is killer.
He is a helpful and nice professor.

Uwe is extremely helpful both in class and in office hours. He has a passion for cybersecurity and wants everyone in his class to succeed. Sometimes he will go on tangents in lecture, but overall he covers the course material well. His assignments are relevant to the course and explained well. Great professor and great person.

Most of this course is based on group project, 30% Tests, Uwe is Knowledegble and will respond to questions really fast and I would recommend him to anyone intrested in field of cybersecurity and Machine learning.

Listen to what others say here. This class is as boring as it gets. Just memorize everything (yes everything,even the tutorials) and you'll do great. He talks so slow repeats the same boring things. This class has more to do with modelling then with Software engineering.

Very nice professor! Extremely helpful when you go to his office during the office hour. He is very patient at explaining concepts that one doesn't understand. Exams and assignments are marked fair. is there a limit to how a person can be boring? this man give you the answer.. NO! Its lecture are not informative at all, if you want to pass the exam just read very carefully required readings, slides, do homeworks and look carefully at ASM examples. Don't take this class..

You must be crazy to take cmpt125 with him. He already explained HALF of 225!! first class on arrays already, then you'll learn linked list, stack/queue..etc. what the heck??? he spent 2 lectures going over EVERY loop and assumes you know everything. good luck to you all if ur in his class.

Not a very good teacher. He doesn't explain much, especially since there are no pre reqs for the class. One of the Best profs I ever had in my life. His knowledge in this field is so deep. I Thoroughly enjoyed every class.He expects more from his students, and always welcomes discussions. He is absolutely the worst ever. No joke. One earlier rater said s/he was able to talk to the department head and got permission to retake the course with someone else. Now why didn't I do that? Fellow students: keep this bit of knowledge handy. You too can take control of the quality of your own education!!!

He's very clear, helpful and easy to talk to but he's one of the hardest profs you'll meet. If you don't have interest for the course, you might as well drop it. Only recommended for people that are good at studying on their own. Odd scaling method, instead of scaling from average, he scales from the highest mark. Which may or may not be helpful.

People need to take the general pattern of ratings here seriously. He is one of those teachers that has a penchant for making a course much harder than it needs to be. No tutorials to practice on the stuff he teaches. Last coding assignment super difficult. Messy/useless notes. Best to rely on textbook instead of his lectures.

Lou is not as bad as many people say. His assignments are VERY difficult, make sure to find some friends good at programming. Mid-Term and Finals are fair.
useless lecture, hard ass assignments... DONT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!

I got an A in CMPT 120 3 years ago, so I took CMPT 126 to get my GPA to 3.00, now I may get a D or F. My beefs with the prof are like that of most people here. Lack of syntax teaching, being unable to carry away useful info from lectures, unapproachable for help, etc. It seems he can teach upper-level courses well, but not lower-level ones.

Explains theoretically but says not a word about syntax that. ONLY answers questions during class. Extract from course outline regarding office hours "you knock, you have about a minute to convince me that your problem is sufficiently urgent that I should continue to interrupt what I was doing in order to give your problem further attention."

Just thought I'd counter some of the negative reviews here - Yes, 250 is a hard course, but Lou was one of the best teacher's I've had. I'm sorry you did poorly, but don't blame him because you didn't get it. He teaches clearly, he is interested, he is willing to help students, and the course follows the text, so just read it for clarifications.

After coming out of CMPT 120 with an A+ with no prior programming experience, this course gave me a rude awakening. He spent no time teaching the syntax, making it incredibly difficult just to finish assignments. I thought there was something wrong with me, but now that I'm taking other CMPT courses with few problems, I realize it's this prof.

If you don't know Java, I would not recommend you taking this course with this prof. No Java Syntax is taught in this course. If you have a strong background in object-orientated programming then maybe this course is for you. This professor teaches in Java in theory. Assignments were impossible to complete, and did not aid in learning the material.

Not recommended. Lectures are irrelevant to assignments and boring. This course picks up where 120 left off in terms of concepts, but leaves no time for students to learn Java syntax. No in-class demonstrations in Java IDE. Assignments were difficult and seemed to have no point. However, his midterm and final exam were fair and not to difficult.

Expected to know how to program in java. Fair exams, the assignments made you think but they were just ridiculous and over the top for a first year/ beginner programmer. Overall prof was a nice guy but I recommend not taking this class with him unless u have some programming background.
never ever thinking about his teaching will be useful and comprehensive. he just thinks you know everything but we are not!!!!!exams and homeworks are ridiculous, just ridiculous!!!!

class was super easy. assignments were a joke. terrible prof though, but the course is easy...what more can i say...
